steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'praise'
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation of Allah's name
Added لِ at beginning to show this belongs to or is for Allah. The initial alif is dropped when لِ is added. This word shows who should receive the praise (الْحَمْدُ) mentioned before it.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the praise (الْحَمْدُ) mentioned before it
Added ال with shadda to turn a basic pointer word into a connector word. This connects 'Allah' to what He did - 'created'.

steps.m: to create or bring something new into existence
he created (subject of the sentence, creates السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word is about Allah creating the heavens and earth.
Changed the root into a noun for the thing above us
Makes it plural to show there are multiple heavens
The word gets an -i ending because it's being described by the verb 'he created' (خَلَقَ)
Adds 'the' to show these are the specific heavens being talked about
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make the common noun meaning 'earth'
Added ال to make it specific
Added و to connect it to the previous word (heavens)
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being created in the phrase 'created the heavens and the earth'

steps.m: to make or set something in a certain way
he made (connected to خَلَقَ - he created)
steps.n: Added vowels to show this happened in the past. This action is connected to Allah's creation mentioned earlier with خَلَقَ (he created).

steps.m: and he made (connected to خَلَقَ - he created)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this with Allah's previous action of creating (خَلَقَ)
Changes from a verb meaning 'to wrong' into a noun meaning 'darkness' by using the pattern fu'l
Makes it refer to one specific instance of darkness
Changes it to show many different types of darkness
Makes it refer to specific darknesses by adding 'the'
Shows this word is being described by another word
Changed from action to the thing itself (light)
Added 'the' to make it specific. The ن gets a shadda when ال is added.
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it with previous words. The اَل changes to ٱل after وَ.

steps.m: and the light (as object of جَعَلَ/made)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object (thing being acted upon) of the verb جَعَلَ (made/created)

This word connects two ideas in the Quran - first Allah is praised (ٱلْحَمْدُ), then (ثُمَّ) it mentions those who reject Him (يَعْدِلُونَ). The shadda (ّ) makes the 'm' sound stronger to emphasize the connection.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun meaning 'who'. It's a fixed grammatical word rather than being derived from a root.
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding ين makes it plural and shows it's the subject of the verb يَعْدِلُونَ (they deviate/turn away) in this ayah. It refers to those who disbelieve (كَفَرُوا).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb showing one person did the action
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word connects to يَعْدِلُونَ (they set up equals) in this ayah, showing the same group doing both actions.
Doubling of middle letter ب creates intensive form showing someone who does this action completely
Adds 'by/with' meaning through بِ prefix. This shows how they are setting up equals with their lord.
Added هِمْ (their) to show it's their lord. This completes the phrase showing how they set up equals with their own lord.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, kasra on second root letter, and damma at end

steps.m: they make equals (with Allah) (referring to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟)
steps.n: Adds ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to 'those who disbelieve' (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟) who are setting up equals with their Lord.
This is a pronoun that refers back to Allah in this context. It's used as the subject of the verb خَلَقَكُم (created you).
This is the base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to what's being described
Added ٱل with shadda to make it a specific relative pronoun. In this ayah, it connects 'He' (هُوَ) with what comes after it (the one who created you).

steps.m: he created (subject of هُوَ ٱلَّذِى - He is the One who)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This is describing what Allah did, as shown by هُوَ ٱلَّذِى (He is the One who) before it.

steps.m: he created you all (object is مِّن طِينٍ - from clay)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show who was created (you all). The next words مِّن طِينٍ tell us you were created from clay.
The shadda on م indicates assimilation that occurs when certain letters precede it in connected speech
Added shadda on م because it comes after the word خَلَقَكُم in speech. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding the kasra vowel mark turns the basic concept into a proper noun

steps.m: clay/mud (as material you were created from)
steps.n: Added tanween kasratayn (ٍ) at the end to show this is any clay/mud, not a specific one. This word describes what Allah created humans from.
This is a connecting word that shows one event happens after another with a pause in between. Here it connects Allah setting a term (قَضَىٰٓ أَجَلًا) with people doubting (أَنتُمْ تَمْتَرُونَ).
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he' form by adding fathas and changing final ي to ى
Added small alif (ٰ) after ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Added small vertical line (ٓ) to show a slight pause in pronunciation
Added vowel marks (fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'a deadline' instead of 'to set a deadline'

steps.m: a set time/term (object of قَضَىٰٓ - he decreed)
steps.n: Adding tanween fatha shows this is the object being decreed in the phrase قَضَىٰٓ أَجَلًا (he decreed a term)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create a noun meaning the time limit itself. This word is what was 'decreed' by Allah in the previous word قَضَىٰٓ (he decreed).
Adds conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'

steps.m: and an appointed time (subject of قَضَىٰٓ)
steps.n: Adds tanween dammah to make the noun indefinite
Added مُ at the beginning and changed the ending to show this is something that received a name (like when you name a baby)
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م and an -an ending to show this refers to a specific appointed time
Adding kasra changes this into a word showing location or presence

steps.m: near, at (ready to connect to something)
steps.n: Adding fatha prepares the word to connect to what comes after it
Adding هُ shows this is about Allah, with ۥ showing extra respect
This word connects two events in order - first Allah created you from clay, then He set a term. The ثُمَّ shows there was a gap or delay between these events.
This is the basic form of the pronoun used when talking to multiple people, like saying 'you all' in English.

steps.m: you all (when talking to a group)
steps.n: Adding م at the end makes it clear we're talking to multiple people, like saying 'you guys' or 'you all' in English.
This root carries the idea of questioning or being unsure about something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed middle vowel to show present tense
Added ت in the middle to show doubting within yourself (reflexive meaning)
Added ون at the end to show many people are doing the action
This is a personal pronoun that refers to Allah in this ayah, who is the one who knows (يَعْلَمُ) what is mentioned after
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the ayah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added U mark at end to show this is the subject/doer
Added AL at beginning to make it specific/definite
This is a basic word showing location or being inside something. In this ayah, it shows Allah is present in the heavens.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'sky' by adding ء and long alif at the end
Added feminine plural ending وات to show multiple heavens/skies
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س (adding shadda) because it comes after ال
Changed ال to ٱل, replaced long alifs with ٰ, and added kasra at end to show it's connected to another word
Basic preposition showing location. Here it connects to both 'the heavens' and 'the earth' to show where Allah exists.

steps.m: and in/within (location in ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ and ٱلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this phrase with the previous statement about Allah

steps.m: and in/within (location in ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ and ٱلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in/on)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and adding ُ at end. This verb connects to تَكْسِبُونَ (you earn) in the ayah, showing what he knows.
Root related to secrecy
Adding the kasra changes this from a root into a noun meaning 'secret'

steps.m: your (all of you) secret (object of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show the secret belongs to multiple people. This word is what Allah knows (يَعْلَمُ) in this verse, along with جَهْرَكُمْ (what you do openly).
Root related to being public or visible
Changes the action into a noun meaning something done openly or publicly. This word is what Allah knows (object of يَعْلَمُ - He knows) in this verse.
Adds 'your' (plural) to the end
Adds conjunction meaning 'and'
Basic meaning of knowing or having knowledge
Adding the basic vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'knew' to present tense 'knows' by adding يَ at the start and changing the vowels
Added وَ at beginning to connect with previous phrase 'He is Allah in the heavens and on earth'
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which'. Here it connects to تَكْسِبُونَ (you earn) to mean 'what you earn'.

steps.m: what (that which) (object of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: Added fatha mark to show proper pronunciation. This word is the object of يَعْلَمُ (He knows) in this ayah.
Root related to earning or gaining something
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to present tense and second person by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural marker ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a basic particle that can either ask questions (what?) or make something negative (not). Here it makes the verb تَأْتِيهِم negative, meaning 'does not come to them'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. The combination وَمَا is very common in the Quran for connecting sentences while making them negative.
Connected the root letters together
Adding ت at start shows this is about 'she'
Adding هم at the end shows this is happening to 'them'
Added هِم at the end to show who receives the action. This refers to the signs/verses (آيَات) coming to the people being discussed.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. It's like saying 'from' or 'of' in English.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a word ending in a vowel (ءَايَةٍ). This is just a pronunciation rule and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: Root letters أ-ي-ي became ءَاي (sign/miracle) by adding a long ا after the first letter
Added ة to show this is one specific sign or miracle
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (any sign) and is being acted upon by the word مِنْ (from) before it
This is a basic preposition that shows origin, source or starting point. It is not derived from a root.

The shadda is added because this word comes after a voweled letter. This helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it connects the word ءَايَةٍ (sign) to تَأْتِيهِم (comes to them), showing where the signs come from.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear
a sign, miracle, or clear proof
steps.n: The ة at the end shows this is one sign/miracle
The hamza (ء) was written differently but means the same thing - it's still 'sign' or 'miracle'
Added ات to show there are many signs
The word changed to show it belongs to 'their Lord' by adding a kasra (the small line under) at the end. Also, the middle alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) on top.
The root 'r-b-b' becomes 'Lord' and the kasra ending shows it belongs to or is connected to something after it (like 'Lord of...')

steps.m: their Lord (referring to those who reject the signs in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding هِمْ shows this Lord belongs to or is connected to them
This is a basic exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. It's one of the most common Arabic words.

steps.m: except (excluding what comes after from مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ 'any sign')
steps.n: The word إِلَّا is actually a complete word meaning 'except'. It's not a transformation from إِل. The shadda and alif are essential parts of the word that help it connect to and exclude what comes after it from مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ (any sign).
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he was' in the past
Added alif after first letter because و is a weak letter that changes to alif in past tense
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word is connected to مُعْرِضِينَ (turning away) in this ayah.
This is a preposition showing distance or topic

steps.m: away from it (referring to آيَة - sign)
steps.n: This word shows what they are turning away from - the signs (آيَة). It connects to مُعْرِضِينَ (turning away) in the ayah.

steps.m: to turn away or aside from something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at the beginning and changed vowels to show someone who does this action

steps.m: those who turn away (object of كَانُوا)
steps.n: Added ين with fatha to make it plural and show it's the object of كَانُوا (they were) in this ayah
This is an emphasis particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before. Here it connects to the verb كَذَّبُوا (they denied)

steps.m: to deny, to reject as false

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this isn't just simple denial, but strong or repeated rejection. This word describes what they did when the truth came to them (جَآءَهُمْ).
The وا ending shows this is a group (they) doing the action in the past. They are the ones who denied the truth when it came to them.
The second ق is doubled with shadda to strengthen the meaning. This word is what they denied in كَذَّبُوا (they denied)
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Adding kasra at the end shows this word follows the preposition بِ

steps.m: with/by the truth (describing how they denied it)
steps.n: Adding بِ shows this truth was the means or way they denied something. In this ayah, it shows they denied something even though it came with/by the truth.
This is the basic negative particle that shows something didn't happen
Adding ّا changes this from just 'not' to 'when' - it connects two events in time, showing one happens right after another. Here it connects with جَآءَهُمْ (came to them)
The root letters changed to make past tense 'he came' - the middle letter ي became ا
Changed alif to madda (آ) to show emphasis in the action of coming

steps.m: came to them (فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِيهِمْ - contrasts with future coming)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who was affected by the coming. This past coming contrasts with the future coming mentioned right after in فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِيهِمْ (soon will come to them).
Added vowel marks to show this is a word about future time. سَوْفَ is stronger and more certain than سَ in indicating future time.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of what came before. This connects to the next word يَأْتِيهِمْ (it will come to them) to show what will happen.
The root أتي becomes يَأْتِي by adding يَ at the start to show 'he' in present tense, like saying 'he is coming' instead of just 'come'
Added هِمْ at the end to show who receives the action (them). This connects to the previous words in the ayah فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا۟ ("for they denied") by referring back to the same group.
Adding the vowels turns it into a noun for one piece of news
This makes it plural, showing many pieces of news
The U sound at the end shows this word is doing the action (subject)
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the word أَنۢبٰٓؤُا۟ (news/information) in this ayah. It's showing that the news/information they used to mock will come to them.
Added vowels to connect the letters together
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter
Adding وا changes 'he' to 'they' and shows this happened in the past
Added kasra to show how the letter is pronounced
Added هِ to show what is being referred to (it/him)

steps.m: with/by it/him (referring to الْحَقِّ - the truth)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This word refers back to 'the truth' (الْحَقِّ) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to mock or make fun of

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show they're doing this repeatedly and for their own amusement
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding 'did' at the start of an English sentence
Adding ل adds emphasis to show this is definitely true

steps.m: did not/have not (asking about يَرَوْا۟ - they see)
steps.n: Adding م makes the question negative. This word is asking about يَرَوْا۟ (they see) in this ayah, asking 'Have they not seen?'
Changed the root letters into past tense 'he saw' by adding the vowel marks (fat-ha)
Added يَ at beginning to make it present tense. The أ is dropped due to Arabic rules for weak letters.
Added وْا to show multiple people. The word comes after أَلَمْ which makes it a negative question: 'did they not see?'

steps.m: how many (referring to أَهْلَكْنَا - we destroyed)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about quantity. In this ayah, it's asking about how many generations were destroyed, showing emphasis on the large number.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added أ (hamza) at the start to change from 'he perished' to 'he made something/someone perish' (meaning 'he destroyed')
Added نا at the end to change from 'he destroyed' to 'we destroyed'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects to قَبْلِهِم (before them) to show 'from before them'
This forms a noun meaning 'before' that works with مِن (from) to tell about time. The fatha and sukun connect the letters to make it a noun.
The kasra shows this word is owned by something (in this case هِم meaning 'them')
The possessive pronoun هِم is attached to show who/what is being referred to
Same as مِن but with emphasis (shadda) due to assimilation with following letter
Forms a noun meaning a group of people living in the same time period
Adds tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (any generation) and is being acted upon by the word مِن (from) before it

steps.m: to give power or make firm

steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he gave power'
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Adding نا shows that 'we' did the action instead of 'he'
Adding هُمْ shows who received the power
This is a preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects with الْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where something is located.
Basic noun form of 'earth/land' with marks to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added 'al' (ٱلْ) at the beginning to make it specific and kasra (ِ) at the end because it comes after the word 'in' (فِى) in the ayah

steps.m: what (connects to مَّكَّنَّاهُمْ - We established them)
steps.n: In this ayah, مَا is being used to compare two situations - what was given to previous nations versus what was given to the current addressees. It's connecting to the word مَّكَّنَّاهُمْ (We established them) to ask about the power and resources Allah gave to previous nations.

steps.m: not (makes يَرَوْا negative in the past)
steps.n: This is a special word that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَرَوْا) to show that something did not happen in the past, like changing 'they saw' to 'they did not see'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more intense
Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed middle vowel to kasra for present tense
Combined the basic elements: لَ (for) and كُمْ (you all)
The shadda (ّ) on ل adds emphasis to show certainty or importance
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ makes the verb about causing something to be sent. This is a verb, so it doesn't have case endings like nouns do.
The نا at the end shows that 'we' did the action. This is a past tense verb, not affected by case endings.
Added و to connect this action with the previous actions mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'sky' by adding ا and ء at the end
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س because it comes after ال

steps.m: the sky (object of أَرْسَلْنَا - we sent)
steps.n: Added the َ at the end to show this is the object receiving the action of the verb أَرْسَلْنَا (we sent)
This is a preposition showing something is on top of or over something else
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns

steps.m: upon them (referring to the previous generations mentioned in قَبْلِهِم)
steps.n: Added هِم at the end to show this applies to 'them' (a group of males or mixed group)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to show continuous action
Added مِ prefix and changed vowels to show something that does the action repeatedly

steps.m: pouring abundantly (as an object in the sentence)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. The last letter gets a sukun when more letters are added after it.
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The last letter of جَعَلَ gets a sukun (ْ) before adding نا.

steps.m: and we made/put (something into something) (connected to أَرْسَلْنَا - we sent)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this with the previous action (أَرْسَلْنَا - we sent). Both actions were done by the same 'we'.
Changed from a root meaning 'to flow' into the plural word for 'rivers' by adding أ at the start and ا after ه. Like turning 'flow' into 'rivers'
Adding ال makes it refer to specific rivers. This word is what was 'made' by جَعَلْنَا (We made)

steps.m: the rivers (object of جَعَلْنَا that تَجْرِى flows)
steps.n: The َ ending shows this is what was made (object). These rivers are described as تَجْرِى (flowing) in the next ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb

Changed to present tense feminine form. The ت at the beginning shows it's feminine (like 'she' or 'it' for feminine things). In this ayah, it's describing 'the rivers' (الْأَنْهٰرَ) which is feminine in Arabic.
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. Here it connects 'before them' to 'generation', meaning 'how many generations from before them'.
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Added kasra to connect it to the next part
Added هِمْ to show it's beneath them. This word describes where the rivers (الْأَنْهٰرَ) are flowing.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes the verb causative - instead of just perishing, it means to make something perish. This is a verb, so it doesn't have case endings like nouns do.
نا means 'we' when attached to verbs. This is a past tense verb with 'we' attached to it.
Conjunction prefix meaning 'then/so'
Object pronoun suffix meaning 'them' as the recipients of the destruction

steps.m: to commit a wrong or sin

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form for a single sin
Changed vowels and added و to make it plural, showing multiple sins
Added بِ at the beginning to show these sins were the cause
Added هِمْ at the end to show these sins belonged to them

steps.m: to start, to bring into existence
to create, to bring forth (something new)
steps.n: Transforms the basic meaning into a causative form meaning 'to create'. This verb takes قَرْنًا (generation) as what is being created.
Adding نَا shows that 'we' did the action. In the Quran, when Allah uses 'we', it's the royal 'We' showing majesty and power.
و connects this action to the previous actions Allah mentions (giving power, sending rain, making rivers flow)

steps.m: from (connects قَرْنٍ to what comes after)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something comes from or after something else. Here it connects 'generation' (قَرْنٍ) to what follows it in the ayah.
Changes from root letters to basic noun meaning 'after'
Added kasra to show this connects to what comes after it
Added هِمْ to show this refers to the people mentioned before in the ayah

steps.m: to join together in time or space

steps.n: Root 'join together' becomes 'generation' by adding vowels - like people joined in the same time period

steps.m: a generation (object of أَهْلَكْنَا - we destroyed)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being destroyed in the phrase 'we destroyed a generation'

steps.m: to come after or be behind

steps.n: The long alif at start makes this a comparative word meaning 'other/another'
Added kasra to prepare for plural ending
Added ين to make it plural. This word describes قَرْنًا (generation) in the ayah.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical scenario. In this ayah, it introduces what would happen if Allah sent down a physical book.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it in the conversation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows this action happens multiple times or with extra effort
Added sukun to prepare for adding 'we' ending
Added 'we' pronoun to show who did the action
The ى at the end changes to يْ when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added ك at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. This ك means the same as 'you' or 'your' in English.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: a writing, something written (object of نَزَّلْنَا - we sent down)
steps.n: This pattern changes the verb into a noun meaning 'something written'. In this ayah, it's what was sent down.

steps.m: a written record/book (object of نَزَّلْنَا - we sent down)
steps.n: The ا with tanween (ًا) at the end shows this is the object - the thing being 'sent down'

steps.m: in (connects قِرْطَاسٍ to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects the word قِرْطَاسٍ (parchment) to the previous part of the sentence.
Connected the letters and added vowels to form the basic noun. This word follows the preposition في (in) in this ayah.
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is any paper/parchment (not a specific one) and to show it follows the word في (in).

steps.m: to touch or feel something physically with the hand

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action together

steps.m: they touched/felt it with their hands
steps.n: Added هُ to show what was touched (referring to the كِتٰبًا 'book' mentioned earlier)

steps.m: so they touched/felt it with their hands
steps.n: Added فَ (so/then) to connect this with the previous part about sending down a book on parchment
Changes from the root letters to make the simple word for 'hand'
Changes from 'hand' to 'hands' by adding letters to make a broken plural - not a regular plural
Adds preposition ب meaning 'with/by' to show how something is done

steps.m: with their hands (touched it with their hands)
steps.n: Adds possessive pronoun 'their' to show who the hands belong to. This word relates to فَلَمَسُوهُ (they touched it) in the ayah, showing how they touched it.
Added vowels to make past tense form. The و changes to ا which is common in Arabic verbs with و in the middle.

steps.m: surely would say (response in وَلَوْ 'if' condition)
steps.n: The لَ adds emphasis and shows this is the result of the 'if' condition started by وَلَوْ earlier in the verse
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something
Added ن at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Added ٱلْ at the beginning with a shadda to make it specific. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) to tell us who did the action.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word is the subject (doer) of قَالَ (said) in this ayah.

steps.m: this is not (used with إِلَّا to mean 'nothing but/only')
steps.n: This is a negative particle used in a special pattern with إِلَّا to restrict something. In this ayah, it helps say that something is 'nothing but' or 'only' something else - like saying 'This is nothing but clear magic' instead of just 'This is not clear magic'
Added ٰ (small alif) above ه to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added آ at the end which is a Quranic spelling variation of ا, used to show emphasis in writing
This is the base form of the word that means 'except'. It's used to exclude something from what was mentioned before.
The shadda (ّ) and alif (ا) are added to strengthen and complete the word. In this ayah, it connects what comes before with the word سِحْرٌ (magic), meaning 'this is nothing except magic'.
Changed the action of doing magic into the noun 'magic' itself. This word is being described as 'clear/obvious' (مُّبِينٌ) in this ayah.
Added two dots above the last letter to show this is a complete word meaning 'magic'
Added مُ at the beginning to show this describes something that does the action of making things clear
The م has a shadda because it merged with another م before it, not to add emphasis
Added tanween to show this is describing the word سِحْرٌ (magic) in the ayah
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb. This is a common change in Arabic for words with و in the middle.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he said' to 'they said', showing that a group of people did the action
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
This is the first part of the compound word that expresses a conditional meaning

steps.m: if not/why not (addressing قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Adding لا changes the meaning to express wondering why something didn't happen or hasn't happened

steps.m: if not/why not (addressing قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: The alif is lengthened to show emphasis in the question/exclamation
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he caused to descend/he sent down
steps.n: Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'cause to descend' instead of just 'descend'

steps.m: was sent down/was caused to descend
steps.n: Changing the vowels makes it passive - something was sent down instead of someone sending down
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced. This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else.
Changed the ending to prepare for connecting with the pronoun. The ى changes to يْ when something is attached after it.
Added هِ at the end which means 'him'. This shows who is being affected by the preposition. Here it refers back to the angel (مَلَكٌ) mentioned in the verse.
Changes from the root meaning 'possess/control' to mean 'angel' - a special being with powers given by Allah
The tanween (double damma) shows this is indefinite - meaning 'an angel' rather than 'the angel'
Combined two separate particles: و (and) + لو (if) into one word

steps.m: and even if (connects to أَنزَلْنَا - we sent down)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: وَ (wa) + لَوْ (law). This word introduces a hypothetical situation that connects to أَنزَلْنَا (we sent down) in the next part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action causative - someone is making something else descend
Adding نَا shows that 'we' did the action. In this ayah, this refers to Allah using the royal 'We'

steps.m: to have power or authority over something

steps.n: Changed the root 'to rule/have power' into 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a (fatha-fatha)

steps.m: an angel (object of أَنزَلْنَا - we sent down)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what's being sent down. This word is what receives the action in the phrase 'we sent down an angel'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it
Added لَ at beginning to show this would definitely happen in a certain situation

steps.m: surely it would be settled/decided (as a result of أَنزَلْنَا مَلَكًا)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the ل with shadda, showing strong connection to previous statement about 'if We sent down an angel'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: the matter (subject of لَّقُضِىَ - would be settled)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb لَّقُضِىَ (would be settled) in this ayah
Basic form of the word that shows something comes next in order
Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects what happens first (sending down an angel) with what happens next (no more waiting/looking).

steps.m: not (negates يُنظَرُونَ - they are given respite)
steps.n: Basic negative particle that negates the verb that follows it (يُنظَرُونَ). In this ayah, it means they will not be given any delay or respite.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to 'he is looked at/given time' - someone else is doing the action to him now
Added ون to show multiple people are receiving the action
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations - things that could happen but haven't happened yet.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This combination of وَ + لَوْ is commonly used to mean 'and even if' when introducing a hypothetical situation.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb meaning 'he made/set'
Added نٰ to show 'we' are doing the action. This appears in a conditional statement starting with لَوْ (if), followed by another جَعَلْنٰهُ.

steps.m: we made/set him/it (as مَلَكًا - an angel)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what is being made/set. In this ayah, it refers to making/setting something as an angel (مَلَكًا).
Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb meaning 'he ruled'

steps.m: an angel (object of جَعَلْنٰهُ - we made it)
steps.n: Changed from verb 'ruled' to noun 'angel' and added ًا at end to show it's receiving the action
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added لَ at start to add emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
The لَ becomes لَّ with shadda for extra emphasis, like saying 'most definitely'
Added نا to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: surely we would have made him (into مَلَكًا - an angel)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who the action was done to. This word is part of a conditional statement with لَوْ (if) earlier in the verse, showing what would have happened.
Root letters got vowels (fatha and damma) to make the basic word for 'man'

steps.m: a man (object of جَعَلْنٰهُ - we made him)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween alif) at the end to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, Allah is saying if He made an angel, He would have made him a man.

steps.m: to mix up, confuse, or make unclear

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to turn the root letters into 'he confused' in the past tense
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
Added لَ to add emphasis meaning 'surely/definitely'

steps.m: and surely we would have confused
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and changed final fatha to sukun before the suffix
The ى at the end changes to ي with sukun (ْ) when connecting to pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِم at the end to show who is being affected by the preposition (them). This هِم means 'them' and always comes at the end of words.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'what' or 'that which'
The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the word. Here it strengthens the sense of confusion being discussed in the verse, connecting to the word يَلْبِسُونَ (they confuse).

steps.m: to mix, confuse, or make unclear

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. قد is a particle that shows certainty or completion of an action.
Added لَ at the beginning which adds emphasis to the certainty.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it.

steps.m: to mock or make fun of

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show trying to do something
Changed vowels to show that someone received the mocking instead of doing it
Added helper alif to make it easier to pronounce the word starting with sukun

steps.m: to send or be sent as a messenger

steps.n: Root letters got short 'u' vowels (rusul) to make the plural form of messenger
Added بِ meaning 'with' at the start and tanwin at the end to show it's indefinite. This word shows who was mocked in the sentence.
Basic form of the word meaning 'from'
The shadda (ّ) appears because this word comes after a word ending in ن or ل, causing the sounds to merge together. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding fatha and sukun creates the noun meaning 'before' that can be used in time or space

steps.m: before you (possessed by you, after مِن)
steps.n: The ك at the end shows this belongs to 'you'. The ِ before ك shows this word follows مِن (meaning 'from/before') in the sentence.
The weak letter ي changes to ا when we add the past tense vowels. This shows the action happened in the past.

steps.m: and so it surrounded (as a result of their mocking)
steps.n: The فَ shows this happened as a result of the mocking mentioned before. It connects this consequence to the previous action.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English
Adding ال makes it more specific, like adding 'the' in English
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people or things
Adding بِ at the start shows how something is done or what it's done with. The other changes (ٱ and ّ) help with pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people (they) did the action. This word is the subject of فَحَاقَ (then surrounded/befell) in this ayah.
The small circle (۟) is just a pronunciation helper that shows the وا is part of the verb ending. It doesn't change the meaning.
This is a preposition meaning 'from' that connects to the verb سَخِرُوا (they mocked/ridiculed) in this ayah
Added هم which means 'them' (masculine plural). This shows who was being mocked in the ayah - the messengers mentioned earlier.

steps.m: that which (connects to يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ - they used to mock)
steps.n: This is a fundamental particle that can mean 'what' or 'that which' depending on context. It is not derived from a root.

steps.m: that which (connects to يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ - they used to mock)
steps.n: Added shadda for emphasis and to connect smoothly with previous word. This is a common change when ما follows certain words.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Basic preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'. In this ayah, it connects to the verb سَخِرُوا (they mocked)

steps.m: with/by him/it (object of سَخِرُوا - they mocked)
steps.n: Added هِ which refers back to what was being mocked. This connects to the verb سَخِرُوا (they mocked) to show what they mocked.
Added ۦ which is just a pronunciation mark to show the هِ is connected to what comes after it. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to mock or make fun of
steps.n: Basic meaning of mocking or making fun of something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show trying or wanting to do the action
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) Dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) Adding damma (ُ) on first letter, 3) Adding sukun (ْ) at the end. This is telling one person to do something.

steps.m: to move from place to place

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a command by adding the vowels 'i' and 'u'
Added وا to make the command apply to a group instead of one person. This word is followed by فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ which tells where to move (in the land/earth).
Basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it connects the verb سِيرُوا۟ (travel) with ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth), showing where to travel.
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a common spelling of this preposition.
The root letters are combined with a fatha (short 'a' sound) to make the basic word for earth/land
Adding kasra because it comes after the preposition فِى (in/on)
Adding the definite article to specify we're talking about THE earth, not just any land
This is a connecting word that shows one action happens after another. Here it connects 'travel' (سِيرُوا) with 'see/observe' (ٱنظُرُوا), showing you should first travel and then observe.

steps.m: to look at or observe something carefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to 'look!' by adding U sound at start and making it a command to one person
Added plural command ending وا and changed hamza to wasl form ٱ to show this is addressing multiple people
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding the vowels makes this into the question word 'how'
Added basic past tense vowels to make the base past tense form
Changed to hollow verb form with alif due to middle weak letter و
Changes from basic root to feminine noun meaning 'outcome'
Adds the nominative case ending to show this word is the subject
The alif changed to a small alif (ٰ) above the letter. The word means 'end result' and is the subject of كَانَ (was).
Adding مُ at beginning shows who does the action, while ِ on middle letter helps connect the parts
Adding shadda (ّ) shows they do this again and again
Adding ين makes it refer to many people who do this
Adding ٱلْ points to specific deniers. This word describes what happened to these specific deniers in the past, as shown by عٰقِبَةُ (end/fate) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by putting a dot under the ق and dropping the middle و

steps.m: whoever (asks about what is in the heavens and earth)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about people. Here it's asking about who owns everything in the heavens and earth.

steps.m: for whoever (asks about what is in the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show this is about belonging or ownership

steps.m: for whoever (asks about what is in the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل because it comes after the word قُل (say), which ends in a similar sound
Basic question word used to ask about things (in this ayah, asking about what belongs to whom)
Added shadda to show strong connection with previous word لِّمَن (to whom). This emphasizes the question 'to whom belongs what?'
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects 'what' (ما) with 'the heavens and earth' (السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ), asking about what is in them.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb pattern
The root سَمَوَ becomes سَمَاء (sky/heaven) first, then وات is added to make it plural (heavens). The و that was changed to ء comes back before adding ات.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda
Changed 'al' to a shorter 'al', used small alifs instead of regular ones, and added a kasra (i-sound) at the end because it comes after the word 'in/fee'
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Adding ال makes it specific/definite
Adding conjunction و to connect with previous phrase
Added kasra at the end to show this is possessed by Allah in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he said' to a command 'say!' by changing middle vowel to 'u' sound
The middle letter و is dropped because it's between two different vowels in a command form. This is a common pattern with hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)
This is the unique name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is used exclusively as God's proper name.
Added ل at the beginning to show this belongs to or is for Allah
Added vowel marks and shadda for proper pronunciation. This word answers the question 'for whom?' (لِمَن) from earlier in the verse.
Adding fathas creates the past tense verb meaning 'he wrote'. This word connects to عَلَىٰ (upon) which follows it in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or over something else. In this ayah, it connects to نَفْسِهِ (Himself) to show what Allah prescribed upon Himself.
Added the small alif (ٰ) after ى to show it's a long vowel sound at the end. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, or relate to the self/soul

steps.n: Added vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Added ِهِ at the end to show this belongs to Him (Allah). The kasra (ِ) before هِ shows this word is affected by the word عَلَىٰ (upon) that comes before it.

steps.m: a mercy, an act of kindness
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding vowels and ة at the end to show one specific instance of mercy
Added ال (the) at the start. The ل combines with ر making it stronger (shown by ّ)

steps.m: the mercy (object of كَتَبَ - he prescribed)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this is what is being prescribed/written by Allah in this verse

steps.m: to gather or bring things together

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Added لَ at beginning to show emphasis and changed ending to َ to show it's connected to what comes next
Added نَّ to make the meaning stronger and more certain

steps.m: surely he will definitely gather you all
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being gathered - you all
This is a basic preposition that shows movement or direction towards something. In this ayah, it connects to يَوْمِ (day) to show 'to the Day'.
Added an alif with a small mark (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word إِلَىٰ (to) in إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ القِيٰمَةِ (to the Day of Resurrection)
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'the act of standing'

steps.m: a specific rising/establishment (referring to Day of Resurrection)
steps.n: Added ة to specify the particular instance of rising that refers to the Day of Resurrection
Added ال to specify this is THE Resurrection mentioned in the Quran

steps.m: the Resurrection (connected to يَوْمِ - Day of)
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show connection to previous word يَوْمِ (Day of). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this style.

This is a negative word that makes the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) into 'they do not believe' in this ayah. It's one of the most basic and common words for saying 'no' or making something negative in Arabic.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and turn the action into a noun
The fatha at the end shows this word is affected by لا which completely negates it. In the ayah, this means 'there is no doubt at all'.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'in'
Added هِ at the end to show what is being referred to (it)
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ال with a shadda to make it specific
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is the subject of خَسِرُوٓا۟ (they lost) in this ayah.
Adding vowels to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'to lose'
Added وا with a long vowel mark (ـٓ) and small و above to show multiple people did the action
Changed from single soul/self to plural (3-10) by adding أ at start and changing vowels to make أَنْفُس pattern
Shows these souls/selves are what was lost, connecting to the verb خَسِرُوا (they lost) in the ayah
Attached masculine plural pronoun meaning 'their'
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' (referring to those who lost themselves in the previous part of the ayah)

steps.m: so they (who don't believe in لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. This connects to the next phrase where it says they don't believe (لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ)
This is a negative word that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. In this ayah, it makes 'they believe' into 'they do not believe'

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure
steps.n: The root letters convey the basic concept of safety and security
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to Form IV to show active faith/belief rather than just feeling secure
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The hamza changes form due to pronunciation rules.
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action
This is a basic preposition that shows something belongs to someone
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it in the ayah
Added هُۥ at the end to show this belongs to Him (Allah). The ۥ mark after ه shows this is pronounced with a long vowel.

steps.m: whatever (refers to all things that سَكَنَ/dwell)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that refers to all things that dwell/rest in the night and day. In this context, it's being used to encompass everything in creation, showing Allah's complete ownership.

steps.m: to be still or quiet, to settle in a place
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of being still or dwelling somewhere

steps.m: it settles/becomes still (refers to whatever exists مَا in the night)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. In this ayah, this word describes how whatever exists (مَا) settles or becomes still in the night.
This is a basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else. Here it connects what 'settles/rests' (سَكَنَ) with 'the night' (ٱلَّيْلِ).
This root is about darkness and night
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in/during) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
This form turns the flowing of light into the noun for the time when light flows - daytime
Added 'the' at the beginning and kasra at the end because this word is connected to 'the night' (ٱلَّيْلِ) in the sentence
Adding conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: he (refers to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is the basic pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah it refers back to Allah who is being described.

steps.m: and He (refers to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to the previous phrase, showing that this is an additional description of Allah
This root is about hearing and listening

steps.m: One who hears everything completely (divine attribute)
steps.n: Forms an intensive pattern showing complete/perfect hearing. When used as Allah's attribute, it indicates His perfect awareness of all sounds.
Adding ال makes it definite and specific. The س gets shadda because it's a 'sun letter' that combines with the ل sound. As Allah's name, it means He hears everything perfectly.

steps.m: The All-Hearing One (subject of وَهُوَ - and He is)
steps.n: The ُ shows this is the subject (predicate) after وَهُوَ (and He is) in the ayah وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing)
This root refers to complete, certain knowledge - not just basic knowing

Adding ي between the last two root letters and the vowels makes this word mean someone who has complete and perfect knowledge. When used as عَلِيم, it is one of Allah's names showing His perfect knowledge of everything.

steps.m: The All-Knowing (description of Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it definite (like 'the' in English). The ُ shows this is describing Allah (هُوَ) in the phrase 'and He is The All-Knowing'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma (ُ) on first letter, and 3) adding sukun (ْ) on last letter. This makes it a command telling one person to speak.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'other than'

steps.m: is it other than? (object of أَتَّخِذُ)
steps.n: Added أَ at the start to make it a question and َ at the end because it's the object of the verb أَتَّخِذُ (I take)
Added shadda to show emphasis on the ل
Changed first letter to waṣla form (ٱ) and added kasra at end to show possession. This word is the object of أَغَيْرَ (other than) in the question 'Should I take other than Allah as a protector?'

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changed to Form VIII pattern which shows doing something for oneself
Changed from 'he' to 'I' by adding أَ at start

steps.m: to protect, guard, be close to

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to protect/be close to'

steps.m: a protector/guardian (object of أَتَّخِذُ - I take)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what's being taken as indicated by the verb أَتَّخِذُ (I take)

steps.m: to create by splitting/breaking open something new

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he created'
Changed the word to show who does the action by adding alif after first letter and kasra after second letter
Added kasra at the end to show this describes what comes after it (the heavens and earth)

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb pattern
The root سَمَوَ becomes 'heavens' by adding ات for plural. The middle و changes to ا in singular, but comes back in plural form.
Added ٱل (the) and doubled the س with shadda to show it's definite

steps.m: of the heavens (possessed by فاطِرِ - Creator)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show it's possessed by the Creator (فاطِرِ). The alifs are written as long alifs (ٰ) in the Quran.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific earth we live on
Adding وَ connects this to the previous word السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens), showing they are mentioned together

steps.m: and the earth (possessed by/connected to فَاطِرِ - Creator of)
steps.n: The ِ ending shows this word is connected to فَاطِرِ (Creator of) from earlier in the ayah
This is a pronoun that means 'he'. The ُ shows it's a subject and the َ helps with pronunciation.
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this pronoun to what came before it
Changed the vowels to show it's a basic present tense verb
Added يُ at the start to show it's happening in the present/future
This is a negative particle that makes things 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates يُطْعَمُ (is fed), showing that Allah does not need to be fed.
Added و which means 'and', connecting this negative to the previous statement about Allah feeding others. The complete meaning shows the contrast: He feeds but is not fed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show passive voice - something being done to someone/something
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start, and made passive by changing vowels
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' as a command. The middle letter و gets dropped - this happens a lot with words that have و in the middle when making commands.
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger. It's connected to the verb قُلْ (say) to emphasize what is being said.
Added ي at the end to show this is about 'I'. This connects to the next word أُمِرْتُ (I was commanded).
Changed the writing style of ي to ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it. This doesn't change the meaning, just the way it looks.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from active (he ordered) to passive (was ordered) by changing the vowel marks
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' was the one who received the command
This particle connects two ideas and shows what someone wants, needs, or is ordered to do. Here it connects 'I was ordered' (أُمِرْتُ) with 'I be' (أَكُونَ), meaning 'I was ordered that I be'.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
The أَ prefix shows that 'I' am doing the action in the present/future
The َ (fatha) at the end shows this is in the subjunctive mood, used after certain words meaning 'that'
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he was first' instead of just 'first'
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'to make something first' instead of just 'being first'

This is a special word (called an 'interrogative/relative pronoun' in grammar terms) that asks about or refers to people. Here it connects to the next word أَسْلَمَ (submitted/surrendered) to mean 'whoever submits/surrenders'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning changes the meaning to actively choosing to enter into peace/security with Allah through submission
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word negates the verb يُطْعَمُ (is fed), showing that Allah does not need to be fed.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by adding تَ at start. Also changed to present tense by changing vowels. The و is part of the root word meaning 'to be'.

steps.m: you will definitely be/exist (command from Allah)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to show strong emphasis. This is used here because it's a divine command.
Basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or belonging to a group
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (the polytheists). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The مُ prefix with the middle letter having kasra (ِ) creates a noun for someone who does this action. In Islamic context, this specifically refers to someone who associates partners with Allah.
The ين ending makes this plural and shows it's being used as an object

steps.m: the polytheists (those being warned not to be among them)
steps.n: The ال makes it specific. In this ayah, it refers to a group being warned against. The word appears after مِنَ (from/among) showing what group not to be part of.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ before إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) changing first vowel to damma, and 3) adding sukun at the end. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak, followed by what he should say.
This is a particle that adds emphasis to statements. It's like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' at the start of a sentence.

steps.m: indeed I (subject of أَخَافُ - I fear)
steps.n: Adding ي at the end makes this about 'me/I'. The ن gets doubled (shadda) when combined with ي. This word connects to أَخَافُ (I fear) that comes after it.
The ي is written as ى and elongated with ٓ to add extra emphasis to the statement
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense. The و changes to ا because it comes between two vowels.

steps.m: I fear (subject of قُلْ - he said)
steps.n: Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' and ُ at end to show present tense. This word comes after قُلْ (say/said) in the ayah, showing what was said.

steps.m: if (introduces a conditional statement about عَصَيْتُ/disobeying)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it connects the condition of disobeying with its consequence.
Connected the letters and added vowels. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.

steps.m: I disobeyed (connected to أَخَافُ - I fear)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past. The ى changes back to يْ when we add endings. This word connects to أَخَافُ (I fear) - both showing what 'I' am doing.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps grow
steps.n: The doubled ب shows this is someone's main quality or role

steps.m: one who cares for and helps grow (connected to next word)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word connects to what comes after it
Adding ى at the end shows this is my Lord. This word is connected to عَذَابَ (punishment) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed verb into a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is what is being feared in the phrase 'I fear'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun for 'day'
Added tanween kasra to show this is 'a day' that is being described by the word عَظِيمٍ (great) and is connected to عَذَابَ (punishment)
Root letters that express the concept of greatness or mightiness
Added vowels to make it past tense - 'he/it became great'
Changed the verb into an adjective by adding ي between the second and third root letters
Added tanwin kasra at the end to match the word it describes (يَوْمٍ - day)

steps.m: who/whoever (subject of يُصْرَفْ - is turned away)
steps.n: This is the basic form of the word that means 'whoever' or 'who'. In this ayah, it introduces a condition - whoever has something turned away from them.

steps.m: whoever (subject of يُصْرَفْ - is turned away)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the pronunciation of the م. This is a common pronunciation feature when certain words come before مَن.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The sukun at the end shows this is in jussive mood, often used in conditions
This is the basic preposition meaning 'away from' or 'from'. It shows separation or distance from something.

steps.m: from him (being turned away from punishment)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who is being turned away from. In this ayah, it refers to being turned away from punishment on that day.

steps.m: to pass through time, to happen during a day

steps.n: The root forms a noun meaning 'day'
Added fatha to connect this word to what follows it
Added ئِذٍ which means 'at that time', making it refer to a specific day being discussed
This is an emphasis particle that adds certainty to what follows it
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. This word connects to رَحِمَهُۥ (He had mercy on him) to show the result of what happened before.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy

steps.m: He had mercy on him (object of يُصْرَفْ - is turned away)
steps.n: The suffix هُ shows who receives the mercy. The small ۥ helps connect the sounds. This word relates to يُصْرَفْ (is turned away) in the ayah - describing Allah's mercy on the one who is turned away from punishment.
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to something. It's like pointing your finger and saying 'this'
Adding ل with a kasra (ِ) changes 'this' to 'that', making it point to something farther away. The alif (ٰ) is added to show it's stretched out when saying it
Adding كَ at the end makes it more specific, like saying 'that specific thing' instead of just 'that'
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what comes after it (ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْمُبِينُ - the clear success), like saying 'and that is the clear success'
Changes from verb 'to succeed' into the noun 'success'
Adding ال makes this specific success being referred to in the ayah, which is the ultimate success of salvation in the hereafter

steps.m: the supreme success (subject of this part of the ayah)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this word is the subject. In this ayah, it's describing the ultimate success/salvation that comes from Allah's mercy.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who makes clear (describes الْفَوْزُ - the success/triumph)
steps.n: The مُ prefix and ِ vowel pattern create an active participle - something/someone that does the action. Here it's describing 'the success/triumph' as being clear or evident

steps.m: the clear, the evident (describes الْفَوْزُ - the success/triumph)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at start to make it definite, and ُ at end shows it's describing 'the success'
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + إن (if)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start - this يَ shows 'he' is doing the action now or in the future

steps.m: he touches/affects you (in the context of if Allah affects you with harm/good)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show who is being affected. In this ayah, it's talking about if Allah affects you with either harm or good.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.

steps.m: to harm, hurt, or damage something

steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) to double the middle letter and damma (ُ) on first letter to make it a noun meaning the effect or result of harm
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is any general harm/hardship (like saying 'a hardship' instead of 'the hardship')

steps.m: with harm/hardship (connected to يَمْسَسْكَ - touches you)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this harm is the tool or means of the touching action (like saying 'touch with harm'). This connects to the verb يَمْسَسْكَ (touches you) in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. This word negates كَاشِفَ (remover) in this ayah.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed the word to show who does the action. This word comes after لا (no/not) in the ayah, showing there is no remover [of harm].
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the action is for. This word connects to كَاشِفَ (remover) in the ayah, showing there is no remover for him (of harm) except Allah.
Added shadda to emphasize the middle letter. This makes the word stronger, like saying 'absolutely except' or 'truly except'.

steps.m: except (in this ayah: nothing can remove harm except Him)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it's used to show that only Allah can remove harm - no one else.
This is a personal pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah and connects to the word قَدِيرٌ (able) to form the complete meaning 'He is able'
This is a compound particle made of 'wa' (and) + 'in' (if). It connects conditions to previous statements.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يَ at start to show present/future tense, and changed vowels to sukun to match the pattern

steps.m: he touches/will touch you (object of إِن if/when)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show you are being touched. This word comes after إِن (if/when) in the ayah, showing this is a conditional action.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'good'
Added بِ at the beginning to show this good is being used as a tool or means (in this case, what Allah touches you with)

steps.m: with good (object of يَمْسَسْكَ - touches you)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this word is receiving the action of 'touching' in the phrase 'if He touches you with good'
This combines 'fa' (then) with 'huwa' (he) to connect a result with what came before
This word comes from the idea of being high or elevated, which became a preposition meaning 'upon' or 'over'

steps.m: upon/over (something that has power over كُلِّ شَىْءٍ - all things)
steps.n: The small alif (ىٰ) at the end is just a writing style that doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it shows Allah's power over all things.
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a word meaning total inclusion
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, meaning 'of every thing'
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it into a noun. This word belongs to كُلِّ (every/all) in this ayah.
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this word is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is owned by something else

steps.m: to determine, measure, or have power over

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make an adjective meaning 'able/capable'
Adding ي intensifies the meaning to show complete or perfect power
The tanween (double damma) at the end shows this is describing Allah as having complete power, like adding 'the' in English

steps.m: he (subject of ٱلْقَاهِرُ and ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ)
steps.n: This is a personal pronoun that refers to Allah, who is the subject of both descriptions that follow: 'the Dominant' and 'the Wise, the Aware'

steps.m: and he (subject of ٱلْقَاهِرُ and ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ)
steps.n: The و at the start connects this statement to the previous one, showing this is an additional description of Allah

steps.m: and He (subject of ٱلْقَاهِرُ and ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ)
steps.n: Added the standard vowel marks to complete the pronoun. The capital 'He' is used since this refers to Allah.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed pattern to show who does the action by adding alif after first letter and kasra under second letter
Added ال to show this is THE dominator (Allah), and damma at end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Root letters got vowel marks to make the word mean 'above/over'
Added fatha at the end to show this word describes what comes after it (His servants)
Connected the root letters with basic vowel marks to form singular noun
Changes singular 'servant' to plural form showing multiple servants
Adds kasra to show this word is possessed by what follows it
Adds هِ ('His') at the end to show these are Allah's servants. Connected to the word فَوْقَ (above) in this ayah.
This is a basic pronoun that means 'he'
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word is the subject (doer) of ٱلْقَاهِرُ (the Subduer) in this ayah.

steps.m: to judge wisely, to make wise decisions
steps.n: The root letters convey the basic idea of wisdom and judgment
Added ي between the last two root letters and vowels to show this describes someone with deep wisdom

steps.m: The One who is very wise
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to show we're talking about a specific wise one (in this case, Allah)
Added ُ at the end to show this is describing Allah directly in the ayah

steps.m: to know deeply or be fully aware
steps.n: The root letters convey the idea of having deep knowledge
Forms a word for someone who has complete and perfect awareness. When used as Allah's name, it means He is perfectly aware of everything, inside and out.
Adding ال makes it specific and definite. The ُ shows this is describing Allah (connected to هُوَ/He in هُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ/He is the All-Wise)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is telling one person to speak.
Adding the vowel mark makes it readable

steps.m: which one/what (asking about something in قُلْ أَىُّ شَىْءٍ أَكْبَرُ)
steps.n: Added a damma (u-sound) at the end because it's asking 'which thing?' as the main subject. The double-y letter is written differently when it has a shadda mark.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'thing'
The letter ي changes to ى when it comes in the middle of a word
Added the two dots under (ٍ) to show this word is owned by أَىُّ (which) in the phrase 'which thing'
The ُ (damma) in the middle shows this is a natural quality
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative/superlative form. This word describes شَهٰدَةً (testimony) in this ayah.
Added ُ at the end to show this word is describing something in the main part of the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The alif is just a different writing style - same word and meaning
Changed from a verb (to witness) to a noun (a testimony) by adding ة and tanween
Basic vowels (fatha) added to make the root letters into 'he said' (past tense)
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by making it a command - the middle letter was dropped and the first letter got a u-sound
Added kasra at the end to connect with the next word in the ayah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added ي to show someone who does this action as their role or characteristic
Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a' instead of 'the')
Root letters got vowel marks (َ and ْ) to make a word meaning 'between' things
Added ى at the end to show this means 'between me'. This word pairs with وَبَيْنَكُمْ (and between you) in the next part of the ayah to express 'between me and you'
Root meaning 'separate' becomes a word meaning 'between' - like the space separating things

steps.m: and between you all (connected to بَيْنِى/between me)
steps.n: Added the second person plural suffix to show who is being separated or what's between. This word pairs with بَيْنِى (between me) in the ayah to show the space between the speaker and those being addressed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice to show the receiving of divine guidance rather than giving it

steps.m: and divine guidance was communicated (to the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous statement. From context, we know this refers to revelation given to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
Basic preposition meaning 'to/towards' that shows direction or destination

steps.m: to me (receiving what was revealed in أُوحِىَ)
steps.n: Added ىَّ (pronounced 'ayya') at the end to show this means 'to me'. This word connects to أُوحِىَ (it was revealed) earlier in the ayah, showing that something was revealed to the speaker.
Basic demonstrative element meaning 'here' or 'this'
Added ذا to specify 'this' for masculine singular objects. In this ayah, it points to القرءان (the Quran) which comes right after it.
Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha mark for proper pronunciation. This word points to 'the Quran' which follows it directly in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'the thing that is read/recited', specifically referring to the divine revelation
Added ال to make it definite, specifically referring to THE divine revelation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the verb أُوحِىَ (was revealed) in this ayah

steps.m: to warn or alert about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding أُ prefix and changing middle vowel to kasra
Changed from 'he was warned' to 'I warn you all' by adding كُم (meaning 'you all') at the end

steps.m: so that I may warn you all
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why the warning is being given
Basic preposition that shows how something is done
Added the pronoun هِ (him/it) to show what the preposition connects to. Here it refers back to القُرْءَانُ (the Quran) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic relative pronoun meaning 'whoever' or 'who'. The small ۢ mark is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: and whoever (connects to لِأُنذِرَكُم - to warn you)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is part of what is being warned about in لِأُنذِرَكُم (to warn you).

steps.m: to reach or arrive at something
steps.n: Basic meaning of reaching or arriving at something
This is the basic past tense form showing someone reached something. In this ayah, it refers to what has been revealed reaching its destination.
