Starting with the basic particle إِنْ and adding emphasis with shadda
Adding كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: is it true that you all
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this statement into a question, expressing surprise or disbelief

steps.m: to witness or testify to something you saw
steps.n: Basic meaning of witnessing or observing something
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'he witnessed'
Changed to present tense and second person (you) by adding تَ at start and ُ at end
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added لَ at beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'

steps.m: that (connecting what comes before with what follows)
steps.n: Basic form of the particle that connects clauses together, like saying 'I know that you are here' in English
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English
This is a preposition showing that one thing is together with or alongside another. In this ayah, it's asking about taking other gods along with Allah.
Added emphasis to middle letter to show this is the one true God
Added kasra at end to show possession or connection, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to
steps.n: Basic meaning related to divinity and worship
Changes the concept of worship into the one being worshipped
Adds feminine marker to make it refer to a female deity
Adds alif at start to make it plural
Adds tanween fatha to show this is the object being discussed

steps.m: to come after or be delayed

steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'after/delayed' to mean 'other/another' by adding ى to make it feminine, like saying 'another one' for girls/women
Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
The weak letter و is dropped because it comes between a damma and sukun
Added fatha to show basic pronunciation of the word
Added shadda to make the 'no' stronger and more emphatic, like saying 'definitely not' instead of just 'not'
Added alif with maddah (آ) to show the long vowel sound is stretched even more for emphasis
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb
Changed from 'he' to 'I' by adding أَ at start, and changed vowels to show present tense instead of past
This root represents the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Changed from basic root to a command by adding damma (u) and making middle letter silent
The middle weak letter و is dropped because it comes between a damma and sukun in the command form. This is a common rule for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Adding مَا changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction, like saying 'nothing but' or 'only'

steps.m: He (referring to Allah in this context)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah. It's being used to emphasize that Allah alone is the One God.

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changes the basic idea of worship into a word for someone/something that is worshipped. This word tells us what هو (he) is in this ayah.
The ٌ at the end shows this is talking about any god in general, not a specific one. This helps emphasize that هو (He/Allah) is one god among all possible gods that could be worshipped.
Added alif above و to emphasize oneness

steps.m: one, single (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added two dots on top to show this is describing Allah as One - like saying 'Allah is One' instead of just the number one
This is a special word that adds emphasis to statements. The shadda (ّ) makes the emphasis stronger.

steps.n: The ن of مِن changes into م and gets a shadda because it's next to another م. This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic.

steps.m: to make something a partner or equal with something else

Changed from 'he knows' to 'they know' by adding ون at the end, making it plural (multiple people knowing)

steps.m: son (someone who is descended/built from another)

Starting with the basic form of the relative pronoun that means 'who' or 'which'

steps.m: those who (subject of يَعْرِفُونَ - they recognize)

Added pronunciation marks (alif with maddah and small high waw) that don't change the meaning

Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the name for God in Arabic.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.m: this (pointing to something nearby) (subject of إِلَّآ أَسٰطِيرُ)
steps.n: Changed the final alif to a long alif (آ) for emphasis when pausing. This word is the subject of the phrase that follows it: إِلَّآ أَسٰطِيرُ (except tales)
This is a basic particle used to exclude something from what came before it. In this ayah, it's used to introduce what the disbelievers are saying about the Quran.
Added alif to help with pronunciation at the end of the word
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) because it comes at the end of the word and is being paused on when reading
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he wrote' (verb) to 'a line' (noun) - like changing 'write' to 'writing'
Added 'u' sound at start and changed middle vowel to make a word meaning 'stories passed down'
Added feminine ending to show one specific instance
Changed to broken plural pattern for multiple stories
Added a u-mark (damma) at the end to show this word is the main subject after 'except/only'
The doubled و (with shadda) makes it mean 'first' instead of just the action of coming first
Adding ين makes it refer to many people or things that are first
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the first ones' instead of just any 'first ones'
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males
The و at the beginning means 'and'
This is the basic meaning of stopping or preventing something
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يَ at the beginning to show this is happening now, and added ْ (sukun) to make it easier to say
Changed ى to وْ and added نَ at the end to show many people are doing this. This word appears with يَنْـَٔوْنَ (they keep away) in the ayah, showing two things these people do.
This is a preposition showing distance or separation from something

steps.m: away from him/it (referring to what they prevent others from following)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who/what is being kept away from. In this ayah, it refers to what they prevent others from following while they themselves stay away from it too.

steps.m: to keep away or distance oneself
steps.n: This root is about physical or emotional distance
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing ending to make it ongoing action
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The ى changes to و before ون.
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
This is a basic preposition that shows separation or moving away from something

steps.m: away from him (object of يَنْهَوْنَ and يَنْـَٔوْنَ)
steps.n: Added هُ (him) to show who they are staying away from. This word connects to two verbs in this ayah: يَنْهَوْنَ (they forbid) and يَنْـَٔوْنَ (they stay away).
This is a conditional particle that introduces the condition in يُهْلِكُونَ (they destroy). It shows that one thing depends on another happening first.

steps.m: and if (connects to previous statement وَهُمْ يَنْهَوْنَ)
steps.n: The وَ at the start means 'and' - it connects this condition to the previous statement about them forbidding others (وَهُمْ يَنْهَوْنَ)
This is the basic meaning of destruction or perishing
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added hamza at start to make the verb causative - making someone do the action
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is the basic form of the exception particle that excludes something from what was mentioned before.
Added alif at the end to emphasize the meaning. This is like adding emphasis in English by stressing a word.
The regular alif (ا) is written as a long alif (آ). This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.
This root is related to breathing and life essence
Added vowels to make the basic word for 'self' or 'soul'. This is what's being destroyed in the phrase 'they destroy only themselves'.
Changed from singular 'self' to plural 'selves' by adding أ at the start - this is a common way to make plurals in Arabic
Added هُمْ at the end to show these are their own selves they are destroying
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb negative. Here it negates يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive), making it 'they do not perceive'
The وَ connects this negative statement to what came before it in the ayah
This root is about awareness and perception
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations. The sukun (ْ) shows it's a complete word.

steps.m: and even if (connecting to previous statement)
steps.n: The و connects this conditional phrase to what came before it, showing these are related thoughts
Connected the root letters with vowels (َ) to make 'he saw' in the past tense
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed the middle hamza to make it easier to pronounce
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) and a small alif (ٓ) to show the long vowel sound more clearly

steps.m: when (referring to وُقِفُوا۟ - they are made to stand)
steps.n: This is a time word that connects two events. Here it shows when these people are made to stand before the Fire. It usually refers to past events, like saying 'at that time' or 'when that happened.'

steps.m: to stop or make something stand still

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: they were made to stop (in front of النَّارِ)
steps.n: Added وا to show many people were stopped, and added the small و mark (۟) to show it's pronounced fully. In this ayah, they were stopped in front of the Fire.
This is a preposition that shows the people were made to stand over/upon the fire. It connects the verb وُقِفُوا (they were made to stand) with ٱلنَّارِ (the fire).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'fire' (related to light/heat). The و from the root changes to ا.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to something after it
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and shadda on ن because it comes after ال
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to show many people did the action
Added فَ to connect this with what happened before - they were made to stand before the Fire, and then they said...
This is a particle that expresses a wish
Added نا which means 'we'. This word expresses a wish about ourselves.
يٰ is used to express strong emotion or calling out

steps.m: to send back or return something

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels and doubling middle letter (shown by shadda)
Changed the first vowel to damma to make it passive
Added نُ at beginning to show 'we' and changed vowels to show present tense
This is a basic negative word used to say 'no' or 'not' to something
Added the fatha mark to show how to pronounce the word correctly

steps.m: and not (connects to نُرَدُّ - we are returned)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the verb نُرَدُّ (we are returned), showing what they wish won't happen.

steps.m: to deny or reject something as false

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added shadda to show stronger or repeated denial
Added نُ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed middle vowel to kasra. The َ at the end shows this is in the subjunctive mood after وَلَا (and not) in the ayah.
Forms the singular noun for one sign or verse
Forms feminine plural of آية (sign/verse)
Added kasra to show possession - these are the signs of our Lord
Added بِ at beginning to show 'with/by'. The hamza and alif are written specially because they come after بِ.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow (possessed)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this word belongs to something else
Added نَا at the end to show this is our Lord. This whole word is owned by آيات (signs) in the phrase آيات ربّنا (signs of our Lord)
Added نَ at beginning to mean 'we', and vowels changed to make it present tense 'we are'
Added وَ at beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and we will be (connected to نُرَدُّ - we are returned)
steps.n: Added َ at end because this verb is connected to نُرَدُّ (we are returned) in the ayah, showing both actions happen together
Basic preposition showing source or belonging
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (the believers)
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning to feel secure
Changed from a past action (trusted) to describe a person who believes/trusts
Added ين to show multiple believers
Added ال to point to specific believers
Added fatha at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of)

steps.m: No! Rather... (contradicts what was said before about hiding their beliefs)
steps.n: This is a word that shows the opposite of what was just said. Here it shows that instead of successfully hiding their beliefs (as they wanted), their beliefs became clear for everyone to see.
Added fathas to make it past tense. The و will change in the next step due to Arabic spelling rules.
The و changes to ا due to Arabic spelling rules for weak letters
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the preposition ل

steps.m: for/to them (appears before مَّا كَانُوا۟ يُخْفُونَ - what they used to hide)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُم (them) to show who the preposition is directed to. This combination shows what was revealed or became apparent to them.
This is a basic particle that introduces relative clauses or questions. Here it's connecting to كَانُوا۟ (they used to) to refer to 'what they used to...'
The shadda shows this word is connected in pronunciation to the previous word بَدَا (became apparent). This connection doesn't change the meaning, just the pronunciation.

steps.m: to be or exist in a state/condition

steps.n: Adding the fatha mark makes this a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha completes the past tense verb pattern
Added plural ending وا to show multiple people. This word is connected to يُخْفُونَ (they hide) in the ayah, showing who was doing the hiding.
Basic past tense verb showing something was hidden or concealed
Changed to Form IV verb pattern which shows actively doing something to something else
Changed from past tense (he hid) to present tense (he hides) by adding يُ at the start and changing the middle vowel
Added ون ending to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: from (connects to قَبْلُ to mean 'from before')
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something starts from in space or time. Here it combines with قَبْلُ (before) to show these things were hidden in the past.

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make the word mean 'before' in time or space
Added damma at the end to show this word is in the nominative case
This is a conditional word that introduces a hypothetical situation. It's often used to talk about things that could have happened but didn't.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This word connects to رُدُّوا۟ (they were returned) in the ayah.

steps.m: to return or send back to a previous state/place

steps.n: Adding fatha vowels creates past tense active verb form
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Adding shadda shows this happened multiple times or with emphasis
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding وا at the end, showing multiple people were sent back
The middle letter و changes to ا when making past tense. Added fatha marks to show it's past tense.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they'
Adding لَ at the beginning shows this is what would definitely happen in a hypothetical situation (mentioned in previous ayah: if they were sent back)
Starting with the basic question word ما which means 'what'
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose or reason. In this ayah, it refers to what they were forbidden from doing.

steps.m: to prevent or stop from doing something

steps.n: Past tense verb changed to passive voice - meaning 'was prevented' instead of 'prevented'
Added وا at the end to show this happened to a group of people
This is a preposition that shows something moving away from or being separated from something else

steps.m: from it, away from it (referring to what they used to hide)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (meaning 'it') to show what is being moved away from. In this ayah, 'it' refers back to what they used to hide.
Starting with the emphatic particle إنَّ which adds certainty to what follows
Added هُمْ to show who the statement is about. This connects to لَكٰذِبُونَ (they are liars) in the ayah.
Added وَ to connect this statement with what came before it in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Adds emphasis to show this is definitely true
Changes the word to show we're talking about many people (masculine plural)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed to common past tense form by adding alif
Added وا to show many people did the action

steps.m: and they said (introducing their statement: إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّا حَيَاتُنَا ٱلدُّنْيَا)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This introduces what they said in the next part of the verse.

This is a negative particle that denies something. In this ayah, it's used with إِلَّا (except/only) to emphasize the negation of what follows. The people are saying 'This is nothing but our worldly life.'
This is a basic pointing word that forms the base of many pronouns. It's not derived from a root.
Adding the kasra (ِ) and ي makes it specifically refer to a feminine subject (she/it)
Adding the fatha (َ) shows this is the main subject doing something in the sentence. Here it's the subject of إِنْ (if/indeed).
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning. The shadda makes the 'l' sound stronger to emphasize the exception.
Added alif at the end to complete the word. This is a common ending for Arabic particles.
Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from verb 'lived' to noun 'life' and added ة to make it a specific instance of life
Added damma (ُ) to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added نا to show this is our life
The root meaning 'near' became a word for 'worldly life' - like how this life is closer to us than the afterlife
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال
This is a negative particle that makes something not true. Here it's saying something is not going to happen.
Added و at the beginning to connect this idea with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is the basic form of the independent pronoun meaning 'we'. Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it pronounceable.
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb. In this ayah, they are saying 'we are not going to be resurrected.'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed from 'sent/resurrected' to 'person who gets resurrected' by adding مـ at start and changing vowels
Made plural to show many people being resurrected

steps.m: among those who are resurrected (predicate of نَحْنُ)
steps.n: Added بِ to show 'among/from'. This word tells us what 'we' (نَحْنُ) are not in the sentence.
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios
The و connects this conditional phrase with what came before. لَوْ is used for hypothetical scenarios that are unlikely or impossible.

steps.m: to see with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into past tense
Changes from past to present tense. The middle letter أ drops out and the ending changes to ى to make it easier to say
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by changing يَ to تَ at the start
Added alif maksura (ى with two dots) to show this is the end of the word

steps.m: you would see (addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٓ) to lengthen the sound, showing this is a conditional statement (would see)
This is the basic form of the word that shows a specific time in the past. In this ayah, it connects to وُقِفُوا۟ (they are made to stand) to show when this happened.
Added sukun (ْ) on the last letter to show where the word stops
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added وا to show many people were stopped, not just one person
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or upon something else
Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to رَبِّهِمْ (their Lord) to show they were brought before their Lord.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or develop something to its fullest potential
master/lord (one who nurtures and takes care of others)
steps.n: The doubled letter shows this is someone who nurtures and takes care of others completely. In Arabic, رَبّ specifically means someone who has authority while also being responsible for caring for those under their authority.
Added kasra to connect this word with what comes after it, showing it belongs to or is connected to something
Added هِمْ to show this is their Lord - the One who nurtures and takes care of them
Added vowels to show this happened in the past and who did it
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters. This is the basic past tense form that gives present meaning.
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question, like adding a question mark in English
The basic pointing word starts with ها which can become هٰ. This part points to something.
Adding ذَ makes it specifically point to a masculine thing that's close by
The ا at the end helps point to something specific being discussed
The doubled letter shows this is a strong, definite truth
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific truth being discussed
Adding بِ shows how something is done. This word is part of the question 'Is this not with the truth?'
Adding kasra shows this word follows the preposition بِ in the question 'Is this not with the truth?'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
This is a special word used to strongly affirm something after a negative question. In this ayah, it's answering the question 'Is this not the truth?' (أَلَيْسَ هٰذَا بِٱلْحَقِّ)
Adding ىٰ (alif maqsurah) at the end makes the word stronger and more emphatic. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: to nurture, care for, raise up

steps.n: The word 'lord/master' has been made possessive (like 'my lord') by adding the kasra at the end
The نا suffix is added to رَبِّ (master/caretaker) to mean 'our master/caretaker'. The second part about genitive case is incorrect and not relevant to this transformation.

steps.m: and our master/caretaker (speaking to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding و to connect this with the previous statement. In this ayah, people are speaking directly to Allah, acknowledging Him as their master.
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form. This word is connected to what comes after it - it introduces the direct speech أَلَيْسَ (is not)
The middle letter و changes to ا because قول is a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common change in Arabic that doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed to command form for multiple people

steps.m: so taste/experience! (all of you) (command in response to قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a response or result of what was said before
Changed to a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is the direct object (what is being tasted/experienced) of the command 'taste/experience' (ذوقوا).
Added ال at beginning to make it specific. This punishment refers to the specific punishment for denying the truth mentioned earlier in the conversation with their Lord.
Added kasra to show this is connecting to what follows

steps.m: because of what (you were doing in كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' to specify what the preposition بِ refers to. This combination connects to the next phrase 'you were disbelieving' to mean 'because of what you were doing'
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The و drops out when we add a sukun after ك, and the first vowel changes to damma to compensate
Added تُمْ at the end to show that multiple people are being addressed

steps.m: to reject or deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense and second person by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action (in this ayah, addressing those who denied the truth when faced with their Lord)
This is a special word that comes before verbs to emphasize that something has definitely happened. Here it emphasizes that those who denied have certainly lost.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels. This word follows قَدْ which emphasizes that the loss has definitely occurred.
Changed middle vowel to kasra to show this is about experiencing or feeling the loss rather than just losing something

steps.m: those who (points to specific people or things)
steps.n: This is a special demonstrative pronoun used to refer to a group. Consists of definite article ال + ذي (who/which) + ن (plural marker)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter makes the meaning stronger - showing complete rejection or denial

steps.m: they denied/rejected (subject of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show that a group of people (they) did the action. This word connects to قَالُوا (they said) later in the verse.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'meeting' by adding long alif and hamza
Added بِ at beginning to show this is done 'with' or 'by' something. This connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) in the ayah to mean 'meeting with Allah'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the next word (اللَّهِ) and lengthened the alif
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one and only God
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word before it (بِلِقَآءِ meaning 'meeting with'). Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.
Connected the base letters with fatha to form the basic word
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning
Added ىٰٓ at the end to make it work as a preposition showing endpoint. The maddah (ٓ) is optional and helps with pronunciation.
This is the basic form of the word that means a point in time

steps.m: when (referring to جَآءَتْهُمُ - when it comes to them)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes it specifically talk about future events. In this ayah, it's talking about when the Hour (ٱلسَّاعَةُ) will come to them suddenly.
Added vowels and connected letters. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
The alif is lengthened to show emphasis or intensity of coming
Added ت at the end to show feminine subject (it/she)

steps.m: it came to them (subject of بَغْتَةً)
steps.n: Added هُمُ (them) at the end to show who it came to. The word means 'it came to them' and connects to 'suddenly' later in the verse.
Forms feminine noun meaning 'a specific time/hour' from root meaning related to time
Adds definite article to make it refer specifically to the Day of Judgment. The س doubles (gets shadda) because it's a 'sun letter'
Adds ُ to show this is the subject doing the action of 'coming' in جَآءَتْهُمُ (came to them)
Changes verb into noun meaning 'sudden occurrence'
Adds tanween to make it work as an adverb meaning 'suddenly'

steps.m: suddenly (describes how جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ 'the Hour came to them')
steps.n: Shows how something happened - suddenly/unexpectedly (describes the Hour coming to them)
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to show many people did the action

steps.m: to feel deep regret or sorrow

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters to make a word meaning 'regret' or 'sorrow'
Added feminine ending to make it a specific, concrete instance of regret
Added calling particle to express intense emotion and distress
Added alif at end to emphasize deep sorrow and distress

steps.m: Oh, our great regret! (expressing collective anguish)
steps.n: Added نَا (na) at the end to change 'Oh regret!' to 'Oh our regret!' - showing many people feeling sorry together
This is a basic preposition that shows something is physically or metaphorically on top of something else. In this ayah, it shows the burdens are carried 'on' their backs.
The alif at the end is written with a special mark (ىٰ) to show it's being stretched in pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with proper reading.

steps.m: that which (refers to what is being neglected in فَرَّطْنَا)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it refers to the things that were neglected, connecting to the verb فَرَّطْنَا (we neglected).
The doubled ر makes the meaning more intense, showing serious neglect

steps.m: we neglected (referring to what we neglected in فِيهَا)
steps.n: Added 'we' as the doers and changed the last vowel to sukun. This word connects to فِيهَا (in it) in the next part of the verse, showing what was neglected.
Basic preposition meaning 'in'
Added ها at the end to show this means 'in it' where 'it' refers to something feminine (in this case, referring back to الساعة - the Hour)
Added vowel marks to show this is a pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them'
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَحْمِلُونَ (they carry) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added يَ prefix to indicate present tense, with appropriate voweling pattern for present tense verb
Changed from 'he carries' to 'they carry' by adding ون at the end - connects to 'their burdens' (أَوْزَارَهُمْ) in the ayah
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun for a single burden
Forms broken plural from singular وِزْر following pattern أَفْعَال to show multiple burdens
Added هم to show these burdens belong to them. This word connects to يَحْمِلُونَ (they are carrying) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end. In this ayah, it shows their regret over what (مَا) they neglected.

steps.m: to appear on the surface, be visible, emerge

steps.n: Root letters for 'appear' became a noun meaning 'back' by adding vowels
Changed vowels and added و to make it plural (more than one back)

steps.m: their backs (what they carry their burdens on)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show the backs belong to them. In this ayah, it refers to where they carry their burdens of sins.
This is an attention-getting word that tells listeners to focus on what comes next - like saying 'Listen up!' or 'Hey!' in English
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: how evil! (describing ما يَزِرُونَ - what they bear)
steps.n: The long alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show exclamation. This word is expressing how evil the thing they are bearing is.

steps.m: what (referring to the negligence mentioned in فَرَّطْنَا)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb فَرَّطْنَا (we neglected) to show what was neglected

steps.m: to bear or carry a heavy burden

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form past tense verb
Changed to present tense. The initial و drops because it's a weak letter at the start of present tense verbs. Added ي for present tense, changed middle vowel to kasra, and added damma at end.
Added masculine plural ending to show multiple people doing the action

Starting with the basic particle ما which can mean either 'what' (for questions) or 'not' (for negation). In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's negating 'the worldly life' (ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَآ)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to mean the concept of life itself instead of the action of living

steps.m: the life (subject of إِلَّا لَعِبٌ - is nothing but play)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and 'u' at the end because this word is the subject being described in the ayah as 'nothing but play'
Basic adjective form
Root letters made into a simple word meaning 'near' by adding vowel marks
Added يَا at the end to change 'near' into 'this world/worldly life' - a special feminine form
Changed regular alif to long alif for emphasis
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال

steps.m: except (excludes لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ from ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَآ)
steps.n: This is a special word that shows something is not included. In this ayah, it shows that worldly life is nothing but play and amusement, excluding all other possibilities. Think of it like saying 'The worldly life is ONLY play and amusement.'
Added vowel marks to transform the verb into a noun. The fatha (َ) on ل and kasra (ِ) on ع create a pattern that turns actions into things.
Added tanween (ٌ) to show this is talking about play or amusement in general, not a specific instance. This word appears in an ayah that says worldly life is nothing but play and amusement.

steps.m: to play around, be distracted, waste time
steps.n: This form makes the action into a noun
Adding these vowel marks turns it into a noun meaning 'the act of being distracted'
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a' distraction). This word is paired with لَعِبٌ (play) in describing worldly life.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to لَعِبٌ, showing that worldly life is both play AND distraction.

steps.m: to dwell, to circulate, to move about

steps.n: Changed from general word for 'dwelling' to specific 'house/abode' by changing middle letter from و to ا and adding ُ (damma) at end
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د with shadda
Added لَ for emphasis
Added و to connect this phrase to what came before

steps.m: to come after, be later than

steps.n: Added long alif and kasra to create active participle meaning 'later/coming after'. This describes دَّارُ (the abode).

steps.m: the life to come, next life
steps.n: Added feminine ending ة to create a noun referring specifically to the next life

steps.m: the life to come, the next life
steps.n: Added definite article ال to make the noun definite

steps.m: the life to come, the next life (contrasted with ٱلدُّنْيَآ)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is being contrasted with ٱلدُّنْيَآ (worldly life) in the verse
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'good thing' or 'better thing'
Added double-damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action. In this ayah, it's saying the Final Home is better than the worldly life which is just play and amusement.
This is the base word meaning 'those who'. The shadda on ل shows emphasis.
Added لِ (for) at the beginning to show purpose or direction
The ل gets a shadda for emphasis. This word connects to يَتَّقُونَ (they fear Allah) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm
steps.n: Basic past tense form with weak letter ي at the end
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra
Added تّ with shadda to show the action is done to oneself
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English

steps.m: then do/will? (asking about what happens next)
steps.n: Adds the idea of 'then' or 'so' to show this follows from what was said before

steps.m: then will you not? (asking why something isn't happening)
steps.n: Adds 'not' to make this a negative question. In this ayah, it's asking 'then will you not use your minds?' after explaining that the worldly life is just play and amusement.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the beginning
Added ونَ to show that multiple people are being addressed
This is a special word that adds emphasis and certainty to the verb that follows it (نَعْلَمُ). In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah definitely knows about the Prophet's sadness.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The نَ at the beginning shows 'we' are doing the action. The ُ at the end shows this is happening in the present. This word comes after قَدْ which adds emphasis, making it mean 'we certainly know'
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. Here it emphasizes that 'it truly grieves you'

steps.m: indeed it (referring to what they say)
steps.n: The هُ at the end means 'it' and refers back to what the disbelievers are saying. The ۥ is just a pronunciation helper.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he made someone sad' in the past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لَ at start to emphasize that this definitely happens

steps.m: surely he makes you sad (object of نَعْلَمُ/we know)
steps.n: Added كَ to show who is being made sad. This whole phrase is what is being known in نَعْلَمُ (we know).

steps.m: the one who (that يَقُولُونَ refers to)
steps.n: Added ال with a shadda to make it definite. This word connects to يَقُولُونَ (they say) by pointing to the people who are saying something.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening in the present, and added vowel marks to show the basic present tense form
The و changes to و with a dhamma because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)

steps.m: they say (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people (masculine plural) are doing the action. This refers back to the disbelievers mentioned earlier.
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before, showing a result or consequence

steps.m: and indeed they (subject of لَا يُكَذِّبُونَكَ - they do not deny you)
steps.n: Added هُمْ (they) at the end to show who the statement is about. This connects to the next phrase where they are the ones who 'do not deny you'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يُكَذِّبُونَكَ - they deny you) into its opposite meaning. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Added fatha mark on ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense - showing active and deliberate rejection rather than just disbelief
Adding يُ at the beginning shows this happens in the present or future time
Adding masculine plural suffix to indicate 'they'

steps.m: they deny/reject you as false (object of يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end shows you (Prophet Muhammad) are the one being rejected. This word is connected to يَقُولُونَ (they say) in this ayah.
Basic form of the word that shows contrast with what came before
Adding shadda with fatha makes the word stronger, like saying 'but indeed' or 'but certainly'
Adding و at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, showing that this new contrasting idea is connected to the previous thought

steps.m: to wrong, oppress, or act unjustly

steps.n: Basic past tense verb meaning 'he wronged/oppressed'. Made by adding the vowels (fat-ha) to the root letters ظ-ل-م.
Changes from verb (to wrong) to noun meaning 'wrongdoer'
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Added ين to make it plural and show it's the object of لٰكِنَّ (but/however)

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Adding ة changes this into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Forms the feminine plural for multiple signs
Adds the genitive case ending since this word is possessed by Allah in the phrase 'signs of Allah'
The prefix بِ adds the meaning 'with' or 'by' to the word. This word is the object of يَجْحَدُونَ (they deny/reject)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed to the unique name for The One God by adding ال and emphasizing the ل with a shadda. The first letter changes to match the ل sound.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word before it (بِـَٔايٰتِ meaning 'signs')
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it past tense
Added يَ prefix and changed vowels to make it present tense
Added ون suffix to show multiple people doing the action. This word comes after لا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they do not deny'
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added لَ at beginning to add extra emphasis
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form by adding damma (ُ) at start, kasra (ِ) after first letter, and shadda (ّ) on middle letter. This shows the action was done to someone rather than by someone.

steps.m: she was denied/rejected (referring to رُسُلٌ 'messengers' in this ayah)
steps.n: Added تْ at the end to show this happened to a feminine subject (the word رُسُلٌ 'messengers' is treated as feminine in this context)
Basic meaning of sending something or someone
Changed to pattern فَعُول to show someone who does this action a lot
Changes from singular to plural by changing the middle vowel from u to u and dropping the long vowel
Adds tanween to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence. This word is what كُذِّبَتْ (were denied/rejected) is talking about.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. It has no root since it's a helper word.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a vowel sound at the end of the previous word. This makes it easier to pronounce smoothly when reading.

steps.m: to be in front, to come before
steps.n: Basic meaning of something coming before something else
Becomes a noun meaning 'before' in time or space. Here it is possessed by the pronoun ك (you), forming قَبْلِكَ meaning 'before you'.
Adds 'you' (masculine singular) as the possessor. The ِ before ك shows possession. This whole phrase comes after مِن (from), showing we're talking about messengers from before you.
Basic meaning of being patient or persevering
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Adds plural subject marker وا with damma before it to show multiple messengers were patient

steps.m: so they endured patiently (the messengers' response to كُذِّبَتْ/being denied)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this patience was their response to being denied
This is the basic form of the preposition, showing the concept of being high or above
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition showing relationship. In this ayah, it connects to what the messengers were denied.
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the ى is pronounced like a long a sound

steps.m: that which (refers to the hardships mentioned in كُذِّبُوا۟ وَأُوذُوا۟)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that refers to the hardships (being denied and harmed) that the messengers faced. It connects these hardships to the verb صَبَرُوا (they were patient).
Basic meaning of telling a lie or being false
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from active to passive by changing vowels (damma then kasra) to show someone was rejected, with doubled middle letter for emphasis

steps.m: they were denied/rejected as false (referring to رُسُلٌ/messengers)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show this happened to multiple messengers, as mentioned in the ayah
Basic meaning of causing harm or injury
Changed to passive voice to show receiving harm rather than causing it. This is related to يُؤْذِي (he harms) in other verses.
Added plural suffix وا to show multiple messengers were harmed. This refers back to رُسُلٌ (messengers) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: and they were harmed (connected to كُذِّبُوا۟ - they were denied)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous phrase about messengers being denied (كُذِّبُوا۟). Shows they were both denied AND harmed.
Added fatha and shadda to strengthen the meaning. This particle shows when something continues up to a certain point.
Added the elongated alif (ىٰ) which helps connect this word to what comes after it. In this ayah, it connects to أَتَىٰهُمْ (came to them).
Added the small alif (ٓ) which is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Basic meaning showing movement from one place to another
Added fatha marks and changed the final ي to ى to show this happened in the past
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show proper pronunciation
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was affected by the action (them)
Basic meaning of helping or giving victory
Root letters formed into a noun meaning 'help/victory' by adding the vowels fatHa and sukoon
Added damma to show this is the main subject (in this case, what came to them)
Added نا at the end to show this is our help/victory. This word is the subject of أَتَىٰهُمْ (came to them) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes something not happen or exist
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This word negates مُبَدِّلَ (changer), saying there is no changer of Allah's words.

steps.m: to change or replace something with something else
steps.n: Basic meaning of changing one thing for another
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added shadda to show the change is done with intensity or repeatedly

steps.m: one who changes/replaces (subject of لا in this ayah)
steps.n: Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to kasra to show who does the action. This word comes after لا (no/not) in the ayah, showing there is no one who can change.
Basic meaning of speaking or saying words
Changed from action to the thing itself - a single word
Changed from singular (one word) to plural (many words)
Added لِ at beginning meaning 'for/to' and ِ at end showing this belongs to Allah. This word is connected to مُبَدِّلَ (changer) in the phrase 'no changer of Allah's words'.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added kasra at end to show this means 'of' a deity
This is a particle of emphasis that strengthens the meaning of what follows
Adding لَ at the beginning makes the emphasis even stronger, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'
Adding وَ connects this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Basic meaning of coming or arriving
Connected the root letters ج-ي-أ with the vowel marks َ (fatha) to make the past tense verb 'he came'
Added ا (alif) after ج to make it a hollow verb, which is common for verbs with ي in middle
The alif is written as آ to show proper pronunciation
Added ك at the end to show the action was directed to 'you'
This is a preposition showing the starting point or origin of something. In this ayah, it connects with قَبْلِكَ (before you) to mean 'from before you'
Basic meaning of giving news or information
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter adds intensity to the meaning
Changed from 'he informed' (a verb) to 'news' (a noun) that is receiving the action. The kasra shows something else is acting on this news.
Adding ى shows this news belongs to or is about the messengers mentioned in the next word (ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ)
Basic meaning of sending something or someone

steps.m: one who is sent with a message
steps.n: Added مُ at start to show someone who receives the action of being sent (like a messenger), with vowels showing it happened to them
Added ين at the end to show there are many messengers

steps.m: the messengers (who were denied and harmed)
steps.n: Added ال at start to show these are specific messengers. In this verse, it refers to the messengers who were denied and harmed by their people, showing Muhammad ﷺ that he wasn't alone in facing rejection.
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition, like saying 'if something happens' in English
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Added fatha vowels to make the past tense form
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb
The ُ (damma) shows this is a natural quality
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for connecting with the next part
Added ك at the end to show who is being acted upon (you)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added إِ at start and changed vowels to transform verb into noun meaning the act itself
Added damma (u sound) to show this word is doing something in the sentence
Added هُمْ to show possession by them
This is the basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else, like saying 'if you study, you'll learn'

steps.m: then if (connects to ٱسْتَطَعْتَ - you are able)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. It connects the condition 'if you are able' to what was said before.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show trying or seeking to do something
The weak letter و changes to ا in this form
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did this action in the past
This is a connecting word that introduces what someone wants to do or what might happen. Here it connects to تَبْتَغِىَ (you seek), showing what the person might try to do.

steps.m: to seek or search for something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show present tense, changed middle vowel to kasra for present tense form
Added ت between first and second root letters to show doing something for oneself or with extra effort

steps.m: you seek earnestly (as part of أَن تَبْتَغِىَ - that you seek)
steps.n: Added fatha at end because this verb comes after أَن (that), which makes verbs end in fatha
Connected the root letters with fatha to create a noun for the result of digging through

steps.m: a tunnel/passage (object of تَبْتَغِىَ - you seek)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being sought in the phrase 'if you are able to seek a tunnel'
This is a basic preposition showing location. Here it connects with ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where something is located.
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Basic root meaning showing the concept of earth or ground
Adding marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make the basic word for 'earth'
Adding definite article makes it refer to the specific earth
Adding kasra at the end shows this is the location where the tunnel (نَفَقًا) would be
This is a connecting word that joins two options together. Here it connects two things you might seek: a tunnel in the earth OR a stairway to the sky.
The doubling of the middle letter (ل) makes this a noun meaning a tool for climbing
The ًا ending makes it 'a ladder' instead of just 'ladder' - it shows we're talking about one specific ladder
This is a basic preposition that shows location. Here it connects 'tunnel' (نَفَقًا) with 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ) to show where the tunnel would be.
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high/elevated
Adding ال makes it specific ('the sky'). The س gets doubled (shadda) because it's a 'sun letter' that combines with the ل from ال
The ِ ending shows this word comes after فِى (in/into)
Connected the root letters together to form the base word

steps.m: you/she comes/brings (subject of أَن in أَن تَبْتَغِىَ)
steps.n: The تَ prefix shows this is happening now. This word follows أَن (that) which makes it subjunctive, meaning it's describing a possible action.

steps.m: so you/she comes/brings to them (connected to تَبْتَغِىَ)
steps.n: The فَ here shows this is the result or purpose of the seeking (تَبْتَغِىَ) mentioned before

steps.m: to show, to be clear, to be apparent

steps.n: Adding ة changes it from a verb root into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Added dots under the last letter (tanween) to show it's 'a sign' (not 'the sign') and something is happening to it

steps.m: with a sign/miracle (object of تَأْتِيَهُم - bring to them)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ (with/by) at the beginning. This word is what's being brought in the phrase 'bring to them with a sign'
This is a conditional particle that usually means 'if' or 'even if'

steps.m: and even if (connecting to previous statement about their rejection)
steps.n: Adding و here doesn't just mean 'and' - when و comes before لَوْ it often changes the meaning to 'even if' to show something would still be true even in an unlikely situation. Here it connects to the previous statement about the disbelievers' rejection.
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
The weak letter hamza (ء) combines with alif to make a long vowel sound
Changed إِلَه to اللَه by adding ال and merging letters. This special word refers only to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of شَآءَ - willed/wished)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added damma to show this is the subject of the verb شَآءَ (willed/wished) in this ayah
Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb showing someone did the action
Added هُمْ to show who was gathered

steps.m: He would have certainly gathered them (in response to لَوْ/if)
steps.n: Added لَ to show this is the result of a condition (if Allah had willed). This لَ works with لَوْ earlier in the ayah to show what would have happened.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else. Here it shows their turning away is weighing heavily upon/affecting the Prophet.
Added vowel marks and changed final ي to ى to create the noun meaning 'guidance'
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific guidance
Added the diacritic ٰ (alif khanjariyah) to show the long vowel sound at the end
Basic negation particle

steps.m: so not, then not (connects to previous statement about if Allah willed)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before
Connected the root letters together. The middle و is a weak letter that stays because it's essential to the meaning.
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added نَّ at the end to add emphasis, making it mean 'you should certainly be'
Basic preposition indicating source or origin
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word (الجٰهِلِينَ). This shows it means 'from the ignorant ones'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Added ين to show we're talking about multiple people

steps.m: the people who lack knowledge (after مِنَ/from)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from)
Basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that those who listen will definitely respond.
Adding مَا changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction, showing that ONLY those who listen will respond, excluding others.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting the action

steps.m: he responds/will respond (to الَّذِينَ يَسْمَعُونَ - those who listen)
steps.n: Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word describes what 'those who listen' do in response.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that means 'who'
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people

steps.m: those who (subject of يَسْمَعُونَ - they hear)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning with a shadda to make it specific. This word connects to يَسْمَعُونَ (they hear) in the ayah, describing who does the hearing.
Connected the root letters together with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they hear (referring to those who respond/listen in this ayah)
steps.n: Adds ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers to those who listen and respond mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Adding vowels to make the basic noun form
Added ى to make it plural, referring to those who are dead
Added ال to make it definite (specific dead ones)
Added 'wa' (and) at the beginning to connect with what came before
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on ب, and damma on ث
Added هُمُ at the end to show who is being raised. This word is what Allah (اللَّهُ) does to them.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this is the subject
Basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else
Added damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) to show proper pronunciation. This word connects what happens after people hear (يَسْمَعُونَ) in this ayah.
This is a preposition that shows direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'to him'. In this ayah, 'him' refers back to Allah mentioned earlier (اللَّهُ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it
Added يُ at the beginning to show this happens in the present, and adjusted vowels accordingly
Added ون to show this happens to multiple people. This word describes what happens to those who Allah resurrects (يَبْعَثُهُمُ) in this verse.
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
The middle letter و changed to ا and وا was added at the end to make it 'they said' instead of 'he said'
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it
This is the first part of the compound word that shows a condition or wish

steps.m: why not (asking about نُزِّلَ - being sent down)
steps.n: Adding لَا changes the meaning from 'if' to 'why not'. In this ayah, people are asking why a sign wasn't sent down to Muhammad (peace be upon him).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added damma at start and kasra with shadda in middle to show something was sent down by someone
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Changed the ending to prepare for connecting with the pronoun. The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns.

steps.m: upon him (connected to نُزِّلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ (him) at the end. This shows who the action was done to - in this case, what was sent down upon.
Root letters أ-ي-ي became ءَايَ by adding a long ا to make the noun for 'sign'
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of a sign
Added two dots on top (tanween) to show this is 'a sign' that people were asking about being sent down
Basic preposition with kasra vowel mark
Added shadda on م because it comes after the word-ending vowel of the previous word لَوْلَا. This is a pronunciation helper that shows the م sound is emphasized when connected in speech.
Connected the root letters and added fatha to show basic past tense meaning
Doubled the ب letter to make it stronger, changing from verb to noun meaning
Added kasra to show this word is owned by or connected to something
Added هِ to show it belongs to him
Added a double letter mark (shadda) because it comes after مِن, and added a small ي at the end to help show how to pronounce the 'his' sound
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he said' to command 'say!' by changing middle vowel to u-sound
The middle letter و is dropped to make it shorter and easier to say as a command. This happens a lot with words that have و in the middle.
This is a common emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah is truly capable (قَادِرٌ). The shadda shows the strong emphasis by doubling the ن.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (object of إِنَّ)
steps.n: The word for 'god' (إِلَه) becomes the proper name 'Allah' (ٱللَّه) with a fatha mark at the end because it comes after إِنَّ. The first letter changes to connect smoothly with previous words.
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action. This describes Allah's ability.
Added tanween damma (ٌ) at the end to show this is describing Allah in the sentence

steps.m: upon/over (connected to يُنَزِّلَ - to send down)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to show the correct pronunciation. This word shows what يُنَزِّلَ (to send down) is happening upon.

steps.m: upon/over (connected to يُنَزِّلَ - to send down)
steps.n: Added alif maksura (ى with ٰ on top) to show this is the standard written form of the word

steps.m: upon/over (connected to يُنَزِّلَ - to send down)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٓ) above to show this word is being stretched slightly in pronunciation
Basic particle that connects two parts of a sentence, here introducing what Allah is capable of doing
Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda intensifies the basic meaning, indicating repeated or intensive descent

steps.m: that he sends down (object of أَن)
steps.n: Changed to present tense and added subjunctive ending because it comes after أَن (that)
Changed from a verb root meaning 'to show' into a noun meaning 'sign' by adding hamza and long alif at the start
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of a sign

steps.m: a sign/miracle (object of يُنَزِّلَ - to send down)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object of the verb يُنَزِّلَ (to send down) in this ayah
This is the basic form of the word that shows contrast with what came before
Added emphasis to show this is a strong contrast

steps.m: and yet/but still (connecting to previous statement)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement with what came before
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing something increased or became abundant
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'more' or 'most'
The word 'most' gets 'them' attached to it (هُمْ) and becomes the object (what's being known) in 'they know most of them'
This makes the next word negative - it says something is not happening
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The prefix ي shows this is happening now (present tense) and 'he' is doing it

steps.m: they know (referring to those who reject the signs)
steps.n: The suffix ون changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural). In this ayah, it refers to those who reject Allah's signs.

steps.m: not (negates the existence of creatures mentioned after)
steps.n: This is a negation particle that denies the existence of something. In this ayah, it's working with مِن to emphasize there is not any creature except that they are communities like you
The و connects this negation to what came before, showing this is an additional statement

steps.m: from/of (connects دَآبَّةٍ to what comes after)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts, what something is made of, or connects parts of a sentence. Here it connects the word دَآبَّةٍ (creature) to what comes after it.
The doubled middle letter shows this happens with intensity or repeatedly
Makes the word feminine and turns it into a noun meaning 'a creature'

steps.m: any creature (that moves on the ground)
steps.n: Added kasratin to show this word comes after مِن (from/of) in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment
Added the ى at the end which is a common spelling variation for this preposition. This word connects to 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ) to show location.
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in)
Makes it specific by adding ال (the) at the beginning
This is the basic negation word that makes something negative, like adding 'not' in English. Here it negates the word طٰٓئِرٍ (bird)
The و connects this negation with what came before

steps.m: to fly or move through air

steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'one who flies'. The alif (ا) and hamza (ئ) pattern shows who does the action.
The tanwin (ٍ) at the end shows this is any general bird, not a specific one. The word follows مِن (meaning 'of' or 'any') in the ayah, which is why it has this ending.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he flies (with بِجَنَاحَيْهِ - with his two wings)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word connects to بِجَنَاحَيْهِ which tells us how the flying happens - with his two wings.
Added alif to make it into a noun meaning the thing that leans out from a body (wing)
Added يْ at the end to show exactly two wings
Added بِ (bi) at the start to mean 'with' - like saying 'with wings' instead of just 'wings'

steps.m: with his two wings (acted on by يَطِيرُ)
steps.n: Added هِ to show these are the bird's own wings that it uses to fly
Added a shadda (double letter mark) to emphasize the meaning. This makes the exception stronger.

Added a long alif at the end to help connect this word smoothly to the next word أُمَمٌ (communities). This particle creates an exception, telling us that while no creature was left out, they are all communities like you.

steps.m: to be a source or origin, to be a mother/leader

steps.n: The ُ (damma) changes the word into a plural noun meaning nations or communities
The ٌ (tanween damma) makes the word indefinite, meaning 'nations' rather than 'the nations'

steps.m: to be like or similar to something

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and make it into a noun meaning 'example'
Changed to plural by adding أ at start and ا after ث - like changing 'example' to 'examples'

steps.m: your likes (referring to creatures like you)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show it means 'your' when talking to many people. In this ayah, it's telling humans that animals are communities like them.
This is the basic form of the word ما which can mean 'what', 'that', or work as a negative particle depending on context. In this ayah, it's working with فَرَّطْنَا to say 'we have not neglected'.
The dot above the م (meem) is combined with the letter to make it flow better with the word after it. This makes the 'no' or 'not' stronger.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
The shadda (double letter) makes the meaning stronger
Changed the final vowel to sukun to prepare for adding 'we'
Adding نا makes it 'we' instead of 'he/it'
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects 'creature' (دَآبَّةٍ) with 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ) to say 'creature in the earth'.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book) by changing the middle vowel to a long ā sound. Like changing 'wrote' into 'writing'.
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
The kasra ending shows this word comes after the preposition فِي (in/within) in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added fatha and sukun to make it into a noun meaning 'thing'. The hamza at the end becomes ء.

steps.m: any thing (being described in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is referring to any general thing. The ي is written as ى when it comes before hamza.

This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with some time or space between them. Here it connects to the word يُحْشَرُونَ (they will be gathered) to show this gathering happens after what was mentioned before.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows direction or destination
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show this is connected to the next word. In this ayah, it shows the direction 'to their Lord' (إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ)

steps.m: to care for, raise, look after

steps.n: Root meaning 'to care for' becomes 'lord/master' - the kasra at the end connects it to 'their' (هِمْ) to show whose lord

steps.m: their Lord (being gathered to in يُحْشَرُونَ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this is their Lord. This word connects to يُحْشَرُونَ (they are gathered) showing they are gathered to their Lord.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding يُ at start and changing vowels to show the action is being done to someone
Added ون to show multiple people are being gathered
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to things. This is a special word that doesn't come from a three-letter root.
Added ال at the beginning and ي at the end to change it from pointing at something to describing who or what we're talking about
Added ن at the end to make it talk about many people or things instead of just one
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it to what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb كَذَّبُوا (they denied) in this ayah.

steps.m: to deny or reject something as false

steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) to show the denial is strong or emphatic. This is like saying someone really, really denied something.
Added وا at the end to show a group of people did this action. In this ayah, they are denying Allah's signs (بِـَٔايٰتِنَا).

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Forms feminine noun meaning 'sign' or 'miracle'. The initial hamza and alif combine into آ
Changed to plural form to show multiple signs
Added 'our' to show these are Allah's signs

steps.m: with/by our signs (object of كَذَّبُوا - they denied)
steps.n: Added preposition بِ meaning 'with/by'. This word is what is being denied in the phrase 'those who denied Our signs.'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the first vowel from fatha to damma to show something was done to the subject
Changed from a verb (he was deaf) to a plural noun (deaf ones) by doubling the م and adding tanween
Adding damma (ُ) makes this a plural noun meaning 'mute ones'

steps.m: and mute ones (connected to صُمٌّ/deaf ones)
steps.n: Added 'and' (وَ) at the beginning to connect it with 'deaf ones' (صُمٌّ) before it, and added tanween damma (ٌ) at the end to show it's indefinite
This is a preposition that connects words by showing something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to the word 'darknesses' that comes right after it.
Root letters got vowels to make a simple noun meaning darkness
Changed from single darkness to plural by adding -umaat ending
Added definite article and doubled first letter
Added kasra at end because it follows the word فِى (in/within) in the ayah
This is a special word that means 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it refers to the person who Allah wills something for. It connects to the verb يَشَإِ (He wills) that comes after it.
Added fatha vowels to create past tense. The middle root letter ي becomes ا in this form.
Changed from past tense (شَاءَ) to present tense (يَشَاءُ) by adding يَ at the start
Added kasra at the end to make it jussive mood
Changed to the proper name for Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The first ا changes to ل because it comes after ال.
Added shadda to show doubled ل, changed initial alif to wasl form (ٱ), and added ُ to show this is the doer of the action in يُضْلِلْ (He misguides).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed the basic verb 'lost the way' into 'makes someone lose the way' by adding yu- at the start - this is how we make one person cause another person to do something

steps.m: may he make (someone) lose the way
steps.n: The ending changes from 'u' to no vowel, making it a 'what if' or 'maybe' meaning instead of just stating a fact

steps.m: He (Allah) makes him lose the way (object of يَشَإِ - if He wills)
steps.n: Added attached pronoun هُ meaning 'him' as direct object. This word is the object (in accusative case) of the verb يشإ (wishes/wills) that comes before it.
This is a special word used to talk about any person who does something. Here it refers to whoever Allah wills to guide or misguide.
Added 'and' to connect this clause with the previous statement about those who deny Allah's signs. The و shows this is part of a larger description of guidance and misguidance.
Changed root letters to past tense by adding the vowel 'aa' in middle and 'a' at end to mean 'he wanted'
Changes from past to present by adding يَ at start and ُ at end

steps.m: he wills/wishes (subject of يُضْلِلْهُ - he misguides him)
steps.n: Changed to 'if he wills' by dropping the ending. Works with مَن (whoever) to mean 'whoever He wills'. Links to 'he misguides him' later in the verse.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening in the present and the subject is 'he'
Added a stop mark (ْ) at the end to show this is part of an 'if-then' statement - like saying 'if he makes/puts'

steps.m: he makes/puts him (object of يَشَأْ - he wills)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who the action is being done to. This word is connected to يَشَأْ (he wills) in the ayah
This is the basic form showing the concept of being in a higher position
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition that shows one thing is positioned above another

steps.m: upon, on (preposition showing path to صِرٰطٍ)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is a complete word, not the start of a longer word. Here it connects to صِرٰطٍ (path) to show what is being positioned upon.
Adding the basic vowels to make it readable
Added alif in elongated form (ٰ) after ر to lengthen the sound
Added two dots under (ٍ) to show it's 'a path' after the word على (on/upon)
Root letters got vowels to make an adjective meaning 'straight'
Added مُسْتَ at the start to make it mean 'completely/perfectly straight' instead of just 'straight'
Added shadda on م to emphasize the quality
Added tanween kasra to show this describes an indefinite path (صِرٰطٍ) mentioned in the ayah
Changed the root into a command by adding a 'u' sound (damma) and making the middle letter silent (sukun)
The middle weak letter 'و' is dropped to make the command shorter and easier to say
Adds أَ at the start to turn 'see' into a question meaning 'did you see?' or 'tell me about'
Adds تَ at the end to make it 'you' (talking to one male person)
Changes 'tell me' to 'have you seen/tell me' by adding 'ta' at the end

steps.m: tell me (what you all think about)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ at the end to make it 'you all' instead of just 'you'
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it connects with كُنتُمْ (you are) to mean 'if you are'.
Added fathas to make past tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added superscript alif (ٰ) after ى to show it's a long vowel sound
Added كُمْ at the end to show the action was directed at multiple people being addressed
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun meaning 'punishment'. This word is connected to اللَّهِ (Allah) in this ayah to mean 'punishment of Allah'.

steps.m: punishment (subject of أَتَىٰكُمْ - came to you)
steps.n: The damma (ُ) shows this word is doing the action in أَتَىٰكُمْ (came to you). It's like saying 'punishment came' where 'punishment' is doing the action of coming.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
This is a unique word that comes from إِلَه but has special changes to show it refers to the one true God. The ال at the beginning and the shadda on ل make it definite and emphasize its uniqueness.
This is a conjunction that connects two options: 'punishment of Allah' or 'the Hour'. It shows these are two possibilities being asked about.
Connected letters with fatha marks and changed ي to ى because it's at the end of the word

steps.m: she arrived/came to (subject of عَذَابُ ٱللَّهِ أَوْ ٱلسَّاعَةُ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he came' to 'she came' by adding ت at the end because it's talking about either Allah's punishment or the Hour (both feminine in Arabic)
Added plural masculine 'you' كُمُ to show who is being addressed (the disbelievers in the context)
Added ة to make it a noun for one instance or unit of time

steps.m: the Hour (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and doubled the س with shadda. In this context, 'the Hour' specifically refers to the Day of Judgment.

steps.m: the Hour (subject of أَتَتْكُمُ - came to you)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in أَتَتْكُمُ (came to you)
Added vowels to the root letters to make a word meaning 'not' or 'other than'

steps.m: other than (object of تَدْعُونَ - you call)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تَدْعُونَ (you call)

steps.m: is it other than? (object of تَدْعُونَ - you call)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make this a question meaning 'is it other than?'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda make it specific and definite. The kasra at the end shows it's being possessed by or connected to something else in this sentence.
