The prefix تَ shows this is happening now (present tense) and is done by 'you'. The ُ (damma) on ع shows it's active voice.

steps.m: you all call upon (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: The نَ at the end shows this is addressing multiple people (you all/you plural)

This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if Allah's punishment comes to you' for the question 'would you call upon other than Allah?'
Changed from root letters to command form meaning 'be!'
Added ت to make it past tense and indicate the subject
Changes from verb (to be truthful) to noun (truthful person)
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple truthful people

steps.m: truthful ones (plural, in this position in the sentence)
steps.n: The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) and a final fatḥa is added to show its grammatical position

steps.m: No! Rather... (contradicts what came before and introduces إِيَّاهُ تَدْعُونَ)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a correction or contradiction. In this ayah, it's saying 'No! Rather, it is Him alone you call upon...'

steps.m: specifically (used to emphasize who receives an action)
steps.n: This is a special pronoun used for emphasis when referring to objects
The هُ shows we're talking about 'him'. This word is being emphasized as the object (receiver) of the verb تَدْعُونَ (you call), showing that they call ONLY Him.

steps.m: to call upon, pray to, or invite

steps.n: Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' (one person) doing the action. The ْ (sukun) on د and ُ (damma) on ع help with pronunciation.

steps.m: you all call upon (plural subject of تَنسَوْنَ)
steps.n: The نَ at the end changes 'you' to 'you all' (plural). This word is connected to تَنسَوْنَ (you all forget) in the ayah, showing the same group is doing both actions.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end

steps.m: so/then he removes (what you call upon)
steps.n: Adds فَ to show this happens as a result of the previous action (calling upon Allah). The object being removed is what was called upon in distress.

steps.m: that which (referring to the false gods they associate with Allah)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun used to refer to things. In this ayah, it specifically refers to the false deities or partners that people associate with Allah, which they will forget about when facing hardship.

steps.m: to call upon, pray to, invoke

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the root letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action. The middle letter gets a sukun, and the last vowel changes to damma. The ا changes back to و because it's in present tense.

steps.m: you all call upon (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: Added نَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action together
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination
Changed ى to ي with sukun (ـيْ) to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is just a spelling change that happens before pronouns.
Added هِ (hi) at the end to show this means 'to him'. The 'him' refers back to Allah who they are calling upon in their time of need.

steps.m: if (introduces condition for شَآءَ - He wills)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'if' to what Allah wills (شَآءَ). It is a primitive word that cannot be broken down further.

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Added fathas to show this is something that happened in the past
Changed to a hollow verb pattern with long alif
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed ending to show present tense
Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Added وَ at start to connect this word to what came before it
This is a relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'whatever'. It is an indeclinable particle.

steps.m: what/that which (object of تَدْعُونَ - you call upon)
steps.n: Added fatha to show proper pronunciation. In this ayah, this word connects to تَدْعُونَ (you call upon), referring to whatever/that which you call upon.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding ت changes it from past to present tense and makes it mean 'you' or 'she'
Changed from 'you/she' to 'you all' by adding ون at the end and changing the first vowel from 'a' to 'u'. In this ayah, it refers to people making false partners with Allah.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing the middle vowel to sukun (ْ) makes the verb mean 'to cause someone to do something'
Adding نَآ at the end shows 'we' did the action. The long alif (آ) at the end shows this word connects to what comes next in the sentence.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. Here it connects to the word أُمَمٍ (nations/communities) to show who the message was sent to.
Added an alif that looks like ى (called alif maqsurah) at the end to show this is how the word is written when it appears at the end of a phrase
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word

steps.m: to be a source or reference point

steps.n: Root letters أمم became أُمّ (umm) by adding damma and combining the double م into one letter with shadda, making the word for mother
Changed from 'mother' to plural 'nations' by adding vowel marks
Added tanwin kasra to show this word is being acted upon by the preposition إِلَىٰٓ (to) in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to

The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the م because it comes after a connecting letter. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to أُمَمٍ (nations) to show 'from nations'.

steps.m: to come before or face towards

steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the noun meaning 'before'
Added kasra at the end because another word owns or possesses it (like 'before of/belonging to something')
Added ك at the end to show this means 'before you'. This whole phrase مِن قَبْلِكَ means 'before you' in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُم to show who was seized
Added فَ to show this action happened as a result of something before it
Root letters joined to make a noun meaning 'strength' or 'harm'
Added اء at the end to make a feminine noun meaning a more intense form of hardship
Added ال to make it specific
Added بِ (bi) meaning 'with' at the start to show they were tested with hardship

steps.m: with the hardship (object of أَخَذْنٰهُم - we seized them)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition بِ
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show intensity
Added اء at the end to make it into a noun meaning intense hardship
Added ال to make it specific
Added و at beginning to mean 'and' and ِ at end to show it follows something
This is the basic particle that expresses hope or expectation for something to happen

steps.m: so that they might (subject of يَتَضَرَّعُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them'. This word connects to يَتَضَرَّعُونَ (they humble themselves) to show the hoped-for result.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the action more intense
Adding ت at the beginning shows the action is done to oneself
Adding يَ at start and ُ at end makes it present tense
Adding ون shows multiple people doing the action
This is a basic interrogative particle that expresses reproach or encouragement
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from previous statement
The alif is elongated to show emphasis in the questioning tone

steps.m: when (connects to جَآءَهُم - came to them)
steps.n: This is a time particle that shows when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it connects to جَآءَهُم (came to them) to show when the people didn't humble themselves.
Basic past tense verb form

steps.m: came to them (referring to بَأْسُنَا our punishment)
steps.n: Adding هُم at the end shows who the action was done to - them (masculine plural). In this ayah, it refers to the people who received our punishment (بَأْسُنَا).
Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'might/strength' with vowel marks
Added نَا meaning 'our'. This word is doing the action of coming to them in the verse.
Root letters got vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was humble'
Adding ّ (shadda) on ر shows the action is done with extra strength or intensity
Adding تَ at the start shows someone choosing to do the action to themselves

steps.m: they humbled themselves (in response to بَأْسُنَا - Our punishment)
steps.n: Adding وا shows many people did this action. This word shows their response to Allah's punishment (بَأْسُنَا) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is the base particle that shows contrast or exception. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) here.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This shows contrast with what came before - they didn't humble themselves, and yet/but their hearts became hard.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb

steps.m: hardened/became hard (referring to قُلُوبُهُمْ - their hearts)
steps.n: Changed the final و to ت because this is describing a feminine subject (hearts/قُلُوب which is feminine in Arabic). Added sukun at the end to show it's at the end of a phrase.
The root meaning of turning/changing becomes 'heart' since the heart constantly changes states. This word is what becomes hard/harsh (قَسَتْ) in this ayah.
Changes the word to show multiple hearts by adding و and changing the vowels to ُ
Adds ُ to show these hearts are doing something (becoming hard)
Adds هُمْ to show these hearts belong to them
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show someone actively making something beautiful. This word connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they were doing) in this ayah.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement, showing this happened in addition to something else
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added the pronoun هُمُ meaning 'them' to show who the action is for
Added ي after first root letter with vowel markings to create a noun for someone/something characterized by this quality
Adding ا makes it refer to someone who is excessive in rebellion/evil
Added ال to specify this particular devil (Satan)
Added ُ to show this is the doer of the action زَيَّنَ (made attractive) in this ayah
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that refers to the things or actions being done. In this ayah, it's the object of يَعْمَلُونَ (they were doing), showing what they were doing.

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state/condition)

steps.n: Added fatha marks to show it's past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter.
Adding وا changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural). This word connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work) showing who was doing the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they work/act (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers back to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the verse.
Starting with the basic particle that shows things happening together in time
Adding ا at the end changes this from a verb meaning 'gather' to a word meaning 'when' that connects two events in time (like: when this happened, that happened)
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this follows from what happened before
Basic past tense verb meaning 'forgot'
Changed to show multiple people did the action. The ي changes to و because it's a weak letter at the end. This word is connected to فَتَحْنَا (We opened) showing what happened after they forgot.

steps.m: that which (object of نَسُوا - they forgot)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects what was forgotten with the verb 'they forgot'. In this ayah, it refers to the reminders or warnings they were given.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda on middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Changing the vowels to damma-kasra pattern makes it passive, showing the action is being done to someone
Adding وا at the end shows that a group of people were reminded

steps.m: him/it (referring to what they were reminded of)
steps.n: This is the basic pronoun form meaning 'him' or 'it', referring back to what they were reminded of in the previous part of the ayah (ما ذُكِّرُوا)

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to what they were reminded of)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ which shows how something is done or used

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to what they were reminded of)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ۦ) after هِ which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters together with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word shows what happened in the past after they forgot (نَسُوا) what they were reminded of.
Starting with the basic preposition عَلَى which shows something being on top of or over something else
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting with the next part. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِمْ which means 'them' to show who is being affected by the preposition
Connected the root letters and added alif to make the basic singular noun form
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and changing internal structure. This is a common pattern for making plurals in Arabic.
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the middle letter to show completeness
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, making the meaning 'of all things'
Connected the letters and added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this thing is possessed by كُلِّ (every). The ي is written as ى which is just a spelling variation.
Basic form of the word that shows a limit or endpoint
Added vowel mark and shadda to show correct pronunciation
Added ending that makes it work as 'until'. In this ayah, it connects to the word إِذَا (when) to show 'until when'

steps.m: when (indicating something that will happen in the future)
steps.n: This is a time particle that introduces a condition or event expected to happen. In this ayah, it's used to show the moment when those who were given good things suddenly find themselves in despair.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense. This pattern is used for feelings and emotions.
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action

steps.m: what (that which was given to them)
steps.n: Starting with the relative pronoun مَا which refers to what they were given
Added the preposition بِ to show this connects to أُوتُوٓا۟ (they were given) in the next part of the ayah
Adding madda over alif (آ) is a common Quranic spelling variation that doesn't change meaning
Changed to passive form to show receiving rather than giving
Added 'و' and 'ا' at the end to make it plural (they)
The word means 'they were given' in both cases - the extra marks just show proper pronunciation but don't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added sukun on ذ to prepare for attachments
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added هُم at the end to show who was taken/seized. In this ayah, it refers to those who rejected Allah's signs.
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'sudden occurrence'
Adds tanween to make it work as an adverb meaning 'suddenly'

steps.m: suddenly (describing how أَخَذْنٰهُم/We seized them happened)
steps.n: The word already has tanween - it's an adverb describing how suddenly something happened
This is a basic particle that shows something happening unexpectedly or at a specific time

steps.m: and then suddenly (connects to مُّبْلِسُونَ - they were in despair)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous action. In this ayah, it connects to how they were suddenly in despair after Allah's punishment.

steps.m: they/them (the people who were given blessings)
steps.n: This is a pronoun used to refer back to the people mentioned earlier in the verse who were given blessings and became happy with what they were given
This pronoun is being used as the object of the verb أَخَذْنٰ (We seized), showing these people were seized suddenly

steps.m: to lose hope or be in despair

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The مُ prefix and middle ِ vowel change this from an action to describing someone experiencing that state
The ون ending makes this plural (more than two people), and the shadda on م emphasizes the state of despair

steps.m: it was cut off completely (subject is دَابِرُ - last remnant)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice to show something was done to the subject دَابِرُ (last remnant)

steps.m: and so it was cut off completely (subject is دَابِرُ - last remnant)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of what came before. The فَ here indicates the consequence or result.

steps.m: to come after or be behind something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (he followed) to a noun meaning 'one who follows'

steps.m: last remaining part (subject of قُطِعَ - was cut off)
steps.n: Changed from kasra to damma to show this word is the subject (the thing that was cut off)

steps.m: to stand/rise up (especially as a group)

steps.n: Changes verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'those who stand together' -> 'people'
Added 'the' to make it refer to a specific group of people
Added kasra at the end to show this describes who did the wrongdoing (ظَلَمُوا۟) in this ayah
Added ن and changed the ending to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ letter to make it specific. This word connects to ظَلَمُوا۟ (they wronged) - it tells us who did the wronging.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا to show a group of people did this action. This word describes who was cut off in فَقُطِعَ (was cut off)
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'praise'. This word is part of a phrase meaning 'all praise belongs to Allah'.
Added 'al' (ال) at the beginning to mean 'the', and a damma (ُ) at the end to show it's the subject
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This is part of the common phrase 'الحمد لله' (all praise belongs to Allah).
This is the base word Allah, which is the proper name of God in Arabic. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a three-letter root.
Added ل at the beginning which means 'for' or 'to'. When ل combines with ال, the ا is dropped and the ل gets a shadda (double mark).

steps.m: for/to Allah (connected to الْحَمْدُ - the praise)
steps.n: Added the kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is connected to الْحَمْدُ (the praise) in the phrase 'praise belongs to Allah'.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show this describes someone who really takes care of others
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to الْعٰلَمِينَ (the worlds) that comes after it, making the phrase 'Lord of the worlds'
Changed from root meaning 'to know' into a noun meaning 'something special/noticeable' by adding vowels
Added an alif to make it into a noun meaning the whole world/universe
Added ين to make plural form. This word is possessed by رَبِّ (Lord of)
Added ال to make definite and elongated alif for proper pronunciation. The word remains possessed by رَبِّ (Lord of)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
Adding أ (hamza) at the start turns this into a question meaning 'have you seen/considered?' - like asking someone to think about something
Added ت at the end to show you're asking one person 'have you seen/considered?'

steps.m: tell me/have you all considered (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: Changed from addressing one person to addressing many people by adding تُمْ at the end

steps.m: if (introduces the condition 'what if Allah took away...')
steps.n: This is a conditional word that introduces a hypothetical scenario. In this ayah, it starts the condition 'if Allah took away your hearing and sight...' which is followed by a question about who could restore them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to يَأْتِيكُم (comes to you) in the ayah, showing what Allah could do.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject doing the action in this sentence.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he heard) to a noun meaning the sense or ability of hearing itself. This word is what Allah takes away in the phrase 'if Allah took away your hearing.'
Added كُمْ to show this is the hearing belonging to multiple people being spoken to. The َ (fatha) is added before كُمْ to connect it smoothly.

steps.m: to see or perceive with the eyes

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun form
Changed to plural form by adding أَ at start and lengthening middle vowel
Added conjunction وَ (and) to connect this word with سَمْعَكُمْ (your hearing) from earlier in the ayah
Added كُمْ to show possession by multiple people being addressed. This word is what Allah takes in the phrase 'if Allah took your hearing and your sight'
This root relates to finishing or completing something definitively, like putting a seal on a document
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something Allah did in the past. This word works with يَأْتِيكُم (comes to you) later in the ayah.
Added وَ to connect this action to Allah taking away their hearing and sight (أَخَذَ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Basic preposition showing something is physically or metaphorically on top of something else
The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is the end of the word. This helps with reading but doesn't change the meaning. Here it shows that something (sealing) is being done on top of their hearts.
Connected the root letters together. A heart is named this way because it constantly changes states by turning blood from one type to another.
This is the plural form of قَلْب (heart)
كُم shows these hearts belong to multiple people. This word is what was sealed (خَتَمَ) in the ayah.
Interrogative pronoun meaning 'who', with shadda for emphasis. The shadda on initial meem adds emphasis.

steps.m: to be worshipped, to be taken as deity

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The small alif makes the 'aa' sound longer

steps.m: a god (subject of يَأْتِيكُم - brings to you)
steps.n: Added double damma (ٌ) to show this is indefinite (a god vs. the god) and is doing the action in يَأْتِيكُم (brings to you)
The root letters غ-ي-ر get vowel marks (َ ْ ُ) to make the word غَيْرُ meaning 'other than'. It connects to الله after it to mean 'other than Allah'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that refers only to the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession - Allah is the one who owns and controls your hearing and sight in this verse
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, with ْ (sukun) on أ and ِ (kasra) on ت to complete the present tense pattern

steps.m: he comes to you all (object of أَخَذَ)
steps.n: Added كُم at end to show the action affects multiple people. This word is one of the objects of أَخَذَ (he took) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing how something is done

steps.m: with it (referring back to سَمْعَكُمْ - your hearing)
steps.n: Added هِ which refers back to something mentioned before (in this case, their hearing that Allah might take away)
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed the vowels to make it an imperative (command) verb
Added helper alif at start because Arabic words can't begin with a sukun
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something. In this ayah, it's asking about how Allah shows His signs.
Adding these vowel marks helps pronounce the word correctly as a question word
Adding the fatha at the end shows this is a complete question word
Added vowel marks to make it 'he turned/changed' in the past tense
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more thorough
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start and ُ at end. This word connects to آيَات (signs) which comes after it in the ayah.
Forms the singular noun for 'sign' by converting the root into a noun pattern with a long vowel
Forms regular feminine plural from singular آية

steps.m: the signs (object of نُصَرِّفُ - we explain in different ways)
steps.n: Makes the word definite with 'the' and shows it's the object being explained in different ways

This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. The ُ (damma) and َّ (shadda) make it stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it connects to the word يَصْدِفُونَ (they turn away) to show what happens next.
This is a separate pronoun that refers to the disbelievers being discussed. It connects to the next word يَصْدِفُونَ (they turn away) as its subject.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: they turn away from something (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Changed from 'he turns away' to 'they turn away' by adding ون at the end to make it plural
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it a command form. The damma (ُ) and sukun (ْ) show it's a command.
The middle weak letter (و) is dropped to make it easier to say as a command
The أَ at the start makes this a rhetorical question asking for consideration, not just literal seeing
Adding تَ shows this is addressing a single male person directly

steps.m: have you all considered? (addressing the disbelievers being spoken to in عَذَابُ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to address multiple people. This question leads into mentioning Allah's punishment (عَذَابُ ٱللَّهِ) in the rest of the verse.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a possible situation. Here it starts a condition about 'if Allah's punishment comes', connecting to the verb أَتَىٰكُمْ (comes to you) that follows it.
Added fatha marks to create past tense verb form. The ى with alif above (ىٰ) at the end is because this is a weak root ending in ي

steps.m: he came to you all (object of قُلْ - say)
steps.n: The suffix كم shows who received the action - 'you all'. This word is part of what is being said in the command قُلْ (say)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif to make it a noun meaning 'punishment'. The U ending shows this is the subject doing something. In this ayah, it's connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) to mean 'Allah's punishment'.
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped and the first lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, since this word belongs to عَذَابُ (punishment). Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he surprised) to a noun meaning 'one sudden event'
Adds -an ending to make it describe HOW something happens (like 'suddenly')
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two possibilities: Allah's punishment coming suddenly (بَغْتَةً) or openly/publicly (جَهْرَةً). It's like saying 'either...or' in English.

steps.m: to be clear, visible, or done openly

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'the act of being clear/visible'
Added ة to show one specific instance of the action

steps.m: openly, visibly (describing how عَذَابُ ٱللَّهِ might come)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this describes how something happens. In this ayah, it describes how Allah's punishment might come - in an open/visible way.
This is a question word that turns a statement into a yes/no question. In this ayah, it's asking about the verb يُهْلَكُ (destroyed/punished).
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed from active (he destroyed) to passive (to be destroyed)
This is a basic particle used for exception, not derived from any root
Added shadda and alif to complete the word. The shadda emphasizes the 'l' sound, while the alif helps with pronunciation.

steps.m: to stand upright, rise up, establish

steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject or in nominative case

steps.m: the people (subject of يُهْلَكُ - is destroyed)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite (like 'the' in English). The ُ ending shows this is the subject of the verb يُهْلَكُ (is destroyed) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from describing an action to describing someone who does that action
Makes the word plural to show many people who act unjustly
Adds ال to point to specific wrongdoers and doubles the ظ letter for joining with ال
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions) or 'not' (for negation). Here it means 'not' because it's negating the verb نُرْسِلُ (We send).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added نُ at start to show 'we', changed vowels to show present tense, and added ُ at end to show it's doing the action. This word connects to 'the messengers' (ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes from verb (sending) to noun (one being sent)
Makes the word plural by adding ين ending

steps.m: the messengers (who are being sent by Allah)
steps.n: Added the definite article ال to specify these particular messengers, with ٱ replacing the ا due to connecting to previous word
The shadda (ّ) emphasizes the 'ل' to show a strong exception. This is a basic particle used to exclude something from what was mentioned before it.

The alif (ا) at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it in the sentence. In this ayah, it connects to مُبَشِّرِينَ (bringers of good news) to show that the messengers are only sent as bringers of good news.
Adding مُ at beginning and ِ on middle letter creates active participle pattern showing doer of action

steps.m: one who gives much good news
steps.n: Adding the shadda (double letter) makes it mean 'one who gives good news often/repeatedly'

steps.m: one who gives much good news (in a chain of words)
steps.n: The word gets a kasra (-i) sound at the end because it's connected to words after it
Regular sound masculine plural ending added to singular form

steps.m: as givers of much good news (object of نُرْسِلُ/we send)
steps.n: The fatha shows this word receives the action of sending (نُرْسِلُ) in the verse
Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle turns 'warn' into 'warner' (someone who warns others)
Added ين to show multiple warners. This word is being acted upon by نُرْسِلُ (we send) in the ayah - Allah is saying He sends the messengers as warners.
Added وَ meaning 'and' to connect this with مُبَشِّرِينَ (givers of good news) - Allah sends the messengers as both warners and givers of good news
This is a question word that can mean 'who' or 'whoever' depending on context. Here it means 'whoever' because it's introducing a conditional statement about anyone who believes.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. It connects this part to the previous statement about messengers being warners and bearers of good news.
Connected the letters and added basic past tense vowels
Added alif after first letter to show that someone actively chooses to believe or trust in something. This word connects to فَلَا خَوْفٌ (then no fear) which follows it in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a past tense verb
The أ at the start makes the action more direct - instead of just being right, it means to actively make something right
The وَ at the start means 'and'
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no'. It's used to negate what comes after it.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. In this ayah, it's saying 'then there will not be fear' as a result of believing and doing good deeds mentioned before.
Added vowel marks to turn the action into a noun representing the feeling itself
Added double-damma to show this is the subject doing the action in the phrase 'there is no fear'
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ when adding a suffix
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being acted upon (them)
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it

steps.m: and not (connected to فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous statement. This connects to the earlier phrase 'then no fear upon them'
This is a pronoun that means 'they' (for a group of males or mixed group). In this ayah, it's connected to the verb يَحْزَنُونَ (they grieve) to show who is doing the grieving.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to point to specific people
Added و at the beginning to connect it to what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَمَسُّهُمُ (touches them) in this ayah.

steps.m: to deny or reject something as false

steps.n: Adding a shadda (ّ) on ذ makes the denial stronger and more emphatic. The fatha marks (َ) connect the letters together.

steps.m: they intensely denied/rejected (the signs/verses mentioned in بِـَٔايٰتِنَا)
steps.n: Adding وا changes 'he' to 'they' (a group of people) in the past tense. This word connects to بِـَٔايٰتِنَا (our signs/verses) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Forms feminine singular noun meaning 'sign' or 'miracle'. The initial hamza and ya merge into a long alif (آ)
Changes the word to plural form to show multiple signs or miracles
Added 'our' to show these signs belong to Allah

steps.m: with/by our signs (object of كَذَّبُوا - they denied)
steps.n: Added preposition بِ meaning 'with/by'. This word shows what was denied by the disbelievers in the phrase 'those who denied our signs'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present/future tense, and doubled middle letter with shadda. This word describes what العَذَابُ (the punishment) does.

steps.m: touches/will touch them (the punishment touches them)
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show who receives the action - refers to those who denied the signs mentioned in the previous part of the verse
Changed from action to the thing itself (punishment). This word is the subject that does the action of touching/afflicting in يَمَسُّهُمُ (touches/afflicts them).

steps.m: the punishment (subject of يَمَسُّهُمُ - touches/afflicts them)
steps.n: Adding definite article to make it 'the punishment'

steps.m: the punishment (subject of يَمَسُّهُمُ - touches/afflicts them)
steps.n: Adding ُ (damma) to show this word is doing the action of touching/afflicting
This is a connecting word that refers to their actions mentioned in يَفْسُقُونَ (they defiantly disobey)

steps.m: because of what/that which (connects to يَفْسُقُونَ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is the reason or cause for the punishment mentioned before (يَمَسُّهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ - the punishment will touch them)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word is the subject of يَفْسُقُونَ (they defiantly disobey) in this ayah.

steps.m: he departed from the right way
steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
The ي at the start shows it's happening now or in the future
Adding ون shows that many people are doing the action together (they disobey)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Dropping the middle weak letter و, and 3) Removing the final vowel. This word connects to the next statement لَّآ أَقُولُ (I do not say) as part of Allah's command to the Prophet ﷺ about what to say.
Basic negative particle with alif to extend the sound

Added shadda (ّ) to emphasize the 'L' sound and changed alif to alif madd (آ) to extend the vowel sound even more. This makes the negation stronger, like saying 'absolutely not' instead of just 'not'.

steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is what was done/committed. This word is what was 'done' in the phrase 'whoever among you does an evil deed'.

steps.m: to not know, to be unaware

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ة and alif changes the verb (being unaware) into a noun meaning one specific act of unawareness
Adding بِ at the start shows how something was done - in this case, actions done while being unaware
Adding tanwin kasra (ةٍ) shows this describes how something was done. In this ayah, it describes how someone did wrong (عَمِلَ) - they did it while being unaware.
Basic particle showing sequence of events
Added damma for proper pronunciation
Added shadda with fatha to show emphasis and indicate a time gap between events. In this ayah, it connects the previous mention of someone doing wrong with their later repentance.

steps.m: to return from wrong to right
he returned from wrong to right
steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
This is a preposition showing where an action starts or comes from. Here it connects the verb عَمِلَ (did) with كُمْ (you all) to show which people the action came from.
Added a small ن mark (called a 'noon ghunna') that shows how to pronounce the ن with a nasal sound when it connects to the next word
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something
Added pronoun هِ meaning 'it/his', referring back to سوء (evil deed) mentioned earlier in the verse
Added ۦ after هِ which is just a pronunciation helper, doesn't change meaning
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added 'أ' at the start changes meaning from 'became right' to 'made something right' - like changing from happening on its own to making it happen

steps.m: and he made (something) right (after تَابَ - repented)
steps.n: This word follows تَابَ (repented) in the ayah, showing the sequence of actions: first repenting, then making things right
This is the base emphatic particle that shows certainty about something
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added هُ with extension mark (ۥ) at the end to show this refers to 'he'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
This form shows someone who does something a lot - here, someone who forgives again and again
The word gained a tanween (double damma) at the end, making it a complete standalone description of Allah being very forgiving
Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who has this quality very strongly
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite - meaning 'a' merciful one rather than 'the' merciful one
The shadda (double letter) was added to make the word stronger/more intense in meaning
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'that'
Adding لِ makes the demonstrative more emphatic
Adding كَ makes it mean 'that' (pointing to something far away)
Adding كَ at the beginning adds extra emphasis to the comparison
Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it
Added vowels to connect the letters together and make it a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intensive - showing that the explanation is very detailed and thorough
Changed to present tense with 'we' as the subject. In this ayah, this refers to how Allah explains His signs in detail.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Changes from basic root meaning 'to show' into a noun meaning 'something that shows/proves' (like a sign or miracle)
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs. This is what is being detailed/explained in نُفَصِّلُ (we explain in detail)
Adds kasra at the end to show this word is affected by a preposition or being possessed
Connected root letters with vowels to make basic past tense verb
The prefix است changes the meaning to 'seeking to become' clear rather than just 'being' clear
Changed from past to present tense by adding تَ prefix and changing middle vowel from ا to ِ
Added لِ (li-) at the start to show purpose - 'so that' or 'in order to'

steps.m: and so that it becomes clear

steps.n: Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to create a noun meaning the result or place of the action
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject doing the action in تَسْتَبِينَ (becomes clear)

steps.m: to do wrong, to commit crimes

steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle turns this into a noun for someone who does the action. Like how 'crime' becomes 'criminal' in English.
Adding ين at the end makes it plural (more than one). This word is connected to سَبِيلُ (path) in the ayah, showing 'path of the criminals'.
Adding ال at the start points to specific criminals being talked about, not just any criminals
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) Dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) Adding damma (ُ) on first letter, 3) Adding sukun (ْ) on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
This is a word that adds emphasis to what follows, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: indeed I (subject of نُهِيتُ - I was forbidden)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show this is about 'I/me'. The ن gets doubled (with shadda) and takes a kasra (ِ) before adding the ي to make the sounds flow better.

steps.m: to prevent or stop someone from doing something

steps.n: Changed the vowels to show this is happening TO someone instead of someone doing it. Like changing 'stop' to 'was stopped'.

steps.m: I was prevented (from أَنْ أَعْبُدَ - that I worship)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' am the one being prevented. This connects to the next words أَنْ أَعْبُدَ which explain what I was prevented from doing.

Changed to present tense first person form. The أَنْ before it makes it subjunctive (with fatha ending instead of damma)

steps.m: those who (referring to the false gods that are worshipped)
steps.n: Added ال ('the') at the start to make it mean 'those who' instead of just 'who', and the ل is added to help pronounce it smoothly
Added vowels to connect root letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Prefix تَ changes 'he' to 'you', making it speak to one person

steps.m: you all call upon (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: Added نَ to show multiple people are being addressed
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from. In this ayah, it connects what they call/worship (تَدْعُونَ) with 'besides/other than' (دُونِ), showing they worship from besides Allah.
Adding damma (ُ) to make it a complete word
Added kasra at the end to show connection to the following word اللَّهِ (Allah)

Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of دُونِ (besides/other than). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasla since it comes after another word.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form. The middle و is dropped because it's a weak letter. This word starts verse 6:56 where Allah commands Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers.
Starting with the basic negative particle لا and adding fatha for pronunciation
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on ل makes the word stronger and more emphatic, like saying 'absolutely not' instead of just 'not'
The alif is elongated (آ instead of ا) to show a longer pronunciation, which adds to the emphasis
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added shadda to emphasize the action

steps.m: I follow (responding to لَّآ 'no' in the ayah)
steps.n: Changed from 'he followed' to 'I follow' by adding أَ (a) at start to mean 'I', and changing vowels to show present tense
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding fatha marks
Added أَ at the start to make it mean 'more' or 'greater' (like English -er), with a silent mark after to make it easier to say
Changed from single desire/whim to plural form by adding آء at the end, like changing 'wish' to 'wishes'

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizing ضَلَلْتُ - I would go astray)
steps.n: This is a particle that adds emphasis to the word after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the speaker would definitely go astray if they followed their desires instead of Allah's guidance.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: I wandered off the right path
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to mean 'I' did the action
This is a basic particle that refers to a specific time or situation

steps.m: then/in that case (shows consequence of نُهِيتُ - I was forbidden)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif (ًا) changes this from just referring to time to showing a result or consequence. In this ayah, it shows the result of being forbidden from worshipping false gods.
This is the base negative particle. The آ is just a spelling variant of ا that doesn't change the meaning.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This و works like 'and' in English.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the pronunciation clear. This is a personal pronoun meaning 'I'.
Added a special mark (۠) to show extra emphasis on the pronoun 'I'. This emphasizes that the speaker is specifically talking about themselves.
This is a preposition showing source or origin
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word الْمُهْتَدِينَ (the guided ones). This makes the words flow together smoothly when reading.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The ي changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show the action happens to oneself

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on second letter, 3) Dropping the weak middle letter و. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.

Adding ى at the end shows this is about 'me/I'. The kasra (ِ) comes before ى because they go together. In this ayah, this word connects to عَلَىٰ to mean 'I am upon'.

steps.m: upon (preposition showing something is on top of or over something else)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. It's one of the fundamental words in Arabic that isn't built from a root.

Added shadda because this word connects to the next word رَّبِّى (my Lord). This connection makes the pronunciation stronger.

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.m: my Lord (subject of يَقُصُّ - He narrates)

steps.m: with it (referring to what was denied)

Changed to command form with shadda on middle letter to show emphasis
Added present tense prefix يَ and final vowel ُ. This word relates to الْحَقَّ (the truth) which comes after it in the ayah, showing what is being narrated.

Added ي with kasra to show this means 'with me'. This word connects to تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (you seek to hasten) in the ayah, showing what they're trying to hasten.

This is a basic pronoun that refers back to Allah in this context. It's the subject (doer) of the verb يَعْلَمُ (he knows).

The shadda (ّ) is added to show emphasis and stronger pronunciation when connecting with the previous word
Changes from root meaning into a noun meaning 'above'
Added كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed, with ِ connecting it to the word. This comes after مِن (from) in the phrase مِن فَوْقِكُمْ meaning 'from above you'.
Simple connecting word that gives choices between options. Here it connects 'punishment' (عَذَابًا) with other types of punishment mentioned after.

steps.m: from (showing the starting point of عَذَابًا/punishment)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it shows where the punishment would come from.
Adding vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'under'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِن (from/of) in the ayah
Added vowels to make the basic singular noun form
Changed the word to a broken plural form by adding أ at the start and changing the vowels. This is a common way to make plurals in Arabic.

steps.m: your feet (referring to the group being addressed in this verse)
steps.n: Added ِكُمْ at the end to show these are the feet belonging to the group being spoken to

steps.m: or (connecting عَذَابًا مِّن فَوْقِكُمْ with مِن تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِكُمْ)
steps.n: This word connects two possible sources of punishment mentioned in the ayah - either from above or from below. It shows these are alternative possibilities.
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha

steps.m: he confuses you all (object of يَبْعَثَ - 'that he send')
steps.n: The word changed to include 'you all' (كُم) as the object - showing who is being confused
Added vowel marks to form the plural noun for groups

steps.m: into groups (object of يَلْبِسَكُمْ - confuse/divide you)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of the verb يَلْبِسَكُمْ (divide/confuse you)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added alif at beginning to show causing someone else to do the action
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: and he makes (someone) taste (connected to يَبْعَثَ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous verb يَبْعَثَ (send/raise up)
This turns the idea of splitting into a word meaning 'a part'. Here it is the object of يَبْعَثَ (he sends), showing what is being sent.
Adds accusative case marker to indicate the word is the object of the verb

steps.m: some of you (as the object of يَبْعَثَ - what is being sent)
steps.n: Adds second person plural masculine pronoun suffix to show possession
Changes from verb to noun meaning a single instance of might/severity
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of tasting (يُذِيقَ) in the verse
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make the noun meaning 'some' or 'part'. This word is connected to بَأْسَ (might) in the verse.
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this word is indefinite (not specific) and is being possessed by another word.

steps.m: to look at something carefully or think about it deeply

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added hamza prefix and changed vowels to make it a command to one person
The word is already a complete command form meaning 'look!'
This is the basic form used to ask about the way or manner of something
Adding these vowel marks creates the standard question word 'how'
Adding the final fatha mark completes this question word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something thoroughly or repeatedly
Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing it, changed middle vowel to kasra and added damma at end to show it's happening now

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident
a sign or verse (something that shows or makes clear)
steps.n: Root meaning 'show/make clear' became a noun meaning 'a sign' by adding special vowels and changing letters
Changed from singular to plural form
Added kasra at end to show it's being possessed or after a preposition
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
This is a particle that expresses hope or expectation for what follows
Added هُمْ (they) at the end to show who the hope is about. This connects to the next word يَفْقَهُونَ (they understand) by showing who will do the understanding.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb

steps.m: they understand deeply (present tense, referring to those who reject the signs)
steps.n: Changed from 'he understands' to 'they understand' by adding ون at the end, showing it's now talking about a group of people
Added basic past tense vowels to make it a complete word meaning 'he denied'

steps.m: he denied intensively/repeatedly (subject of هُوَ الْحَقُّ)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows doing something strongly or repeatedly. This word is connected to هُوَ الْحَقُّ (it is the truth) in this ayah.
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added هِ (hi) at the end to show this means 'it' or 'him'. This word connects to قَوْمُكَ (your people) in this ayah, showing what they denied.
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to stand firmly or rise up

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks. The root meaning of standing firmly evolved to mean a group of people who stand together as one.
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action in the sentence - these people are the ones who denied/rejected (كَذَّبَ)
Added كَ at the end to show these are specifically your people. This word is the subject doing the denying/rejecting (كَذَّبَ) in this verse.
This is the basic independent pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it'. In this ayah, it's connected to the word الْحَقُّ (the truth), showing that 'it is the truth'.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it
Adding vowel marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he made true) to a noun (truth) by dropping letters and adding ُ at the end to show it's the subject being denied

steps.m: the truth (subject of كَذَّبَ - they denied)
steps.n: Added ال to specify we're talking about absolute truth, not just any truth. This is the subject that your people denied.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma (ُ) on first letter, 2) Dropping the middle weak letter و, and 3) Making the last letter silent. This tells one person to do something.
This is a special verb that only comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. It's used to negate things.
Added vowels to make it mean 'is not' - this word is special because even though it looks like past tense, it means present tense
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' and removed the weak letter ي since it comes before تُ
Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda
This is a preposition that comes from the idea of being above something. It's one of the basic Arabic words that doesn't come from a three-letter root.
The ى at the end changes to يْ to make it ready for adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: upon you all (object of لَّسْتُ - I am not)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this affects 'you all'. In this ayah, this word is connected to لَّسْتُ (I am not), showing who the speaker is not over/upon.

steps.m: to take care of, to protect
guardian, protector, one who takes care of
steps.n: Added ي to make it mean 'one who protects' instead of just 'to protect'
Added بِ at beginning to show this is done 'with' or 'by' something. This word is being acted upon by لَسْتُ (I am not)
Added ٍ at the end because it follows the preposition بِ. This shows it's indefinite (like saying 'a guardian' instead of 'the guardian')
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a word meaning 'all' or 'every'
Added kasra at the end because this word is being acted upon by the preposition لِ
Added لِ at the beginning to show this word connects to نَبَإٍ (news/information) in the ayah
Added shadda on the ل to emphasize the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it into a noun meaning 'news'
The hamza at the end gets a special seat when it has a kasra

steps.m: a news (that will come to pass)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is any news, not specific news. In this ayah, it refers to news that will definitely come to pass.

steps.m: to settle or stay fixed in one place

steps.n: Basic vowels (fatha) added to make past tense verb meaning 'he settled'
Added است at the beginning to show something settling down or becoming stable on its own

steps.m: a place or time of settling
steps.n: Changed to a noun showing where or when the settling happens
Makes the word indefinite and nominative case
This is a particle that shows something will happen in the future

steps.m: and will (connects to تَعْلَمُونَ - you will know)
steps.n: Added و at the start to connect this word with what came before it. This word pairs with تَعْلَمُونَ (you will know) to say 'and you will know'
Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ونَ to show that multiple people are being addressed
This is a basic word that means 'when' - it's used to talk about time or conditions
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did this action, with sukun (ْ) before it to help pronunciation. In this ayah, Allah is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ about what he sees.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Added ال (the) to make it mean 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Added ين to show we're talking about multiple people. This word describes the people who are the subject of يَخُوضُونَ (they engage in)

steps.m: to plunge/dive into (especially in speech or discussion)

steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show ongoing action, with ُ vowels to show it's happening now

steps.m: they are plunging into (discussion) (subject of فَأَعْرِضْ - so turn away)
steps.n: Added ونَ at the end to show many people are doing this action. This word explains what the people are doing that causes the command فَأَعْرِضْ (so turn away)
Basic preposition showing location or being within something. Here it shows people engaging in (within) discussion of Allah's verses.
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing convention and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'sign'
Adding ات makes the word plural - like adding 's' in English
Adding ِ shows this word belongs to or is connected to something after it

steps.m: our signs/verses (that they inappropriately engage with)
steps.n: Added نَا to show these are Allah's signs, and the alif is written as ٰ. This word is the object of يَخُوضُونَ (they engage inappropriately).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Command form meaning 'you turn away!' - made from adding 'a' at start and changing vowels

steps.m: so turn away! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ about those who mock Allah's signs)
steps.n: Added 'so' to connect with previous statement
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'away from'. In this ayah, it's used with the command 'turn away' (فَأَعْرِضْ) to show who to turn away from.

steps.m: from/away from them (object of فَأَعْرِضْ - turn away)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being turned away from. This connects to the command فَأَعْرِضْ (turn away) earlier in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to the basic particle form
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning of the limit or endpoint
Added the elongated alif (ىٰ) at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to يَخُوضُوا۟ (they engage in discourse).

steps.m: to plunge into/engage deeply in discussion

steps.n: Root letters were connected with vowels (َ and ْ) to make a noun meaning 'the act of engaging in something'
Added present tense prefix and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people doing the action
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it shows being 'in' or 'engaged in' discussing Allah's signs/verses.
Basic Form I past tense verb with root letters ح-د-ث
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (happened) to an adjective/noun (new/narration)
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of the preposition فِى (in/into)

steps.m: to change or be different from

steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the next word, meaning 'other than' something
Added هِۦ at the end to show this means 'other than it'
Basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before
Added ما with emphasis (shown by the shadda) to make the condition stronger. The ن from إِن combines with م to make a stronger م sound (مّ)
Basic past tense verb pattern showing completed action
Changes from past tense 'forgot' to present tense 'is made to forget'
Changes from passive 'being forgotten' to active 'making forget' + adds نّ for strong emphasis

steps.m: he (the Shaytan) will surely make you forget
steps.n: The attached pronoun كَ shows who is being made to forget. The subject 'he' refers to الشَّيْطٰنُ (the Shaytan) which comes right after this word in the ayah.

steps.m: one who is rebellious and distant (from good)
steps.n: Added ي after ش to turn the root into a noun meaning 'one who is rebellious'
Added ا to change from a general description of being rebellious to specifically mean a devil/satan
Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the ش (shown by ّ)
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence
Sequential conjunction that shows this follows from the previous statement
Added the negative particle لا which negates the following verb تَقْعُدْ (sit). Together they mean 'then do not sit'.

steps.m: to sit, remain in a place

steps.n: Changes the verb 'to be distant' into the concept of 'after' (since what comes after is distant from what came before)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes verb into noun showing the concept itself
Adds ى at the end to make it mean 'a reminder/warning'
Adds 'the' (ال) at the start, making it specific/definite
Basic preposition showing being together with or alongside something/someone. In this ayah it connects to ٱلْقَوْمِ (the people) to show who you should not sit with.

steps.m: to stand upright, rise up, establish
a group that stands together, people, folk
steps.n: Changes the verb concept of 'standing' into a noun meaning 'those who stand together' - a common way Arabic creates group words
The kasra ending shows this word is possessed by or connected to الظَّالِمِينَ (the wrongdoers) in this ayah
Adding definite article to make the noun definite

steps.m: to do wrong or act unjustly

steps.n: Changes from an action to someone who does that action. This describes القوم (the people) in this ayah.
Makes the word plural to show it's describing multiple wrongdoers. The ين ending also shows this is connected to القوم (the people) in the sentence.
Adding ال makes it specific. The ظ gets doubled (shadda) because it combines with the ل from ال.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to the verb يَتَّقُونَ (they become righteous) that comes later in the ayah

steps.m: upon/on (shows something is on top of or responsible for the people who fear in this ayah)
steps.n: This is one of the basic prepositions in Arabic that shows position or responsibility. Here it connects to 'those who fear' (ٱلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ) to show what the previous statement applies to.

steps.m: those who - points to specific people being talked about
steps.n: Built from: ٱلْ (definite article) + ذِي (who/which) + نَ (masculine plural suffix). The ل assimilates into ذ causing shadda.
This is the basic meaning of protecting or shielding
Past tense verb meaning 'he protected' - no doubling of letters occurs here
Changes from past to present tense by adding ي at start
ت shows the person is doing the action to themselves
Shadda makes the meaning stronger
Adding ون at the end makes it plural, showing many people doing the action

This is one of the basic prepositions in Arabic that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to the word حِسَابِهِم (their account) to show what the previous word is being separated or distanced from.
This root is about counting and calculating things
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes verb into noun meaning 'calculation' by adding alif and changing vowels
Adds هِم to show possession by 'them'
This is a basic particle that shows where something starts or comes from
Added shadda for emphasis. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the righteous people are completely free FROM any responsibility for the actions of others.
This root became the word for 'thing' in Arabic
Added vowels (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make the basic word for 'thing'
Added tanwin kasra at the end because it comes after the preposition مِنْ. The ي is written as ى when followed by hamza.
This is a basic particle that shows contrast with what was said before. In this ayah, it contrasts with the previous statement about those who fear Allah not being responsible for others' accounts.

steps.m: and however/but still (connects while contrasting with previous statement)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement with what came before, showing both connection and contrast.
This root is about keeping something in mind
Added vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'remembrance'
Added ىٰ at the end to make it mean a specific reminder or warning. In this ayah, it refers to reminding people to be mindful of Allah.
Added vowel marks and shadda to show the basic word for 'perhaps'

steps.m: perhaps they/maybe they (connected to يَتَّقُونَ - they become righteous)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this hope/expectation applies to 'them'. This connects to the verb يَتَّقُونَ (they become righteous) - expressing hope that they will become righteous.
Changed from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'he protected'
Changes from past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start
Adds reflexive meaning - the action comes back to the doer
Adds plural masculine ending to show multiple people doing the action
Changed from basic root form into a command by dropping the و and adding kasra at the end - tells someone to leave something
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
This is a special demonstrative word composed of: ٱلْ (the) + ذِي (who/which) + نَ (plural suffix). Used to refer to a group being discussed.

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِتَّ to make it mean 'took for himself' instead of just 'took'
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action
Form VIII pattern shows doing action for oneself
The kasra and long ي transform the root into a noun meaning a complete system or way of life
The fatha shows this is the object of the verb اتَّخَذُوا (they took/adopted)
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is their religion
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to play'

steps.m: play/fun (as the object being acted upon in اتَّخَذُوا دِينَهُمْ لَعِبًا)
steps.n: Changed from 'he played' (a verb) to 'play/fun' (a noun) by adding tanween. Shows religion is being treated as a game in this ayah.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the act or result of being amused
Added ًا to show this word is being acted upon by the verb اتَّخَذُوا (they took)
Added وَ (and) to connect this word with لَعِبًا (play) before it

steps.m: to deceive/mislead someone by making something seem better than it really is

steps.n: Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show strong deception. This word relates to الْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا (worldly life) which is doing the deceiving.
Added ت to show the subject is feminine because الْحَيَوٰةُ (life) is a feminine word in Arabic

steps.m: she deceived them (referring to those who take their religion as play and amusement)
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show who was deceived - referring back to الَّذِينَ (those who) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Adds conjunction meaning 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he lived' becomes the noun 'life' by adding و and changing the ending to ة
Adding ال makes it definite. This word is the subject of the verb غَرَّتْ (deluded) in this ayah.
The ُ shows this word is doing the action in the sentence (it is the subject that deluded them)
Changed the root into a feminine noun describing something close or worldly. The و changed to ي because it's easier to say.
Added ال to make it specific. This word describes 'life' (الْحَيَوٰةُ) in the ayah, showing what kind of life distracted them.
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more direct - like the difference between 'mention' and 'remind strongly'
Changed vowels to make it a command form - telling someone to do the action
Added sukun (ْ) at the end to complete the command form
Added و (and) at the beginning to connect this command with the previous phrase in the verse
Added kasra to show how the letter is pronounced
Added هِ to show what is being referred to. This connects back to something mentioned before.
Added pronunciation helpers (ۦٓ) that don't change the meaning

This is a particle that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it introduces what will happen (تُبْسَلَ meaning 'be given up to destruction'). It's like saying 'Leave those who... [so] that they be given up...'
Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he' did the action
Added تُ at the start to show something is happening to someone (not them doing it), with a quiet ب to make it easier to say
The root letters ن-ف-س became the noun نَفْس by adding the vowel marks. This makes it mean a soul/self instead of just 'breathing'. It's the one doing the action in the sentence.

steps.m: a soul, a self, a person (doer of تُبْسَلَ)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite - meaning 'a soul' rather than 'the soul'
This is a combination of two particles: بِ (with/by) and مَا (what/whatever)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb showing completed action

steps.m: she earned (referring to نَفْسٌ - a soul)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine (نَفْسٌ/soul). This word describes what a soul has earned through its actions.
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
This is a special verb that only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning. In this ayah, it negates نَفْسٌ (soul/person), showing that no soul/person has any protector besides Allah.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the letter lam

steps.m: for her/it (feminine) (refers to نَفْسٌ - a soul)
steps.n: Added the feminine pronoun ها with a fatha on the ه to show it refers to something feminine (in this case نَفْسٌ meaning 'a soul')
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'. Here it connects with دُونِ (besides/other than) to show something coming from another source.

steps.m: to be below or less than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below/less than'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes right after it in the ayah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining letters together
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

steps.m: to protect, be close to, take care of

steps.n: Changed from a verb meaning 'to protect' into a noun meaning 'protector/guardian'
Adding shadda emphasizes the protecting role
The tanween shows this is indefinite - like saying 'a protector' instead of 'the protector'
Basic negation particle that means 'not' or 'no'

steps.m: and not (negates having a شَفِيعٌ - intercessor)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous statement. This word negates having an intercessor, connecting to the word شَفِيعٌ that follows it.

steps.m: he helped by speaking for someone
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: someone who helps by speaking for others
steps.n: Changed to show the person who does the action (like changing 'help' to 'helper')

steps.m: a helper/defender (who speaks for others) (subject of لَيْسَ)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this is a subject that is not specific. This word is one of the subjects of لَيْسَ (is not) in this ayah.
This is a compound particle made up of 'wa' (and) plus 'in' (if)
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: you compensate/offer as equal (in relation to كُلَّ عَدْلٍ - every compensation)
steps.n: Changed from 'he was just' to 'you offer as equal' by adding تَ at start to make it 'you', and putting a stop mark (sukun) at end to make it a command. Links to كُلَّ عَدْلٍ (every compensation) in the ayah.
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is receiving the action of تَعْدِلْ (compensate/offer as ransom) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic noun form

steps.m: any justice/compensation (as ransom in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is an indefinite noun being used as compensation or ransom in this context
Starting with the basic negative particle لا which shows simple negation

steps.m: not at all (negating يُؤْخَذْ - will be accepted)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the negation. This makes the 'no' stronger, like saying 'absolutely not' or 'not at all'. In this ayah, it's emphasizing that no ransom will be accepted from these people.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense passive voice by adding يُ at start, changing vowels, and adding sukun at end
This combines the preposition 'min' (from) with the feminine pronoun 'ha' (her/it)
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun meaning 'those'
The alif with madda (ٰ) and the small alif (ٓ) show that we're pointing to something far away
The ئِكَ ending specifies that we're pointing to people rather than things
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ذ letter to connect them smoothly. This word connects descriptions to people or things, like saying 'the one who came'.
Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word is describing the people who took their religion as play and amusement.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added hamza with damma at start and sukun on first root letter for passive voice form

steps.m: they were given up to destruction
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show this happened to a group
This is the basic preposition that shows how or why something happens

steps.m: because of what (connects to كَسَبَتْ - earned)
steps.n: Added ما which means 'what' to ask about or refer to something. Together with بِ it means 'because of what' or 'due to what'. In this ayah, it connects to كَسَبَتْ (earned) to mean 'because of what it earned'
Basic past tense form of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word relates to أُبْسِلُوا۟ (they were given up to destruction) in the ayah, showing what these people earned led to their fate.
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or possession
Added هُ to show this is about a male person or thing
Added م with sukun to change from singular (him) to plural (them)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Transforms verb 'to drink' into noun meaning 'a drink'
Adding double dammah makes it 'a drink' instead of 'the drink'
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from' in English. It's a building block word that doesn't come from a root.
Added a shadda (ّ) on the م because it comes after another م in the previous word (شَرَابٌ). This is just for smooth pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels and ي to make a word that means something with intense heat. In this ayah, it refers specifically to boiling water mentioned as a punishment.
Added tanwin kasra to show this word describes what kind of drink (شَرَابٌ) it is in the phrase 'a drink of boiling water'

Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the result of the action. This word pairs with شَرَابٌ (drink) to describe what awaits those who are mentioned.
Adding و at the beginning connects this word to شَرَابٌ (drink) mentioned before, listing two things they will receive
Adding ٌ shows this is a subject (nominative case) and indefinite
Adding ي between second and last root letter with kasra before it makes it describe something that strongly causes the action. Here it describes عَذَابٌ (punishment) in the ayah.
Adding tanween (double dammah) makes the word indefinite, meaning 'a painful' rather than 'the painful'

steps.m: because of what, due to what
