steps.n: This is a combination of بِ (meaning 'with/by') and مَا (meaning 'what'). Together they mean 'because of what'
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle weak letter و changes to ا in past tense Form I verbs
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action (in this ayah, referring to those who take their religion as amusement)
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters into 'he said' in the past tense
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, and 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on middle letter, and changing final vowel to damma. The weak letter و stays because of the damma.
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by replacing يَ with نَ at the beginning
Added أَ at beginning to make it a question
Added ا with small و above it to show multiple people are asking

steps.m: from (connects دُونِ with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects the word دُونِ (besides/instead of) to the verb أَنَدْعُوا (should we call), showing we're calling to something besides Allah.
Adding damma (ُ) changes it from root to usable word
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'besides Allah'
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped due to frequent use. The middle letter gets a shadda to emphasize its importance.

Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by or connected to هُدَى (guidance) in the phrase 'guidance of Allah'. The alif at the start became a wasl form (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word.

steps.m: what (object of أَنَدْعُوا - should we call)
steps.n: This is a question word that asks about things (not people). In this ayah, it's asking about what things people might worship besides Allah. It's the object of the verb أَنَدْعُوا (should we call/worship).
This is the basic word for making something negative in Arabic. Here it comes before the verb يَنفَعُنَا to say something does not benefit us.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Added نَا at the end to show who receives the benefit (us)
Combines 'and' (و) with 'not' (لا)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to ُ, and combined the two ر letters with a shadda to show the strong harmful action
Added نَا at the end to show that 'us' is receiving the harm
Adding fathas makes this a past tense verb meaning 'he did the action'
Changed to passive by using 'u' sound at start and doubling middle letter - means something was done to it rather than it doing something
Added نُ at beginning to show 'we' and changed final vowel to ُ to show present tense
Added وَ at beginning to connect with previous phrase
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or upon something else
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) which is just a spelling variation of ى at the end of words
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show this word connects to the next word أَعْقَابِنَا (our heels)

steps.m: to follow behind or leave tracks/footprints

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning the result/consequence of something
Changes singular 'heel' to plural 'heels' by adding أ at start and ا after second root letter. This is a common pattern for making plurals in Arabic.
Adds نَا meaning 'our' with ِ before it because it comes after the word عَلَىٰٓ (upon) in the ayah. So together it means 'upon our heels' - describing returning to a previous way.
Root letters ب-ع-د (meaning distance/separation) were joined together with vowels to make a word meaning 'after'

steps.m: after (showing time or sequence after هَدَىٰنَا)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it in the sentence

steps.m: when (connecting هَدَىٰنَا 'He guided us' to what follows)
steps.n: This is a time word that connects two events in the past. In this ayah, it connects 'He guided us' with what happens after, showing when one thing happened in relation to another.

steps.m: to guide, direct, or show the right way

steps.n: Adding vowel marks creates basic past tense verb meaning 'guided'
Added نَا at the end to show who receives the guidance (us). This word connects to the previous part of the ayah about not being guided by false gods.
Changed from any deity to specifically The One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of هَدَىٰنَا - guided us)
steps.n: Added emphasis with shadda and made it the subject by adding damma. In this ayah, Allah is the one who guided us (هَدَىٰنَا).
Starting with the basic relative pronoun ذِى which points to someone or something
Added ال (the) at the beginning, but when ال meets ذ, the ل combines with ذ to make a stronger ذ sound (shown by ّ)

steps.m: like the one who (connected to ٱسْتَهْوَتْهُ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the beginning to show comparison. This word connects to ٱسْتَهْوَتْهُ (seduced him) in the next part of the ayah, comparing someone to a person who was led astray.

steps.m: to desire or incline towards something

steps.n: Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Adds 'ast' at start to show making someone else desire/incline towards something, like luring them
Added feminine past tense marker. In this ayah, the subject is الشياطين (the devils), which takes feminine singular agreement
Added object pronoun هُ (him) to show who was lured. This refers to the person led astray by the devils mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to be rebellious or act like a devil

steps.n: Adding ي makes it mean 'to do something in the way of a devil'
Added ا after second root letter ط to form the intensive noun pattern that indicates a being characterized by extreme rebelliousness. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb استهوت (led astray).
Changes from one devil to many devils by breaking up the word pattern - a special way to make this word plural
Adding definite article to make the noun definite
Adding shadda to emphasize the middle letter
Final damma shows this word is doing the action (subject) in relation to استهوت (led astray)
This is a preposition that shows where something is located. In this ayah, it shows where the confused/bewildered person (حَيْرَانَ) is - in the earth/land.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters

steps.m: the earth (possessed by/belonging to something mentioned after)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the earth' and the kasra (small diagonal line) at the end shows it belongs to something after it
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this is something that happened to someone

steps.m: extremely confused and lost (describing someone in the ayah)
steps.n: The ان ending shows this is a very strong state of confusion. In this ayah, it describes someone who has been led astray by devils.
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it's a preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to
Added elongation marks (ۥٓ) to connect with the following verb يَدْعُونَ (they call). These marks help with pronunciation and show connection to the next word.

steps.m: to accompany or be a close friend

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to accompany'
Changes from 'he accompanied' to mean 'companions' by using the plural pattern أَفْعَال - adds أَ at start and ا after ح

steps.m: companions (as the doers of an action)
steps.n: Added ٌ at the end to show these companions are doing something, and changed ا to ٰ which is just a different way of writing the same sound
Added vowels to connect the letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a special type of word.
Changes from past tense (he called) to present tense (he calls) by adding يَ at start
Adds plural masculine subject marker
Adds object pronoun 'him'. This word يَدْعُونَهُۥٓ (they call him) is connected to أَصْحٰبٌ (companions) in the ayah - the companions are the ones doing the calling.
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to ٱلْهُدَى (guidance) to show 'toward guidance'

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Root meaning 'guide' becomes a special noun meaning 'guidance' or 'right path'
The definite article ال is added as a prefix, with the standard hamzat wasl spelling ٱ
Changed from basic verb 'to come' into a command meaning 'come!'. The middle letter gets a kasra sound and first letter changes shape to match.
Added نا meaning 'us' to show who is being commanded to come. Like saying 'come!' vs 'come to us!'
Changed the root 'say' into a command by adding the vowel marks ُ and ْ (like saying 'Say!')
The weak letter و is removed to make the command shorter and easier to say
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah's guidance is the true guidance.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to guide' into the noun 'guidance'
Adding ى at the end makes it a complete noun meaning 'guidance'
Changed from any deity to specifically The One God by adding ال and merging letters. The first ل gets a shadda to show the merger.
Added ِ at the end to show this connects to the word دُونِ (besides/other than) that comes before it in the ayah. Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects to previous word.
This is a pronoun that refers back to 'guidance' (هُدَى) mentioned in this ayah. It's used to emphasize that Allah's guidance specifically is the true guidance.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changed from 'to guide' into 'guidance' by adding the vowels u and a. The ى at the end shows this is a special noun meaning guidance

steps.m: the guidance (object being called to in يَدْعُونَهُۥٓ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific guidance. In this ayah, this is what people are being called to.
Changed the verb to show that someone else is doing the commanding to us, rather than us commanding others
Added نا to show that 'we' are the ones being commanded. This word connects to لِنُسْلِمَ (to submit) in the next ayah, showing what we were commanded to do.
Added conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'

steps.m: to submit, surrender, be at peace

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added alif at beginning to create Form IV, which makes the meaning more specific to Islamic submission
Changed from past tense 'he' to present tense 'we' by adding نُ prefix and changing middle vowel to kasra

steps.m: so that we submit (to Allah's will)
steps.n: Added لِ prefix to show purpose - this connects to the previous part of the ayah
Doubling the ب adds emphasis to show complete mastery/lordship
Adds لِ meaning 'to/for' and ِ showing possession. This word is connected to الْعٰلَمِينَ (the worlds) in this ayah.
Forms a noun meaning 'that which is known/distinguished' - referring to all of creation
Adds plural ending to show multiple worlds/realms of creation

steps.m: the worlds (referring to all realms of creation)
steps.n: Added definite article to specify all worlds/realms of creation, and elongated alif for proper pronunciation
This is a special word that introduces what someone should do
The و at the start connects this word to the command that follows (أَقِيمُوا۟ meaning 'establish'). Together they mean 'and that you should establish'

steps.m: to stand or establish something firmly

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing someone else to do the action
Changed from past tense 'he made stand' to present tense 'I make stand' - middle letter changes from و to ي
Changes from 'I establish' to a command for multiple people
Basic vowels (fatha) added to make the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he prayed/worshipped'
Changed the final و to ا and added ة to make it a feminine noun for a single instance
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ص with shadda
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb أَقِيمُوا۟ (establish)

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: This is not a transformation - 'wa' is a separate connecting word meaning 'and', not derived from waqa (to protect)
Command 'protect' with 'wa' (and) added at start - Form VIII means doing it for yourself
The ت combines with another ت to make a stronger 'taa' sound (ت + ت = تّ), showing extra care and mindfulness

steps.m: and all of you be mindful!
steps.n: Makes it a command for multiple people

steps.m: and all of you be mindful of Him!
steps.n: Adds object pronoun showing who to be mindful of
This is a pronoun that refers to a male person or thing
The و connects this with what came before it. This pronoun is the subject of the verb تُحْشَرُونَ (you will be gathered) in this ayah.
Starting with the base word that means possession or ownership
Adding ال changes this from showing ownership to connecting descriptions with what's being described
The 'al' at the start changes to make it flow better when reading - the little hat (ٱ) helps connect it to words before it
The final ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top. This word connects back to هُوَ (He) in the previous part of the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
Added sukun before adding pronoun, which is required by Arabic pronunciation rules
Added هِ at the end which means 'him'. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah who will gather everyone together (as mentioned in تُحْشَرُونَ - 'you will be gathered')
Changed from root letters to a past tense verb meaning 'he gathered' by adding fathas
Changed vowels to show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it
Changed from past tense 'was gathered' to present tense 'are being gathered' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels. Also changes from 'he' to 'you'
Added ون to show this refers to many people being gathered
This is a pronoun that points to someone or something being talked about. In this ayah, it's referring to Allah who is described as ٱلْحَكِيمُ (the Wise).
Added و with fatha at the start to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Base word that points to someone or something. In this ayah, it connects to the verb خَلَقَ (created) to tell us who did the creating.

steps.m: the one who/which (connects to خَلَقَ - created)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'

steps.m: the one who/which (connects to خَلَقَ - created)
steps.n: Changed spelling to Uthmani script with hamza wasl (ٱ) and shadda (ّ). This doesn't change the meaning, just how it looks and sounds.

Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show this is something that happened in the past. This word is connected to يَقُولُ (he says) later in the ayah, showing Allah's power to both create and command.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding اء at the end
Added ات to make it plural. The ء changes back to و before the plural ending.
Added ال to make it specific. The س gets doubled (shadda) because it's a sun letter.
Changed to show it's being acted upon (the thing being created). The middle alif is written differently but pronounced the same.
Connected the letters together with fatha and sukun to make it a proper word
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific earth rather than any land
Adding و connects this with the previous phrase about the heavens (السَّمٰوٰتِ)
Adding fatha at the end shows this is the object of the verb خَلَقَ (He created)
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'truth'
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'with' or 'by' truth
Added ال to make it specific - 'the truth' instead of just any truth

steps.m: with/by the truth (describing how Allah created the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition بِ. This word describes how Allah created the heavens and earth - with truth/purpose, not in vain.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous phrase
Added fatha at the end to show this is the time when something happens
Changes root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ after second letter. The و stays because it's part of the root.

steps.m: to come into being, exist, happen

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to command form. The و is dropped because it's a weak letter. This is Allah's command that makes things exist, connected to the word يَكُونُ (it becomes) that follows it in the ayah.

steps.m: to come into being, exist, happen

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense form. This word is the result of Allah's command كُن (be!) in this ayah, showing how when He commands something to be, it becomes.

steps.m: and so it becomes/will be (immediate response to كُن)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens immediately after Allah's command كُن (be!). The فَ here shows the immediacy of creation - as soon as Allah commands something, it happens.
Changed from verb 'to say' into noun 'a saying' by adding vowel marks fatHa and sukoon
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action of being (يَكُونُ) in the ayah
Added هُ to show the word belongs to Him (Allah)
Added fatha to first letter and shadda to make it a noun
Added 'al' to make it definite and damma because it's the subject of قَوْلُهُ (his word) in this ayah
Connected the preposition 'li' (for/to) with pronoun 'hu' (him) and added vowel marks. This shows that something (the dominion) belongs to him.

steps.m: and for him (connected to الْمُلْكُ - dominion)
steps.n: Added 'wa' (and) to connect this phrase with what came before it in the verse
Changed the verb (to rule) into a noun (kingdom) by adding damma and sukun. This pattern is used to show the result of the action - here the realm or authority that comes from ruling.
Added ال to show this is THE specific kingdom being discussed (Allah's kingdom), and added ُ at the end to show this word is the subject of ولَهُ (and to Him belongs)
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added fatha at the end to show this word describes when Allah says 'Be'. The fatha shows it's answering the question 'when?'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it is blown (into الصُّورِ - the Horn)
steps.n: Added يُ at the beginning and changed vowels to show this is happening to something (passive voice) rather than someone doing it
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment
Changed the final ي to ى, which is a common writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to blow, to make a sound

steps.n: Changed to passive voice by adding damma and sukun marks
When a letter has shadda (ّ), it means that letter is doubled/repeated with more emphasis
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific horn/trumpet mentioned in the Quran (the one that will be blown on the Day of Judgment)
Adding kasra because it comes after the preposition فِى (in/into) in يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ (is blown into the horn)

steps.m: to know deeply or be certain of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changes verb into a noun meaning 'one who does the action' by adding alif after first letter and kasra after second letter
The alif is written as a small alif above the ع for this specific word
Adds damma at the end to show this is the subject. In this context, it refers to Allah as the one who has complete knowledge of all things.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'something hidden'. This word is owned by عٰلِمُ (Knower), forming the phrase 'Knower of the unseen'.

steps.m: of something hidden/unseen (possessed by عٰلِمُ - Knower)
steps.n: The ِ ending shows this word belongs to or is connected to عٰلِمُ (Knower)
Added ال to make it specific - referring to all things that are hidden from human knowledge, but known to Allah. This word is possessed by عٰلِمُ (Knower), meaning 'Knower of the unseen'.
Basic past tense form - added vowels to make 'he witnessed'
Changed from 'he witnessed' (a verb) to 'witnessing' (a noun) by adding alif and taa
Added shadda for emphasis
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it follows another word
Added و at beginning to connect it to previous word
Basic pronoun for 'he'. This pronoun is the subject of the verb خَلَقَ (created) in this ayah.
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to judge wisely or make wise decisions
one who is full of wisdom
steps.n: Added ي between the last two root letters and vowels to show this describes someone who has complete wisdom
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and ُ at end to show this is describing Allah. When used for Allah, this means His wisdom is perfect and complete.

steps.m: to inform, be aware of details

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this into a word for someone who knows about something

steps.m: one who is thoroughly aware (of all things)
steps.n: Adding ي shows this is a permanent, intensive quality - especially when referring to Allah
ٱلْ makes it definite to refer specifically to Allah's attribute. The ُ shows this word is describing Allah (connected to هُوَ earlier in the ayah)
This is a time particle that refers to past events

steps.m: and when (connecting to قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: The و at the beginning connects this phrase to previous events in the story. This word introduces a new scene where Ibrahim speaks to his father.
Added vowels to make the past tense form
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ because و changes to ا in hollow verbs
This is a proper noun of Hebrew origin meaning 'father of many'. While it doesn't come from an Arabic root, it's an important name in Islamic tradition referring to Prophet Abraham.
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject doing the action in the sentence - Abraham is the one who said something to his father.

steps.m: to nurture/care for (as a father does)

steps.n: The root letters أ-ب-و combine to make the simple word for 'father'. The و at the end drops off in this form.
Adding ي at the end makes it mean 'my father' - like adding 'my' in English
Added هِ to show possession (his)
Added لِ to show direction/purpose. This word appears in a conversation with Ibrahim.
This is a proper name, likely of foreign origin, referring to Prophet Ibrahim's father mentioned in this verse
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this is a proper name being directly spoken to
This root carries the basic meaning of taking or acquiring something
Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he took' in the past
Changed to present tense form with reflexive meaning (doing something for oneself)
Added ت and made it stronger (with shadda) to change from 'takes' to 'you take' - like saying 'you really make it yours'

steps.m: do you take (as your own)? (addressing Abraham's father)
steps.n: Added أَ at beginning to make it a question, specifically asking Abraham's father about his taking of idols as gods
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic noun form
Changes from one idol to many idols by using the plural pattern أَفْعَال
Added ًا to show these idols are being taken/worshipped by someone (connected to أَتَتَّخِذُ meaning 'do you take')
Root letters form a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
Changes spelling to match Quranic orthography with hamza on alif
Adds ة at the end to make it mean 'female god/goddess' instead of just 'god'

steps.m: deities (as the object being taken/adopted in أَتَتَّخِذُ)
steps.n: Adds tanween fatha to show this is the object being taken/adopted in the phrase 'Are you taking idols...'
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'definitely'
Added ي at the end to show 'I' am doing the action. The ن gets doubled (shown by ّ) when ي is added.
The ي is written as ى and lengthened (shown by ٓ ) when it comes at the end of a word that's in the middle of a sentence

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Connected the root letters ر-أ-ي with vowels to make 'he saw' in the past tense. The ي at the end changed to ى
Adding أَ at the start makes it mean 'to make someone see/show'
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end due to weak letter rules
Added كَ to show who is being seen (you)
Changes the verb concept of standing into a noun meaning the group that stands together as one community
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added كَ at the end to show these are 'your' people (speaking to Ibrahim)

steps.m: in (connects ضَلٰلٍ 'error' to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects the word 'error' to the previous statement about Ibrahim's father and people.

steps.m: in (connects ضَلٰلٍ 'error' to what comes before)
steps.n: The ي changes to ى when written in the Uthmani script of the Quran, but the meaning stays the same

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Added the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he went astray'
Changed the word to show the concept or state of going astray rather than the action itself
Changed the regular alif to a dagger alif (written above) - this is just a writing style change that doesn't affect meaning
Added the tanwin kasra mark to show this word comes after the word في (in/within)
The root ب-ي-ن becomes مُبين (clear) and adding shadda (ّ) makes it stronger - like saying 'very clear'
The tanwin kasra ending shows this word describes ضَلٰلٍ (error) in the ayah
Starting with the basic comparison particle ك
Added ذا (this/that) with لِ to form the demonstrative pronoun 'that'
Added كَ at the beginning to show comparison

steps.m: and like that (connects to نُرِىٓ - We show)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what follows - 'and in this way We show Ibrahim...'

steps.m: to see with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added alif at beginning to make it mean 'cause to see' instead of just 'see'
Changed أ to نُ at start to mean 'we' instead of 'I'. Changed vowels to show it's happening now.
Added elongation mark (ٓ) after ى to show extended pronunciation at end of word
As a foreign proper name, it does not follow standard Arabic morphological patterns. The initial إ is part of the base word, not a prefix.
Added vowel marks and fatha at end to show this is the object (receiver) of the verb نُرِىٓ (We show). The alif with bar (ٰ) is a common spelling variation in Quranic text.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding وت makes it into a special noun showing complete or total control/rule

steps.m: the dominion (of السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is the object of نُرِىٓ (We show)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb 'to rise' into a noun 'sky' by adding a long aa sound and hamza at the end
Added feminine plural ending ات and returned the و that was changed to ء
Added ال (written as ٱل) to make it definite, and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: the heavens (in relation to وَٱلْأَرْضِ - and the earth)
steps.n: Changed the spelling to use alif maqsura (ٰ) instead of alif and added kasra at the end because it's connected to 'and the earth' in the ayah
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Added ال to make it specific
Added و to connect with previous phrase about the heavens (السَّمٰوٰتِ)
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to مَلَكُوتَ (dominion) in the phrase 'dominion of the heavens and the earth'
Added يَ at the beginning to make it mean 'he is' in present tense, and added vowels to make it readable
Added a fatha (َ) at the end to show this is part of a longer sentence
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose
Added و at beginning to connect to previous phrase
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to
Added fatha at the end to connect this word to the next word (ٱلْمُوقِنِينَ) which means 'those who are certain'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show causing someone else to be certain
Changed to active participle form to show someone who has this quality
Added plural ending to show multiple people

steps.m: the ones who are certain (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning and final vowel to show this is the object of مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
This is the base word meaning 'when'. The shadda on م shows emphasis, and the ا at the end makes it work with other words.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else, connecting it to the previous action.
Added fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: it became dark (referring to الَّيْلُ - the night)
steps.n: Combined the doubled letter with shadda. This word describes how the night (الَّيْلُ) fell over Ibrahim (as) in this story.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting with the next part. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Added هِ at the end to show who is being referred to (him)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic word for 'night'. This word is doing the action of covering/becoming dark (جَنَّ) in this ayah.
Added 'the' (ال) which causes the first letter to double (shadda)
Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing the action
Added fathas after each letter to make it past tense and show that 'he' did the action

steps.m: he saw (past tense verb in فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْهِ ٱلَّيْلُ رَءَا كَوْكَبًا)
steps.n: Because this word ends with ي, it changes to ا in the past tense. The hamza (ء) is written instead of أ when it has a fatha and comes after a fatha.

steps.m: to shine brightly like a star

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to create the noun meaning 'star'. This is what Ibrahim saw in the story.
Added ًا (tanween) at the end to show it's 'a star' - this marks it as the thing that Ibrahim saw
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this is past tense. This is the basic form before the weak letter changes.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
This is the basic demonstrative element that points to something
The ذا is added to make it specifically mean 'this' for masculine singular objects nearby. In this ayah, it's pointing to the star (كَوْكَبًا) that Ibrahim saw.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps others grow (subject of قَالَ)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping. This word is used by Ibrahim when he speaks (قَالَ - he said).
Adding ي at the end shows Ibrahim is talking about his own protector. He uses this word when speaking (قَالَ - he said).
Basic temporal conjunction particle meaning 'when'. In this ayah, it connects two events: 'when' something happened, then something else followed.

steps.m: and/so when (connects to أَفَلَ - it set)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this event follows something before it, like saying 'and then' or 'so then' in English

steps.m: and/so when (connects to أَفَلَ - it set)
steps.n: The final alif is lengthened to آ (alif madd). This is a common feature in the Quran that adds a slight pause or emphasis at certain points in verses.

steps.m: it set/disappeared (referring to كَوْكَبًا - the star)
steps.n: Added fatha vowels to make past tense verb. This word describes what happened to the star (كَوْكَبًا) that Ibrahim saw.
Added fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to what Ibrahim said next: 'This is my Lord'
The و (weak letter) changes to ا, which is very common in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
This is the basic negative particle that negates what comes after it. Here it negates أُحِبُّ (I love), making it 'I do not love'.
Added fatha on lam to show proper pronunciation.
The alif is written with a special elongation mark (آ) to show emphasis in pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter (shown by shadda) makes the meaning stronger
The أُ at the start shows 'I' and the middle ِ shows it's happening now. The ُ at the end shows it's a complete statement.

steps.m: to disappear from sight (like stars setting)

steps.n: Changed into the doer form to describe someone/something that sets or disappears
Made plural to refer to multiple things that set/disappear

steps.m: the ones who disappear/set (objects that Ibrahim saw setting)
steps.n: Added 'the' to specify these particular celestial objects that Ibrahim saw setting in the story
This is the base word meaning 'when' - it's a particle that isn't derived from a root
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he saw' in the past

steps.m: he saw (followed by ٱلْقَمَرَ - the moon)
steps.n: Because this word ends in ي, it changes to ا when it's at the end of a word. The hamza (ء) replaces the أ to make it easier to pronounce.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's being acted upon by the verb رَءَا (he saw)
Added ا after first letter and vowel marks to show this describes something that is currently rising. In this ayah, it's describing the moon (ٱلْقَمَرَ).
Added ًا at the end to show this describes what Ibrahim saw (رَءَا meaning 'he saw')
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: he said (describing what Ibrahim said when he saw the moon rising)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce.
Basic demonstrative element meaning 'here' or 'this'
Added ذا to make it specifically mean 'this' for pointing to something nearby
Added the alif mark (ٰ) and fatha marks for proper pronunciation. In this ayah, it's being used to point to the moon that Ibrahim saw.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or develop something to completion
master/lord (one who nurtures and takes care of others)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares for others completely

steps.m: master/lord (being described or connected to something)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word is being connected to something that comes after it

steps.m: my Lord (the one who nurtures and takes care of me)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is MY lord - the one who takes care of ME specifically
Basic conditional/time particle with fatha on first letter and shadda on second letter
Added alif at the end to extend the sound
Added maddah over alif to show long vowel sound

steps.m: then when (followed by أَفَلَ - it set)
steps.n: Added fa at beginning to show this happens after something else. This word connects to أَفَلَ (it set) which comes after it.

steps.m: it set (referring to الْقَمَرَ - the moon)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, this word describes what happened to the moon (الْقَمَرَ) that Ibrahim was observing.

steps.m: he said (introducing what Ibrahim said about the moon)
steps.n: Changed from basic root 'say' to past tense 'he said' by adding vowels. Shows Ibrahim is about to speak about the moon.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it comes after fatha - this is a common change with hollow verbs
This particle adds emphasis to what follows

steps.m: truly if (introducing what Abraham said about needing guidance)
steps.n: Added إِن (if) to create a conditional statement with emphasis. This combination introduces what Abraham said about needing guidance from his Lord.
This is a negative word that makes يَهْدِنِى (guide me) into 'did not guide me'. The sukun (ْ) at the end shows it's complete.
The shadda (ّ) makes the 'l' sound stronger because it comes after another ل in لَئِن

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'he guided'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added نِي at the end to show the action is being done to 'me'
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The root verb means 'to care for', but doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'one who cares for others a lot' - like a master or caretaker

steps.m: my master/caretaker (addressing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adds ي at the end to show this is 'my' master. The ِ is added before ي to help pronunciation. This word appears when Ibrahim is searching for his true Lord, looking at the moon.
Added damma (u sound) to make it a command meaning 'be!'
Added أَ at beginning to show first person (I)
Added لَ at beginning to show emphasis and certainty
Added نَّ at end for extra emphasis, showing strong certainty
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or separation from something
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word الْقَوْمِ (the people). This is a helper vowel that makes pronunciation easier.

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: Changes the concept of standing together into a noun meaning the group that does this - like how a 'people' or 'folk' stand together as one
Added the kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word follows مِنَ (from/of)
The definite article is added to specify 'the people' rather than just any people

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb
Changed from a past-tense verb (he went astray) to a noun meaning someone who does the straying - like changing 'ran' to 'runner'
Added ين to show it's talking about many people

steps.m: the ones who have wandered off (part of القوم/the people)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and a final َ because it belongs to 'the people' (القوم)
This is the basic word meaning 'when' that shows something happened in the past and led to something else happening
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else, connecting it to the previous events in the story

steps.m: to see with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he saw (subject of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Because this word ends in ي, it changes to ا in the past tense. The hamza (ء) replaces the أ to make it easier to pronounce. This word is what happened before 'he said' (قَالَ) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'sun'. This word is what is being seen in the phrase 'when he saw (رَءَا) the sun'.

steps.m: the sun (object being seen by رَءَا)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to make it specific ('the sun' instead of just 'sun'). The ش gets a shadda because it comes after ال.

steps.m: the sun (object being seen by رَءَا)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being seen in the phrase 'when he saw (رَءَا) the sun'

steps.m: to break forth, to emerge (like the sun or moon)

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it mean 'it emerged' in the past tense
Changes from 'it emerged' to 'emerging/rising' by adding alif after first letter

steps.m: emerging, rising (feminine form because الشَّمْسَ is feminine)
steps.n: Makes the word feminine to match الشَّمْسَ (the sun) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: emerging, rising (describing الشَّمْسَ as the object being seen)
steps.n: Adds tanween to show this is describing the sun as the object being seen in رَءَا (he saw)
Added fatha vowels to make past tense form
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is common for hollow verbs
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby. In this ayah, Ibrahim (AS) is pointing to the sun.

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares in a complete way
The kasra shows this word is connected to something that comes after it

steps.m: my Lord (the subject of قَالَ/he said)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' Lord. This word is what Ibrahim said when he spoke (قَالَ) in this verse.
Connected the parts together with fatha to make the basic demonstrative pronoun that points to something nearby

steps.m: this (pointing to something near and emphasizing it)
steps.n: The elongated alif (آ) adds emphasis when pointing to something specific - in this case, pointing to the sun (الشَّمْسَ) that Ibrahim was observing
Basic root meaning related to size and greatness
Added vowels to make a past tense verb showing a quality

steps.m: bigger/greater (than the moon and stars Ibrahim saw before)
steps.n: Added أَ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form. In this ayah, Ibrahim is comparing the sun's size to other celestial bodies he saw.
This is the basic word meaning 'when' that shows something happened in the past and led to something else
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else, connecting it to the previous events

steps.m: then when (connecting to أَفَلَتْ - it set)
steps.n: The alif is elongated at the end to show a natural pause in reading, as this word connects to 'it set' (أَفَلَتْ) in the next part

steps.m: to set or disappear below horizon

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense. This word describes what happened before he (Ibrahim) spoke (قَالَ).
Added ت with sukun to show this is about the sun (which is feminine in Arabic) setting. This happened before Ibrahim spoke.
Added short vowels 'a' to make it mean 'he said' in the past
Changed from قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا

steps.m: to stand upright, rise up, establish

steps.n: The root meaning of 'standing/establishing' becomes a noun for a group that stands together as one community

steps.m: O my people! (being addressed by قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: يٰ is added at the start to call out to someone (like 'O' or 'Hey'). The ِ at the end shows it means 'my people'. This word comes after قَالَ (he said) as the beginning of what was spoken.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' before a sentence in English.

steps.m: Indeed I (speaking to قَوْمِ - my people)
steps.n: Added ي (meaning 'I/me') at the end. The ن gets a kasra (ِ) before ي. This shows Ibrahim is speaking about himself to his people.

steps.m: to be free from something or innocent of blame

steps.n: Root letters got vowels and extra ي to make it mean 'someone who is innocent'

steps.m: one who is free/innocent (subject of إِنِّى)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this is the main subject. The long ي (ىٓ) is just a spelling variation.
Combined the two basic words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together

steps.m: from what (that you associate with اللَّهِ)
steps.n: The ن changes to م and gets a shadda because it's followed by another م. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Vowels changed from past tense (sha-ra-ka) to present tense (yush-ri-ku) pattern
Added تُ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a particle that adds emphasis to a statement

The ي at the end shows this is about me (the speaker). The ن gets doubled (with shadda) to connect smoothly with the ي. This word emphasizes the statement that follows where Ibrahim says 'I have turned my face...'

steps.m: to direct/turn something towards something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more deliberate and intensive
Added sukun to prepare for adding the subject pronoun

steps.m: I deliberately directed (my face towards the One who created the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action. In this ayah, the speaker is directing their face (وَجْهِىَ) towards Allah who created the heavens and earth (لِلَّذِى فَطَرَ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ)

steps.m: face (object of وَجَّهْتُ - I have directed)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning the physical face. This is what is being directed in the phrase 'I have directed my face'.

steps.m: my face (object of وَجَّهْتُ - I have directed)
steps.n: Added the letter ي with kasra before it to show possession (my), and an extra alif at the end for pronunciation
This is the basic demonstrative word that points to something. Adding ي makes it refer to someone or something specific.
Adding ال and doubling the ذ with a shadda makes it specifically point to someone or something we're talking about

steps.m: to the one who (connects to فَطَرَ - created)
steps.n: Adding لِ shows direction or purpose - here it points to who created the heavens and earth. The ي is written as ى at the end of words.

steps.m: to create by splitting into existence

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word tells us what Allah did to the heavens and earth - He created them.
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
The verb 'to rise up' became a noun meaning 'sky' - named because it rises above us
Made the word plural to show multiple heavens/skies
Added 'the' and doubled the س to show it's specific heavens being talked about

steps.m: of the heavens (object of فَطَرَ - created)
steps.n: Changed ending to -i sound (kasra) to show the heavens is being acted upon by the word 'created'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun. This word is what was created (object of فَطَرَ) in this ayah.
Added ال for definiteness and fatḥa case ending showing it's the object
Added conjunction وَ meaning 'and'

steps.m: to turn away from falsehood toward truth

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make an adjective meaning 'someone who turns away'

steps.m: one who firmly turns away from falsehood
steps.n: Adding ي creates فَعِيل pattern indicating firmly established quality

steps.m: as one who firmly turns away from falsehood (describing وَجْهِىَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word describes how Ibrahim turned his face (وَجْهِىَ) to Allah. The ًا ending shows it's describing the manner or state.
This is a negative particle that makes the following statement 'I am of the polytheists' into 'I am not of the polytheists'
Adding وَ connects this negation to Ibrahim's previous statement 'I have turned my face'. The وَ shows these ideas are connected - he turned to Allah AND is not among the polytheists
This is a common Quranic spelling variation that extends the vowel sound without changing meaning
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'I'. It's one of the fundamental pronouns in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Added a small alif (۠) above the final alif. This is a pronunciation mark that shows this word should be read with a long 'aa' sound at the end.
This is a preposition that connects words, showing where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects to مُشْرِكِينَ (those who associate partners with Allah)
The َ is added when it comes before ال

steps.m: one who associates partners with God (active participle)
steps.n: The مُ prefix with the ِ vowel pattern creates an active participle - someone who does this action regularly or as a characteristic

steps.m: those who associate partners with God
steps.n: The ين ending makes this plural, showing it refers to multiple people who do this action

steps.m: the polytheists (those being rejected in this verse)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ (the) and َ makes this specific and shows these are the polytheists being specifically discussed in the context. This word appears in a verse where Prophet Ibrahim is rejecting polytheism.
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show intensive action
Added alif after first letter to show this is about the person doing the arguing
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Changed regular alif to long alif to show emphasis

steps.m: and he debated intensively with him (قَوْمُهُۥ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who he debated with. This refers to his people (قَوْمُهُۥ) mentioned right after this word in the ayah.

steps.m: to stand firm, rise up, establish
people, folk, nation (subject of حَآجَّ - argued with him)
steps.n: The root ق-و-م meaning 'to stand' becomes قَوْم meaning 'people' - like a group that stands together. In this verse, these are the people who argued with Ibrahim (as).
The word gets a damma mark (u sound) at the end to show it's doing the action
Added هُ with an elongation mark (ۥ) to show possession - referring to Prophet Ibrahim's people who were arguing with him
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it 'he said' in the past tense. Shows Abraham speaking back to those who argued with him.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
Added alif after first letter and shadda on last letter to show intense debate
Added ت to show either 'you' or 'she' is doing the action
Adding question particle to create interrogative form
Added plural marker to show multiple people debating
Added object pronoun to show who is being debated with
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱللَّهِ (Allah) to show 'in [the matter of] Allah'
Added shadda to emphasize the L sound, showing the special nature of this divine name
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word فِى (in) before it. The alif at the start changed to a special connecting form (ٱ).
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation of this emphasis word
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changed from basic root to past tense 'he' form by adding fathas and writing ى at the end
Added superscript alif (ٰ) after ى to show it's a long vowel
Added نِ at the end to show the person being guided is 'me'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Extended the alif to show emphasis in pronunciation

steps.m: and not (connected to أَخَافُ - I fear)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what comes before it. This connects to the verb أَخَافُ (I fear) in the ayah.
Basic past tense form for emotion/state verb
Regular weak letter change for hollow verbs in past tense
Changed to first person present tense by adding أَ at beginning and ُ at end

steps.m: what (that which you associate with اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Basic interrogative/relative pronoun used to ask about or refer to things. In this ayah, it's asking about what they associate with Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Vowels changed from 'sha-ra-ka' to 'shri-ku' to make it mean 'I/he associates' instead of 'associated'
Added تُ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action

steps.m: you all associate partners (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are being addressed
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added pronoun ه (him/it) to show who/what is being referred to
Added pronunciation helpers (small و and elongation mark) that don't change the meaning
Starting with the basic negation particle ل and adding إِ at the beginning to strengthen the meaning
Adding shadda on ل and alif at the end changes the meaning from simple negation to exception. This word connects to رَبِّى (my Lord) in the ayah, showing what is being excepted.
The regular alif (ا) is written as a long alif (آ) in the Quran. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
This is a connecting word that introduces what comes next. Here it connects to يَشَآءَ (he wills) to show what might happen. It's like saying 'except that he wills' in English.
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past tense
The weak letter ي changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Adding يَ at the beginning changes it from past to present/future tense
Adding the long vowel ا makes it a present tense pattern
Doubling the middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares very completely
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to something after it
Added ى at the end to show this is 'my' Lord. This word is the subject (doer) of وَسِعَ (encompasses) in this ayah.
Added fatha and sukun to show how to say the word correctly
Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb تُشْرِكُونَ (you associate/join). The hamza is written smaller (ـٔ) following Arabic writing rules.
Connected the root letters together to form the basic past tense verb
Added vowels to show this is a past tense verb describing a quality or state of being vast/encompassing
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly

steps.m: my Lord (connected to يَشَآءَ - what He wills)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' lord. The kasra (ِ) comes automatically before ى. This word is connected to يَشَآءَ (wills/wishes) in the ayah.
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a word meaning 'all' or 'every'

steps.m: every, all (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic word for 'thing'. The hamza (ء) at the end is how we write it.
Added kasratayn (ٍ) to show this word is being possessed by كُلَّ (every/all). The ي is written as ى when it has a sukun.
Changed the action of knowing into the thing itself - knowledge. Like how 'to write' becomes 'writing'.

steps.m: knowledge (as the thing that is encompassed by رَبِّى - my Lord)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon by وَسِعَ (encompassed). In this ayah, it means that Allah's knowledge encompasses everything.

steps.m: is it true that...? (questioning what follows)
steps.n: This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding 'is it true that...?' at the start of a sentence
Added فَ which shows this question follows from what was just said

steps.m: then will you not? (connects to تَتَذَكَّرُونَ - remember/take heed)
steps.n: Added لَا which makes the question negative. This combination أَفَلَا is often used to encourage reflection or point out something obvious, like saying 'won't you then...?' In this ayah, it connects to the verb تَتَذَكَّرُونَ (remember/take heed).

steps.m: to remember or keep in mind

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he remembered'
Added shadda to middle letter to show intensive meaning
Added تَ at beginning to show doing the action to oneself - changed from reminding others to reflecting oneself
Added تَ prefix to change from 'he' to 'you', and changed ending to ُ to make it present tense
Added ونَ to show plural 'you'
Added vowel marks to make the word pronounceable: fatha on ك and sukun on ي

steps.m: and how (connects to أَخَافُ - I fear)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this question to the verb أَخَافُ (I fear) that follows it
The middle weak letter و changes to ا in past tense
Changed to first person present tense by adding أَ at beginning and ُ at end
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it refers to what the people have associated with Allah.
The alif is elongated (آ) to show a longer vowel sound. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to join or associate something with something else
to make something be joined/associated with something else
steps.n: Changes to make something join with something else (like making someone worship idols alongside Allah). The أ at the start shows this causing/making meaning.
Adds تُ at the end to mean 'I' did the action in the past
Added م to change from singular to plural 'you'. This word is part of a longer phrase أَشْرَكْتُم بِٱللَّهِ meaning 'you all associated partners with Allah'
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb تَخَافُونَ (you fear) into 'you do not fear'
The و connects this negative word to the previous statement, showing a contrast between what they fear and don't fear
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و becomes ا in this form.
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed final vowel to ُ to show present tense
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a particle that emphasizes and connects what comes after it, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: that you all (emphasizes what follows about you all)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is about 'you all' (when speaking to multiple people)

steps.m: to join or associate things together

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at start and sukun on first root letter changes meaning to 'make something happen'
Added تُ at the end to change from 'he' to 'I' in past tense
Changed تُ (meaning I/you/she) to تُم (meaning you all) to show multiple people are being addressed
This is the base word - the proper name of God in Arabic. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the ب that comes before it
The ب at the beginning shows this is being used as a tool or means. The ٱ is added to help pronunciation when connecting to the previous word.

steps.m: what (connects to أَشْرَكْتُمْ - you have associated)
steps.n: This is a basic question word that connects to the verb after it (أَشْرَكْتُمْ). In this ayah, it's asking about what they have associated with Allah.
This is the basic form of the negative particle
Adding the vowel marks shows how to pronounce it and indicates it negates the verb يُنَزِّلْ (send down) that follows it, changing its meaning to 'did not send down'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or repeated
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels - both mean 'send down repeatedly'
Changed final vowel to sukun because it comes after لَمْ (not yet)
Added kasra to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: with/by it (referring to what they associated with Allah)
steps.n: Added pronoun 'hi' (it/him) to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to what they associated as partners with Allah.
Added small alif (ۦ) after the pronoun to help with pronunciation
This is a common preposition in Arabic meaning 'upon' or 'on'. Since it's a preposition, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
The ى changes to يْ when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
The suffix كُمْ shows this is addressing multiple people (you all)
Added damma and sukun to form basic noun pattern
The ان ending makes it stronger - like going from 'power' to 'complete authority'

steps.m: any authority/proof (that He did not send down)
steps.n: Added tanween (double marks) and alif at the end to show this is receiving the action - it's the authority being talked about

steps.m: to point to or specify something

steps.n: Root letters أ-ي-ي became the question word أَيّ by adding a fatha mark and combining the two ي letters into one doubled letter
Added damma at the end because this word is the subject of أَحَقُّ (more deserving) in this ayah
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this question with the previous statement. This word is asking which of two groups (ٱلْفَرِيقَيْنِ) is more deserving (أَحَقُّ) of security.

steps.m: to split or separate into distinct parts
a distinct group (that is split/separated from others)
steps.n: Changes the idea of splitting into a noun for one of the resulting split groups

steps.m: two distinct groups (that are split/separated from each other)
steps.n: The ending ين shows there are exactly two groups being discussed
The ال makes it refer to specific groups that were already mentioned - in this case, the believers and those who associate partners with Allah
Adding vowel marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes verb into comparative adjective meaning 'more/most true'
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling safe
Changed from a verb (feeling safe) to 'the safety' by making it a noun and adding 'al' at the start
Adding the preposition ب to indicate 'with'

steps.m: with the security/safety (related to أَحَقُّ - more deserving of)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word is connected to أَحَقُّ (more deserving of) in the question 'which of the two groups is more deserving of security?'
Basic conditional word that means 'if'. This word introduces a condition that must be true for something else to happen. Here it connects to كُنتُمْ (you were) to say 'if you were'.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the إ to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'in'
Added damma and sukun to make the basic command form. The و (weak letter) is dropped.
Added تُ at the end to change from a command ('be!') to past tense meaning 'I was'
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding م at the end to show it's talking to a group
Basic past tense form indicating knowledge/awareness
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from third person (he) to second person (you) by replacing يَ with تَ
Added ونَ to show that multiple people are being addressed
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. This is like changing 'who' to 'the one who'
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added an alif at the start to change from 'feeling safe' to 'actively putting trust/faith in something'
Added plural masculine past tense ending to show multiple people did the action. This word is connected to يَلْبِسُوٓا۟ (they mixed/confused) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce this word that negates past actions
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This لَمْ negates the verb يَلْبِسُوٓا۟ (mix/confuse) that comes after it.

steps.m: to mix or confuse one thing with another

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural marker وا to show multiple people doing the action. This verb follows لَمْ (not) in the ayah, making it negative: 'they did not mix'

steps.m: to feel safe, secure, or trust

steps.n: Changed pattern to make an abstract noun meaning the state or concept of believing/having faith
Added هُم at the end to show the belief belongs to them, with fatha (َ) to connect it

steps.m: to do wrong, to be unfair

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun that means the act of doing wrong
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with' and ٍ at end to show it's receiving the action of mixing/confusing
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun meaning 'those'

steps.m: those (referring to the believers mentioned before)
steps.n: Added ئِكَ to show we're pointing to something far away that was just mentioned (in this case, the believers who didn't mix their faith with wrongdoing)
Added هُمُ (them) to لَ (for/to) to show who the security belongs to in this ayah. This word connects الْأَمْنُ (security) to the people being discussed.
Changed from the action of being safe to the noun 'safety' by adding vowel marks. This word is the subject in 'security belongs to them' (لَهُمُ ٱلْأَمْنُ).

steps.m: the security (subject of belongs to them)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject of the sentence
Added damma to show this is a complete pronoun meaning 'they'
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This word is the subject of مُّهْتَدُونَ (guided ones) in this ayah.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show receiving guidance rather than giving it
Changed from a past tense verb (he was guided) to a noun meaning someone who is guided

steps.m: the guided ones (those who are guided)
steps.n: Added shadda on م and ون at the end to show plural. The ي is dropped before adding ون.
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to something feminine that is far away, like saying 'that' in English when pointing to something not close to you
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Added damma on first letter and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning 'proof'
Added ة at the end to show one specific instance of proof
Added ُ to show this is the subject (doer) of تِلْكَ (that)
Added نَآ at the end to show this proof belongs to us. This word is the subject that تِلْكَ (that) points to at the start of the ayah.
Changed from basic 'to come' to causative 'to give/grant'. The ى at the end is because this is a weak verb.
Changed ى to يْ before adding a suffix
Added نٰ to show 'we' as the doers of the action

steps.m: we gave it/her (referring to the proof/argument حُجَّتُنَآ mentioned before)
steps.n: Added هَآ to show what was given. This refers back to حُجَّتُنَآ (our proof/argument) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is a non-Arabic proper name (إسم علم أعجمي) that was incorporated into Arabic. It does not follow standard Arabic morphological patterns or have an Arabic root.
Added vowel marks and fatha at end to show this is the object (receiver) of the verb 'we gave' (ءَاتَيْنٰهَآ). The long alif (ٰ) is a spelling variation of ا.
This is a basic preposition showing that something is on top of or over something else. Here it connects 'Ibrahim' to 'his people', showing Ibrahim's position over them.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on ع and ل
Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which is a small alif written above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Changes the verb concept into a noun meaning the group that does this action - people who stand/establish together as a community
The kasra ending shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Adds هِۦ to show these are Ibrahim's people. The word comes after عَلَىٰ (upon/against) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: we raise (degrees/ranks of مَّن نَّشَآءُ - whoever We will)
steps.n: Changes to present tense 'we' form by adding نَ at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and adding ُ at end. This word connects to دَرَجٰتٍ (degrees/ranks) which comes right after it.

steps.m: to rise step by step, to ascend gradually

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form a noun meaning the general concept
Added ة to show one specific rank or level
Changed to plural form by adding ات, with the alif becoming elongated (ٰ)
Added tanwin kasra to show this word is being acted upon by نَرْفَعُ (We raise)
Added a shadda to emphasize the م because it comes after the word إِبْرٰهِيمَ. This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb نَرْفَعُ (we raise) to show who is being raised in ranks.
Added fatha marks and the weak letter ا replaced ي to make the past tense verb
Added ن at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed the ending to ُ to show it's happening now
Added emphasis with shadda on ن and extended the alif with آ for pronunciation
This is the basic form of the word that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'that' in English
Adding the shadda (ّ) on ن changes this from a simple connecting word to one that adds strong emphasis. Here it emphasizes that 'your Lord is truly wise and knowing'

steps.m: lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and shadda on second letter to create the noun meaning 'lord' - someone who provides complete care and nurturing
Added ك at the end to show possession (your). The word is in accusative case because it's the subject of إِنَّ (indeed/verily), which puts its subject in accusative case.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into an intensive adjective showing someone who has a lot of wisdom
Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Changed from 'he knew' (past action) to 'All-Knowing' (permanent quality) by adding ي to make it a special name showing Allah always knows everything
Added double damma marks (tanween) to show this is one of Allah's complete and perfect names

steps.m: to give generously as a gift

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) to help pronunciation

steps.m: and we gave (as a gift) (connected to هَدَيْنَا - we guided)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement about guidance (هَدَيْنَا)
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the preposition
Added the pronoun هُ meaning 'him'. This shows who is receiving the action of وَهَبْنَا (We granted).
Added the elongation mark ۥٓ which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning

This is a proper name of Hebrew origin (יִצְחָק/Yitzhak) meaning 'he laughs/will laugh', referring to Ibrahim and Sarah's reaction when told they would have a son in their old age. In this ayah, Allah mentions gifting Isaac to Ibrahim.
The fatha at the end shows that Isaac is the object being gifted in this ayah (connected to وَوَهَبْنَا meaning 'and We gifted')
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object (receiver) of the verb وَهَبْنَا (We granted)
Added wa (و) at the beginning to connect this name to the previous names in the list of those whom Allah granted to Ibrahim
The doubled ل with damma makes this into a word meaning 'all' or 'every'
Adding ًا shows this word is receiving the action of guidance (هَدَيْنَا meaning 'We guided')
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to show basic past tense meaning. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of a past tense verb.
The ى changes back to يْ (with sukun) because we're adding something after it
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This is the main verb that tells what we did.
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to show proper pronunciation of the name
Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show Noah is receiving the action of guidance (هَدَيْنَا - we guided)

steps.m: and Noah (as the object of هَدَيْنَا)
steps.n: Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this name to the list of other prophets mentioned

steps.m: to guide or show someone the right way

steps.n: Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'guided'
Added نَا at the end to show 'We' did the guiding. This word is being acted on by the word كُلًّا (meaning 'all') that comes before it in the ayah.

steps.m: from (connects قَبْلُ 'before' to what precedes it)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word قَبْلُ (before) to the previous statement about guidance, indicating 'from before' or 'beforehand'.

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to create the noun meaning 'before'
Added damma at the end to show this word is in the nominative case
This is a preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects to 'his descendants' (ذُرِّيَّتِهِۦ) in the next ayah.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, showing that these descendants are in addition to those previously mentioned.
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show emphasis
Added damma on first letter, kasra under second letter, and ي with shadda to create a noun showing relation
Added ة at end to make it a feminine noun meaning a group of descendants
Added kasra to show this word is owned by something (comes after مِن)
Added هِۦ at the end to show this belongs to him
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew (דָּוִד/Dawid), so it doesn't come from an Arabic root. In this ayah, David is one of those who was guided by Allah.
The َ (fatha) at the end shows that David is the object being guided in this sentence
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: ُ (u) on س, َ (a) on ل, ْ (no vowel) on ي, and ٰ (long a) on م
Added و with fatha (َ) at the beginning to mean 'and'
This is a proper name for the prophet Job, originally from Hebrew 'Iyyov'. The shadda on ي shows emphasis.

steps.m: and Job (object of وَهَبْنَا - we granted)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. The َ (fatha) at the end shows this is the object of the verb وَهَبْنَا (we granted) in this ayah.
Added damma marks to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: and Joseph (object of وَهَبْنَا - We granted)
steps.n: Added و meaning 'and' at the start. The fatḥa at the end shows this is the object (receiver) of the verb وَهَبْنَا (We granted) in this ayah
This is a proper name of Hebrew origin, so it doesn't follow typical Arabic root patterns
Added و at the beginning to connect this name to the previous names mentioned in the ayah (David, Solomon, Job, and Joseph)
This is a proper name of Hebrew origin, so it doesn't follow typical Arabic root patterns
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. Added fatha at the end because this name is the object of وَهَبْنَا (We granted) from earlier in the verse.
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'that'
Added لِ to make 'that' stronger - like saying 'that right there!'
Added كَ (ka) at start and end to change 'that' into 'like that/similarly' - shows comparison

steps.m: and likewise, and similarly (connects to نَجْزِى)
steps.n: Added و to connect this to the next verb نَجْزِى (we reward), showing how/in what manner the rewarding happens
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb

Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. Changed the middle vowel to sukun and the last vowel to kasra to show it's happening now or in the future. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at the start and changed vowels to show someone who does the action
Added ين to make it refer to many people who do good
Added ال to mean 'the' and َ at the end because this word is affected by نَجْزِى (we reward) in the ayah
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew, referring to the prophet Zakariya who was the father of Yahya (John). The doubled ي with shadda shows emphasis in the name.
Added و at the beginning to connect this name to the list of other prophets being mentioned
ى at end is special way to write long a sound

steps.m: and Yahya (connected to زَكَرِيَّا before it)
steps.n: Adding و connects this name to Zakariya mentioned before it in a list of prophets

steps.m: Jesus (peace be upon him) - a name meaning 'salvation' in Aramaic
steps.n: This is a proper name for one of Allah's prophets, borrowed from Aramaic (Yeshua/Isa). While it doesn't have an Arabic root, it carries the meaning of salvation or deliverance in its original language.

steps.m: and Jesus (peace be upon him) (connected to وَزَكَرِيَّا وَيَحْيَىٰ - and Zakariya and Yahya)
steps.n: The prefix و means 'and' - it connects Jesus to the other prophets mentioned before him (Zakariya and Yahya) in this list of righteous people
This is a proper noun of non-Arabic origin (Hebrew: אליהו/Eliyahu), adapted into Arabic. It doesn't follow typical Arabic root patterns since it's a borrowed name.
Added و at the beginning to connect this name to the previous prophets mentioned (Zakariya, Yahya, and Isa)

steps.m: and Elias (a prophet mentioned in this list)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this name is part of a list of prophets being mentioned
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the middle letter to show completeness

steps.m: all (referring to Zakariyya, Yahya, Isa, and Ilyas)
steps.n: Added double damma (ٌ) to show this word is the subject describing the prophets mentioned before it
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to
The strong م sound shows this word is important in connecting to the next word
The fatha at the end shows this connects to the next word الصَّالِحِينَ (the righteous ones), making it mean 'from among the righteous ones'
Changed from root 'to be righteous' into 'righteous' and added kasra to link it to next word
The ين ending makes it plural for masculine words
Adding ٱل (the) makes it refer to specific righteous ones. The ص gets a shadda because it comes after ٱل
The fatha at the end shows this word is the object of مِنَ (from/among) in the phrase مِّنَ ٱلصَّالِحِينَ (from among the righteous ones)
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the name. The name combines إسم (name/hear) with إيل (God) to mean 'God has heard'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this name to the list of other prophets mentioned in the verse

steps.m: Elisha (a prophet's name meaning 'God is salvation')
steps.n: This is the base form of the prophet's name. In Arabic names often have meanings - this one comes from words meaning God and salvation.
Adding the definite article ال makes it refer to this specific prophet. The ا in ال gets a special mark (waslah) because it comes in the middle of speech.

steps.m: And The Elisha (connecting to the previous prophet's name إِسْمٰعِيلَ)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this name to the previous prophet mentioned (Ismail). This is part of a list of prophets being mentioned together.
Added diacritical marks to show proper pronunciation of the name

steps.m: Yunus/Jonah (as object in list with إِسْمٰعِيلَ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this name is part of a list of objects (connected to other prophets mentioned)

steps.m: and Yunus/Jonah (connected to إِسْمٰعِيلَ in the list)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this name to the previous prophets mentioned in the list
This is a proper name derived from the root meaning. Prophet Lut was known for sticking to/adhering to God's message.
The ًا at the end shows this name is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: and Lut (connected to إِسْمٰعِيلَ and other prophets mentioned)
steps.n: The وَ connects Lut to the list of other prophets mentioned before him in the verse

steps.m: to be complete, whole, or all-encompassing

steps.n: The doubled ل with damma makes this into a word meaning 'all' or 'every', showing completeness

steps.m: all of them (object of فَضَّلْنَا - we favored)
