steps.n: Adding ًا shows this word is receiving the action of favoring/preferring from the verb فَضَّلْنَا (we favored)

steps.m: and all of them (object of فَضَّلْنَا - we favored)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous prophets mentioned (Ismail, Al-Yasa, Yunus, and Lut)
Adding the shadda (doubled letter) makes the meaning stronger and shows doing something deliberately or with intensity
The نَا at the end shows 'we' did the action. This word connects to العٰلَمِينَ (the worlds) through عَلَى (over) in the ayah.
This is a preposition showing that Allah preferred these prophets over all the worlds. It connects the verb فَضَّلْنَا (We preferred) with what was preferred over (ٱلْعٰلَمِينَ - the worlds).
Root meaning 'to know' becomes a noun meaning 'world' - like all the things we can know about
Makes the word plural to show multiple worlds or realms. Used after على (upon/over) in this ayah.

steps.m: the worlds (object of فَضَّلْنَا عَلَى - we preferred over)
steps.n: Adds 'the' to specify these particular worlds. The alif after ع is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this word. This word shows who was being preferred over in the phrase 'we preferred over'.
This is a basic preposition showing that we're talking about being from among a group. Here it connects to 'their fathers' (ءَابَآئِهِمْ) in the next part of the ayah.
The و at the start means 'and', connecting this phrase to what came before. This و shows that this is part of a list of things being described.
Added fatha to make it a noun meaning father
Single word 'father' becomes plural 'fathers' by adding extra letters and changing the word shape

steps.m: their fathers (connected to وَذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ - and their descendants)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show possession. This word is paired with 'their descendants' in the ayah, showing a contrast between ancestors and future generations.

steps.m: to spread out, scatter (specifically about offspring/seeds)

steps.n: Forms a noun meaning descendants who spread out from their ancestors
Added هِمْ ('their') to show these are the descendants of the people mentioned in ءَابَآئِهِمْ (their fathers) from earlier in the verse
Root meaning 'brother' changed to make many brothers (plural)
Adding kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
هِمْ means 'their' when added to the end of a word
Adding conjunction prefix وَ meaning 'and'
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
This form shows doing the action for oneself with extra effort
نا at the end means 'we' did the action. This is a verb, not a noun, so it doesn't have case endings.
هُمْ means 'them' when added to a verb
Adds conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'

steps.m: to guide to the right path

steps.n: Changed from basic root to past tense 'he guided' by adding fatha marks
نا at the end means 'we' did the action. This is a verb, not a noun, so it doesn't have case endings.
The alif at the end is written with a small line on top (ٰ) instead of a regular alif
Adding هُمْ at the end makes it 'them' - the people being guided
Adding و at the start connects this word to what came before it, meaning 'and'
This is a basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something. It connects the previous verb هَدَيْنٰهُمْ (We guided them) to صِرٰطٍ (path).
Added a small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced like a long 'aa' sound at the end. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Adding the basic vowels to make the word readable
Added alif with overbar (ٰ) which is a common spelling variation in the Quran
Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and comes after the word إِلَىٰ (to)
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who is becoming/seeking to be straight
steps.n: Added مُسْتَ at the start to show someone is trying to become or seeking something, like adding '-ing' in English
Added ي in the middle to show this is a strong, permanent quality of being straight/upright
Added shadda on م and changed the ending to match the word it describes (صِرٰطٍ)
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's elongated when speaking
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something nearby to pointing to something farther away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to هُدَى)
steps.n: Adding كَ makes it point to something even farther away. In this ayah, it's pointing to هُدَى (guidance) that was mentioned later.

steps.m: to guide or direct to the right path

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) between the root letters to make the basic verb meaning 'he guided'
Changed first vowel to damma and final ي to ى to create the abstract noun meaning guidance. This word is connected to اللَّهِ (Allah) showing it's divine guidance.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to extend the sound
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he guides (someone whom يَشَآءُ - He wills)
steps.n: Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) and the last root letter gets a kasra (ِ) in present tense. This word is connected to يَشَآءُ (He wills) in the ayah, showing Allah guides whom He wills.
Added ى at the end which is common in present tense verbs. This makes it clear the action is happening now.
This is a preposition that shows how something is done
Added the pronoun هِ (him/it) to show what is being referred to. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark. This refers back to Allah's guidance (هُدَى) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to whoever Allah guides among His servants.
Added fatha vowels and converted weak letter ي to ا due to past tense formation
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing the ending vowel to ُ
Added long alif to stretch out the sound, showing the ongoing nature of the action
This is a preposition showing that something comes from among a group. Here it shows that those being guided are from among Allah's servants.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to worship' into plural noun meaning 'worshippers'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by something (comes before 'His')
Added هِۦ at the end to show these are His servants. The word follows مِنْ (from/of) in the ayah.
Starting with the basic conditional particle لو which expresses a hypothetical condition

steps.m: and even if (connects to يَهْدِى - guides)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition to the previous statement about guidance. This combination shows that even if something happened, it wouldn't change the main point.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adds masculine plural past tense ending
The base past tense form shows something becoming invalid or worthless. In this ayah, it refers to the deeds mentioned in يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/used to do)

steps.m: surely would have become worthless (their deeds)
steps.n: The لَ prefix shows this is the result part of a conditional statement starting with وَلَوْ (and if) in the previous ayah. It means if they had done something (associated partners with Allah), then this would have been the result.
This is a helper word that shows either physical distance (away from) or what something is about
Added هُم at the end to show this applies to a group of people (like saying 'them' in English)
Basic relative pronoun that connects actions or descriptions to things
The shadda (doubled م) adds emphasis to show this refers to all of their actions in the past. This word connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they used to do) in this ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the past tense
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work) in the next part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
Adds يَ at the start to change from past to present/future tense
Makes the verb plural masculine. This verb describes what they used to do, as indicated by كَانُوا (they were) in the ayah.
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun that points to things or people
The alif with madda (ٰ) extends the sound to show distance
Adding ئِ shows this refers to multiple people or things
The كَ at the end completes the demonstrative pronoun. This word points to the people mentioned in ٱلَّذِينَ (those who) that follows it.
Added ٱلْ at the beginning and merged it with ذِي, causing the ل to get a shadda. This makes it more specific, like changing 'who' to 'the one who'.
Added نَ at the end to make it plural. This word refers back to أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ (these people) mentioned just before it.
Basic meaning of bringing or coming

steps.m: to give, to cause to come
steps.n: Changes from 'to come' to 'to make come/give'
Changed final ى to يْ before adding suffix
Added نَا to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: we gave them (referring to the Book, Wisdom and Prophethood mentioned after)
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show who received these things, with ٰ replacing the alif for easier pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb form
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book) by adding alif and changing first vowel
The ٱلْ makes it 'the book' instead of just any book, and the َ at the end shows it's being acted upon

steps.m: to judge wisely, to make wise decisions
steps.n: Basic meaning of judging or being wise

steps.m: judgment, wisdom (the result of judging wisely)
steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'judge/be wise' to a noun meaning 'the judgment/wisdom itself'

steps.m: the wisdom (connected to الكتاب - the Book)
steps.n: Added 'al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the', and a small 'a' mark at the end because it connects to 'the Book'

steps.m: and the wisdom (connected to الكتاب - the Book)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this with الكتاب (the Book) mentioned before it
Basic meaning of giving information
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic noun form

steps.m: the act of telling important news
steps.n: Changed vowels and added و with shadda to show the action or job of delivering news

steps.m: prophethood (the role and status of someone who tells divine news)
steps.n: Added ة to make it into the special role or status
Added ال to make it specific
Added fatha at the end to show this is something being given to them in the ayah
Added و to connect with previous words ٱلْكِتٰبَ وَٱلْحُكْمَ (the book and the wisdom)
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a possible situation
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about giving the Book, wisdom and prophethood.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed final vowel to sukun because this word comes in a conditional phrase starting with إِن (if)
Basic preposition showing how something is done or where it is
Added هَا to show this refers to something feminine mentioned before (in this case النُّبُوَّةَ meaning prophethood)
This is the attention-getting part that starts many pointing words. The alif is written as a small mark above the ه
The small vertical line above shows this word should be stretched when pronounced
Added the core part that means 'this/that' with ُ to show it's complete
Added لَآءِ at the end to make it plural, showing we're pointing to multiple people or things
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. قَدْ is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it, showing that something definitely happened or will happen.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about if they disbelieve.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added a shadda (doubled middle letter) to show doing something with more intensity or officially
Starting with the basic preposition بِ which shows how something is done
Added هَا which refers back to النُّبُوَّةَ (prophethood) mentioned earlier in the ayah. Since النُّبُوَّةَ is feminine, we use the feminine pronoun هَا.

steps.m: to stand firm, rise up, establish

steps.n: The root meaning of standing/rising becomes a noun meaning a group that stands together as one community
Adding ًا shows this word is indefinite (a/some) and is receiving the action of the verb وَكَّلْنَا (we entrusted)
This makes it into the common negative word meaning 'not'
Added plural masculine ending وا to show multiple people
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or what tool is used
Added هَا which refers back to النُّبُوَّةَ (prophethood) mentioned earlier in the verse. Since النُّبُوَّةَ is feminine, we use the feminine pronoun هَا.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
This form shows someone who does the action, like changing 'reject' to 'rejecter'
The ين ending makes this refer to multiple people

steps.m: with/by disbelievers (connected to ليسوا - they are not)
steps.n: The بِ at the beginning means 'with' or 'by'. This word is connected to ليسوا (they are not) in the ayah, making the full meaning 'they are not disbelievers'
This is the basic form of the word used to point to multiple things or people
The small alif (ٓ) shows that these people or things are far away
The ئِكَ ending makes it specifically about pointing to people rather than things
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people

Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific and point to particular people. Also added the shadda (ّ) to strengthen the sound and َ at the end to show it's connected to what comes after it in the sentence.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Added fathas to create past tense verb. This word is related to اقْتَدِهْ (follow) in the ayah, showing what Allah guided that should be followed.
Changed final ي to ى (alif maqsurah) which is normal for past tense verbs ending in ي
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'a deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject/doer form
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific/definite
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'guidance' by adding damma and fatha marks, and changing ي to ى at the end
Added بِ at the beginning to show how the guidance is used
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before
Added هُمُ at the end to show the guidance belongs to them

steps.m: to follow a path or example

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show doing action for oneself
Changed from 'he followed' to 'you follow!' (command form), and dropped the weak letter at the end
Added a pause sound at the end to help with pronunciation when stopping
Connected the letters together with basic past tense vowels
Changed to a command form. The middle و is dropped because it's a 'weak' letter that often disappears in commands. The ُ (damma) on ق shows it's a command.
This is the basic negative particle used to say 'no' or make something negative
Adding shadda makes the 'no' stronger, like saying 'definitely not'
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer, but the meaning stays the same
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he asked' to 'I ask' by adding أَ at start and ُ at end to make it present tense 'I' form
Added كُمْ to show who is being asked. This connects to the word أَجْرًا (payment) which shows what is being asked for.
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns
Added هِ at the end to mean 'him'. This shows who should be followed in the command ٱقْتَدِهْ (follow).

steps.m: to pay, to give a reward

steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it a noun meaning the result of paying/rewarding

steps.m: a reward (object of أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ - I ask you)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the thing being asked for in the sentence. This word is what's being asked for when the Prophet says 'I do not ask you'
This is a negative particle that comes before the thing being negated. Here it negates هُوَ (it/he) in the phrase 'it is nothing but...'
This is a basic pronoun used to refer to something masculine mentioned before. In this ayah, it refers back to ذِكْرَىٰ (reminder/remembrance).
This is a basic exceptive particle that shows something is excluded from what came before it
The shadda and alif at the end add emphasis to show this is definitely an exception
Changed from basic root meaning 'remember' into the noun 'reminder' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: a reminder (for لِلْعٰلَمِينَ - for all worlds/beings)
steps.n: Added ىٰ at the end to make it mean 'a reminder' specifically, connecting it to 'for all worlds' that comes right after it
Added alif after first letter and vowels to create a noun meaning 'that which is known' - referring to all of creation as something known by Allah
Added ين to make it plural. This word refers to all the different types of creation or realms of existence.
Added لِ (for/to) and الْ (the) at the beginning. This shows who the 'reminder' (ذِكْرَىٰ) is meant for.

steps.m: for the worlds (with grammatical case ending)
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this is in the accusative case
This is a compound particle made of و (and) + ما (what/not). It can be used for questions or negation.

steps.m: to measure, value, or appreciate properly

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: they valued/appreciated (subject of ما meaning 'they did not')
steps.n: The suffix وا changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural). This word follows ما which makes it negative - 'they did not value/appreciate'
Changed from 'a deity' to 'The Deity' - the one true God. The ال makes it specific and the word became a proper noun.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being acted upon in this verse)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a mark (fatha) at the end to show it's receiving the action, like when you say 'I love Allah' instead of just 'Allah'

steps.m: to measure, estimate correctly, be true

steps.n: Added vowels (َ) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he measured'
The root حَقَقَ (he measured) becomes حَقَّ by combining the repeated letters with a shaddah (double mark). This makes it mean 'true/real', showing they didn't give Allah His real worth.
This is the basic concept of measuring or determining value
Changes verb into noun meaning 'the act of measuring/determining'
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by حَقَّ (true/proper)
Added 'his' (هِ) at the end with a small elongation mark (ۦٓ) to show it belongs to Allah
This is a word that points to when something happened in the past. Here it connects to when 'they said' (قَالُوا) something.
These are the basic letters that give us the meaning of speaking or saying something
Adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) changes the root into past tense 'he said'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas
Adding وا at the end shows that a group of people (they) did the action
This is a basic negative particle that shows something didn't happen. Here it negates the verb أَنزَلَ (sent down) to say 'did not send down'.
The alif is lengthened for emphasis in pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to send down or bring down

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he sent down' in the past tense
Adding أَ at the beginning makes this word mean 'caused to send down'. Allah (اللَّهُ) is the one doing the sending down in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'one who is worshipped'
Added the u-mark at the end to show this is the subject
This is a preposition that shows something is upon or over something else. In this ayah, it connects to the word بَشَرٍ (human being) to show what Allah didn't send anything upon/to.

steps.m: to have skin/outer appearance, to interact with people

steps.n: Added fathas to create the noun meaning 'human being' - this pattern is often used for describing living things
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of the word عَلَىٰ (upon) in the phrase 'upon any human being'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it's used with شَىْءٍ (thing) to mean 'of anything'
The shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger, which adds emphasis to the word. This emphasis shows up because the word comes after a vowel sound in ما

steps.m: to want or wish for something
steps.n: The root letters show the basic concept of willing or wishing
Root letters ش-ي-أ get vowels (َ ْ) to make the common noun 'thing'
The hamza at the end changes to ء when it comes after a sukun
The ي changes to ى when it has a sukun and comes before hamza

steps.m: of/from a thing (after مِن in this ayah)
steps.n: Added kasratayn at the end because it comes after the word مِن (from/of) in this ayah
These are the basic letters that give us the idea of saying something
Changed from verb 'to say' into noun 'saying' by adding damma and sukun marks
The و is dropped to make it a command. This is common with hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)

steps.m: who (asking about the one who أَنزَلَ/sent down)
steps.n: This is a question word asking about who did something. In this ayah, it's asking who sent down the Book that Moses brought. It connects directly to the verb أَنزَلَ (sent down) that comes right after it.
Adding fathas connects the letters and makes it a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes the verb causative - instead of just descending, it means making something descend. In this ayah, Allah (اللَّهُ) is the subject who does the action of sending down.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he wrote' changes to a noun meaning 'what was written' (a book) by changing the vowels and adding a long 'aa' sound in the middle
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being sent down. Also changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) as per standard Quranic spelling.
This is a relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence. Here it connects 'the Book' with what comes after it, describing which Book is being talked about. It's like saying 'the Book that...'
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Changed to standard spelling of past tense 'came' by adding alif
Changed to emphatic spelling with madda to show extended sound
This is a preposition that shows how something is done

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to الْكِتٰبَ - the Book)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ (him/it) to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to الْكِتٰبَ (the Book) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to الْكِتٰبَ - the Book)
steps.n: Added the small ۦ mark after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: Moses (the prophet mentioned in the Torah/Old Testament)
steps.n: This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew/Egyptian meaning 'drawn out of water'. In Arabic, it's written with a special mark (ىٰ) at the end called an alif maqsurah with a small alif above it to show it's a foreign name.
The root letters were shaped into a noun meaning 'light' by adding the vowel mark ُ (u sound)
Added ًا to show this is the object being brought. In this ayah, it refers to the light (guidance) that Musa brought to people.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to guide' into the noun 'guidance'
Adding tanween alif makes the word indefinite
Connects this word to نُورًا (light) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Forms the word for 'people' from the root meaning of being sociable
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Adds the meaning 'for/to' before the word
The ل is doubled (has a shaddah) and there's a kasra at the end to show this word connects to what comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Prefix تَ changes it to present tense 'you' form
The ُ (damma) at the end indicates present tense indicative mood
Suffix ونَ shows that many people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action

steps.m: you all make/transform it (referring to الْكِتٰبَ - the scripture)
steps.n: Adds هُ to show what is being transformed - refers back to الْكِتٰبَ (the scripture) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Takes the root letters for 'paper' and makes it into a specific single piece/sheet of paper
Changes the word to plural form by rearranging vowels and adding ي
Adds fatha at the end to show this word is the object of تَجْعَلُونَ (you make/render)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed from active (he shows) to passive (it is shown)
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added ها to show what is being shown (referring to the scrolls/pages mentioned in the verse)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changes from 'he hid' to 'you hide' by adding تُ at the start
Changes from 'you hide' (singular) to 'you all hide' (plural) by adding ون at the end
Adds 'and' (wa) at the start
Added vowel marks (fatha and kasra) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'it increased'
Added ي to change from a verb (action) to an adjective meaning 'a lot of something'
Added ًا to show this describes how something is done
Adding shadda makes it mean 'to make someone else know/learn'
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added تم to show 'you all' were the ones who were taught
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
This is a basic Arabic particle used to ask 'what' or refer to 'that which'
The shadda makes the م stronger. Here it connects with the next word تَعْلَمُوا to mean 'what you knew'
This is a negative particle that changes the verb after it (تَعْلَمُوٓا۟) to mean the opposite and makes it refer to the past. It's like adding 'did not' before a verb in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added damma at end to show it's in the normal (nominative) case
Adding وا changes it from singular 'you know' to plural 'you all know'
Adding ا after وا makes it a command form for a group
This is the basic form of the pronoun used when talking to someone
These are actually two different words - أَنْ means 'that' while أَنتُمْ is the plural 'you'
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Extended the alif to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This root relates to fatherhood
Changes from root letters to the simple word meaning 'father'
Changes from singular 'father' to irregular plural 'fathers' with a longer sound
Adds damma (ُ) to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Adds 'your (plural)' meaning by adding كُمْ at the end
This is the basic meaning of speaking
Changed from basic 'say' to a command meaning 'say!' by adding marks above and on middle letter
The weak letter و is dropped because it comes between two sukuns

steps.m: one who is worshipped, a deity
steps.n: Changed the concept of worship into a word for the one who receives worship
Added ال to specify The One True God, with special spelling and emphasis on the ل to show uniqueness and importance. The ُ shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence.
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects what Allah says with what happens next.

steps.m: to leave alone or let be
steps.n: This root means to leave something alone or abandon it
Command form made by dropping و and adding the vowel mark َ (fatha) - means 'leave!' or 'let it be!'
Adds هُمْ at the end to show who is being left alone. In this ayah, Allah is commanding the Prophet to leave the disbelievers in their false beliefs.
This is a basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it shows that something is written within/in the papers (قَرَاطِيسَ).
Changes the verb into a noun that names the action itself, like changing 'dive' to 'diving'
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word connects to the next part (هِمْ)

steps.m: their diving into (refers to the disbelievers in the ayah)
steps.n: The هِمْ at the end means 'their' and refers back to the disbelievers mentioned earlier who were denying Allah's revelation
Connected the root letters together with vowels to make a past tense verb
The يَ prefix shows this is happening now or in the future
The ون- ending changes 'he plays' to 'they play' - like adding 's' in English but for multiple people instead of one person
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
Added ه with alif above it (هٰ) at the beginning to make the word stronger and clearer when pointing to something
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show the result of writing - the written thing (book)

steps.m: a book (subject of أَنزَلْنٰهُ - We sent it down)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is indefinite (any book) and is the subject of the verb 'We sent down' in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning to show causing something to happen. Changed first vowel to sukun.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: we sent it down (object of كِتٰبٌ)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what was sent down (referring to كِتٰبٌ - the book). The alif in نا changes to ٰ before هُ.

steps.m: to increase, grow, or bring forth good

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif makes the blessing more emphatic/strong

steps.m: blessed (something that has been filled with good)
steps.n: Changed from 'he blessed' to 'something that is blessed' by adding مُ at the start and ٌ at the end - like changing 'bless' to 'blessed'

steps.m: to confirm or verify something as true

steps.n: Added shadda to show strong or repeated confirmation
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show this describes someone who does the action

steps.m: one who confirms strongly (describing كِتٰبٌ - the Book)
steps.n: Added shadda on م and ُ at end to show this describes the Book mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added kasra under ذ to show it's describing a masculine singular thing
Added ال at the beginning to change it from a demonstrative (this/that) to a relative pronoun (who/which/that)
Changed to special Quran spelling with a different alif (ٱ) and a double L sound

Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create a preposition. This word appears before يَدَيْهِ (his hands) in this ayah, forming the phrase 'between his hands' which is an Arabic expression meaning 'before him' or 'in his presence'.
Basic root meaning related to hands and extension
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic singular noun
Added ي with fatha before it to make the dual form
Added sukoon on the final ي to complete the dual form

steps.m: his two hands (that which is between/before مُصَدِّقُ)
steps.n: Added هِ ('hi') at the end to mean 'his' two hands - showing who the hands belong to

steps.m: to warn or alert someone about danger
steps.n: Basic meaning of warning or alerting
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes to present tense and second person (you)
Adds fatha at the end to show this is the purpose of sending down the Book mentioned earlier in the verse
Adds لِ to show purpose - this explains why the Book was sent down

steps.m: and so that you warn (the purpose of sending down the Book)
steps.n: Adds و to connect this purpose with other purposes mentioned in the verse

steps.m: to lead or be the source/origin of something
steps.n: The root letters show the basic meaning of being a source or leader
Changed the word to mean a source or origin point, like a mother. Here it describes the main city that other towns follow.

steps.m: to settle or dwell in a place
steps.n: Basic meaning of gathering or collecting
Changes from basic root to plural noun meaning settlements
Adds the definite article to specify these particular villages/towns
Adds an alif maqsurah (ى) to complete the noun pattern
Basic relative pronoun referring to people who do something. In this ayah, it refers to those who believe.
Adds و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, showing these are additional people who will believe in the Book.
Basic meaning of surrounding or encompassing
The root becomes a noun meaning the area around something. The word حَوْل is possessed by the pronoun ها which refers back to أُمَّ ٱلْقُرَىٰ (Mecca), showing who/what is being surrounded.
Adds feminine pronoun ها referring to Mecca (أُمَّ ٱلْقُرَىٰ). This shows what area is being surrounded.
Added ال to make it specific and shadda to strengthen the meaning
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This word describes people who believe, as shown by the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) that follows it.

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure
steps.n: Basic meaning of safety and security
Adds يُ at the start to make it 'he' in present tense, with ؤ showing it comes from أ root

steps.m: they believe (referring to those who believe in the hereafter - بِٱلْءَاخِرَةِ)
steps.n: Adds plural masculine ending to show multiple people believing

steps.m: to come after, to be delayed
steps.n: Basic meaning of coming after or being delayed
Changes root 'to come after' into 'last/final' by adding long aa sound at start and i sound in middle - like changing 'write' to 'writer'

steps.m: the final life, what comes after (feminine)
steps.n: Adds ة at the end to make it feminine, changing it from 'last/final' to specifically mean 'the final life/afterlife'
Added definite article to make it refer to the specific concept of the afterlife in Islamic terminology

steps.m: in the Hereafter (object of يُؤْمِنُونَ - they believe)
steps.n: Adds 'in' at start and kasra at end to show this is what they believe in. This word is connected to يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) in this ayah.

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added an alif at the start to make it mean 'choosing to believe' instead of just 'feeling safe/trust'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Basic preposition that shows how something is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: in/with/by it/him (refers to كِتٰبٌ - the Book)
steps.n: Added هِ to show what the preposition connects to - in this case referring back to the Book mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: in/with/by it/him (refers to كِتٰبٌ - the Book)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they' and sukun (ْ) shows where to stop
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of يُحَافِظُونَ (they preserve/maintain) in the next part of the ayah.
Basic preposition showing something is on top of, upon, or responsible for something else

steps.m: upon/on (preposition showing position in relation to صَلَاتِهِمْ - their prayers)
steps.n: Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a complete word form. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning.
The weak letter و changes to ا and we add ة to show one specific prayer
The kasra at the end shows this prayer belongs to someone
Added هِمْ to show the prayer belongs to them. This word is the object of يُحَافِظُونَ (they guard/maintain) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Adding ا shows two or more people did this action together
Adding يُ at start and changing vowels shows this happens in the present
Adding ون shows many people are doing this
This is a question word that can also mean 'whoever' when used in statements. It's used to talk about people, like saying 'the one who' or 'whoever' in English.
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, just like how we use 'and' in English to connect ideas

steps.m: to do wrong, to be unfair

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters together, making a past-tense verb

steps.m: more wrong (than those who lie about Allah)
steps.n: Changed the word to show comparison with others mentioned in the ayah who lie about Allah
Combined two separate words (مِن meaning 'from' and مَن meaning 'who'). The ن from مِن combines with م from مَن, creating a double م (written with shadda).
Added shadda mark to show the doubled م sound
Added kasra at the end to connect it with the next word افْتَرَىٰ (invented/fabricated). Together they ask 'who is more unjust than the one who invented...'
Added fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added اِفْتَ to change meaning from 'cutting' to 'making up/inventing something false' - like cutting up the truth
Changed spelling of alif to match Uthmani script and added alif maksura at end

steps.m: upon/against (used with اللَّهِ to show making false claims against Allah)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something being directed against or attributed to something else. In this context, it's used with Allah's name to describe making false claims against Him.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَلَى (upon/against) in the ayah
This is the basic concept of lying or deceiving
The vowels change it from action of lying to describing someone/something as lying
The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of افْتَرَىٰ (invented/fabricated). The full phrase افْتَرَىٰ كَذِبًا means 'invented a lie'

steps.m: or (connecting two false claims: saying lies about Allah OR saying 'revelation came to me')
steps.n: This is a conjunction that connects two false claims being discussed in the ayah: making up lies about Allah, or falsely claiming to receive revelation. The word أَوْ here shows these are equally wrong choices.
This is the basic meaning showing the action of speaking or saying something
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in words with و in the middle.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end.
Adding أ at the beginning makes the meaning more direct and purposeful - it's not just communication, but specifically divine revelation
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it. In this ayah, this refers to false claims of receiving divine revelation.
This is one of the basic Arabic prepositions that shows direction or destination, like 'to' in English
The ي (ya) at the end with a shadda combines with إلى to mean 'to me' instead of just 'to'
Adding the basic connecting word 'and'
Added لَمْ which makes the next verb (يُوحَ) negative in the past tense. It's like saying 'did not' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed to passive present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The ى at the end is because it's a weak verb.

steps.m: it is revealed (in a conditional statement)
steps.n: The ى is dropped because this word appears in a conditional context in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
Added ي with sukun (يْ) to prepare for attaching the pronoun. This is a connecting form that appears before pronouns.
Added هِ to show it means 'him'. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah since the context is about people falsely claiming revelation from Him.

Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it a noun. The hamza (ء) at the end is because of the root letter أ. This word refers to what was not revealed in the phrase 'nothing was revealed to him'.
Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite (any thing). The ى is just a writing variation of ي.

steps.m: who (asking about the person who is more unjust)
steps.n: This is a question word asking about people. In this ayah, it's being used to ask rhetorically about who could be more unjust than someone who lies about Allah.

steps.m: and who (connecting to previous statement about injustice)
steps.n: Added و to connect this question to the previous statement, showing this is part of a larger discussion about injustice
These are the three basic letters that give us the core meaning of saying something
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he said (referring to someone who falsely claims revelation)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا to make it easier to say. This is common in Arabic words with و in the middle.

steps.m: to send down or bring down

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra
Added سَ at beginning to mean 'will' (future), and changed from 'it was sent' to 'I will send' by changing the verb form
Added kasra and sukun to make the noun 'similar/like' from the basic meaning

steps.m: like/similar to (what أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ - Allah has sent down)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word describes what will be sent down, connecting it to أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ (Allah has sent down) later in the verse
This is a relative pronoun referring to what Allah revealed in this context, connecting to أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ (Allah revealed)
The alif is written with a madda mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb

steps.m: he sent down (something that سَأُنزِلُ refers to)
steps.n: Changed from someone doing the action themselves to making something else do the action. This word is referenced by سَأُنزِلُ (I will send down) in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the u-mark at the end to show it's in the nominative case
This is a basic word that introduces hypothetical situations, like saying 'what if' in English

steps.m: and if/and even if (connects to أَوْ قَالَ)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement with أَوْ قَالَ (or he said), showing another example of wrongdoing

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense 'he saw' by adding fatha marks
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed to present tense form where hamza drops out
Added alif maqsura with superscript alif for pronunciation clarity
This is a time particle that connects two events in the past. Here it connects 'if you could see' (تَرَىٰٓ) with what happens after.
Added kasra at the end because it's followed by the word الظَّالِمُونَ starting with ال.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: someone who does wrong (like saying wrong-doer)
steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show who does the action, like adding '-er' to words in English (teach → teacher). This word is part of the subject that you would see (ترى) in this ayah.
Added ال (the) at the start and doubled the ظ letter with shadda to show we're talking about specific wrongdoers

steps.m: the wrongdoers (the ones you would see)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show we're talking about many wrongdoers, not just one

steps.m: in/within (connects غَمَرٰتِ with what comes before)

steps.m: in/within (connects غَمَرٰتِ with what comes before)
steps.n: The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning or pronunciation.
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the root letters into a word meaning 'overwhelming'
Added ة to show one specific instance of being overwhelmed
Changed from singular to plural form by adding alif and taa at the end to show multiple overwhelming experiences
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a noun meaning the result of dying
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it follows غَمَرٰتِ (depths/floods of)
Changed the root 'to rule/control' into a noun meaning 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a
This is a broken plural form that changes the internal structure to show multiple angels
Added definite article to specify these particular angels
Added 'and' (wa) at the start and 'u' sound at the end
Added vowels to connect the letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changed the word to show who does the action (active participle)
Added damma to show it's in the subject position
Changed from singular to plural by adding و
Added وا to make it plural masculine nominative
Changes singular 'hand' (يد) to plural 'hands' by adding أ at start and changing the word pattern to make it plural
Added هِمْ at the end to show possession. This word is the object of بَاسِطُوٓا۟ (stretching forth) in this ayah.
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to make causative form
Changed vowels to make present tense 'I' form

steps.n: This is a primary preposition showing direction or opposition. Here it's used with the verb افْتَرَى (fabricated) to show who is being wronged by the fabrication.

Added ال to specify THE one true deity, doubled the ل with shadda, and added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Changed from basic root letters to a noun by adding vowels - now means 'other than' something
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of what they say (تَقُولُونَ)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb

steps.n: Adding و connects this to the previous statement. This word relates to تَقُولُونَ (saying) in the ayah, showing what they were doing - speaking falsely about Allah.
This is a helper word that shows something is being separated or moved away from something else. In this ayah, it shows people are turning away from Allah's signs (ءَايٰتِهِۦ).

steps.m: to show clear proof or evidence

steps.n: The root letters أ-ي-ي form آية by combining أ and ي into آ, turning it from 'showing proof' into 'one sign/miracle'
Added ات to show multiple signs or miracles
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِ to show these are Allah's signs, with ۦ as an emphasis marker
The ُ (damma) in the middle shows this is about having a natural quality
Added است to show someone forcing themselves to be great/big in a bad way (being arrogant)
Changed to present tense and added 'you' marker
Added plural ending to show multiple people
Added vowel marks to the base particle قد which shows certainty or completion
Added ل at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what comes before it, like saying 'and'
Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding long 'aa' sound in middle
Changed to passive voice by adding kasra (i sound) to first letter
Added تُم at the end to change from 'was come' to 'you all came' - تُم shows multiple people did the action
Added و at the end to prepare for attaching a pronoun - و helps connect pronouns to verbs
Added نا at the end to show 'us' - now means the action was done to 'us'
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to make it a past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (he separated) to a plural word meaning 'one by one' by changing vowels and adding a special ending
Basic comparison particle that shows similarity

steps.m: just as (connects to خَلَقْنٰكُمْ - we created you)
steps.n: Adding ما makes the comparison more specific and connects it to what follows. Here it connects to how Allah first created us.
Connected the letters with fathas to make it a past-tense verb
Added نٰ to show that 'we' did the creating. In the Quran, when Allah says 'we', it shows His majesty and power, not that there are multiple gods.

steps.m: we created you all (referring to all humans being addressed)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who was created - in this case, all the humans being spoken to
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a basic word meaning 'first'

steps.m: first (describing خَلَقْنٰكُمْ - how Allah created us)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger. The final fatha shows this word is describing how Allah created us, mentioned in خَلَقْنٰكُمْ (He created you).
Added fatha and combined the repeated ر with shadda to show strong/repeated action
The ة changes it from an action to a single instance of that action
Adding tanween kasra (ٍ) makes it indefinite and shows it describes أَوَّلَ (first) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb meaning 'he left'
Added ت at the end and changed the last vowel to sukun to show that one person is being addressed
Changed ت to تُم to show multiple people are being addressed

steps.m: and you (plural) left (what وَرَآءَ ظُهُورِكُمْ - behind your backs)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word describes what the people left behind their backs.
Basic particle that can mean 'what' when asking about things or referring to things. Here it refers to the things that were left behind.
The shadda (ّ) with fatha (َ) adds emphasis to show importance of what was left behind
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda on و makes the action more intensive or emphatic
Adding نَا changes the subject to 'we' and puts a sukun before it

steps.m: we gave you all much authority
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows who received the action (you all)
Added fatha after و and ر, and added alif (ا) to make the noun meaning 'behind'
The alif got longer (آ) and got a small mark (ء) at the end. It connects with 'your backs' to mean 'behind your backs'

steps.m: to appear, be visible, or be on someone's back

steps.n: Changes from the verb root 'to appear/be visible' into the noun meaning 'back' (like your back)
Changes from singular 'back' to plural 'backs' by changing the vowels from a-a to u-u
Adds kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Adds pronoun showing the backs belong to multiple people being addressed
This is a negative particle that makes the verb نَرَىٰ (we see) into 'we do not see'

steps.m: and we do not (negates نَرَىٰ - 'we see')
steps.n: The و (and) connects this phrase to the previous statement, joining two related ideas
Changed to present tense form. The weak letter ي at the end changes to ى, and the hamza (أ) drops out in present tense
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by replacing يَ with نَ at the start. The alif at the end (ىٰ) shows it's a weak verb
This is a preposition that shows something is together with or accompanying something else. It's similar to how we use 'with' in English.

steps.m: with you all (referring to those being addressed in the ayah)
steps.n: The كُمْ suffix shows this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, Allah is addressing those who claimed to have partners with Him, telling them that these supposed partners are not 'with you' anymore.

steps.m: to help others by speaking for them
those who help others by speaking for them (plural)
steps.n: Changes the word to mean multiple people who do this action. The آء at the end makes it plural, like adding 's' in English.

steps.m: your helpers who speak for you (addressing many people)
steps.n: Added كُمُ at the end to show these helpers belong to or are connected to many people being spoken to
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people

steps.m: those who (referring to your partners mentioned in شُفَعَآءَكُمُ)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and point to particular people - in this case, referring to the false partners or intercessors mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: those who (referring to your partners mentioned in شُفَعَآءَكُمُ)
steps.n: Added shadda on ذ and fatha at the end to help with pronunciation. Also changed the initial alif to a wasl form (ٱ) since it connects with the previous word

steps.m: to claim falsely or without proof

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form, showing someone made a false claim
Changed the final vowel to a sukun (ْ) to prepare for adding the suffix
Added تُمْ at the end to show that multiple people (you all) did this action in the past
This is an emphasis word that connects what comes before with what comes after, like saying 'indeed' or 'that' in English
Added هُمْ at the end to show we're talking about 'they/them'. This connects to the word شُرَكٰٓؤُا۟ (partners) in the ayah.

steps.m: in (preposition showing location or being within something)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing location or position

steps.m: in you all (partners that were claimed to be شُرَكٰٓؤُا۟)
steps.n: Added كُمْ (kum) at the end to mean 'you all' instead of just 'in'. Like adding 'you' to make 'in you'

steps.m: to partner with or share something

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to share' into noun meaning 'partner'
Changed vowels and added اء at the end to make it plural
Added ُ and و at the end to show these partners are the ones doing the action in the sentence
This is a basic particle that shows something has definitely happened or emphasizes a statement

steps.m: certainly/indeed (emphasizing جِئْتُمُونَا - you have come to Us)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to make the emphasis even stronger. This combination is very common in the Quran to show absolute certainty about something that happened.
Added 3 fathas to make it 'he cut' in the past tense
Adding shadda shows the cutting is done thoroughly or completely
Adding تَ shows the action happens by itself rather than someone doing it

steps.m: it has definitely become completely broken apart (between كُمْ)
steps.n: Adding shadda on ت adds emphasis and shows certainty. This word describes what happened between 'you all' (كُمْ) in this ayah.
Forms a noun showing the space or relationship that separates things. Here it acts as the subject of تَّقَطَّعَ (has been severed).
Changes from 'between' as a noun to 'between' as a preposition that connects things
Adds 'you all' (كُمْ) to the end to show who/what is between
Adding fathas makes it a past tense verb showing someone got lost

steps.m: and he became lost (connected to تَّقَطَّعَ - it was cut off)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to the previous verb تَّقَطَّعَ (it was cut off), showing these actions happened together
This is a preposition that shows separation or distance. In this ayah, it shows how the false gods are separated from the disbelievers.

steps.m: away from you all (referring to the disbelievers)
steps.n: Adds كُم to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, Allah is telling the disbelievers how their false gods have become separated from them.

steps.m: what/that which (object of تَرَكْتُم - you left)
steps.n: This is the basic form of the word that refers to things or concepts (not people). In this ayah, it refers to what was given to them that they left behind.

steps.m: what/that which (object of تَرَكْتُم - you left)
steps.n: The shadda is added to emphasize the word, making its pronunciation stronger. This doesn't change the meaning but adds emphasis.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
The weak letter و is dropped and the vowels change due to it being a hollow verb

steps.m: you all were (in relation to زَعَمْتُمْ - you all claimed)
steps.n: Added تُمْ to show that multiple people (you all) did this action in the past. This word relates to زَعَمْتُمْ (you all claimed) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changes to present tense with تَ prefix showing 'you' and damma showing ongoing action
Adds ون ending to show multiple people are doing the claiming together
This is the basic form of the word that shows something is certain or conditional
Adding the shadda makes the word stronger and changes it from possibly meaning 'if' to definitely meaning 'indeed'. Here it emphasizes the word Allah (ٱللَّهَ) that comes right after it.
Changed إِلَه to اللَّه, which is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ّ (shadda) shows emphasis.
Added ٱ at the beginning and َ at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
This is the basic meaning of splitting or breaking something open
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: one who splits (predicate of إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ)
steps.n: Changed the word to show who does the action. This describes Allah as the one who splits things open.

steps.m: to love, to be a seed

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter turns it from a verb into a noun meaning 'seed'
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Adding kasra for genitive case

steps.m: to have a core or center

steps.n: The root ن-و-ي becomes 'the pit/stone' when you add ال (the). The ن doubles (gets shadda) because it comes after ال.

steps.m: and the pit/stone (object being split by فَالِقُ)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects it to the previous word (grain). The alif at the end is written long to show it's being paused on. This word is being acted upon by فَالِقُ (splitter) in the ayah.
Changed the pattern to make it mean 'cause something to happen' instead of just happening

steps.m: He (Allah) brings out/causes to exit (the living from the dead)

Adding fatha (َ) to make it a past tense verb

steps.n: Adding kasra (ِ) to show this describes being in a state of life

Added fatha at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word (الْحَىِّ). This combination shows that the dead comes from the living.
The و changes to ي and gets doubled (with ّ) to show something that has this quality strongly
The ِ at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the word مِنَ (from) in the ayah

steps.m: the dead (being acted upon by مِنَ)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to a specific dead thing rather than any dead thing
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who brings out (active participle of يُخْرِجُ in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed to active participle form to show who does the action. This word relates to يُخْرِجُ (brings out) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added و to connect this word to what came before it in the ayah
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
The weak letter و changes to ي and gets doubled with a shadda

steps.m: the dead one (being brought out from in يُخْرِجُ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and ِ at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from)
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it shows that the living comes from (مِن) the dead.
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word (الْمَيِّتِ) which starts with ال. This helps with pronunciation.

steps.m: the Living One (source of مِنَ ٱلْمَيِّتِ - from the dead)
steps.n: The word means 'the Living One' and has a kasra mark under the last letter. It connects to the phrase 'from the dead' that comes after it. The letter ي is written in a special way (ى) when it has kasra.

Added لِ which helps connect to the next part. This is just how Arabic joins these parts together smoothly.

steps.m: that is (addressing multiple people) (pointing to Allah in previous ayah)
steps.n: Added كُمُ at the end to show this is speaking to multiple people. In this ayah, it's pointing back to Allah who was mentioned before.

This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.
This is a question word asking 'how' or 'in what way'. It's used to ask about the manner or way something happens.

steps.m: so how/in what way (are you turned away from the truth)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this question to the previous statement about Allah's power. It shows this question follows from what was just mentioned.

steps.m: to turn away from truth, to deceive

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he turned away' to 'you are turned away' by adding تُ at start and changing middle vowels to show someone else is doing the action

steps.m: you (all) are turned away (from the truth)
steps.n: Added ون to show this applies to multiple people

steps.m: one who splits (describes Allah as the one who splits الإصباح/daybreak)

Adds the u-vowel at the end to show this is the subject/doer in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun showing the action of causing morning to break
Added the definite article and genitive case ending because it's the object of فَالِقُ (Cleaver of) in this ayah

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set

Adding وَ (and) at the start connects this to the previous statement about Allah splitting the daybreak, showing He also made other things
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word for 'night'. This is what Allah made into a place of rest.
Added 'the' (ال) which combines with the first letter ل to make a stronger ل sound
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلَ (made/appointed)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: a place/time of rest (object of جَعَلَ)

Added 'al' to make it specific. The shadda (ّ) appears on the ش because it comes after 'al'.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb جَعَلَ (made/placed)
Added و at beginning to connect this with the previous phrase. The alif in ال changes to ٱ after و.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic noun
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلَ (He made/placed)

Changes from verb to noun showing intensity of calculation
The tanween (ًا) shows this is indefinite (any calculation) and is being acted upon by the verb جَعَلَ (He made/set) in this ayah.

The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show distance
Adding لِ changes 'this' to 'that', showing something farther away
The كَ at the end makes it specifically point to something that was just mentioned. In this ayah, it's pointing to how Allah made the night for rest and the sun and moon for calculating time.

Changed from 'he measured' to 'the act of measuring' by using pattern تَفْعِيل which makes nouns from verbs
Added ُ at the end to show this describes what 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) is in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding ي in the middle makes this describe someone who has this quality very strongly
Adding ال makes it specific/definite - 'the' mighty one rather than just 'a' mighty one

steps.m: of The Mighty One (possessed by تَقْدِيرُ/determination)

Added ي between second and third root letters with vowels to create intensive form showing complete/perfect knowledge

Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it

1. Changed وَا to وٰ which is just a writing style change

This is a personal pronoun that refers to Allah who is being talked about as the one who sent down rain. It's the subject (doer) of the verb أَنزَلَ (sent down).

This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from or originating from somewhere. In this ayah, it connects to السَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven) to show where the water comes from.
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word السَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: from it (referring to نَبَاتَ - the plant/vegetation)

Added تَ at beginning and ا after first root letter to show things affecting each other

The ٍ shows this word is being described by what comes after it in the ayah

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. It's like saying 'from' or 'of' in English.
The م gets a shadda (double mark) because it comes after another word ending in a similar sound. This is just for easier pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Adds vowels to make the basic noun form for 'grapes'
Changes to broken plural form to show multiple grapes or grape clusters
Adds genitive case ending to show this word is being described in the sentence
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun
Added ون to make it refer to olives (the fruit) rather than oil
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ز with shadda
Added و to connect to previous word and َ at the end to show it's part of a list
Forms the basic noun for pomegranate by adding vowels and doubling the middle letter
Adds the definite article 'the'
Adds 'and' at the beginning
Adds the accusative case ending to show this is an object in the sentence

steps.m: to look like or match something

steps.n: Connected the letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who or what is doing the looking-like
Added ت between first and second letters to show things look like each other, not just one thing looking like another
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing something in the sentence
Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and', and َ at the end to show this describes what comes after it

steps.m: to look alike or be similar

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding fatha (a) vowels

steps.m: they became similar to each other
steps.n: Added تَ at start and changed meaning to show things becoming similar to each other
Changed from a past action (things became similar) to describing something that is similar now

steps.m: being similar (to other things mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: The ٍ shows this describes how fruits and plants mentioned earlier look alike
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to 'look!' by adding U-sound at start and making it a command to one person
Changed to plural command by adding وا at the end and changing the last vowel to damma. The alif at start changed to hamzat wasl (ٱ)
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to ثَمَرِهِ (its fruit) to show what we should look towards.
Added an alif that shows the word ends with a long 'aa' sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
Added fatha vowels to make the basic noun form for the physical fruit itself
Added -i ending to show it belongs to something (like saying 'fruit of')
Added هِ to show possession - 'his/its fruit'
Added ۦٓ after هِ to show proper pronunciation of 'his/its'

steps.m: when (connects to أَثْمَرَ - it bears fruit)
steps.n: This is a word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects to the word أَثْمَرَ (it bears fruit) to tell us to look at the fruits when they appear.

steps.m: when (connects to أَثْمَرَ - it bears fruit)
steps.n: The alif is written long (آ) to show it should be pronounced clearly and with emphasis. This doesn't change the meaning, just helps with pronunciation.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: it bore fruit (verb in إِذَآ أَثْمَرَ - when it bears fruit)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show the fruit appears by itself. This verb is part of the phrase 'when it bears fruit' in the ayah.
The root letters are connected together and voweled to form a noun meaning the state of being ripe. This describes the ripeness of the fruit (ثَمَر) mentioned earlier.
Added the possessive pronoun هِ (his/its) to show this ripeness belongs to the fruit. This word is in the genitive case because it follows the preposition إلى (to) in انظروا إلى (look at).
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this to the previous things we're told to look at. The ۦٓ is a pronunciation mark showing how to extend the sound.
This is an emphasis particle that strengthens what comes after it. The shadda (ّ) makes the emphasis stronger. In this ayah, it emphasizes that there are definitely signs in what was just described.
This is one of the basic prepositions in Arabic. In this ayah, it connects 'that' (ذٰلِكُمْ) with 'signs' (لَءَايٰتٍ) to show that the signs exist within or as part of what was mentioned before.
The basic demonstrative pronoun with alif changed to alif waslah to show distance
Added لِ to show this is pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) for you all
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is being pointed out to multiple people

steps.m: to show, make clear, be evident

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'a sign'
Changes the word from singular (one sign) to plural (many signs)
Adds 'for/to' meaning to the word
Makes the word indefinite (any signs)

steps.m: to stand together as a group

steps.n: The root meaning of standing together becomes a word for a group of people. This word connects to يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) in the next ayah, showing these are believing people.
Adds two small diagonal lines under the word to make it mean 'a people' instead of 'the people'

steps.m: for a people (who believe in يُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Adds preposition لِ meaning 'for/to'

steps.m: for a people (who believe in يُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Added emphasis to the ل by adding shadda, showing these signs are specifically for these people
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start. The hamza (ء) changes to ؤ when it comes after damma.

steps.m: they believe (referring to a group of people who don't believe in the signs mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to those who don't believe in Allah's signs mentioned in the previous part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form. This word shows who did the action of making partners for Allah.
Adding وا changes it from singular 'he' to plural 'they' - showing a group of people made partners for Allah
Added وَ (and) to connect this action with previous statements. The small mark ۟ at the end is just for proper recitation.
This is the base word Allah, which is a proper name and doesn't come from a standard root pattern

steps.m: to/for Allah (object of جَعَلُوا - they made)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show direction/purpose and ِ at the end to connect it to the next word. This word shows who they wrongly assigned partners to.

steps.m: to partner with or share something

steps.n: Added vowels and ي to change from 'to share' into 'a partner/sharer'
Changes from single partner to multiple partners
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb جَعَلُوا (they made)

steps.m: to hide or be hidden from sight

steps.n: Adding the kasra changes it from a verb (to hide) into a noun (hidden beings)

steps.m: the jinn (partners of اللَّهِ according to disbelievers)
steps.n: Added 'al' to specify these particular jinn that some people wrongly made partners with Allah

steps.m: to create, make, or form something from nothing

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense. This word relates to جَعَلُوا (they made) in the ayah, showing what the same people did.
Adding هُمْ means 'them' (masculine plural)
Adding وَ at the beginning means 'and'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding وا makes it plural 'they'

steps.m: and they made up (falsely) (connected to جَعَلُوا in previous ayah)
steps.n: Added و to connect this action with جَعَلُوا (they made) from the previous part, showing these actions are related
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the preposition ل
Added هُ which means 'him' to show who the action is for
Added the elongation mark ۥ after هُ to show the extended pronunciation
Added vowels to make the singular noun form for 'son'
Added ن at the end to make it plural. This word is part of what the disbelievers falsely attributed to Allah, along with daughters mentioned in the next word (بَنٰتٍ).

steps.m: to build, establish, or create offspring

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added ات to make it mean multiple females
Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object being falsely attributed to Allah in this ayah

steps.m: and daughters (being falsely attributed to Allah)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this word with the previous one (بَنِينَ/sons)
Basic noun form meaning 'other than' or 'without'
Added بِ (bi) at the start and a kasra (i) at the end to make it mean 'without'. It connects to the word عِلْمٍ (knowledge) to mean 'without knowledge'
Changed from action (knowing) to the concept itself (knowledge)

steps.m: any knowledge (in بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ - without any knowledge)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite and is being acted upon by the word بِغَيْرِ (without)

steps.m: to swim, float, glide smoothly; (by extension) to be free of all imperfections
absolute perfection and freedom from all faults
steps.n: Changes the root meaning 'to be free of faults' into a special word that praises Allah as perfect and free from any faults

steps.m: absolute perfection and freedom from all faults (in accusative case)
steps.n: The word is now in accusative case, which is often used when praising Allah
The هُ suffix means 'His' and refers to Allah
The ا after ح makes it into the special noun form that means 'perfection'

steps.m: to rise, be high, or elevated

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and ا in middle to show 'doing the action to oneself' - like saying 'he made himself high'
Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase
This is a basic word that shows what something is about or concerning

When عَنْ and مَا come together, the ن changes to م and gets a shadda (double mark). This makes it easier to say.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he described) to present tense (he describes) by adding يَ at start

steps.m: they describe (referring to those who attribute false qualities to Allah)
steps.n: The plural form shows this refers to multiple people who are falsely describing or attributing qualities to Allah, as mentioned in the context of the ayah

steps.m: to create something completely new and unprecedented

steps.n: Forms an intensive adjective describing one who creates/originates in a completely new and unprecedented way

steps.n: The ُ ending shows this is the main subject doing the creating/originating. This word describes Allah as the one who created the heavens and earth in a completely new way, without any previous example.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic word form
The و changes to اء to create a noun meaning the place or thing that is elevated
Added the feminine plural ending ات, with و returning before the plural ending
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning, which causes the س to double (get a shadda)

Added ال to make it specific ('the earth') and ِ to show it's connected to 'the heavens' (السَّمٰوٰتِ) in the phrase
Added وَ at the beginning to connect it with 'the heavens' (السَّمٰوٰتِ)
This is the basic question word used to ask about manner, way, or location

Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the word 'for' refers to. This word connects to وَلَدٌ (child) in the ayah, showing the meaning 'a child for him'
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a writing style to show the pronunciation more clearly
Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb

Added vowel marks to show this is a negative particle that makes verbs refer to the past. It's used with تَكُن (be) in this ayah to say something was not true in the past.
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it in the ayah
ت at start shows it's happening now (present tense) for 'she'
The و is dropped because it comes after لَمْ which requires this shortened form
This is a basic preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it's connected to the verb يَكُونُ (to be/exist) to show who something is for.
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show importance or certainty
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the preposition points to. The small و (ۥ) is just a pronunciation helper.

steps.m: to accompany or be a companion

steps.n: Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action. This creates the basic form for someone who accompanies.
Adding ة at the end changes this from male companion to female companion
Two changes: 1) Changed َا to ٰ as an alternate spelling, and 2) Added ٌ at the end to show this is the subject of تَكُن (was not) in the ayah

steps.m: to create, make, form something new

steps.m: and he created (connected to بَدِيعُ/Originator in previous phrase)

Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation of the basic pronoun

The preposition بِ shows how Allah is عَلِيمٌ (All-Knowing) - He is knowing 'with/of all things'

steps.m: of a thing (acted upon by خَلَقَ)

Added لِ to make 'that' point to something farther away (like pointing with your finger from far away)
Added كُم to show we're addressing multiple people

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion

steps.n: The doubled ب with shadda shows this is someone who completely embodies the quality of nurturing and developing others

steps.m: Lord (as the main subject of ذٰلِكُمُ)

Changed the regular alif (ا) to a long alif (آ) to show it should be pronounced longer.

god, deity (object of لَآ meaning 'there is no')
