steps.m: god, deity (object of لَآ meaning 'there is no')
steps.n: Added alif extension (ٰ) and fatha ending to show it's affected by لَآ
Starting with the two separate words that combine to form this particle: إِنْ (if) and لَا (no/not)
The نْ merges into the ل creating a doubled letter (shown by ّ). The meaning shifts from 'if not' to 'except'.
This is a pronoun that refers back to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah. It emphasizes that Allah alone is being discussed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'one who creates'. This word is connected to كُلِّ (all/every) to mean 'creator of all'.
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject describing Allah in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the first vowel to damma and doubled the last letter to create a word meaning 'all'. This word is describing شَىْءٍ (thing) in this ayah.
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after عَلَىٰ (upon) in the ayah, showing it means 'of all'
The root letters ش-ي-أ are arranged into the basic noun pattern to make شَيْء meaning 'a thing'. When it comes after كُلِّ (every/all), they together mean 'every thing'.
Adding the genitive case ending ٍ (kasratan) shows this word is the object of كُلِّ (of/belonging to)
The tanween kasra (ٍ) was already there - the only change is writing the hamza (ء) with a different letter shape (ى instead of ي). The meaning stays the same.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he worshipped' to a command by adding اُ at start and putting a sukoon (no vowel) at the end
Makes the command plural by adding وا
Adds فَ to show this is a result or sequence
Adds هُ to show who is being worshipped (referring to Allah)
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah mentioned earlier (ٱللَّهُ)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or in charge of something else. In this ayah, it connects with وَكِيلٌ (guardian) to show Allah is guardian over all things.
The doubled ل shows this means absolutely everything. This word describes شَىْءٍ (thing) in this ayah.
Adding kasra (ِ) shows this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, like saying 'of every thing'
Root letters ش-ي-أ get vowels (fatha and sukun) to make the common noun 'thing'
Added tanwin kasra at the end because this word follows كُلِّ (every/all) which makes it possessed. The ي is written as ى when it has a sukun.

steps.m: to take care of or manage something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show someone who does this action as their role

steps.m: a guardian/trustee (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added double damma to show this is describing Allah as a guardian or trustee in this verse
Basic negative particle that tells us something is not happening
Added shadda and fatha to emphasize the negation. This makes the 'no' stronger, like saying 'absolutely not' or 'definitely not'. Here it strongly negates تُدْرِكُهُ (perceive/grasp Him)

steps.m: to reach or grasp (physically or mentally)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding تُ prefix and changing vowels
Added هُ at the end to show who is being reached/grasped. This word is negated by لَّا (not) in the ayah, so the full meaning is 'Vision cannot reach/grasp Him'

Added ٱلْ to make it specific (like saying 'the' in English)
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence. The alif is written as ٰ which is just a different way to write it.

steps.m: he/it (refers to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers to Allah in this context. It's used to emphasize that Allah is the one being discussed.

steps.m: and He (refers to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the start to connect this word with what came before it. This connects the two statements about Allah: that vision cannot encompass Him, and that He encompasses all vision.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form

steps.m: he/it reaches/perceives (subject of the verb that acts upon الْأَبْصٰرَ)

Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to the previous phrase. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يُدْرِكُ (perceives/comprehends) in this ayah.
The root letters ل-ط-ف convey the idea of gentleness and subtlety
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding ي between second and third root letters makes it mean 'one who has the quality of gentleness in an intense way'
Adding ٱلْ with shadda shows this is one of Allah's names, describing Him as having perfect gentleness
Adding damma shows this is the subject doing the action of perceiving (يُدْرِكُ) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: one who is deeply aware (of all things)

Adding ٱلْ makes it definite and changes the ending to ُ. This is one of Allah's names, showing He has complete awareness of everything.
This is a special word that adds certainty to the verb that follows it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the clear proofs have definitely come to you.
The weak letter ي changes to ا, and we add fathas. This word connects to بَصَآئِرُ (clear proofs) which comes after it.
The alif is made longer to emphasize the action of coming
Added كُم at the end to show the action was done to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: to see clearly or understand deeply

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding ي makes it describe someone who has this quality strongly
Adding ة turns it into a feminine noun meaning a specific instance of insight

steps.m: clear insights/proofs (that help you understand the truth)
steps.n: Changed to special plural form that shows multiple strong pieces of evidence or insights. The ُ at the end shows this word is doing something in the sentence.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop
steps.n: Added basic vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: Lord (one who cares for and develops everything)

Changed to 'my Lord' by adding kasra at the end
Added كُمْ to show it belongs to multiple people being addressed
Basic word meaning 'whoever' - used to start conditional statements

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the seeing more clear and direct. The sukun (ْ) is added on ب because it comes after أَ
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added لِ at beginning to show this is for/to something
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from something before it
Added kasra at the end because it's connected to the next word
Added هِۦ at the end to show this belongs to him
This is a basic question/conditional word that means 'whoever'. In this ayah, it's used to talk about any person who might do something.
Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it in the ayah
Added vowels to show this happened in the past. This word connects to فَعَلَيْهَا (then upon him) in the ayah, showing the consequence of becoming blind.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation of the basic word meaning 'upon' or 'against'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before

Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added alif with madda to show long vowel sound
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
This is the basic pronoun used when referring to yourself

Added a small mark (۠) above the final alif to show this is a pronoun, not just any word ending in alif
This is the basic form of the preposition meaning 'upon' or 'over'

Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show someone who does this action intensively

steps.m: as a guardian (of عَلَيْكُم - over you)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to mean 'as/with' and added tanwin kasra at end to show it's indefinite. This connects to عَلَيْكُم (over you) in the ayah to say 'I am not a guardian over you'
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Added لِ and كَ to change 'this' to 'that'. The ٰ (alif) helps with pronunciation.
Added كَ at the beginning to show similarity or comparison

steps.m: and like that (connects to نُصَرِّفُ - we explain in detail)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this phrase to what follows - 'and like that we explain in detail the signs'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled ر shows that the action happens multiple times or with extra effort

steps.m: we explain in detail/change repeatedly (present tense)
steps.n: Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing it, and changed middle vowel to kasra for present tense

steps.m: we explain in detail/change repeatedly (statement of fact)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show this is stating a fact (not a question or command)

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Changed root letters to make a noun meaning one sign or proof. The long آ combines the first two letters.

steps.m: signs or clear proofs (acted upon by نُصَرِّفُ)
steps.n: Added ات to make it plural. This word is what's being explained/detailed by the verb نُصَرِّفُ (we explain in detail).
Added ٱلْ to make it specific - these particular signs being discussed
The word got a kasra (ِ) ending because it's receiving the action of 'we explain' (نُصَرِّفُ). Just a spelling update with ء and ٰ.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و becomes ا in this form.
Changes from past tense (he said) to present tense (he says) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
The suffix وا indicates masculine plural subject (they)
The prefix ل adds the meaning 'so that/in order to'
و connects this clause to what came before

steps.m: to study/learn (especially through reading or practice)

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he studied' by adding the fat-ha marks
Added تَ at the end to change from 'he' to 'you' and put sukun on س because it's now followed by another letter
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show intensive explaining
Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed vowel pattern
Added لِ at start to show purpose - 'so that' or 'in order to'

steps.m: so that we explain it thoroughly
steps.n: Added هُ at end to show what's being explained (it)

steps.m: and so that we explain it thoroughly
steps.n: Added وَ at start to connect this with previous statement
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added لِ at beginning to show this word is receiving the action (in this case, receiving knowledge from the signs mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite (any people) and is followed by the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show this is happening now. The ْ (sukun) shows where to pause briefly.
Added ونَ at the end to show many people (they) are doing this action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added اِتْ at start to show doing the following action by choice/for oneself
Adding shadda (doubling) on ت shows doing the action strongly or repeatedly
Changed from past tense 'he followed' to a command 'you follow!' by changing middle vowel and adding stop mark at end
Added ا (alif) at start to help pronounce word beginning with two consonants
This is a special connecting word that links two parts of a sentence. Here it connects 'follow' with 'has been revealed'
Added vowel mark to show it's pronounced 'ma' - this word points to what should be followed
Changed regular alif (ا) to elongated alif (آ) for emphasis in pronunciation. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to reveal divine knowledge or inspire

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: he caused to be revealed (divine knowledge)
steps.n: Adding أ at the beginning makes it mean 'caused something to happen' instead of just 'happened'

steps.m: it was revealed (as divine inspiration to مَآ)
steps.n: Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it. This word describes what happened to مَآ (what) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination
The ى changes to ي before adding a suffix

steps.m: to you (object of أُوحِىَ - was revealed)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this is directed 'to you'. This word connects to أُوحِىَ (was revealed) in the ayah, showing what was revealed to.

steps.m: from (connects رَبِّكَ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or someone. Here it connects 'your Lord' (رَبِّكَ) to what was revealed before.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures in a complete and perfect way
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is connected to something after it, like saying 'Lord of...'

steps.m: your Lord (the one being addressed in إِلَيْكَ)
steps.n: Added كَ to show this is talking about 'your Lord', connecting to the same 'you' mentioned earlier in إِلَيْكَ (to you)

steps.m: your Lord (the one being addressed in إِلَيْكَ)
steps.n: Added shadda on first letter because it comes after the word مِن
This is a basic negative word that means 'no' or 'not'. It's a tool word that doesn't come from a root.
Added fatha mark to show how to pronounce the first letter.
The alif is elongated to add emphasis to the word. This is common when this word appears at the start of the important phrase 'There is no god except Allah' (لا إله إلا الله).

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changed the first letter from أ to إِ and added vowels to make it a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha at the end. This word is being negated by لَآ (no/there is no) in this ayah.
Basic form of the word that means 'except'
Added shadda and alif to strengthen the exception meaning. In this ayah, it's making an exception saying 'there is no god except Him (هُوَ)'
This is the basic form of the pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it'
Adding وَ makes this an independent pronoun that can stand alone, rather than being attached to another word
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to imperative (command) form by adding alif and changing vowels
Adds و to connect this command with the previous command اتَّبِعْ (follow!) in the ayah
This word shows movement away from something or talking about something
Added kasra at the end to connect it smoothly with the next word ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (the polytheists). This shows we're talking about turning away from the polytheists specifically.

steps.m: to share or associate partners with something

steps.n: Adding vowels makes this a complete past tense verb
The مُ prefix with a sukun on the middle letter creates a noun for someone who does this action
The ين ending makes this plural, showing we're talking about multiple people

steps.m: the polytheists (those who associate partners with Allah)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and fatha at the end because it's the object of the verb أَعْرِضْ (turn away from)

steps.m: and if (connects to previous statement شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this conditional statement with what came before it. This word introduces a hypothetical situation that connects to Allah's will (شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ) in this ayah.

steps.m: he wanted/wished (subject of conditional لَوْ - if)
steps.n: Added short 'a' sounds (fatha) to make it mean 'he wanted' - this comes after the word 'if' (لو) to show what someone wished for
Changed to a hollow verb form by adding a long 'aa' sound in the middle
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of the verb شَآءَ (willed/wished) in this ayah.
This is a relative pronoun that can mean 'what' or 'that which'. No morphological transformation needed as this is a basic particle form.
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation. This word is the subject of the verb أَشْرَكُوا (they associated partners) in this ayah.
Added elongation mark (آ) to show proper pronunciation length. This doesn't change the meaning, just helps with correct recitation.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb form
Adding أ (hamza) at the start changes the meaning to 'make something/someone a partner' instead of just 'being a partner'
The suffix وا makes this a plural masculine past tense verb
This is a negative particle that means 'not'
The و connects this with what came before. This word negates the verb جَعَلْنٰكَ (We made you) in this ayah.
Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb form
Adding sukun prepares the word for attachment of pronouns
The نٰ shows that 'we' did the action

steps.m: we made/appointed you (as guardian over حَفِيظًا)
steps.n: The كَ shows that 'you' is who was appointed. This connects to حَفِيظًا (guardian) later in the ayah to complete the meaning of who you were appointed as.
Form used for feminine superlative (meaning 'highest')
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning

steps.m: upon/over them (object of جَعَلْنٰكَ - we made you)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'them' (referring to the disbelievers). This word shows who the action of 'making' (جَعَلْنٰكَ) affects.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Forms intensive active participle on pattern فَعِيل meaning 'one who guards/protects extensively'
The word gets an -an ending (tanween) because it's being used as an object in the sentence, showing what Allah did not make the Prophet

Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement
The آ is a lengthened form of the alif that adds emphasis to the negation

steps.m: you (when talking to one male person)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun used to directly address one male person. In this ayah, it shows that Allah is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ directly.
This is a preposition showing something being above or on top of something else
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns at the end

steps.m: upon them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in أَشْرَكُوا)

Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb

Added تَ at start and changed first vowel to show present tense for 'you'
Added وا at the end to show plural 'you'. This is a negative command (don't do this!)

steps.m: from (connects دُونِ to what comes before)

Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'beneath/lower'
Added kasra at the end to show connection to the word اللَّهِ that follows it

Added يَ at start and changed middle vowel to ُ to show it's happening now or will happen

Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create the base noun

Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs

This is a preposition showing direction towards their Lord (رَبِّهِم) in this ayah
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a special form of the word used at the end of phrases

Added مَ at the beginning and ِ after ج to show where or when something returns
Adding shadda (double mark) on م makes the meaning stronger
Added هُمْ at the end to show it belongs to them, and ُ to connect the parts. This word shows where they return to their Lord (رَبِّهِم) in this ayah.

Adding shadda on ب makes the meaning more intense and thorough

steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show the relationship between the informing (يُنَبِّئُهُم) and what they used to do

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state/condition)

Added وا to show plural masculine 'they'. This word connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work) in the next ayah, showing what they were doing.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form

steps.m: they do/perform (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show plural masculine 'they'. This refers back to those who insult Allah without knowledge mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made someone swear/take an oath
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Added و to connect this action with what came before it
This is the unique proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is used exclusively as God's name.
Added بِ at beginning to show swearing by something, and ِ at end because it's being acted upon. This word is what they're swearing by in وَأَقْسَمُوا۟ (and they swore).

Added the vowel marks 'fatha' and 'sukun' to make it mean 'effort' instead of 'to make effort'

steps.m: utmost effort (object of وَأَقْسَمُوا - they swore)

Changes from singular 'oath' to plural 'oaths' by adding letters at the start and middle

Added fatha marks and connected letters to make past tense verb
Added extra alif to show emphasis in the coming action
Added ت with sukun to show feminine past tense
Added هُمْ to show the object (them)

Transforms the root into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
The word gets a damma (u sound) at the end to show it's doing the action of 'coming' in this ayah

steps.m: to trust, believe in, have faith
steps.n: Root meaning relates to safety and trust
Root becomes 'he' present tense verb by adding yu- at start and vowels in middle
Adds لَ at start to emphasize the meaning, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
The doubled ل makes the emphasis even stronger, like saying 'most definitely'
Adding نَّ at the end makes the verb super emphatic, like saying 'I REALLY promise'
Added kasra to the preposition ب to make it connect with what follows
Added the feminine pronoun ها which refers back to the sign (ءَايَةٌ) mentioned earlier in the verse
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

Adding ة makes it refer to one specific sign or miracle
Adding ات makes it plural, showing multiple signs or miracles
Adding ال makes it refer to specific signs or miracles being discussed
Adding ُ shows this word is doing something in the sentence - it's the subject that is 'with Allah'
Starting with the basic root letters that express the concept of being present or near something

Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word بِ (by/with) before it. The alif at the start changed to a special connecting form (ٱ).

steps.m: and what (connects to يُشْعِرُكُمْ - makes you perceive)

Added كُمْ at the end to show the action is done to 'you all'
Added fatha and shadda to show emphasis
Added ها at the end to show what is being referred to (it/she)
Lengthened the final vowel for emphasis and pronunciation
This is a fixed word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects to the word جَآءَتْ (it came) to show when something will happen.
Added the vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce it: 'i-dha'
Basic meaning showing the action of coming or arriving

Added a long alif to show emphasis in the coming action
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine. In this ayah, this refers to ءَايَةٌ (sign) doing the action of coming.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) that comes after it, making it mean 'they do not believe'. It's a basic word that doesn't come from a root.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'to trust'

Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added plural ending to show multiple people believing
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Adding shadda shows the action is done multiple times

steps.m: and we turn/change something repeatedly (their hearts and eyes)

steps.m: their hearts (object of وَنُقَلِّبُ - we turn/overturn)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ to show possession and marks the word as the object being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic noun
Changes from single 'sight' to multiple 'sights' by adding أ at start and ا after ص
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect to previous word أَفْـِٔدَتَهُمْ (their hearts)

steps.m: and their sights/visions (object of نُقَلِّبُ - we turn)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ to show possession and َ to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb نُقَلِّبُ (we turn)
This is a comparison particle that shows similarity between things

steps.m: not (changes يُؤْمِنُوا۟ from present to past negative)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يُؤْمِنُوا۟) from present to past negative, like changing 'they believe' to 'they did not believe'

steps.m: to cause to feel secure/to believe
steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they believe (negated by لَمْ before it)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. The word is negated by لَمْ that comes before it in the ayah.
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected

Added small ي (ۦ) after ه to show it should be pronounced clearly and not rushed
Added vertical line (ٓ) to show the sound is stretched when stopping on this word
Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter adds emphasis to the action

steps.m: to pass, move through, or happen

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb
Changed the verb to a noun and added ة to mean 'one time' of doing the action
Added tanween kasra to show this describes أَوَّلَ (first) in the phrase أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ (first time)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the present tense base form
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being left alone
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. Here it connects with طُغْيٰنِهِمْ (their transgression) to show they are 'in' that state.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed the word to show the intense act of transgression itself
Added kasra at the end to show it belongs to/is connected to something else
Added هِمْ at the end to show this belongs to them
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this is happening now
Adding ون shows multiple people are doing this action. This word describes how they are wandering in their transgression (طُغْيٰنِهِمْ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. This word introduces a hypothetical scenario that didn't actually happen.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This shows this hypothetical scenario is being added to previous statements.
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Added نا at the end to show this is about 'we/us'. This word connects to نَزَّلْنَآ (we sent down) in this ayah, showing who did the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows this is done many times or with strength
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added long alif at end to extend the sound
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns

steps.m: to them (the angels and dead mentioned after)

Adding vowels creates a noun for one who has this quality - in this case referring to an angel as a being with divine authority
This is a special plural pattern for supernatural or important beings
Adding ة shows this is a complete group or category
Adding ال makes it refer to all angels as a specific group

steps.m: the angels (as the object being sent down)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end shows angels are receiving the action of being sent down (from نَزَّلْنَآ - we sent down)
Doubling the middle letter shows this is intensive speaking, like having a serious conversation rather than just saying a word
Added 'and' at the beginning

steps.m: and spoke to them (referring to ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةَ - the angels)
steps.n: Added 'them' at the end to show who is being spoken to. In this verse, 'them' refers back to the angels (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةَ) mentioned earlier.
Changed the word to refer to multiple dead things or people by adding special endings

steps.m: the dead (plural, object of وَكَلَّمَهُمُ - spoke to them)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning. This word is receiving the action of speaking in the phrase 'spoke to them'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Adds the subject pronoun نا to indicate 'we' as the doer

steps.m: and we gathered (connected to نَزَّلْنَآ - we sent down)
steps.n: The و connects this action to the previous action of sending down (نَزَّلْنَآ) in the verse
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

steps.m: upon them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'them' (referring to multiple people). In this ayah, it refers back to the disbelievers being discussed.
Added damma and shadda to make a word meaning 'all' or 'every'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by عَلَيْهِمْ (upon them) in the ayah
Root letters combine to make the simple noun meaning 'thing' - one of the most common words in Arabic
Added the tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this thing is being possessed by the word كُلَّ (every/all) that comes before it. The ي is written as ى when it comes in the middle of a word.
The root letters get two 'u' sounds (damma) to make a plural noun meaning things that are in front

steps.m: face to face, directly before them (حَشَرْنَا - we gathered)
steps.n: The tanween alif shows this describes how things were gathered - directly in front of them, face to face. This relates to how Allah is saying even if everything was gathered right in front of them, they still wouldn't believe.
This is a negative particle that makes something not happen. Here it's saying they were not going to believe.
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes right after نَّ, making the two sounds blend together - like saying 'n-m' really fast
Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to لِيُؤْمِنُوا (for them to believe) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at start. The أ becomes ؤ with a sukun mark.
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: so that they believe (purpose of ما كانوا)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why they wouldn't do something mentioned in ما كانوا (they would not)
This is a basic particle that shows an exception. It's like saying 'but' or 'except for' in English.
The long alif (آ) at the end is a Quranic spelling variant that shows this word connects to the next word يَشَآءَ (He wills). Together they mean 'except that He wills'.
This is a special word that connects two ideas together. Here it introduces what Allah might will/wish (يَشَآءَ). It's like saying 'except that Allah wills it'.
Added fatha vowels and the middle letter ي changed to ا because it's a special 'weak' letter that often changes
Added يَ at the beginning to show this is happening in the present. This word comes after إِلَّآ أَن (except that) in the verse.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of يَشَآءَ (wills/wishes) in this ayah.
Added shadda and fatha at the end to make the word stronger. This emphasizes that what comes after is different from what came before.

steps.m: and yet/but still (connects to previous statement)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it. This shows that even though angels and dead people spoke to them (mentioned in previous verse), they still wouldn't believe.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form meaning 'more/most'
Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them'. This word is the subject of يَجْهَلُونَ (they are ignorant) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Starting with the demonstrative pronoun ذا (this/that) and adding لِ to make it more specific
Adding كَ at the end makes the word more specific and emphatic
Adding كَ at the beginning changes it to mean 'like that' instead of just 'that'
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Adding the vowel marks (fathas) turns the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he made'
Adding نا changes 'he' to 'we' and shows we did this action. This connects back to وَكَذٰلِكَ (and thus) at the start of the verse.
Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to make it mean 'all' or 'every'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is being acted upon by something
Added لِ at the beginning to mean 'for/to'. This word connects to نَبِىٍّ (prophet) in the ayah, showing 'for every prophet'
The word becomes a noun meaning the news or information itself
Root letters got vowels to make it mean 'important news' instead of 'to bring news'
Changed the middle vowel and added ي to show someone who does this action as their role

steps.m: prophet (someone who regularly brings divine news)
steps.n: Added shadda to show this is their main role or profession

steps.m: a prophet (object of لِكُلِّ - for every)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this word is being acted upon by the word لِكُلِّ (for every)
Added vowels to make the root into a noun meaning 'enemy'

steps.m: an enemy (object of جَعَلْنَا - we made)
steps.n: Added ًّا at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb 'we made' (جَعَلْنَا)
The root relates to being distant or remote from good
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: one who is far from good (satan/devil)
steps.n: Adds yaa and alif to create noun for someone/something characterized by this quality
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple devils
Changes from dual form (two devils) to plural form (many devils) and shows it's being acted upon
Changed the root into a noun meaning humans, who are social beings that live together in communities
Added ال to make it specific (the humans) and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word شَيٰطِينَ (devils) in the phrase 'devils of mankind and jinn'
The root relates to something being hidden or concealed
Added kasra and shadda to make it mean a type of being that is hidden from human sight
Added ال to specify we're talking about these specific beings
Added و to connect this word to شَيٰطِينَ ٱلْإِنسِ (devils of mankind) mentioned before it
Adding the coordinating conjunction و connects this to the previous phrase

steps.m: to reveal or inspire something secret/divine
steps.n: The action becomes a noun meaning the revelation itself
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ (kasra). The و at the start of the root stays but moves after يُ
Added ى (alif maqsura) at the end to complete the present tense word
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the basic noun
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of يُوحِى (inspires/suggests) in this ayah

steps.m: some of them (as subject of يُوحِى)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them' (the devils of mankind and jinn mentioned earlier in the ayah)
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects 'some of them' (بَعْضُهُمْ) to 'others' (بَعْضٍ), showing who the whispering is directed to.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end, which is just a writing style that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to divide or separate into parts
steps.n: Changed from verb to noun meaning 'some' or 'part'
Makes it into a common noun meaning 'some' or 'part'
Added a kasra sound (-i) at the end to show this word is connected to 'them' (like saying 'some of them')

steps.m: to beautify or decorate with false attractiveness
steps.n: The root relates to decoration or embellishment
Added vowel marks to transform the root into a noun meaning the result of decorating deceptively
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being whispered. It connects to يُوحِى (they whisper) in the ayah.

Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it's being acted upon by the word يُوحِى (inspires/suggests) in this ayah

Adding the ُ marks makes it a plural noun meaning multiple deceptions
Added و between second and third root letters to intensify the meaning
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is what is being whispered/inspired between them

steps.m: if (hypothetical, showing something that didn't happen)
steps.n: This is a basic conditional word that introduces situations that didn't actually happen. It's like saying 'if something had happened' in English.

The weak letter changes cause hamza to move to end with alif
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb

In this ayah, مَا is being used as a negative particle that negates the verb يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent/fabricate). The phrase وَمَا يَفْتَرُونَ means 'and what they fabricate' or 'and their fabrications'.
This is the basic past tense form
Adding vowels to make it past tense 'he did'

Adding هُ shows what they did (the object)

Added ِ at the end to show this is the object of the verb أَبْتَغِى (I seek). Also changed initial alif to waṣla form since it connects to previous word.

Added أ at start and ت after first letter to show 'I' am doing the action for myself
Added a stop mark (sukoon) at the end to show the word ends here

This is a pronoun without a root. It's used to refer to a male person or thing. The ُ shows it's 'he' and the َ helps connect it to other words.

This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something

The ل merges with ذ creating a doubled sound (shown by ّ), and the initial alif gets a special mark (ٱ)

Adding أَ at beginning makes the meaning causative - showing Allah made something descend rather than descending Himself
Basic preposition indicating direction or destination
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to a pronoun
Added the plural 'you' pronoun كُمُ to show who the action is directed towards
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb

This is the base relative pronoun that refers to a single person or thing

Adding و at the beginning connects this word to what came before it in the sentence
Changed to past tense by adding long 'aa' sound at start and changing ending to make 'aataa' (gave/brought)

steps.m: we gave them (referring to those who were given the Book)

Changed the pattern to create a noun meaning 'that which is written'. This word is the object of أَنزَلَ (he sent down) in this ayah.

steps.m: the book, the scripture (object of أَنزَلَ - he sent down)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this is the object being acted upon. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this Quranic spelling.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he knew) to present tense (he knows) by adding يَ at the start
Changes the verb from singular (he knows) to plural (they know)
This is a connecting word that adds certainty to what follows. It connects to the word مُنَزَّلٌ (revealed) to tell us what is definitely revealed.
Adds 'it' (masculine) to show what is being revealed. This word works with مُنَزَّلٌ (revealed) to tell us 'that it is revealed'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows the action happened many times

steps.m: something that was sent down repeatedly

steps.m: to nurture and develop something to its completion

When مِن comes before رَبّ, the ر gets a shadda to emphasize the meaning, like saying 'truly the master'
Adding kasra shows someone owns or possesses this master/lord (like saying 'my lord' or 'lord of')
Adding كَ shows this is your Lord. This whole phrase مِن رَّبِّكَ means 'from your Lord'
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'truth'
Added ال to make it specific - this is the absolute truth, not just any truth
Added بِ to show this truth is being used as a tool or method (in this ayah, it refers to how Allah sent down the Book with/by the truth)

steps.m: with/by the truth (connected to أَنزَلَ/sent down)
steps.n: Added kasra because بِٱلْحَقِّ is connected to the verb أَنزَلَ (sent down) in this ayah, showing how the Book was sent down
This is a negative particle that makes something not happen

steps.m: so not (connects to يَعْلَمُونَ - they know)

Changed root letters into a noun form by adding vowels (damma/u sounds)
Added تَ to show this is about 'you' in the present tense
Changed the ending vowel to fatha to show this is in the subjunctive mood

steps.m: you will definitely be (negative command in context of فَلَا)

This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word (المُمْتَرِينَ). This is a common change in Arabic to make words flow better when speaking.

steps.m: to doubt or be uncertain about something

Added مُ at start and ت between first and second root letters to show someone doing the action to themselves
Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than one person)
Added ٱلْ at beginning to make it specific and َ at end to show it follows مِنَ (from/of)

steps.m: to complete or finish something fully

steps.n: Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show completeness. This word describes what happened to كَلِمَتُ (word) in this ayah.
Added ت with sukun at the end to show this happened to something feminine (the word كَلِمَتُ)

steps.m: and it (feminine) was completed perfectly (describing كَلِمَتُ)
steps.n: Added و as a conjunction
Changed root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels a-i-a
Changes the verb into a noun meaning one single word or unit of speech
Added damma to show this word is doing the action in the sentence - it's the subject of the verb تَمَّتْ (was completed)

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show complete mastery and care
Added kasra under the doubled letter to show this word is owned by something
Added كَ at the end to show it means 'your Lord'. This word belongs to كَلِمَتُ (word) in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter

steps.m: truthfully (describes how كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ was completed)

Added tanween alif to make it an adverb (describing how something happened) instead of just the concept of justice
Added و to connect this with the previous word صِدْقًا (truthfully), showing Allah's word was completed both truthfully AND justly
This is a basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
The shadda adds emphasis to show there is absolutely no one who can change Allah's words. This لَّا is directly negating مُبَدِّلَ (changer) in this ayah.
The doubled middle letter shows doing something intensively or repeatedly

steps.m: one who changes/alters (something) extensively (negated by لَا)
steps.n: Added مُ at the start and changed vowels to show who does the action. This word is negated by لَا (no) in the ayah, meaning 'there is no one who can change'
Added vowels and ة to make it mean one single word

Added و to make it plural - means 'they' instead of 'he'
Adds purpose/reason meaning 'in order to'

steps.m: so that they argue with you all
steps.n: Adds كُمْ to show the people being argued with (you all)

steps.m: if (introduces a condition about obeying them)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces the 'if' part of the statement. The 'then' part that follows implies the result of obeying them would be shirk (associating partners with Allah).

steps.m: and if (connects this condition to previous warnings)
steps.n: The و (and) connects this condition to the previous warnings about not eating meat without Allah's name and following the devils' whispers
Changed from root letters to past tense verb 'obeyed' by adding vowels
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past
Added مْ (meem) at the end to change from 'I/you obeyed' to 'you all obeyed' - like changing from one person to a group
Added وهم at the end to show 'them' as the ones being obeyed
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
Added كُمْ to show this is addressing multiple people. This word connects to لَمُشْرِكُونَ (are associating partners) in the next ayah, showing who is doing the action.
Added مُ at the start and changed vowels to make it mean 'someone who does shirk (associates partners with Allah)'

steps.m: surely/indeed one who associates partners (with Allah)
steps.n: Added لَ at beginning for emphasis, showing strong certainty about the statement

steps.m: surely they are those who associate partners (with Allah)
steps.n: Added ون to show this refers to multiple people (masculine plural)
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Adding وَ connects this question to what came before it
Adding مَن refers to a person or people being asked about
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is a common change with hollow verbs
Changes from verb 'to die' into adjective 'dead'
Added ًا at the end because this word is being acted upon by كَانَ (was). In the ayah, it's saying 'whoever was dead'.
Adding vowels turns the root into a proper past tense verb meaning 'to live'
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'cause to live' instead of just 'live'
Adding نا shows 'we' did the action

steps.m: and so we gave life to
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this action follows from something before it

steps.m: and so we gave life to him (object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows who received the action. This word describes what happened to the subject of كَانَ (was) in the ayah.

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did this action. The ْ (sukun) was added on ل because you can't have two vowels in a row.

steps.m: and we made (something into something else) (connected to فَأَحْيَيْنٰهُ - then We gave him life)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this with the previous action of giving life
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the letter lam
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the action is for

steps.m: for him (connected to نُورًا - a light)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) to show proper pronunciation. This word connects to نُورًا (a light) in the ayah, showing that the light is for him.
Changed the action of shining into the thing that shines

steps.m: a light (that We made for him)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the thing being made in the phrase 'We made for him a light.' The ًا shows both that it's 'a' light (not 'the' light) and that it's being acted upon.

steps.m: to walk or move on foot

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the past tense form

steps.m: he walks/is walking (through the light mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and final vowel to kasra to match the present tense pattern. This describes someone walking with the light mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing how something is done
Added kasra (ِ) to help connect with next word

steps.m: with him/it (referring to نُورًا - the light)
steps.n: Added pronoun ه (him/it) to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the نُورًا (light) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: with him/it (referring to نُورًا - the light)
steps.n: Added small ۦ after ه which is just a pronunciation helper mark that doesn't change the meaning
This is one of the basic prepositions in Arabic that shows location or position. In this ayah, it's used to show that something is 'in' or 'within' darkness (الظُّلُمٰتِ).
The ى is added at the end to complete the word. This is a spelling feature of Arabic writing that helps show it's a preposition.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: The initial hamza (أ) drops when adding ال in the next step
Added ال (the) at the beginning which causes the ن to double. Added kasra at the end because this word follows the preposition في (in/among)
This is the comparison particle ك that shows similarity between things
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: like whoever/like one who (compared to the guided person mentioned before)
steps.n: Added من (whoever/one who) to specify who is being compared. This creates a comparison between two types of people in the ayah - the guided person mentioned before versus whoever is being described after.
Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'example' by adding the vowel marks a-a
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the verb ليس (is not) in this ayah
Added هُۥ to show the example belongs to him
The shadda adds extra emphasis to the word
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else
The ى at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it. In this ayah, it shows someone is among or within a group of people.
Root letters combined into a simple noun meaning darkness
Changes from one darkness to many darknesses by changing middle letter from sukoon to dammah
Changes from broken plural to regular feminine plural by adding -aat ending
Adds 'the' (ال) to the word and doubles the first letter
Shows this word follows the preposition فِى (in) in the ayah
This is the basic form of this special negation verb. Unlike most Arabic words, it doesn't come from a typical three-letter root that can make different forms.
Adding vowel marks to show it's a past tense verb meaning 'is not'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added alif after first root letter to create active participle

steps.m: able to exit from (مِنْهَا - from it)
steps.n: The letter ب was added at the start to work with لَيْسَ (is not), changing 'one who exits' to 'able to exit'
This is a preposition indicating source or origin
Added emphasis to the م with shadda for pronunciation after connecting with previous word
Added هَا which refers back to الظُّلُمٰتِ (darkness) mentioned earlier in the ayah, showing what is being come out from
Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا (this). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this word.
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ completes the demonstrative word 'that'

steps.m: like that (connects to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the beginning changes it to mean 'like that' or 'similarly'. This connects this part of the ayah to what was mentioned before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice to show the action was done to something. In this ayah, it refers to how their deeds were made to seem attractive to the disbelievers.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from an action to describing someone who does that action
Makes it refer to many people who reject
Makes it refer to specific rejecters rather than any rejecters
The 'li' (لِ) prefix means 'for/to' - showing something is being directed at these rejecters
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the following phrase 'they used to do', creating the meaning 'that which they used to do'. Adding fatha vowel mark for pronunciation.
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha (َ) completes the basic past tense verb pattern
Changed كَوَن to كَانُوا by adding وا at the end to show 'they' (multiple people) were doing something. Works with يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work) in this ayah.
Basic past tense verb meaning 'he did' - no prefix needed
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a demonstrative compound made from ك (like) and ذا (this/that) with ل added for emphasis
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. The ٰ (alif) shows elongation of the sound.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what follows (جَعَلْنَا - We made/placed). This shows the connection between how something was done before and how it's being done now.

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set up

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to connect the 3 root letters, making it a past tense verb meaning 'he made/put'

steps.m: we made/put/set up (main verb that connects to أَكٰبِرَ)
steps.n: Adding نا changes the doer from 'he' to 'we'. This word is the main verb that connects to أَكٰبِرَ (greatest ones) showing what we did to them.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows the location within a town/city.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Added damma and shadda to make it into a word meaning 'every' or 'all'
Added kasra to show this word is connected to قَرْيَةٍ (town/village) that comes after it, meaning 'of every town'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
The ة shows this is a specific place where the action happens

steps.m: of a town/village (possessed by كُلِّ - every)
steps.n: The ٍ shows this town belongs to or is connected to something (in this case, it's possessed by كُلِّ meaning 'every')
Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels
Added أَ at the beginning to show comparison, like saying 'more' or '-er' in English
Changed the word pattern to show we're talking about multiple important people
Added َ at the end to show this word is being affected by the verb جَعَلْنَا (We made)

steps.m: to do wrong or commit a crime

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action
Added ي to show possession or belonging
Added هَا to show these wrongdoers belong to the town mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: so that they may plot/scheme (purpose of جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why something was done in جَعَلْنَا (we made/placed)
This is a preposition that means 'in' or 'within'
Added ها which refers back to the town/village (قَرْيَةٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is a negative particle that negates the verb that comes after it (يَشْعُرُونَ meaning 'they perceive')
Adding و connects this clause to the previous statement, showing these actions happen together
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it past tense
Adding ي at the start and changing the vowels makes it mean 'he plots' (present) instead of 'he plotted' (past)
This word is connected to يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive) in the ayah, showing the same group is doing both actions
This is a basic exception particle that starts with إِ and has a doubled ل (shown by the shadda)
The ا at the end is actually part of the complete word إِلَّا - it's not an addition. إِلَّا is the standard word meaning 'except'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a noun
This is a broken plural form that changes the word's structure to show multiple selves/souls
Added هِمْ to show these are their own selves

steps.m: against their own selves (subject of يَمْكُرُونَ)
steps.n: Added بِ to show they are plotting against their own selves. This word shows who is affected by the plotting (يَمْكُرُونَ) in this ayah.
This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. Here it's used to negate the action in the sentence.
The و at the start connects this negation to the previous statement, showing these ideas are linked together
This is the basic meaning that all words from this root share
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels to ُ
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition

steps.m: and when (introduces a condition in جَآءَتْهُمْ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This word introduces the condition that is completed by جَآءَتْهُمْ (came to them).
The ي changes to ا with a special mark (آ) because it's a weak letter. Added vowels to show past tense.
Added ت to match with ءَايَةٌ (sign) which is feminine in Arabic
Added هُمْ to show who received the sign
Changed the root letters to make a noun meaning 'sign' or 'miracle' - something that shows or proves something
Added ة to make it a specific instance of showing - a sign
Added tanween dammah to show this is the subject doing the action in جَآءَتْ (it came)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
This is a complete negative particle that makes the verb after it negative and puts it in the future tense. Here it negates نُّؤْمِنَ (we will believe) to mean 'we will not believe'.

steps.m: to trust or feel secure in something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start and changing vowels
Added a double ن sound (shadda) at start and 'ah' sound (fatha) at end because it follows لَن which means 'never' - this makes it negative
Added vowel marks and shadda to show this is a preposition indicating a limit or endpoint
Added the alif maqsura (ىٰ) at the end which is an integral part of this preposition. This word connects to نُؤْتَىٰ (we are given) in the ayah, showing until what point they won't believe.

steps.m: to give, to bring, to come

steps.n: Changed to 'was given' by adding u-vowel at start and i-vowel in middle (passive form)
The نُ at the start shows 'we' are receiving something (being given), instead of someone else receiving it
The ى with alif (ىٰ) at the end shows this is something happening to us (we receive/are given something)

steps.m: similar (to what رُسُلُ ٱللَّهِ - the messengers of Allah - were given)
steps.n: Creates a noun meaning 'similar' or 'like' from the basic idea of comparing things. In this ayah, it refers to what the disbelievers are demanding - they want to be given something similar to what Allah's messengers were given.

steps.m: similar (to what رُسُلُ ٱللَّهِ - the messengers of Allah - were given)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by نُؤْتَىٰ (we are given) from earlier in the ayah
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb أُوتِىَ (was given) in this ayah, asking about what was given.
The alif is written with a special elongation mark (آ) instead of regular alif (ا). This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the letters together to form the base word
Changed to passive form - shows someone received something rather than did something
Added و after the first letter because of pronunciation rules with weak letters
Changed the ending to show this verb connects to the word مِثْلَ (meaning 'like/similar to') that comes after it
Changed the pattern to show someone who does the action of sending/delivering
Changed vowels to show multiple messengers. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject of the verb أُوتِىَ (was given) in this ayah.
Changed to the proper name for God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and added shadda on ل. This word shows who the messengers (رُسُلُ) belong to.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَجْعَلُ - He knows)
steps.n: Added ُ to show Allah is the subject doing the action in this sentence. The ٱ is just a different way of writing the same sound as ا.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to the superlative form to show Allah is the most knowing of all. This word is describing Allah in this ayah.
This word is used to talk about a place or location

steps.m: where (shows location in relation to رِسَالَتَهُۥ - His message)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show this is talking about where something happens. In this ayah, it's showing where Allah places His message.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb form
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Same word - present tense verb meaning 'he makes/puts'
Changed from verb 'to send' into noun 'message' by adding kasra (ِ) and alif (ا) in the middle
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific message

steps.m: message (ready to be connected to something)
steps.n: Changed the ending to prepare for connecting to 'his'
Added هُۥ meaning 'his'. This word is what receives the action of يَجْعَلُ (he places/puts) in the verse.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he will hit/strike (the evildoers mentioned in ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟)
steps.n: Added سَ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future. This refers to the punishment that will strike the evildoers mentioned right after this word.
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people

steps.m: those who (will be afflicted with humiliation)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning to make it specific and point to particular people. Also added shadda on ل and fatha at end to connect it with what follows. This word points to the wrongdoers mentioned in the next part of the ayah.

steps.m: to do wrong or commit crimes

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to change from 'doing wrong' to 'committing crimes' - makes the action more serious
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it into a past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to make it into a noun meaning the state of being small, which in this context means humiliation
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb سَيُصِيبُ (will afflict/strike) in this ayah
This is a special word used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'near' or 'with' someone
Adding vowel marks to create the preposition. In this ayah, it's showing that something is 'with Allah' or 'from Allah'
Changed to the unique name for The One God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and added shadda and hamza-wasl for proper pronunciation. This word shows who the messengers belong to in رُسُلُ ٱللَّهِ (messengers of Allah).
Added alif to make it into a noun meaning the result of punishment
Added double dammah to make it indefinite (a/any punishment)

steps.m: and a punishment (connected to صَغَارٌ/humiliation mentioned before)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this punishment with the humiliation mentioned before it in the ayah
Added vowels and ي to make it into a describing word showing a strong quality
Added ٌ to show this is describing the punishment (عَذَابٌ) mentioned in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows how or why something happens

steps.m: because of what (they were plotting يَمْكُرُونَ)
steps.n: Adding مَا changes the meaning to refer to 'what' they were doing. This connects to the verb يَمْكُرُونَ (they were plotting) in the ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added vowel marks to make 'he plotted' in the past tense
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from singular to plural by adding ون at the end
This is a question word that can mean 'who' or 'whoever' depending on context. Here it means 'whoever' because it's introducing a conditional statement about anyone who fits the description.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. It connects this part to the previous statement.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Added يُ at start to show present tense. The middle weak letter و is dropped. The word is the subject of يَشْرَحْ (he expands) in this conditional sentence.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added ال (the) at the beginning and doubled the ل with a shadda to show this is THE one true God. The ه at the end gets a ُ because this word is doing the action in the sentence.

This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence together. Here it connects Allah's will (يُرِدِ) with the guidance (يَهْدِيَهُۥ), showing what Allah wants to do - like saying 'Allah wants that He guide him.'
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to sukun and kasra, and final ى becomes ي

steps.m: he guides him (object of يُرِدِ - he wills)
steps.n: Added َهُۥ at the end to show who is being guided. This word is being acted on by يُرِدِ (he wills) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense and added sukun at end because it's in a conditional sentence after مَن (whoever). This word is followed by صَدْرَهُۥ (his chest) in the ayah.
Basic noun form showing a physical body part
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَشْرَحْ (he expands)

steps.m: his chest (being acted upon by يَشْرَحْ - he expands)
steps.n: Added هُ with elongation mark ۥ at the end to show possession - 'his chest'

steps.m: to submit peacefully, be at peace

steps.n: Added أ at beginning to make it mean 'to cause oneself to submit/surrender' - like choosing to give yourself to Allah's guidance
Changed verb into noun showing the act of submission
Added ال to make it specific, referring to the religion of Islam
Added لِ to show purpose/direction and kasra at end because it follows لِ. This word shows what Allah opens the chest for in the phrase 'opens his chest for Islam'.
This is a question word that became a conditional word meaning 'whoever'. It's the subject of the verb يُرِدْ (wants/desires) in this ayah.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this clause with the previous one, showing contrast between two types of people - those Allah wants to guide versus those He doesn't.
يُ at the start shows this is happening now

steps.m: he wants/desires (in a conditional phrase with مَن - whoever)
steps.n: The و drops out and we add a ْ because this is part of a conditional sentence starting with مَن (whoever). This makes the verb shorter to show it depends on another action.
This is a particle that connects two clauses and shows that Allah wants something to happen. It connects يُرِدِ (wants) with يَهْدِيَهُۥ (to guide him).
Added fatha mark (َ) on أ to show correct pronunciation
Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb
Added يُ at start and doubled the middle letter ل with شدة to change from 'going astray' to 'making others go astray'

steps.m: he leads him astray (object of أَن)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who is being led astray. This word comes after أَن (that) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense form
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, and changed vowels to show it's happening now

steps.m: he makes/sets (in response to يُرِدْ - he wills)
steps.n: Changed the ending to sukun because this verb follows another verb (يُرِدْ) in a conditional statement - when one thing happens, the other follows
Changes from verb root to concrete noun meaning chest/breast
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَشْرَحْ (he expands/opens)
Added هُۥ at the end to show the chest belongs to him. This is referring to whoever Allah wants to guide.
Root letters got vowels to make a describing word meaning 'narrow'
The doubled ي (shadda) intensifies the meaning
Adding tanween fatHa shows this word is describing 'his chest' (صَدْرَهُۥ) in the ayah
Adding vowel marks creates basic past tense verb meaning 'he became tight/restricted'
The word changed from a verb (doing something) to a describing word with tanween, showing how tight/narrow the chest becomes. Like changing 'tighten' to 'tight'.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added أَنَّ to create an emphatic comparison particle. The shadda (ّ) emphasizes the 'n' sound.

steps.m: as if, as though (describing how someone's chest feels tight when climbing high in يَصَّعَّدُ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ)
steps.n: Adding ما makes the comparison hypothetical, connecting to how the chest feels metaphorically tight like climbing up into the sky
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Doubling the middle letter shows the action is done with extra effort or difficulty
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end

steps.m: he struggles to climb upward (in response to ضَيِّقًا حَرَجًا - constricted and tight)
steps.n: Doubled the first root letter too, showing even more difficulty. This connects to the previous words describing the chest being constricted and tight.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. Here it connects with السَّمَآءِ to show location in the sky.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb 'to rise' into a noun 'sky' by adding a long aa sound and hamza at the end
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in/into). The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's a long vowel.
This is a compound word made from four parts: ك (like) + ذا (that) + ل (to) + ك (you)
Combined كَ (like) with ذا (that) to make one word meaning 'like that'
Changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) which is a common spelling variation

steps.m: like that, similarly (describes يَجْعَلُ - He makes)
steps.n: Added لِكَ to complete the demonstrative expression. This word describes how Allah makes something happen in the next part of the verse (يَجْعَلُ).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense form
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows ongoing action by Allah in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun form meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'

Changed to the unique name of The One True God. The ال here is not just 'the' - it makes this word specifically refer to Allah. The doubled ل (with shadda) and the ه at the end make this a unique word different from regular words for 'god'.
Added kasra and sukun to form the basic noun
Added ال to make it specific and shadda on ر because it comes after ال
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يَجْعَلُ (he makes/places)
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. In this ayah, it connects to 'the non-believers' (ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ) that comes after it.
This is the basic demonstrative word that points to something
Changed from pointing at something ('this') to connecting words together ('who/which')
Added ن to make it refer to many people instead of just one
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the ones who' instead of just 'ones who'
Added vowel marks and changed the writing style of alif to make it easier to read
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) that comes after it, changing its meaning to 'they do not believe'. It's a basic word that doesn't come from a root.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding alif at start changes meaning from just feeling secure to having religious faith
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a demonstrative word used to point to something nearby
Added the elongated alif (ٰ) and regular alif to complete the demonstrative word. The elongated alif helps extend the sound.
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This word is pointing to the 'path' (صِرٰطُ) mentioned right after it.
Adding the basic vowels to make it readable
Added alif with overbar (ٰ) to show elongated pronunciation of the 'a' sound
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence. This path is being described by the word مُسْتَقِيمًا (straight) that comes after it.

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who really takes care of others completely

steps.m: master who cares for others (possessed by صِرٰطُ)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this belongs to the word صِرٰطُ (path), making it mean 'path of the master'

steps.m: your master who cares for others (possessed by صِرٰطُ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is your master. The whole phrase صِرٰطُ رَبِّكَ means 'the path of your master'
Added مُسْتَ at the beginning to show seeking or striving for the quality. The middle letter و changed to ي because it's between two kasras.
Added ًا (tanween fatha) at the end to show this word is describing how the path is. In this ayah, it's telling us that 'this is your Lord's path' and this word tells us the path is straight.
This is a word that adds emphasis to verbs
Added the vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. In this ayah, قَدْ emphasizes the verb فَصَّلْنَا (We have explained in detail), making it stronger like saying 'We have indeed explained in detail'
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something thoroughly or with extra care

steps.m: we explained thoroughly (subject of the verb, referring to Allah)
steps.n: Adding نا shows Allah referring to Himself as 'We' - a royal/majestic plural common in the Quran. The ْ (sukun) was added before نا according to Arabic grammar rules.

steps.m: to show, make clear, be apparent

steps.n: Changes the concept of showing/making clear into a concrete thing that shows or makes something clear

steps.m: signs, miracles (object of فَصَّلْنَا - we have explained)
steps.n: Makes the word plural to show multiple signs. This word is being acted upon by the verb فَصَّلْنَا (we have explained) in this ayah.
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'

steps.m: of the signs, of the miracles
steps.n: Shows this word is possessed by or connected to something else

steps.m: to stand firmly, rise up, establish

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning a unified group or community that stands together. This is the basic meaning before any case endings.
Added preposition prefix ل meaning 'for/to'

Added tanwin (double kasra) to show this is indefinite (any people/community). This word is connected to يَذَّكَّرُونَ (they remember/take heed) in the ayah, describing which people are being referred to.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added ت at start and doubled middle letter to show deep, personal remembering
Changed to present tense by replacing ت with ي at start and adding ُ at end
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added fatha to show the basic pronunciation of the preposition ل
Added the pronoun هم (them) to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to دَارُ ٱلسَّلٰمِ (home of peace) in the ayah, showing they possess or have this home.

steps.m: to move around or stay in a place

steps.n: Adding the fatha vowel mark helps create the noun pattern
Added ُ at the end to show this is the main subject being described by السَّلٰمِ (peace). Together they form 'home of peace'.
Root letters س-ل-م became the noun سَلَم by adding the short 'a' vowels (fatHa), changing it from 'peace/safety' as a concept to the noun 'peace'
Added alif to intensify the meaning of peace
Added the definite article ال and doubled the س with shadda

steps.m: of the complete peace (possessed by دَارُ/abode)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by دَارُ (abode), forming 'abode of peace'. Also changed the alif to alif maqsurah (ٰ) which is a common spelling variation.
Adding the vowels changes this from a basic concept of nearness into a word that shows location or presence near something. In this ayah, it shows the location 'with/near their Lord'.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop something to its full potential
Lord (the one who nurtures and develops everything)
steps.n: The doubled ب (shown by ّ) makes this word mean someone who does this caring and nurturing perfectly
The ِ shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else that comes after it

steps.m: their Lord (referring to those who will have the home of peace)
steps.n: Adding هِمْ shows this Lord belongs to them. This connects back to the people mentioned earlier who will have دَارُ ٱلسَّلٰمِ (the home of peace)
This is the basic pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it's the subject of وَلِيُّهُم (their protector) which comes right after it.

steps.m: and he (subject of وَلِيُّهُم - their protector)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects to the previous statement about 'they have the home of peace with their Lord'.

steps.m: to protect or be close to

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
