Added ُّ (shadda and damma) to show this is the one doing the protecting
Added هُم at the end to show who the protector belongs to
This is the basic word meaning 'what' that can be used to ask questions or connect ideas
Added بِ at the beginning to show this word connects the people's actions (يَعْمَلُونَ - they used to do) with their reward mentioned before
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense. This word is connected to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work) in this ayah.
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Added وا at the end to change 'he was' to 'they were', showing multiple people did the action. This group is doing the action in يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start and changing vowels shows this is happening now or in the future
Adding ونَ shows many people are doing this. This word describes what they were doing in the past (كَانُوا)
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make a proper word
Added fatha at the end to show this word is affected by something else in the sentence

steps.m: and on the day (when يَحْشُرُهُمْ/He will gather them)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this to previous statements. This word is connected to يَحْشُرُهُمْ (He will gather them) which comes right after it.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changes to present tense with prefix ي showing 'he'

steps.m: he gathers them (referring to the jinn mentioned later in the ayah)
steps.n: The pronoun 'them' refers to the jinn who are mentioned right after this word in the ayah

steps.m: to gather, collect, or bring together

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a quality or state of being gathered, not just the action

steps.m: all together (describing how Allah will gather them)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this describes how the gathering happens in يَحْشُرُهُمْ (He will gather them)
Added مَ at the beginning to make a noun meaning 'group of people gathered together'
Added fatha at the end because this word is being directly addressed
Added يٰ at the beginning to show we're calling out to this group. This word is followed by الْجِنِّ (the jinn) to specify which group is being addressed.

steps.m: to hide or conceal from sight

steps.n: The doubling of ن (shown by ّ) makes this word mean the beings themselves rather than just the action of hiding

steps.m: the jinn (specific group of spiritual beings)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ at the beginning makes it refer to jinn as a specific group rather than just any jinn
The ِ at the end shows this word belongs to or is possessed by the word يٰمَعْشَرَ (group/assembly) that comes before it, making it mean 'group of the jinn'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to the verb that follows it (ٱسْتَكْثَرْتُم). It shows that something definitely happened.
The sukun (ْ) changes to kasra (ِ) because it comes before a word starting with a connecting hamza. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he sought to make something increase
steps.n: The است prefix shows someone is seeking or considering something

steps.m: I sought to make something increase
steps.n: The ت suffix indicates first person singular past tense

steps.m: you all sought to make something increase
steps.n: The م suffix changes the subject from 'I' to 'you all'
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'. It connects two words to show where something comes from or what it belongs to.
The shadda adds emphasis to show a stronger connection
The fatha at the end connects this word to the next word الْإِنسِ (humans/mankind), showing what group is being referred to

steps.m: to be close or familiar with others

steps.n: Changed the root word into a noun meaning 'humans' by adding إِ at the start - this is a common pattern for making nouns from roots
Added ال to specify we're talking about humankind as a whole group
Added kasra at the end to show this word describes where their allies are from in the phrase 'their allies from humankind'
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he said'
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing weak letter و with ا

steps.m: and he said (after يٰمَعْشَرَ ٱلْجِنِّ - O company of jinn)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to what came before. This word comes after Allah addresses the jinn, showing that someone else is now speaking.

steps.m: to be close to and support

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed from single protector to multiple protectors by making it plural (like changing 'friend' to 'friends')
Added damma to show this word is doing the action (like adding 'the' in English)
Added هُم (meaning 'their') at the end to show possession/ownership
This is a common preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it's showing that many jinn came from among mankind.
The م is doubled (has a shadda) because it comes before the word ٱلْإِنسِ (mankind). This is a common rule in Arabic when مِن comes before certain letters.
This root relates to being social and friendly with others
Changes the root into a noun meaning 'humans' - this word was chosen because humans are social creatures
Adds kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from) which requires this ending
Adding ال makes it refer to all humans as a group, not just some humans
Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'he did something'
The root رَبَبَ (nurture/raise) becomes رَبّ (Lord) first, then نَا (our) is added at the end to make 'Our Lord'. Used when calling out to Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added ٱسْتَ at the beginning to show seeking or wanting the benefit/enjoyment. This form is used when someone actively tries to get or achieve something.
Added vowels to make a noun meaning a single part or portion

steps.m: some of us (subject of استمتع - enjoyed/benefited)
steps.n: Added نا meaning 'us' and ُ to connect it properly. This word is the subject of the verb استمتع (enjoyed/benefited) in this ayah.

steps.m: to split or divide into portions

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'a portion' or 'some'. This word is doing the action of enjoying (استمتع) in this verse.
Added بِ at the beginning to show this portion was used as a tool or means - like saying 'with' or 'by means of' in English
Added ٍ (tanween kasra) at the end to show this word is receiving an action

steps.m: to reach or arrive at a destination

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. Changed the ending alif to show emphasis.

steps.m: to set a time limit or deadline

steps.n: Adding the basic vowel marks makes this a complete past tense verb
Adding نا changes it from 'he' to 'we'
Added نَا to show this is our appointed time. This word is what we reached (object of بَلَغْنَا - we reached)
Added kasra to make it point to something specific
Added ال to make it mean 'who/which/that' instead of just 'this/that'. Like turning 'this' into 'which' in English. Links to أَجَّلْتَ (the time You set).
Added a small alif (ٓ) to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word

steps.m: he set a definite time limit
steps.n: The doubled ج makes the meaning more definite and emphatic
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action in the past
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it connects to what comes after it

steps.m: for us/to us (shows we receive or own something)
steps.n: Added نا at the end which means 'us' or 'our'. In this ayah, it shows who is receiving or experiencing something.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Forms the noun 'fire' from the root meaning 'light'
Changes the root into the noun 'fire' by adding vowel marks. The middle letter changes to alif to make it easier to say.
Adds ال to make it definite and doubles the ن with a shadda because it comes after ال
Adds damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. It connects to مَثْوَىٰكُمْ (your dwelling place) - meaning 'the Fire is your dwelling place'
This is the basic concept of staying or residing somewhere
Added مَ at beginning to make it mean 'place of staying'. The ى at the end shows it's a special type of place.
Added alif khanjariya (small alif) above the ى to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: your (all) dwelling place (referring to the Fire mentioned in النَّارُ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show it belongs to multiple people being spoken to. This refers back to the Fire (النَّارُ) mentioned as being their dwelling place.
Added vowels and alif to show who does the action
Added ين to show it's talking about many people

steps.m: those who remain forever (as the object being described)
steps.n: Added a fatha at the end because this word is describing people who are receiving the action (the ones staying in the Fire)
Added kasra vowel mark to show the correct pronunciation
Added the feminine pronoun ها to show what the preposition applies to
Changed the alif to alif madd (آ) which is a common writing style at the end of words, but doesn't change the meaning

This is a special word that shows something is different or left out from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it shows that people will stay in the Fire forever, except for what Allah wills differently.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that acts as the object of the verb شَآءَ (willed/wished). In this context, it means 'what Allah wills' or 'whatever Allah wills'.
This root expresses the concept of wanting or wishing for something
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he willed' in the past
Changed to a hollow verb pattern by adding long alif
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the u-mark at the end to show it's in the nominative case
This is the basic form of the emphasis word
Added kasra and shadda to show this is emphasizing the next word رَبَّكَ (your Lord). The shadda makes the emphasis stronger.
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and make it a noun
The doubled letter shows this is someone who cares completely and perfectly
Added 'your' to show who the Lord belongs to. The word comes after إِنَّ (indeed/truly) which is telling us something about your Lord.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: wise, full of wisdom (describes اللَّهُ - Allah)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (doing something) to a special describing word showing Allah always has this quality of wisdom
This pattern shows someone who has complete and perfect knowledge. When used for Allah, it indicates His perfect and complete knowledge of everything.

The double damma shows this is indefinite but when used as one of Allah's names, it indicates His unique and perfect attribute. This word pairs with حَكِيمٌ (All-Wise) to show Allah's perfect knowledge and wisdom together.
Added لِ and كَ to specify that this is being addressed to someone. The ٰ (alif) is written above to show it's a special demonstrative word.
Added كَ at the beginning to show similarity or comparison

steps.m: and like that (connecting to what follows: نُوَلِّى)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with what follows in the ayah, showing this is how Allah deals with the wrongdoers

steps.m: to be in charge of or close to something

steps.n: Added a shadda (double letter) to show someone actively putting another in charge, instead of just being in charge

steps.m: we put in charge (acting on بَعْضَ)
steps.n: Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. Changed vowels to match the present tense pattern. This verb acts on بَعْضَ (some) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'

steps.m: some/part of (object of نُوَلِّى - we appoint)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb نُوَلِّى (we appoint/turn) in the ayah
Changed the word to show who does the action, like changing 'wrong' to 'wrongdoer'
Added ين to make it plural (many wrongdoers) and show they are being acted upon in the sentence
Added ال (the) at the start to show we're talking about specific wrongdoers. The ظ gets doubled (ظّ) because it comes right after ال
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun

steps.m: some/a part (being acted upon by نُوَلِّى - we give authority to)
steps.n: Shows this word is receiving the action of giving authority in the verse
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added بِ at the beginning to show cause/reason. This word explains why something happens in relation to the verb يَكْسِبُونَ (they earn/do)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb
Adding وا makes it plural (they) and shows this is what they were doing in relation to يَكْسِبُونَ (earning/doing)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The prefix يَ shows this is happening now or in the future

steps.m: they gain/acquire (referring to الظَّالِمِينَ - the wrongdoers)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this refers to a group of people. In this ayah, it specifically refers back to الظَّالِمِينَ (the wrongdoers) mentioned earlier.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added م at beginning to create a noun meaning 'group' or 'assembly' from the verb 'to gather'
Added fatha at the end because the word is being called out to
Added يٰ at beginning to call out to the group. This word is addressing both jinn and humans in this ayah.

steps.m: to hide or be hidden from sight

steps.n: The kasra and sukun pattern creates a noun for a type of being that is naturally hidden from human sight
The shadda makes the meaning stronger by doubling the ن, showing these are powerful beings
Adding ال makes it refer to all jinn as a specific group
This word follows يٰمَعْشَرَ (O company/group of), showing these beings belong to or are part of that group
Changed the root into a noun representing humans as social beings
ال makes it specific - referring to all humans as a category
Adds و to connect with 'the jinn' mentioned before it

steps.m: and the humankind (possessed by/connected to what follows)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show connection to what follows in the ayah
This is a basic negation particle that changes present tense verbs to past tense negative meaning
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question, like asking 'Did not...?'
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now, and added vowel marks to show how to pronounce it
Added كُمْ at the end to show the action is directed towards multiple people being spoken to
Root letters r-s-l got damma vowels to make the plural word 'messengers' (like changing man to men)
Added double-damma (tanween) to show this is the subject doing the actions of telling (يَقُصُّونَ) and warning (يُنذِرُونَ) in this verse
Starting with the basic preposition مِن which shows something coming from or originating from somewhere
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
The ن gets a shadda because it combines with the م sound that comes after it when speaking - this is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning

steps.m: to tell stories or narrate events

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this telling/narrating was done with extra emphasis
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle letter vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation of basic preposition
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for connecting with pronoun
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: The root letters change to make the noun 'sign'. A hamza and long alif are added at the start to create this special pattern.
Makes the word plural by adding the feminine plural ending ات
Adds kasra at the end to show the word is being used as an object of a preposition
Adds the first person possessive pronoun ي to show these are 'my signs'. This word is what is being narrated/recited by the messengers in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed middle vowel to make it present tense 'he warns'
Added ون to make it plural (they)
Added كم to show who is being warned (you all)
Added conjunction و to connect with previous phrase
These are the three basic letters that give us the idea of meeting or encountering something
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'meeting' by adding alif after second letter
Added fatha at the end to show this word is connected to 'your day' (يَوْمِكُمْ) that comes after it. Also wrote the long alif as آ which is just a writing style change.

steps.m: to pass time, to spend a day
steps.n: Basic meaning of a day or time period
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun for 'day'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added كُمْ at the end to show this day belongs to multiple people being addressed
This is the basic pointing word used to indicate something nearby
Added ه at the beginning to draw attention to what's being pointed at, like saying 'look at this'
Added the alif mark (ٰ) over ه and fatha on ذ to help with pronunciation. In this ayah, it's pointing to the day/time being discussed.
Basic meaning of speaking or saying
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb

steps.m: they said (responding to the question about messengers)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are speaking. This is the response from the jinn and humans when asked about the messengers.

steps.m: to witness or testify to something seen
steps.n: Basic meaning of witnessing or giving testimony
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: we witnessed (in response to قَالُوا/they said)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This is part of a conversation where someone is responding to what others said (قَالُوا/they said).
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or against something else
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation: fatha on ع and ى at end to show it's a preposition
Added alif maksura mark (small alif) above the ى to show it's pronounced like a long a sound
Added small vertical line (alif khanjariya) to show slight pause in pronunciation
Forms basic noun by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Forms broken plural of نَفْس using أَفْعُل pattern
Changes ending to show it belongs to/is owned by something
Adds first person plural possessive pronoun نَا meaning 'our'. This word follows the preposition عَلَىٰ (upon/against) in the ayah, making it the object being testified against.

steps.m: to deceive or mislead someone by making false promises
steps.n: Basic meaning of deceiving or misleading
Added shadda (double letter mark) on middle letter to show strong or intense deception

steps.m: she deceived strongly (referring to الْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا - worldly life)
steps.n: Added ت to show a feminine subject did the action. In this ayah, the worldly life (الْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا) is what did the deceiving.

steps.m: she deceived them strongly (referring to the jinn and humans mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show who was deceived - referring back to the jinn and humans (ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added وَ at beginning to connect this with the previous statement

steps.m: to be alive, to have life
steps.n: Basic meaning of living or being alive
Root 'to live' changed to make the noun 'life' by adding vowels

steps.m: life (as a complete state or condition)
steps.n: Adds ة (taa marboota) at the end to make it mean 'life' as a complete state rather than just the concept
Adds definite article to specify 'the life' (referring to worldly life in this context)
Adds nominative case ending because this word is the subject doing the deceiving (غَرَّتْهُمُ) in the ayah
Basic meaning of being close or near
Root meaning 'near' was turned into a special word meaning 'this world' by adding -ya at the end (like how we add -ly in English)

steps.m: the worldly life (contrasted with the afterlife)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the د letter to show it's the specific worldly life being talked about

steps.m: to witness or testify to something seen/known
steps.n: Basic meaning of witnessing or giving testimony
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Added وَ at beginning to connect this with the previous statement
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or directed towards something else
The alif at the end (ى) shows this is a complete word, not attached to what follows. The marks (ٰٓ) help with pronunciation and pausing.
The pattern creates a noun referring to the essence or core of something
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic noun
Changed from singular to plural using the pattern أَفْعُل which adds أَ at start and changes ending to ُ
Added ِ to show this word follows على (upon/against)
Added هِمْ to show these are their own selves
This is a particle that emphasizes and connects to شَهِدُوا۟ (they witnessed) to show what was witnessed
Added هُمْ to show who the statement is about. This connects to the next word كٰفِرِينَ (disbelievers) to complete the meaning of what was witnessed.
This is the basic concept of being or existing
Added vowels to make the past tense form. The middle letter و changes to ا in past tense.

steps.m: they were (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show that many people (they) did the action instead of just one person (he)

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to show who does the action, like changing 'disbelieve' to 'disbeliever'
Added ين to make it plural and show it's being acted upon in the sentence
Added final vowel mark to show its grammatical position in the sentence
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this form.
Adding لِ changes 'this' (for near things) to 'that' (for far things), like pointing with your finger vs pointing far away
The كَ at the end completes the demonstrative pronoun form. It's like adding extra emphasis to show distance.
This is a connecting word that introduces what comes next in the sentence. It works like 'that' in English when you say 'I know that you are here'
This is a negative word that makes the following verb (يَكُن) mean 'was not' instead of 'is not'
The dot above the letter ل merged with the letter after ذٰلِكَ to make a stronger sound
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at the start to make it mean 'he is' (present tense) instead of 'he was' (past tense), and changed middle vowels to match
The word comes after لَمْ (not) which requires removing the final ُ and shortening the word. This makes it mean 'was not' or 'did not exist'

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: Connected the letters and added shadda to show this is someone who really takes care of others
Added damma at end to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Added كَ to show this Lord belongs to or has a special relationship with you
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action (like changing 'destroy' to 'destroyer')
Added َ at the end to show this word is connected to 'towns' (القُرَىٰ) that comes after it, like saying 'destroyer of towns'

steps.m: to settle, dwell, or gather in a place

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make a word meaning 'the act of settling'
Added ة to make it refer to a specific place where people settle
Changed from single village to plural villages by changing the middle vowels and ending
Added ٱلْ to make it definite and ى with alif to show it's the end of the word
Added ُ and ْ to turn the action into a noun meaning the wrong itself
Added بِ meaning 'with/by' at the start and ٍ at the end to show it's indefinite. This word explains how the destroying happens in relation to مُهْلِكَ (destroyer).
Changed from a verb about inhabiting to a noun meaning the people who inhabit a place. This word refers to the people of القرى (the towns).
The هَا at the end means 'its' (referring to something feminine). This word is the subject of غٰفِلُونَ (heedless/unaware) in the nominative case.
و connects this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to describe someone who is heedless. This describes the state of أهل (the people) in the verse.
Makes the word plural to describe multiple people who are heedless
Adds the -a ending to show this is the subject form

steps.m: to be whole, complete, or cover everything

steps.n: The doubled ل (with shadda) shows this means 'absolutely everything'. The ُ (damma) on ك makes it a noun meaning 'all' or 'every'.

steps.m: for all/every (showing this applies to everyone or everything)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to mean 'for' and ٍ (tanween kasra) at the end to show this word is indefinite (not talking about specific things)

steps.m: and for all/every (connecting this to what came before)
steps.n: Added وَ (wa) at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this to the previous statement
Changed from a verb root to a noun by adding fathas between the letters, making it mean 'steps' instead of 'to climb steps'
Added ة to show one single instance
Changed to plural form by replacing ة with ٰت
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of an implied verb meaning 'there are'
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from' that shows where something starts or comes from
Added ما (meaning 'what') and combined it with مِن. The ن changes to م because it's followed by another م

steps.m: from what (connected to عَمِلُوا - what they did)
steps.n: Added emphasis (shadda) on the first م. This word connects to عَمِلُوا (they did) to mean 'from what they did'
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
This is a special word that can mean either 'what' (asking about something) or 'not' (negating something). In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's negating the verb يَعْمَلُونَ (they do).
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does complete caring and nurturing

steps.m: one who cares for completely (subject of بِغٰفِلٍ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this is the doer in relation to the word بِغٰفِلٍ (unaware) in the ayah
Adds كَ to show this refers to you. This word is doing the action in relation to بِغٰفِلٍ (unaware) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action. This word describes what your Lord is not.

steps.m: with/by one who neglects (negated with مَا to say 'your Lord is not at all neglectful')
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with/by' and tanween at end to make it indefinite. The whole phrase مَا رَبُّكَ بِغَافِلٍ means 'Your Lord is not at all neglectful'.
This is a basic preposition that shows what something is about or concerning
Added the question word ما (what) after the preposition

The ن combines with م to make a stronger م sound (shown by the ّ mark). This is just a pronunciation change that happens when these letters come together. The word connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do) later in the ayah.
Added ي at beginning to show present tense, with vowel marks to connect the letters properly

steps.m: they do/work (referring to the deeds mentioned in عَمِلُوا)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. This connects back to عَمِلُوا (they did) earlier in the verse, showing their ongoing actions.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and develops others perfectly
The ُ shows this word is connected to ٱلْغَنِىُّ (the Self-Sufficient) which describes it
The كَ means 'your' (when talking to one person). This shows who the lord belongs to.
Adding وَ connects this to what came before, meaning 'and'

steps.m: to be free from need or want

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it into an adjective describing someone who has this quality
The shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing complete self-sufficiency
The ُ shows this word is doing something in the sentence
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific and shows we're talking about Allah's attribute
This is a special word that connects two nouns to show ownership or possession. Here it describes الْغَنِيُّ (the Self-Sufficient) as being the possessor of الرَّحْمَةِ (mercy).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'showed mercy' (verb) to 'mercy' (noun) by adding ة at the end and changing middle vowel
The ِ shows this word belongs to something. Here, رَحْمَةِ is possessed by ذُو (meaning 'owner of' or 'possessor of').
Adding ال causes the first letter to get a shadda (ّ) mark, making it doubled

steps.m: if (introduces condition for يَشَأْ - He wills)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a possibility. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَشَأْ (He wills) to show that what follows depends on Allah's will.
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel marks َ (fatha)
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense. The ا is dropped and replaced with sukun because it's in a conditional phrase starting with إِن (if).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to make the verb causative - showing someone making something happen
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added sukun at end because this comes after إِن (if) in the ayah

steps.m: he makes you all go away (conditional after إِن)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at end to show who is being made to go away

steps.m: he may/would seek to make (something) replace
steps.n: Form 10 verb (added يَسْتَ) to mean 'seek to make something replace'. Ending changed to sukun because it comes after 'if He wills'

steps.m: and He may/would seek to make (something) replace (after إِن يَشَأْ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous conditional phrase إِن يَشَأْ (if He wills)
This is a primary preposition that shows origin, source or starting point. It is not derived from a root.
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how it's pronounced
Added a small ن mark (called tanwin) after the ن to show a special pronunciation when connecting to the next word
The root meaning of distance developed into a word meaning 'after' in time or sequence
Added كم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This word follows مِنۢ (from) in the ayah.
This is a standalone particle used for questions or relative clauses
Added shadda for emphasis. This word is receiving the action of يَشَآءُ (He wills/wants) in this ayah.
Changed root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowel marks
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action
Added long ا to show the action takes time or is ongoing
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced. This letter ك is used to show that something is similar to something else.
Added ما to make the comparison more general, like saying 'just as' instead of just 'as'.
Changed the last alif to a long alif (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to create, bring into existence, or start something new

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing something to happen

steps.m: He created you all (object of يُذْهِبْكُمْ - He will remove you)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show who was created. This word is connected to يُذْهِبْكُمْ (He will remove you) in the ayah, showing what Allah can do with those He created.
This is a preposition that shows origin, source or starting point
Added shadda (doubling mark) on م because it comes after a connecting word. In this ayah, it shows which people the descendants come from.
Root means 'scatter' - doubled middle letter and added damma to make it mean 'tiny scattered things' (like dust or seeds)
Added ي (ya) to make it mean 'offspring/descendants' - like how children spread out from parents
Added ة to make it refer to a single group of descendants
Added kasra at the end to show possession or relationship
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'people' - the idea comes from people standing together as a group
Added tanween kasra to show this word belongs to or is connected to ذُرِّيَّةِ (offspring/descendants)

steps.m: to come after or be different from something

steps.n: Root أخر became a common word for 'other' by adding vowels and stretching the first sound
Added ين to make it plural, showing we're talking about multiple 'others'

steps.m: others (as object of يَسْتَخْلِفْ - replace with)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of the verb يَسْتَخْلِفْ (replace with)

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizes ما توعدون - what you are promised)
steps.n: This word emphasizes the truth of what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that what is being promised will definitely happen.
Basic particle form that connects ideas. In this ayah, it connects 'indeed' (إِنَّ) with 'you are promised' (تُوعَدُونَ)
In this ayah, مَا works with إِنَّ (indeed) to point to تُوعَدُونَ (you are promised), making the meaning 'Indeed, that which you are promised...'

steps.m: to promise or warn about something that will happen

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense passive voice by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يُ with تُ
Added ونَ to show this refers to multiple people
Changed the root into active participle form. The ي at the end is from the root.
Added tanwin (ٍ) to show indefiniteness. The final ي drops when tanwin is added.
Added laam prefix to emphasize certainty
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Extended the alif to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. In this ayah, it's connecting to the word تُوعَدُونَ (you are promised).
This is the basic form of the pronoun meaning 'you' when talking to one person
Adding م with damma at the end changes this from addressing one person to addressing many people
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action
Added ين to make it plural (more than one). This word is the predicate (khabar) that tells us what أنتم (you all) are not.

steps.m: among those who can make things impossible (predicate of أنتم)
steps.n: Added بِ at the start to mean 'among' or 'with'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma (ُ) on first letter, and 3) adding sukun (ْ) at the end. This is telling one person to speak.

steps.m: people, folk (a group that stands together)
steps.n: The root meaning of standing together becomes a noun for a group that stands together - a people or folk
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is being used as an object of a preposition

steps.m: O my people! (being called by قُلْ)
steps.n: The يٰ at the start shows this is calling out to someone. This word is being called out as part of what is being said (قُلْ - say) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he worked' to 'work!' by adding i- at the start and making the end silent - this makes it a command to one person
Added plural command ending to address multiple people
Adding ٱ at start shows this is a command

steps.m: upon, over (related to مَكَانَتِكُمْ - your position/state)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or over something else. In this ayah, it connects to مَكَانَتِكُمْ (your position/state) to mean 'upon your position/state'.

steps.m: upon, over (related to مَكَانَتِكُمْ - your position/state)
steps.n: Added an alif sign (ٰ) after the ى which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
Adding مَ at the start changes the meaning from the action of existing to the place where something exists
Adding ة changes it from physical place to abstract status/rank

steps.m: your (all of you) status (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: Adding ِكُمْ shows this status belongs to multiple people being addressed
This is the basic emphasis word that adds certainty to what follows, like saying 'truly' in English
Added ى at the end to show this is about 'me/I'. The ن gets a shadda and kasra before ى
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action
Adds the tanween damma to show this is indefinite and is the predicate (what is being said about the subject) in the sentence
This is a basic particle that shows something will happen in the future. It's stronger than just 'will' - it means it will definitely happen, just not right away.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of what came before. In this ayah, it's saying: do what you want, THEN soon you will know the outcome.
ت at start shows it's either 'you' or 'she' doing the action
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a question word asking about people. In this ayah, it's asking about who will have the good end/outcome, connecting to the verb تَكُونُ (will be/become).
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed from past tense 'was' to a noun meaning 'being'. The middle و is kept since it's an important part of the word.
Added ت at beginning to show feminine subject. This matches with عٰقِبَةُ (outcome) which is feminine.
This is the basic particle ل that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for him (referring to عٰقِبَةُ ٱلدَّارِ - outcome of the home)
steps.n: Added هُ with an extension mark (ۥ) to show this means 'him'. This connects to عٰقِبَةُ ٱلدَّارِ (outcome of the home) in the next part of the ayah.
Adding vowels turns the root into a usable past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and ة at end to create a noun meaning 'the result/outcome of something'

steps.m: the outcome/result (of الدَّارِ - this worldly abode)
steps.n: Added damma at end to show this is the subject of تَكُونُ (will be). The alif is written as ٰ in this Quranic spelling.

steps.m: to move around in a space

steps.n: The weak letter و becomes ا. A home is called this because it's a space you move around in.
Adding ٱل makes it specific. The د gets a shadda because it comes after ٱل.
The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to what comes before it in the sentence
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed', making it emphasize what comes after it
Added هُ at the end to show who/what is being referred to
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which helps show pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
This is a negative particle that makes what comes after it negative. Here it negates the verb يُفْلِحُ (will succeed), changing its meaning to 'will not succeed'.
Connected the root letters together with fatha vowels to make a complete word showing past action
Changed to present tense by: 1) adding يُ at start to show 'he' doing something now, 2) changing vowels to show ongoing action. This verb is negated by لا (not) in this ayah.
Changes from the basic verb 'to wrong' into a word meaning 'someone who wrongs others' - like changing 'harm' to 'harmer'
Makes the noun definite
Added the plural ending ون to show multiple wrongdoers. This word is the subject of لا يفلح (will not succeed) in the ayah.

steps.m: to make something into something else, to set, to put

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to show one person did this action in the past
Adding وا shows a group of people (3 or more) did the action

steps.m: and they made/set (a portion for Allah)
steps.n: Adding و connects this to what came before. In this ayah, they are making/setting aside portions of crops and cattle.
This is the base word for God/deity
Adding shadda on ل makes this the proper name for the one true God
Adding kasra at the end shows possession or connection
Adding لِ at the beginning shows this is for or belongs to Allah. This word is the indirect object of جَعَلُوا (they made/set aside)
Starting with the two separate words: مِن (from) and مَا (what)

steps.m: from what (of ذَرَأَ - what He created/multiplied)
steps.n: The ن of مِن combines with the م of مَا, creating a doubled م (shown by the ّ mark). This is a common pronunciation rule when these words come together.

steps.m: to create or bring into existence

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This word describes what Allah did in creating things that the people then divided up wrongly in this verse.
Basic preposition showing the starting point or source of something
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الْحَرْثِ (the crops/harvest)
Root meaning 'plant/cultivate' becomes noun 'crops' by adding vowel marks (fatha and sukun)
Added ال (the) at the beginning and ِ at the end because it follows the word مِنَ (from)
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he was blessed' in the past tense
Changed from a verb about blessings to a plural noun meaning livestock/cattle (which were seen as valuable blessings)
Added 'the' and showing it's connected to something else in the sentence
Added 'and' to connect this word to what came before it
Changes from verb 'to establish' to noun meaning 'a share that is established'

steps.m: a share/portion (object of جَعَلُوا - they made/set up)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is receiving the action of جَعَلُوا (they made/set up) in the verse
Connected the root letters with fathas to make a past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in hollow verbs to make them easier to say.
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action

steps.m: and so they said (connecting to what came before in the ayah)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this action with what happened before in the verse
Basic pointing word that shows something is near
Added ذا to make it specifically point to masculine things that are near

steps.m: this (pointing to something in the ayah)
steps.n: Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha marks to show correct pronunciation. In this ayah, it's pointing to a portion or share that the pagans allocated.
This is the base word Allah, with shadda on ل to show emphasis

steps.m: for/to Allah (shows this portion belongs to Allah)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show 'for/to' and ِ at end to show possession. This shows that something belongs to or is for Allah in this verse.
Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'claim' by adding the vowels 'a' and silence mark
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is telling us how/why something happened
Added ِ at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it
Added possessive pronoun suffix هِمْ to indicate 'their'

steps.m: this (pointing to what they assigned to their partners)
steps.n: A demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby. In this ayah, it's pointing to the portion they assigned to their false deities.

steps.m: and this (pointing to what they assigned to their partners)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous statement about what they assigned to Allah

steps.m: to partner with, to share something with others

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to share' into noun meaning 'one who shares/partners'
Changed the vowels and added اء at the end to make it mean 'many partners' instead of just one partner
Added لِ at the beginning to show these partners are receiving something

steps.m: for our partners (who they claimed as partners with Allah)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show these are 'our' partners. In this verse, it refers to the false gods that people claimed as partners with Allah.
Combined two particles: فَ (then/so) and مَا (what). This combination introduces a result or consequence in the ayah, connecting to the verb يَصِلُ (reaches/arrives).
This is the basic concept of existing or being in a state
Added short vowel marks (fat-ha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'
Added alif after first letter because و is a weak letter that changes to alif in past tense
Added ي between second and third root letters to create a word meaning 'one who shares/associates'
Changed the word to plural form by adding اء at the end and changing vowels
Changed the ending to prepare for adding possession
Adds preposition لِ meaning 'for/to'
Adds possessive pronoun suffix هِمْ meaning 'their'
This is the basic negative word that stops or prevents something from happening
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. In this ayah, it negates the verb يَصِلُ (reaches), showing that something doesn't reach its destination.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

Changed from past to present tense - the و at start becomes يَ and middle letter changes from فَتحة (a) to كَسرة (i). Shows ongoing action of offerings meant for Allah being given to false gods instead.
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects the verb يَصِلُ (it reaches) with شُرَكَآئِهِمْ (their partners), showing what is being reached.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.

steps.m: to/for/of Allah (connected to لِشُرَكَآئِنَا - to our partners)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word لِشُرَكَآئِنَا (to our partners) in the verse, comparing what they assigned to Allah versus their partners
This is a relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'whatever'. In this ayah, it's asking about what belongs to Allah.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it in the ayah
Added vowel marks (fatha) to the root letters to make the past tense 'he was'. This word connects with يَصِلُ (reaches) later in the sentence.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb
This is the basic word for deity or god in Arabic
Adding shadda on ل makes this the proper name for the one true God in Islam
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose or ownership, and ِ at the end to show it's being affected by the لِ
Basic third person masculine singular pronoun that refers back to what was allocated to their partners. This pronoun is the subject of the verb يَصِلُ (reaches/arrives)
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a consequence or result - whatever was allocated to their partners reaches them, in contrast to what was allocated to Allah not reaching Him

steps.m: to reach or connect to something

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he reached'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, ِ in middle, and ُ at end. The weak letter و drops in present tense. This word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) in the ayah using إِلَى (to).
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it shows that something reaches toward Allah.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a spelling variation - it doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to share or be a partner with someone

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to share' into noun meaning 'partner'
Changed the word to show many partners instead of just one
Changed the ending to show these partners belong to or are connected to something
Added هِمْ at the end to show these are their partners. This word is the object of كَانَ (was) in this ayah.
Added short vowel marks (fat-ha) to make it mean 'it was bad' in the past tense
The alif after س makes it mean 'became' rather than just 'was'

steps.m: it was bad (describing their judgment in يَحْكُمُونَ)
steps.n: Added the optional maddah mark (آ) over the alif to show it's a long vowel. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to things or actions. In this ayah, it's the subject of كَانَ (was), asking about what belonged to their partners.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now (present tense) and the ُ at the end shows 'he' is doing it
The ون ending shows multiple people (they) are doing the judging
Starting with the demonstrative word 'this' which is the core of this compound word
Adding ل adds emphasis to the demonstrative word
Adding ك changes 'this' to 'that' or 'like that'
Adding ك at the beginning makes it mean 'like that' = 'similarly'
Adding و connects this word to the verb زَيَّنَ (made attractive) that follows it in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made very beautiful (subject of شُرَكَآؤُهُمْ)
steps.n: Adding shadda (doubling mark) on the middle letter makes the meaning stronger. This word is what their false gods (شُرَكَآؤُهُمْ) did to make evil deeds seem attractive.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he guides (the subject of this verb is اللَّهَ - Allah)
steps.n: Added ya at beginning to show present tense, changed middle vowel to kasra, and added sukun on first root letter. The weak letter ى changes to ي in present tense. This verb describes what Allah does.
Added alif maqsura (ى) at the end to show it's a present tense verb. This is a common pattern for verbs ending in weak letters.
Added fatha after first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning 'people' - those who establish/maintain something
Added ٱلْ to make it definite ('the people') and َ at the end to show it's being acted upon by the verb يَهْدِى (guides)

steps.m: to do wrong, to be unjust

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'wrong/injustice' to past tense verb meaning 'he did wrong'. Like changing 'wrong' to 'wronged'.
Changes from verb 'he wronged' to noun meaning 'wrongdoer'
Makes the word plural to mean multiple wrongdoers
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Adds the accusative case ending
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ before لَّآ أَجِدُ)
steps.n: This is a command telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to say what follows. The middle letter و is dropped because it's a weak letter, and the vowels change to make it a command.
The shadda adds emphasis to the negation. The alif is elongated for further emphasis.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes to first person present tense. The weak letter و changes to أ at the start. This word is what the speaker is commanded to say through the word قُل (say) that comes before it.

steps.m: in/within (connects to مَآ أُوحِىَ - what was revealed)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects to the phrase 'what was revealed', showing we are looking inside or within the revelation.
Added fatha to show this is a question word asking about what is being found
The alif is written with madd (آ) to show it should be pronounced longer. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra (ِ). This shows that something received the revelation rather than gave it.
This is a preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it shows what was revealed 'to' someone.

steps.m: towards me, to me (receiving وحي revelation)
steps.n: Added the first person pronoun ي with shadda (ّ) to show this is happening to 'me'. The fatha (َ) before the ي helps connect the sounds. In this ayah, it shows the Prophet ﷺ is receiving revelation.

steps.m: to prevent or make something off-limits
steps.n: Basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and doubled the middle letter to show this is something receiving the action of being prevented

steps.m: something forbidden (object of أَجِدُ - I find)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of being found in the phrase 'I do not find... forbidden'

steps.m: upon/on (connects طَاعِمٍ to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing position or relationship. In this ayah, it connects the word طَاعِمٍ (one who eats) to what comes before it.

steps.m: upon/on (connects طَاعِمٍ to what comes before)
steps.n: Added an alif symbol (ٰ) after the ى to show it's a long vowel sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
Basic meaning of eating or tasting
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action
Added tanween kasra at the end because this word is being described by what follows it in the ayah (يَطْعَمُهُ - what he eats)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start with damma at end to show present tense, with sukun on middle letter
Added هُ at the end to show 'it' is what's being eaten - like saying 'eats it' instead of just 'eats'
This is a basic exception particle that introduces what is excluded from the previous statement. Here it connects to يَطْعَمُهُ (he eats it) to show what foods are exceptions to the general rule.
The final alif is elongated (آ) for emphasis and to maintain the Quranic rhythm. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a particle that connects ideas and introduces what comes next. Here it introduces what is forbidden (يَكُونَ meaning 'it is'). Think of it like opening quotation marks before someone speaks.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters. This is the past tense form before any other changes.
Added ي at start to show present tense, and changed middle vowel to damma because this is a special pattern for hollow verbs (verbs with و in middle)
Fatha at end shows this verb comes after 'that' or 'to'
Changes from verb 'to die' into adjective meaning 'dead'
Added ة to make it feminine and refer to a dead animal/carcass
Added ً (tanween) to show this is indefinite and is the object of the verb

steps.m: or (connecting مَيْتَةً with دَمًا and what follows)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that gives equal choices between the forbidden foods mentioned in this verse: dead meat, flowing blood, pork meat, etc.
Root letters combined with fatha (َ) to make the simple word for blood
The و at the end drops off because it's a weak letter at the end of the word
Added ًا at the end to show this word is being described or acted upon in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed from active (doing the pouring) to passive (being poured out)
The doubled م makes the meaning stronger/more intense
Added ًا to show this word describes the blood (دَمًا) mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: or (connecting دَمًا 'blood' as another forbidden food)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows another option in the list of forbidden foods. It connects 'blood' (دَمًا) as another item after 'dead meat' (مَيْتَةً) in the list of what is forbidden to eat.
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the basic noun

steps.m: meat (object of يَطْعَمُهُ - he eats it)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. This connects to the verb يَطْعَمُهُ (he eats it) in the ayah.
This is the basic concept of a pig or swine
This changes the basic root into the common noun form for 'pig'
The tanwin kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite (a/an) and comes after the word 'meat' (لَحْمَ) in the phrase 'meat of a pig'
This is a common emphatic particle used to stress the certainty of what follows
The فَ shows this is a result or consequence of what came before

steps.m: so indeed it (referring to the forbidden food)
steps.n: The هُ suffix refers back to the forbidden food mentioned before. This word connects to رِجْسٌ (filth) which describes what that food is.
The ۥ mark shows how to pronounce the 'u' sound in هُ clearly
Changes the action of being dirty into the actual dirtiness itself
The double-damma (tanween) at the end shows this is describing something as being unclean - in this case, it's describing pork (لَحْمَ خِنزِيرٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between different options. In this ayah, it connects different types of forbidden foods: 'dead meat OR blood OR pork meat'.
Changed the root letters into a noun that names the action itself

steps.m: a breaking away (from obedience) (object of أُهِلَّ - was raised)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of أُهِلَّ (was raised)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: it was raised in dedication (referring to animal sacrifices)
steps.n: Changed to passive form to show the action was done to something. Also doubled the last letter to show emphasis. In this ayah, it refers to animals that were dedicated to others besides Allah.
The vowel marks ُ and ِ show this is happening to something (passive voice)
Added vowels to the root letters to make a word meaning 'other than' or 'different from'
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose/direction, and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes after it
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
Added ِ at the end to show this belongs to or follows after the word غَيْرِ (other than). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasla form since it connects to the previous word.
Basic preposition that shows how something is done

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to what was dedicated to other than اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added pronoun هِ (it/him) to show what the preposition applies to

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to what was dedicated to other than اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation
Basic interrogative/relative pronoun meaning 'who' or 'whoever'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added kasra at the end because the next word starts with a hamza (ٱضْطُرَّ)

steps.m: to harm or be in a difficult situation
to be forced into a difficult situation
steps.n: Added ا and ت to show someone was forced into a situation they didn't choose

steps.m: to be forced into a difficult situation
steps.n: When ت comes after ض, it changes to ط to make it easier to say

steps.m: to be forced into a difficult situation
steps.n: The last letter is doubled to show intensity of being forced

steps.m: he was forced (into eating what is forbidden)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show it's past tense passive (something happened to him). The ا at the start becomes ٱ. In this ayah, it refers to someone being forced to eat forbidden food due to necessity.
Basic noun form showing something that is different

steps.m: other than (being acted upon by اضْطُرَّ)
steps.n: Added a mark at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb اضْطُرَّ (was forced/compelled)

steps.m: to seek beyond limits, to transgress

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite and follows the word غَيْرَ (meaning 'without')
Starting with the basic negation particle لا which means 'not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to what came before. In this ayah, it's connecting to the word مُحَرَّمًا (forbidden), adding another thing that is not forbidden.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: a transgressor (after لا in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (he went beyond limits) to a noun (transgressor) and added tanween kasra because it comes after لا
Basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows

steps.m: then indeed (connects to رَبَّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this statement follows from what came before. This word connects to رَبَّكَ (your Lord) which comes after it.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
he cared for and helped grow
steps.n: Adding fatha marks turns it into a past tense verb meaning 'he did this action'

steps.m: your Lord (being acted upon by إِنَّ)
steps.n: The root رَبَبَ (to care for) became رَبّ (Lord) with a shadda, then كَ was added to mean 'your'. إِنَّ makes it 'indeed your Lord'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes the word to show someone who forgives a lot, using the pattern with و that makes qualities stronger

steps.m: Most Forgiving (as a general attribute of Allah)
steps.n: The double mark (tanween) at the end shows this is a complete description of Allah being Most Forgiving, like a title
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy
Changed from an action (showing mercy) to describing someone who has lots of mercy by adding ي
The shadda makes the meaning stronger to show this is Allah's perfect attribute

steps.m: Most Merciful (describing Allah as the doer of mercy)
steps.n: The added double-dots (tanween) at the end shows this is a complete description of Allah, like saying 'The Most Merciful One'
Adds basic conjunction meaning 'and' to connect with previous statement
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Added ال at the beginning to point to specific people being talked about
Added shadda on ل and fatha at the end. This word connects to هَادُوا۟ (became Jews) to tell us who did that action.
Connected root letters with vowels. The middle و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle)

Added وا to show multiple people did this action. In this context, it specifically means 'they became Jews' because this meaning developed from the idea of 'turning/returning' to the right path. This word is connected to حَرَّمْنَا (We forbade) as it shows who the rules were given to.

steps.m: to forbid or make something not allowed

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on ر makes the forbidding more emphatic and complete
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. Also added sukun before نَا as required
Added damma on first letter and shadda on last letter to create the basic word meaning 'all'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the verb حَرَّمْنَا (we forbade)
This is a special word used to show possession or attribution. It doesn't come from a root - it's a basic particle.

steps.m: possessor of, one who has (describing كُلَّ)
steps.n: The word changes to ذِى because it follows كُلَّ (every/all). This shows it's being affected by the word before it.
Changes the word into a noun that describes a natural body part
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is something possessed by ذِى (owner of) in this ayah
Added fatha at the end to help connect with the next word
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this phrase to the previous part of the verse.

steps.m: cattle (because they were used to plow/split earth)
steps.n: Added fathas to make it a noun. The meaning comes from cattle being used to plow fields.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being described by something else in the sentence

steps.m: the cattle (being described by another word)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to specify these particular cattle being discussed
Root letters got fatha marks to make the word mean a flock of sheep/goats
Added ال to specify these particular sheep/goats
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being described or connected to something else in the sentence
Added و at beginning to connect this word with 'cattle' (ٱلْبَقَرِ) mentioned before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The shadda (ّ) intensifies the meaning by doubling the middle root letter

steps.m: we made forbidden (subject of the verb)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word is the main verb in this part of the ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمْ which means 'them' to show who is being affected
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form singular noun
Changes the vowels from 'a' to 'u' and adds 'uu' in the middle to make it plural
Adds fatha to show this word is being acted upon
Adds suffix showing possession by two things (referring to cattle and sheep mentioned earlier)
This is a basic particle used to exclude something from what was mentioned before it

steps.m: except (what follows from شُحُومَهُمَآ - their fat)
steps.n: Adding the shadda and alif makes the exclusion stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it's excluding certain types of fat from the general prohibition mentioned before.
A word that connects to the next part of the sentence, pointing to what was carried. It's like saying 'that which' in English when describing something.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb

steps.m: she carried (referring to what carried fat)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female subject did the action. In this ayah, this refers to what carried the fat that was forbidden.

steps.m: to appear on the surface or back of something

steps.n: Changes from a root meaning 'appear' into the plural noun 'backs' by adding vowels (u-u) and و at the end
Adds damma to show this is the subject
Adds هُمَا to show this belongs to two things (referring to cattle and sheep mentioned earlier in the verse)
Basic conjunction word that shows there are different choices or possibilities
Added kasra at the end because it connects to the next word ٱلْحَوَايَآ (the intestines). This shows it's in the middle of listing things.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a basic past tense verb
Plural form made by adding alif and ya to make a word meaning intestines/guts
The definite article makes the noun definite
Changed final alif to alif madda (آ) because it comes at the end of the word when pausing

steps.m: or (connecting two parts of what was forbidden)
steps.n: This is a connecting word (conjunction) that shows there are two or more choices or possibilities. In this ayah, it connects different types of fat that were forbidden.

steps.m: that which (refers to fat that is mixed with bones)
steps.n: In this ayah, مَا is being used as a relative pronoun to refer to the fat that is mixed with bones, connecting it to the previous discussion about prohibited foods.

steps.m: to mix or blend things together

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added اِ at start and ت between first two letters to show this happens on its own without someone doing it
Changed the beginning alif to a different form (ٱ) - this doesn't change the meaning, just the writing style
Changed the root meaning from an action (being great) to a concrete object (bone) by adding specific vowel patterns. This pattern often creates nouns for solid objects.
Added tanween (the ٍ) to show this is any bone, not a specific one. Like saying 'a bone' instead of 'the bone'.
Added بِ at the beginning to show this bone is being used to do something, like saying 'with a bone' or 'by a bone'
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif with bar (ٰ) shows distance, changing 'this' to 'that'

steps.m: that, that one (masculine) (refers to the punishment mentioned in جَزَيْنٰهُم)
steps.n: Added لِكَ to make it specifically masculine and singular. This word points to the punishment mentioned in the next word جَزَيْنٰهُم (We punished them)
Adds ي with sukun after second root letter to form basic verb pattern
Adding نٰ shows 'we' did the action. This connects to ذٰلِكَ (that) which refers to this punishment.
Adding هُم suffix as object pronoun meaning 'them'

steps.m: to transgress, to cross boundaries wrongfully

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun showing the act or concept of transgression
The word 'transgression' gets 'their' added to it (like saying 'their book' instead of just 'book')
Adding the preposition بِ shows this transgression was the cause or reason for something (referring back to the punishment mentioned in the ayah)
This is a word that makes what comes after it more certain, like adding 'definitely' in English
The و connects this phrase to what came before it, joining it to the previous statement about forbidden foods
The ا at the end shows that 'we' are the ones speaking, referring to Allah in this case
This is the basic meaning of being truthful
Added vowels to connect the letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone who does the action regularly
Added ون to show this refers to multiple people
Added لَ to emphasize that they are definitely truthful
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition, like saying 'if' in English
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. It shows this is a response or result, connecting to the word كَذَّبُوكَ (they deny you)
Adding the shadda (ّ) on the middle letter shows this is a strong or repeated denial. The fathas (َ) connect the letters together.
Adding وا at the end shows a group of people (they) did the action

steps.m: they denied you (as the object of يُرَدُّ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show you are the one being denied. This word connects to يُرَدُّ (is turned away) later in the verse.
Changed the word into a command by adding ُ (damma) on the first letter. The middle letter و drops out because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: then say! (responding to كَذَّبُوكَ - they denied you)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this command comes as a response to them denying you

steps.m: to care for, raise, develop, sustain

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense and turns it into a noun
Adds nominative case ending to show this word is in the subject position
Added كُمْ to show the Lord belongs to all the people being addressed
This is a special word that doesn't come from a typical root. It's one of the 'special five nouns' in Arabic.
Added damma (ُ) to show this word is the subject. In this ayah, it's describing Allah as the possessor of mercy (رَحْمَةٍ).
Adding vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'mercy'
Added ة to make it a specific instance of mercy
Added tanween kasra to show this is being described by the word وٰسِعَةٍ (vast) that follows it

steps.m: to be spacious or contain plenty

steps.n: Changed from action to describing word by adding alif after first letter. This word describes Allah's mercy (رَحْمَةٍ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added ة to match with رَحْمَةٍ which is feminine

steps.m: spacious, vast (feminine, describing an undefined noun)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this describes an undefined noun (رَحْمَةٍ). The alif changed to a long alif (ٰ) for writing style.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This word negates the verb يُرَدُّ (is returned) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Converting active verb to passive voice through internal vowel changes
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show emphasis
The يُ shows this happens in the present/future
Changes from verb to noun meaning a single instance of intensity/strength
Added damma to show this word is doing the action (it's the subject of يُرَدُّ - is turned back)
Added هُۥ to show the might belongs to him. This complete word is the subject of يُرَدُّ (is turned back).
This word shows movement or action away from something
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْقَوْمِ (the people). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added ال to make it specific ('the people') and ِ at the end because it follows عَنِ

steps.m: to do wrong or commit crimes

steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle shows who does the action, like changing 'crime' to 'criminal'
Adding ين makes it plural (more than two). This word describes 'the people' (القوم) in this ayah.
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific (like 'the' in English), and َ shows its connection to القوم
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و changes to ا in past tense.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و.

steps.m: he will say (subject: those who associated partners with Allah)
steps.n: Added سَ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future. This refers to what the polytheists will say in response.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to the relative form
Adding ال with a shadda on the ذ makes it specifically refer to someone or something

steps.m: those who (subject of سَيَقُولُ - will say)
steps.n: Adding ين at the end makes it plural, referring to multiple people. This word is the subject of سَيَقُولُ (they will say) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning changes the meaning from general 'joining things' to specifically joining/associating others with Allah in worship
Added وا to show multiple people did this action, with a small mark (۟) on top to help with recitation

steps.m: if (introducing what the disbelievers will say)
steps.n: A conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios. In this ayah, it introduces what the disbelievers will claim about their shirk (associating partners with Allah), suggesting 'if Allah had willed...'
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
The weak letter ي changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Changed إِلَه to اللَه by adding ال and merging letters. This special word refers only to The One True God in Islam.
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, changed initial alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ), and added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in شَآءَ (willed/wished)
Basic negative particle used to negate what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates أَشْرَكْنَا (we associated partners) - meaning 'we did not associate partners'.
The alif is written in an elongated form (آ) instead of regular form (ا). This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Adding أ (hamza) at the start makes it mean 'to make something join/partner with something else'
Adding نا suffix creates first person plural past tense
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Changed alif to long alif for emphasis in pronunciation

steps.m: and not (negating what follows in أَشْرَكْنَا)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous statement. This word negates the action in أَشْرَكْنَا (we associated partners)
Root 'father' changes to make many fathers/ancestors - like changing 'dad' to 'dads' but in Arabic style
Added first person plural possessive pronoun to indicate 'our fathers'. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb أشرك (to associate partners with Allah) in the negative statement.
This is the basic negation particle that means 'not' or 'no'

steps.m: and not (connected to أَشْرَكْنَا - we associated partners)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous statement. This connects to the earlier verb أَشْرَكْنَا (we associated partners), showing what else didn't happen.
The doubled middle letter shows the action is done thoroughly or to many things
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to the word شَىْءٍ (thing) to show 'of/from any thing'

steps.m: to be a thing or exist

steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it into a noun meaning 'thing'
Added tanween kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِن (meaning 'of/from'). The ى is just a spelling variation of ي.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا (this). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this case.
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ makes it mean 'that specific thing' instead of just 'that'
Adding كَ at the beginning changes the meaning to 'like that' or 'similarly'. This word connects the previous statement about what the disbelievers will say to what happened with previous nations.
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Added shadda to show strong emphasis and intensity of the denial. This word relates to how previous nations strongly denied their messengers, as mentioned in the context of this ayah.
Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at beginning to make it specific. The ل combines with ذ to make ذّ (shown by the shadda). This word is the subject (doer) of يَقُولُ (will say) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with the word شَىْءٍ (anything) to mean 'of anything'
This is a noun used as an adverb to show time
Root letters got vowels (fatha-sukun) to make a word meaning 'before' in time/space
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by something
Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'before them' or 'their before'
This is a basic preposition showing where something ends. It has no root since it's a primitive word.
Added vowel mark and shadda to show correct pronunciation
Added the elongated alif (ىٰ) at the end which is part of its standard spelling. In this ayah, it shows when the disbelievers 'tasted' (ذَاقُوا۟) Allah's punishment.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense singular form
Added long alif (ا) after first letter to make common past tense pattern
Added plural ending وا to show multiple people did the action. In this ayah, they are experiencing Allah's punishment (بَأْسَنَا)

steps.m: to be strong, mighty, or severe
steps.n: This form turns the concept of being strong into a noun meaning strength or might
Adds نَا at the end to show the meaning belongs to 'us/our'
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
