Changed the root into a command by adding the 'oo' sound (damma and waw)
Adding the silent mark (sukun) to make it a firm command

steps.m: do you have? (question to those who claimed in previous ayah)
steps.n: This is a question word that asks for a yes/no answer. Here it's asking the disbelievers if they have any knowledge to support their claims mentioned in the previous ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it a preposition. This word shows location or possession in relation to something.
Added كُم at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, Allah is telling the Prophet to address the disbelievers about what knowledge they have.
This is a connecting word (preposition) that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something. Here it connects 'knowledge' (عِلْمٍ) to 'with you' (عِندَكُم).
The shadda (ّ) is added to help pronunciation when certain letters come before مِن. It's like when we say 'an apple' instead of 'a apple' in English - it just sounds better!
This is the root that carries the basic meaning of knowing or having knowledge
Adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter changes this from an action (knowing) to a thing (knowledge)
The word has a tanwin (double kasra) at the end which makes it mean 'a/any knowledge'. It comes after مِنْ which gives it the 'i' sound at the end.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make the verb causative - changing 'exit' to 'make exit' (bring out)
Added تُ prefix to show 'you' as the doer
Added و to show multiple people doing the action
Added هُ to show what is being brought out

steps.m: and so you all bring it out
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this action to what came before
Added fatha to the basic particle ل which means 'for' or 'to'
Added نَ to show this is about 'us'
Added alif at the end to lengthen the final vowel sound
This is a negative particle that negates the verb تَتَّبِعُونَ (you follow). In this context, it's saying 'you do not follow'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled the middle letter to show doing the action thoroughly
Added ون to change from 'she follows' to 'you all follow'
This is the base form of the exception particle that tells us something is being left out or is different from what was mentioned before
Added alif at the end to complete the particle. In this ayah, it's showing an exception to what the disbelievers will say - they only follow assumptions.
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show doubled final letter. This word is what is being followed (object) in تَتَّبِعُونَ (you follow).
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific assumption. The word is being followed (object of تَتَّبِعُونَ - you follow).
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition, like saying 'if something happens' in English
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
This is a basic pronoun used to address multiple people. It doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: you all (when speaking to a group)
steps.n: The م with sukun at the end makes it clear we're talking to multiple people, not just one person.
This is a particle that creates exceptions - it tells us that what comes before it doesn't apply to what comes after it. Here it's saying they follow nothing except assumptions.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added تَ at start to mean 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense action
Added ون suffix to show multiple people are doing the action. This word appears in a context where Allah is addressing those who make claims without evidence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) adding damma on first letter, 2) adding sukun on second letter, and 3) dropping the weak letter و. This tells one person to do something.
Starting with the basic word Allah/God and adding the prefix ل which shows something belongs or goes to Allah
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation: kasra under first lam, shadda on second lam, and kasra at the end
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. This word shows that the conclusive proof belongs to Allah.
Connected the root letters and added the shadda to show emphasis on the middle letter
Changed from a verb (he proved) to a noun (proof) by adding 'u' sound and 'ah' ending
Adding 'al' at the start makes it specific - not just any proof, but the proof
The dot on top (damma) shows this word is doing something - here it's the main thing being described

steps.m: to reach or arrive at completion

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he reached'
Changed to active participle form to describe 'the proof' (الحجة)
Added feminine marker to match with الحجة which is feminine
Added the definite article to match with الحجة
Changed to match with الحجة (the proof/argument) by adding the -u ending. Also uses the special Quran writing style for the long 'aa' sound.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce this word that introduces an unlikely or impossible condition
Added فَ (fa) at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about Allah having the conclusive argument.

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past tense
The weak letter hamza (ء) combines with alif to make a different spelling

steps.m: to guide or show the right path

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) between root letters and changed the ending ي to ى to make 'he guided' in past tense

steps.m: he would surely guide (in response to لَوْ/if)
steps.n: Added لَ to show this is the result of a condition (if). This is connected to لَوْ (if) earlier in the verse.

steps.m: he would surely guide you all
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being guided (you all). The small alif (ٰ) is added before كُمْ to show the sound clearly.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning changes it from 'gather' to 'cause to gather'
Added ين at the end to make it mean 'all of them' instead of 'gather!'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by dropping first vowel and changing middle vowel to 'oo' sound to make it a command
The middle weak letter و is dropped to make the command shorter and easier to say
Added vowels to make it a command form. This word is used after قُلْ (say) to tell others to do something.
The shadda makes this a stronger, more emphatic command, like saying 'Come on!' or 'Come here right now!' in English. It follows قُلْ (say) as part of telling others to bring their witnesses.

steps.m: to witness or testify to something you've seen or know

steps.n: Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb meaning 'he witnessed'
Changed from a past tense verb (he witnessed) to a noun meaning 'witness' by adding ي in the middle
Changes from single witness to multiple witnesses by adding اء and changing vowels
Added كُمُ at the end to show these witnesses belong to multiple people being addressed
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'. The ل gets a shadda because it comes after ٱ.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is connected to يَشْهَدُونَ (they testify) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from singular (he) to plural (they) by adding ونَ at the end
Basic particle that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'that' in English when reporting what someone said
Added fatha mark on first letter to show correct pronunciation
Changed from 'that' to a stronger 'indeed/that' by adding shadda. It connects to what comes after - the thing being testified about.
Added ال and merged letters to create the proper name for the One True God. The hamza is dropped in this special word.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being witnessed about)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show this is the object of يَشْهَدُونَ (they testify)
Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it mean 'he forbade' in the past tense
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter makes the meaning stronger, showing something is completely or definitively forbidden
Base demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for something close by
Added ها (shortened to هٰ) at the beginning to draw attention to what's being pointed to, like saying 'look, this' or 'here, this'

steps.m: this (pointing to something in the ayah)
steps.n: Added fatha mark to show proper pronunciation. In this ayah, it's pointing to what was mentioned before about Allah's prohibitions.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if they testify'

steps.m: then if/and if (connects to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. It's like saying 'then' or 'so' in English to show what comes next.

steps.m: to witness/testify to something seen or known

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word relates to يَشْهَدُونَ (they testify) earlier in the verse, showing the same group of witnesses.
This is the basic negation word that stops something from happening

steps.m: so not/then not (connects to شَهِدُوا۟ - they testify)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. Here it connects to 'if they testify' (فَإِن شَهِدُوا۟), showing what not to do as a result.
Added sukun-fatha-sukun pattern to make the basic command form

steps.m: you witness (in response to فَإِن شَهِدُوا۟)
steps.n: Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action. This word comes after 'if they testify' (فَإِن شَهِدُوا۟) in the ayah, showing your response to their testimony.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'with'. It's not derived from a root - it's a standalone word.
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them). The damma (ُ) on ه helps connect it smoothly to م.
Starting with the basic negation word لا that means 'not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement. This connects to the earlier phrase فَلَا تَشْهَدْ (do not testify) to add another command.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' as the doer
Added shadda on middle letter and changed vowel to show intensive following
Changed final vowel to sukun to make it a prohibition (because it comes after لا)
Adding vowel marks to make it a complete past tense verb
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple desires or whims
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the verb تَتَّبِعْ (follow) in the ayah
The basic word ذا (this) changes to ذي to make it refer to someone or something we're describing
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding ال at the beginning makes it point to specific people we're talking about

steps.m: those who (plural) (subject of يَشْهَدُونَ - they testify)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and changed the spelling of ال to match how it's pronounced. This word points to the people who are doing the testifying (يَشْهَدُونَ) in this verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger - it's not just lying, but strongly denying or rejecting

steps.m: they strongly denied/rejected (something as false) (subject of يَعْدِلُونَ)
steps.n: Added plural marker وا with damma before it to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to يَعْدِلُونَ (they associate) later in the verse, showing these are the same people doing both actions.
Changed root into a noun for a single sign by combining letters and adding ة
Adds feminine plural suffix -ات
The prefix ب indicates instrumentality or accompaniment (with/by means of)

steps.m: with Our signs (object that was denied)
steps.n: Added نَا to show possession by Allah (referring to Himself as 'We/Our' for majesty). The ِ before نَا shows this word follows a preposition. This word refers to Allah's signs that were denied by disbelievers.
Starting with the base demonstrative word that means 'the one who'. Added vowel marks to show pronunciation.
Added ن at the end to make it plural, changing from 'the one who' to 'those who'
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. The alif also changes to a special form (ٱ) because it comes after و.
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) that follows it in this ayah, changing its meaning to 'they do not believe'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Adding alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The hamza changes to ؤ after يُ.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to delay or come after something

steps.n: Adding vowels changes it to mean 'being in a state of lateness'
Adding ة makes this word feminine, like saying 'the last one' for a feminine noun
Added بِ ('in/with') at start, ال ('the'), and changed spelling to آخِرَة ('afterlife'). The ِ ending follows بِ
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' (for a group of males or mixed gender)
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This pronoun 'they' is the subject of the verb يَعْدِلُونَ (they associate partners) in this ayah.

steps.m: one who cares for and controls everything
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone's main quality - they do this completely and perfectly

steps.m: by/with the one who cares for and controls everything
steps.n: Added بِ at the start to show how something is done or who it's done through
Added هِمْ at the end to show it's their Lord. This word is connected to يَعْدِلُونَ (they associate), showing who they're associating others with.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ

steps.m: they make equal/fair (referring to those who don't believe in the hereafter)
steps.n: Adds ون to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who don't believe in the hereafter (وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْءَاخِرَةِ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the people)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on second letter, 3) Dropping the middle weak letter و. This word starts a series of divine commands that will be listed.
The root letters changed to make 'he rose' by putting a weak ا at the end instead of و
Added تَ at beginning to change from 'he rose' to 'you come up!' - makes it a command/request to someone
Added وْا at the end to show this command is for a group of people
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together
Added أَ at beginning to mean 'I', changed middle vowel to sukun and final vowel to 'u' to make it present tense 'I recite' instead of past tense 'he recited'
The final و drops because this is a jussive/command form - like saying 'Let me recite'

steps.m: what (connects to حَرَّمَ - he has forbidden)
steps.n: In this ayah, مَا is used to connect 'Come, let me recite' to 'your Lord has forbidden.' It's like saying 'what your Lord has forbidden' in English.

steps.m: to forbid or make something sacred/protected
steps.n: This is the basic concept of making something off-limits or protected
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it 'he forbade' in the past tense - like adding 'ed' in English
Added shadda to show this is a stronger, more emphatic forbidding. This word relates to رَبُّكُمْ (your Lord) who is doing the forbidding.

steps.m: to take care of, raise, look after

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the doer of the action in the sentence
Added كُمْ at the end to show this caretaker belongs to or takes care of you all
The ى changes to يْ before adding attachments. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon you all (what حَرَّمَ/forbade is imposed upon)
steps.n: Added plural 'you' pronoun. In this ayah, this word shows that what Allah forbade (حَرَّمَ) is being placed upon the listeners.
Starting with the first part أَنْ which means 'that' - this introduces what someone says or thinks
Added لَا which means 'not' to show this is about not doing something
The نْ from أَنْ combines with the ل from لَا to make a doubled ل (shown by ّ). This makes it easier to say but doesn't change the meaning.
Noun form meaning the act of associating partners with God
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the letters to make it mean 'he joined/associated'
Changed from past tense (he joined) to present tense (joins) by changing the vowels and adding a sukoon at start
Added تُ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are being addressed
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'

steps.m: with/by him/it (referring to what Allah has forbidden)
steps.n: Added pronoun ه (him/it) with kasra to show it's connected to the preposition. This refers back to what Allah has forbidden mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: with/by him/it (referring to what Allah has forbidden)
steps.n: Added small alif (ۦ) after ه as a pronunciation guide to show the vowel is elongated slightly
Root meaning evolved into the noun meaning 'thing'
The root letters ش-ي-أ were joined together with a fatha (َ) and sukoon (ْ) to make the noun 'thing'
Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show this is the object being acted upon. The hamza (ء) changes to (ٔ) when tanween is added.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he gave birth' to 'parent' by adding an alif after the first letter - this makes it mean 'the one who does the action' (like changing from 'teach' to 'teacher')
The dual suffix ين is added to indicate two parents. This is in the genitive case as it follows the preposition بِ.
The preposition بِ is added to show 'with' or 'by' the parents
Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous phrase

steps.m: to do good, to be beautiful

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'it became good/beautiful'
Added إِ at start and turned the verb into a noun meaning 'doing good' - like changing 'be good' to 'goodness'
Added ًا at the end to show this is the object of what we should do to parents, mentioned in وَبِٱلْوٰلِدَيْنِ (and to parents)
This is a negative particle that prevents something from happening. Here it combines with the verb تَقْتُلُوا۟ (kill) to mean 'do not kill'
Added و at the beginning to connect this prohibition with the previous commands in the verse
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed to present tense by adding ت at start and changing vowels
Added وا at the end to show many people are doing this action
Added vowels to form the basic noun for a single child
Changed from single 'child' to irregular plural 'children' by adding alif at start and lengthening middle
Changed alif to alif maqsurah for writing style, no meaning change

steps.m: your children (referring to multiple people being addressed)
steps.n: Added كُم to show possession by multiple people being addressed
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (أَوْلٰدَكُم), making the م sound stronger
Added sukun at the end to show the word stops here. This word connects to إِمْلٰقٍ (poverty) to mean 'from poverty'

steps.m: to lack resources or become poor

steps.n: Changed from 'becoming poor' (verb) to 'poverty' (noun) by adding إِ at start and ا in middle

steps.m: poverty (as reason for مِنْ - from/because of)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this explains the reason (because of poverty). This connects to مِنْ (from/because of) before it in the ayah.
Basic pronoun meaning 'we' - this is a primitive word without a root
Added emphasis with shadda. This word is connected to نَرْزُقُكُمْ (we provide for you) in the next part of the ayah, showing who is doing the providing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: we provide you all (in response to إِمْلٰقٍ/poverty)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who receives the provision. This is Allah's response to the fear of poverty mentioned earlier in the verse.
Particle used for emphasis with pronouns
Adds third person plural masculine pronoun suffix to complete the emphatic pronoun construction
This is a negative particle that tells people not to do the action that follows (تَقْرَبُوا meaning 'approach')
Adding و connects this prohibition to the previous prohibitions in the verse, making it part of the list of things Allah has forbidden
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at the start and changing vowels shows this is you doing something in the present/future
Adding وا makes this refer to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, it's used with لا to mean 'don't come close'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

Adds alif after first root letter to create active participle describing something that is obscene. The pattern فاعل indicates the doer or bearer of a quality. This word is the direct object of the verb تَقْرَبُوا (do not approach), so it takes the accusative case.
Makes the word plural to show multiple obscene things
Adds 'the' to make it specific

steps.m: the very bad things (object of لَا تَقْرَبُوا - do not approach)
steps.n: This is what the verb 'do not approach' is acting on. The alif in ال changes to a different form (ٱ) and the long a sound is written with ٰ.
This is a basic particle (small word) that means 'what'. It's used to ask questions or connect parts of sentences, like 'what' in English.
Added the fatha mark (َ) above م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'ma'. In this ayah, it connects what became apparent with what remained hidden.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. In this ayah, this word describes ما (what/whatever) that becomes apparent.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. The kasra and sukun marks help with pronunciation.

steps.m: from it/them (referring to الْفَوَاحِشَ - the shameful deeds)
steps.n: Adds ها to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to الْفَوَاحِشَ (the shameful deeds) mentioned earlier in the verse, showing what should be stayed away from.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation of the conjunction

steps.m: and what (appears in ٱلْفَوٰحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ)
steps.n: Added ما which means 'what' to create a relative pronoun. In this ayah, it's used with ٱلْفَوٰحِشَ (shameful deeds) to mean 'what appears of them'

steps.m: it became hidden (describing ما which refers to الْفَوٰحِشَ - shameful deeds)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word describes ما which refers back to الْفَوٰحِشَ (shameful deeds) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added لَا which means 'not'. In this ayah, it negates the verb تَقْتُلُوا۟ (kill), making the meaning 'and do not kill'
Added vowels to make it 'he killed' in the past tense
Adding ت at the start and changing the vowels makes it 'you' in the present tense instead of 'he' in the past

steps.m: you (all) end life violently (connected to لَا meaning 'do not')
steps.n: Adding وا at the end shows that this command is for a group of people. The word comes after لَا which makes it a prohibition - 'do not kill'

steps.m: soul/self (acted on by تَقْتُلُوا - do not kill)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'soul' or 'self'. This is what is being acted on by the verb تَقْتُلُوا (do not kill) in this ayah.
Adding 'the' (ال) and changing ending to show it's receiving the action
Adding a double letter (shadda) to emphasize the meaning
Basic feminine relative pronoun form. The ى is an alternative spelling of ي that appears at the end of words.
Added ٱلْ at beginning and doubled the ت with shadda. This makes it a definite relative pronoun. Here it refers to النَّفْسَ (the soul/person) which is feminine.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The doubled middle letter shows this is a strong prohibition. This word tells what رَبُّكُمْ (your Lord) has strictly forbidden.
Changed إِلَه to اللَه to make it a proper name for The One True God. The ال here is part of the name itself, not just meaning 'the'.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of حَرَّمَ - He forbade)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form (ٱ), and added damma at end to show Allah is the subject doing the action of forbidding (حَرَّمَ) in this verse

steps.m: except (what follows is excluded from the prohibition in تَقْتُلُوا)
steps.n: This is a particle that creates an exception - it tells us that what comes after it is not included in the prohibition mentioned before it. In this ayah, it's excluding legitimate cases from the prohibition of killing mentioned in تَقْتُلُوا (do not kill).
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The doubled letter shows this is a strong/definite truth
The definite article makes the noun definite
Adding the preposition بِ to indicate 'with'
The alif in ال changes to ٱ because it connects to بِ before it, and we add a kasra (ِ) at the end to connect it to the next word
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun ذا (this), we add the alif above (ٰ) to show something farther away
Added لِ to strengthen the meaning of distance, showing we're pointing to something far away
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is being addressed to multiple people. This word is the subject of وَصَّىٰ (he advised/enjoined) in this ayah.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Adding shadda makes the meaning more emphatic, changing from simple 'advise' to 'strongly instruct/enjoin'

steps.m: he strongly instructed you all (object of instruction)
steps.n: Adds كم to show who receives the instruction - in this case, all the people being addressed
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added the pronoun هِ (it/him) to show what the preposition connects to. In this ayah, it refers back to the word شَيْئًا (anything), telling us not to associate anything with Allah.
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a common Arabic particle that expresses hope or expectation. The shadda (ّ) emphasizes the meaning.
Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, it connects to تَعْقِلُونَ (you understand) that follows it, expressing hope that the listeners will understand.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed vowels and added ت at start to show this is happening now and you are doing it
Added ون to show many people are doing this together
This is a negative particle that makes something not happen. Here it tells us not to do the action in the next word (approach).
The و connects this prohibition to the previous commands in the verse
This is the basic meaning of being close to something
This pattern shows that something naturally became close/near, like how something can naturally get closer to something else
Added ت at start to make it 'you/she' and changed vowels to show present tense action
Changed middle vowel from 'u' to 'a' to make it negative command form (meaning 'do not come near')

steps.m: you all come near (negative command: 'do not come near' after وَلَا)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to make it a command for a group ('you all'). When وَلَا comes before it, it means 'do not' - so together it means 'do not come near'
The middle weak letter و changed to ا, which is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle

steps.m: to lack something or someone important

steps.n: Changes the concept of lacking into a person who experiences that lack
Shows this orphan owns or is connected to something (in this case, مَالَ meaning wealth)
Makes it refer to a specific orphan. This word is possessed by مَالَ (wealth) in the phrase مَالَ ٱلْيَتِيمِ (wealth of the orphan)

steps.m: except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a particle that has no root. It is used to exclude something from a general rule or statement. In this ayah, it excludes وُسْعَهَا (one's capacity) from what we burden people with.
This is the feminine relative pronoun meaning 'that' or 'which' when referring to feminine things
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) at the beginning to show how something should be done
Changed the alif to a waslah alif (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word in pronunciation

steps.m: it (feminine, referring to the way/manner in بِٱلَّتِى)
steps.n: This is a personal pronoun that refers back to the feminine word بِٱلَّتِى (the way/manner) mentioned just before it. It shows which way to handle the orphan's property.
This root carries the basic meaning of being good or beautiful
Added vowels to make it into a past tense verb showing that something was good

steps.m: better/best (describing the way of dealing with orphan's property)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show comparison, and changed vowels to show it's describing something. In this ayah, it's describing the best way to handle orphans' property.
Added fatha on first letter and shadda on second letter. This word connects to يَبْلُغَ (he reaches) to show 'until he reaches'.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end. This is a common ending for Arabic particles and prepositions.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added fatha at end to show this is part of a longer phrase
Adds أ prefix to make 'more/most' version of the root meaning
Added هُۥ at the end to show it's 'his' strength/maturity. This whole phrase means 'until he reaches his full maturity' in the ayah.
Added alif and hamza at start to make 'I' form - shows doing something completely/thoroughly
Changed to command form for multiple people
Added و at beginning to connect with previous command

steps.m: to measure out (especially grains or liquids)

steps.n: This form turns the action into a noun meaning the act of measuring. The word is the object of the command 'fulfill' (أَوْفُوا) in this ayah.
Added ال to make it specific and َ to show it's being acted upon by the command 'fulfill' (أَوْفُوا)
Changed the word into a noun meaning the tool used for weighing
Added ال to specify this particular scale
Added و to connect with the previous word ٱلْكَيْلَ (the measure), showing both should be given in full
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb أَوْفُوا (give in full)
Changed the action into a thing - like changing 'be fair' into 'fairness'
Added ال to point to a specific fairness, like saying 'the' in English
Added بِ at start to show how something is done. This word describes how the weighing (أَوْفُوا۟) should be done in the ayah.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it comes after the preposition بِ (with)
Basic negative particle that negates the verb نُكَلِّفُ (We burden) that comes after it. This creates the meaning 'We do not burden'
Added shadda to show the action is done with intensity or emphasis
Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing the action, changed middle vowel to kasra, and added final damma to show present tense. This verb acts on نَفْسًا (a soul) in the ayah.

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, be a self

steps.n: The pattern creates a noun meaning 'soul' or 'self'. This word is what receives the action in the phrase 'We do not burden' (نُكَلِّفُ).
Added ًا at the end to show this soul/person is receiving the action of being burdened. Like saying 'We don't burden a soul' where 'a soul' is what's being affected.

steps.m: except (connects to تَقْرَبُوا - do not approach)
steps.n: This is a complete particle that shows an exception to the rule being discussed. In this ayah, it shows there is one acceptable way to handle an orphan's wealth, connecting back to the command 'do not approach'.
Changes the concept of 'being spacious' into a noun meaning 'capacity/ability' - like how 'wide' becomes 'width'
Adds هَا meaning 'its/her' to show whose capacity. This word is what's being affected by نُكَلِّفُ (We burden) in the phrase 'We do not burden any soul except to its capacity.'
This is a conditional particle that shows when something happens. It's used to connect two actions - when the first happens, the second follows.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make the command form - the middle weak letter و drops out
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this in the past
Changed تُ (I) to تُمْ (you all) to show many people being spoken to instead of one person speaking

steps.m: to act with fairness and balance

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he was fair' to 'be fair!' by adding alif at start and changing middle vowel - makes it a command to one person
Added plural command ending to address multiple people
Added فَ to show this command follows from what was mentioned before
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations
Adding وَ (and) at the beginning changes 'if' to 'even if', making the condition more inclusive

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This is a theoretical form before the weak letter changes.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic for weak letters.
This is a demonstrative word that shows possession or relationship. Here it connects with قُرْبَى (relatives) to mean 'one who has a close relationship' or 'a relative'.

steps.m: to be close to or approach something
steps.n: This root carries the basic idea of nearness or closeness
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the state of being close

steps.m: close relative (in relation to ذَا meaning 'one who has/possesses')
steps.n: Added ى with alif on top to make it refer to a person who has the quality of being close. In this ayah, it comes after ذَا (one who has) to mean 'one who has closeness' = a close relative

steps.m: to make a promise or commitment

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun representing the actual promise or commitment itself
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'by/with' and ِ at end to show connection to next word (اللَّهِ)
Added وَ (and) to connect this phrase with the previous commands in the verse
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Changed from the general word for 'god' to become Allah (the proper name) by adding ال (the) and combining letters

steps.m: of Allah (connected to وَبِعَهْدِ - and by the covenant)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection. This word is connected to وَبِعَهْدِ (and by the covenant) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes it from 'fulfill' to 'make fulfill'
Changed from 'he fulfilled' to 'you fulfill!' by dropping the final ى and adding ُ to make it a command
Added plural command ending to address multiple people
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's a demonstrative
Added لِ to show this is pointing to something far away rather than nearby

steps.m: that is for you all (referring to what was commanded before)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is addressing multiple people (you all)
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowel marks (fatha)
Added shadda to show emphasis and strength in the command
Added alif maksura to show the long vowel sound at the end
Added كُم to show the command was given to a group of people
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added هِ to show what the preposition connects to (like saying 'with it')
Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation helper - it doesn't change the meaning
Particle expressing hope or expectation with 2nd person masculine plural pronoun suffix

steps.m: to remember or keep in mind

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'remind' instead of just 'remember'
Adding تَ at start makes it reflexive - doing the action to oneself
Changed from 'he remembered' to 'you all remember' - the ون ending shows multiple people doing the action

steps.m: you all remember deeply (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this is addressing multiple people
This is a special word that introduces important information and makes it stronger
The و is a coordinating conjunction that connects this clause to what came before
Basic demonstrative word that points to something nearby or something that's about to be mentioned
The small alif (ٰ) above the ه adds emphasis and helps with pronunciation
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added alif at the end to show this is masculine singular. In this ayah, it's pointing forward to 'my path' (صِرٰطِى)
This word means a clear, straight path that's easy to follow
Adding ا makes it follow a pattern that means 'path/way'
Added ى at the end to show the path belongs to me. This path is described as مُسْتَقِيمًا (straight) in the ayah.
This root gives the basic idea of standing or being upright
Basic past tense form - added vowels to make 'he stood'
Added مُسْتَ at the start to mean 'seeking to' or 'wanting to' stand upright - like asking to be straight/correct
Changed vowel to ِ to show this is something that is made straight/upright, not something doing the straightening
Added tanween alif to show this word is describing 'my path' (صِرٰطِى) from the previous word in the ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he followed' to 'follow!' by adding اِ at start and putting a shadda on ت to make it a command
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
Added هُ to show who is being followed

steps.m: so follow him! (command to many people)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to connect this command with what came before it
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb تَتَّبِعُوا۟ (follow) into a prohibition (do not follow)
The وَ connects this prohibition to the previous command فَٱتَّبِعُوهُ (so follow it), making it part of a sequence of instructions
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning and doubled the middle letter to show doing something with extra effort or continuously
Added وا at the end to show that many people are doing the action
Added damma vowels to make the plural form that means multiple paths

steps.m: the paths (that divert from صِرٰطِى/My path)
steps.n: Added ٱل to make it specific paths, doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ٱل, and added fatha at the end because these paths are being avoided (object of تَتَّبِعُوا/follow)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled ر to show something happening to itself
Added فَ at beginning to show this happens as a result of something else. This separation happens away from His path (سَبِيلِهِۦ) mentioned later in the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or what it's done with

steps.m: with you all (separates you from سَبِيلِهِ - His path)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, it shows how following other paths separates you all from Allah's path.
This is a preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. In this ayah, it's used with سَبِيلِهِۦ (His path) to show separation from Allah's path.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic noun form
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added هِۦ at the end to show this is Allah's path
The basic demonstrative pronoun with alif changed to alif waslah to show distance
Added لِ to show this is pointing to something farther away (that instead of this)
Added كُمْ to show this is being addressed to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers back to the straight path (صِرٰطِى) that was just mentioned.
Added fatha and shadda to show strong command. The ي changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.
The small alif on top shows this is a command that extends to others
Added كُم at the end to show the command is directed to a group of people
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected

steps.m: with it/him (referring back to صِرٰطِى - my path)
steps.n: Added هِ which refers back to Allah's straight path (صِرٰطِى) mentioned earlier in the verse. The small ۦ after هِ is just to show proper pronunciation.
This word shows hope or expectation for something to happen. In this ayah, it connects to the next word تَتَّقُونَ (you become mindful), showing hope for that outcome.

steps.m: so that you all (shows hope for تَتَّقُونَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is addressing multiple people. This connects the hope expressed in لَعَلَّ to the group being addressed.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form past tense
Changes from past 'he protected' to present 'you protect' - weak ending ي replaces ى
Adds تَ at start to make it 'you' + doubles ت to mean 'protect yourself'

steps.m: you (all) protect yourselves (so that you may)
steps.n: Changed from singular 'you' to plural 'you all', showing multiple people protecting themselves. Appears after 'so that you may' to show what is hoped for
Verb meaning 'to protect/fear' with 'you all' doing the action
Basic form of the connecting word that shows sequence
Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to complete the word. This word connects what came before with what follows, showing that giving Moses the book was the next step in sequence.
Changed the word to mean 'make someone bring' which becomes 'give'. The ى at the end is because this is a special type of word that ends in ي.
Changed ى back to ي with sukun to prepare for adding 'we'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the giving. In this ayah, we gave something to Moses.

steps.m: Moses (the one who was given الْكِتٰبَ/the Book)
steps.n: This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew/Ancient Egyptian that doesn't come from an Arabic root. In the Quran, it refers to the prophet Moses who received the Torah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book/writing) by adding ا in the middle

steps.m: the Book (what was given to Moses)
steps.n: The ٱلْ makes it 'the book' instead of just any book, and the َ at the end shows it's being acted upon
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed into a noun describing how the action was done. This word describes how Allah gave (ءَاتَيْنَا) the book to Moses - in a complete way.
Added ًا to show this word describes how the action was done. This makes it work like an adverb in English (adding -ly).
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or higher than something else
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing something is on top of or higher than something else. In this ayah, it connects to أَحْسَنَ (did good) to show who received the book.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers back to something mentioned before
Added ال with shadda to make it more specific and definite. The alif at the start has a special shape (ٱ) called hamzat al-wasl that helps with pronunciation.
Added a long vowel mark (ٓ) that shows the sound is stretched slightly. In this ayah, it refers to أَحْسَنَ (best/excellent), describing how the Book was given to Moses in the best way.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he did excellently/in the best way (describing how مُوسَى received ٱلْكِتٰبَ)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show doing something in the best way possible. In this ayah, it describes how Allah gave the Book to Moses in the most complete and perfect way.
Added shadda to show intensive meaning of explaining thoroughly
Changed to noun form showing the result of explaining in detail
Added tanween fatha to show this is an object of the verb آتَيْنَا (We gave)

steps.m: and a detailed explanation (object of آتَيْنَا)
steps.n: Added و to connect this word with what came before it
The doubled ل with the ُ sound makes it mean 'every single thing' without exception
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is 'for' every thing
Added ِ at the end because it's connected to the word شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it. Also added emphasis to the ل at the beginning.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'thing'
The letter ي is written as ى when it comes before hamza
Added tanwin kasra to show this word is owned by كُلِّ (every/all) that comes before it

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Added tanwin (ً) to show this is indefinite, like saying 'a guidance' instead of 'the guidance'
Added وَ (wa) at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this word to تَمَامًا (completion) and تَفْصِيلًا (detailed explanation) that came before it in the ayah.
Changes from verb 'to show mercy' into the noun 'mercy'
Added ة to make it a specific instance of mercy
Added و at beginning to connect it to previous words, and ً at end to show it's an object in the sentence
Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation of the base particle
Added emphasis by doubling the first letter with a shadda

steps.m: surely perhaps they/so that they (connected to يُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who the word refers to. This connects to the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) in the next ayah.
Changed from action to the name of the action itself, like changing 'meet' to 'meeting'
Added بِ to show this meeting is how or through what means they believe (يُؤْمِنُونَ)
Added ِ (kasra) at the end to show this word connects to 'their Lord' (رَبِّهِمْ) that comes right after it

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing and care
Adding kasra to prepare for the possessive pronoun
Adding هِمْ shows this is 'their Lord'. This word is the object that 'they believe in' according to the next word يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The hamza changed to ؤ because it came after damma.

steps.m: they believe (referring to those who meet their Lord mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers back to those mentioned in لَّعَلَّهُم (so that they) earlier in the verse.
Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha marks to show proper pronunciation. The alif with overbar extends the 'aa' sound.

steps.m: and this (points to كِتٰبٌ - the Book)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning. This word points to كِتٰبٌ (the Book) mentioned right after it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'wrote' to noun meaning 'written thing'

steps.m: a book (subject of أَنزَلْنٰهُ - we sent it down)
steps.n: Added tanween damma (two small dashes) to show this is 'a book' and it's doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding أ (hamza) at the start changes 'to descend' into 'to make something descend/send down'
Changed from 'he sent down' to 'we sent down' by adding نا at the end - this نا means 'we' and makes it a group action

steps.m: we sent it down (referring to كِتٰبٌ/the book)
steps.n: Added 'hu' (meaning 'it') to the end to show what was sent down (referring back to the book/kitab)

steps.m: to increase, grow, or bring forth good

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding alif makes the blessing more emphatic/strong
Changed from 'blessed' to 'being blessed' by adding مُ at start - like changing from doing to receiving
Added tanween dammah to show this describes the book (كِتٰبٌ) mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added اِتّ at the start to make it stronger - like going from 'follow' to 'follow very closely'
Changed from past tense 'he followed' to a command 'you follow!' by changing the middle vowel from 'a' to 'i' and ending vowel to 'u'
Added plural command ending
Added هُ to show what is being followed

steps.m: so follow him/it! (command to many people)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect with previous statement, showing this is a result or consequence
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The word changed to mean protecting oneself by adding اِت at the start, then ت and ق merged together with a shadda
Changed from 'he protected' to 'you all protect!' - the وا at the end makes it a command for a group
Adds conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
This is a particle that shows hope or expectation for what follows. Here it connects to تُرْحَمُونَ (you will receive mercy) to show the hoped-for result.

steps.m: so that you all (might receive تُرْحَمُونَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to a group of people being spoken to. This connects to تُرْحَمُونَ (you will receive mercy) to mean 'so that you all might receive mercy'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show receiving the action instead of doing it
Added تُ at beginning to make it 'you' instead of 'he' and changed to present tense
Added ون at the end to show this applies to a group
This is a particle that introduces what someone might say or do
Added vowels to connect letters. The middle و changes to ا in past tense - this is a common pattern with hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و in present tense.
Added وا at the end to show we're talking to multiple people
The small mark above ا shows the vowel is stretched out
The small circle helps with pronunciation
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger

When we add مَآ to إِنَّ, it changes the meaning from just emphasis to showing that something is limited or restricted. In this ayah, it's used before أُنزِلَ (was sent down) to limit what was sent down.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show causing something to happen

steps.m: was sent down (referring to الْكِتٰبُ - the Book)
steps.n: Changed vowels to show that the action was done to something rather than someone doing it. This word refers to الْكِتٰبُ (the Book) being sent down.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Forms a noun indicating the result/object of writing
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
The word 'book' gets a u-mark (ُ) at the end because it's doing the action (it was sent down)
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or above something else. It's one of the most common Arabic words.
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to 'two groups' (طَآئِفَتَيْنِ) to tell us the Book was sent down upon/to two groups.

steps.m: to move around or circle something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle form by adding alif after first letter and kasra after second letter
Added feminine marker ة to create a noun for a group
Added dual marker to show exactly two groups
This is a preposition that shows the starting point or source
Added the kasra (ِ) under م to show how this word is pronounced. This word connects to قَبْلِنَا (before us) to say 'from before us'.
Root letters ق-ب-ل got vowels (َ and ْ) to make the word 'before' - like saying 'earlier than'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to something that owns or possesses it
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'before us'
This is a basic word that shows a condition - it tells us 'if' something happens, something else will follow
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Changed the root letters to make 'he was' by adding fathas (the small diagonal lines above)
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action in the past
This is a preposition that shows what we were away from or unaware about. In this ayah, it connects to the word دِرَاسَتِهِمْ (their study) to show what people were unaware of.

steps.m: to study, learn, or examine deeply

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun that means the action itself
Changes the word to show it's connected to something coming after it
Adds هِمْ at the end to show it belongs to them
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action. The word describes someone characterized by unawareness.
Added ين to make it plural. This describes multiple people who are unaware.
Added ل at beginning to emphasize that they were definitely unaware
Added vowel marks and ٰ above ا. The ينَ ending shows this describes multiple people who are heedless. The َ at the end shows it follows كُنَّا (we were)
Basic connecting word that gives choices between options

steps.m: or (connecting تَقُولُوا۟ with what came before)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و. In this ayah, it connects 'you say' with previous possible actions.
Connected the root letters together. The middle weak letter و stays because it's followed by a damma.
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action in the present/future
Added وا at the end to show many people are doing the action. The word appears after أَوْ (or) and shows purpose/result.

steps.m: if only (introduces a hypothetical wish in أَنَّآ أُنزِلَ - 'if only it had been sent down')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces an impossible or unfulfilled wish. In this ayah, it's used to express the hypothetical wish of having received revelation earlier. The vowel marks help show it's a complete word, not part of another word.
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more certain
Adding vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Adding آ (alif) at the end changes 'that' to 'that we' - the long vowel shows it's about us
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding 'أ' at the start makes it mean 'to cause something to descend' or 'to send down' instead of just 'to descend'

steps.m: was sent down (referring to الْكِتٰبُ - the Book)
steps.n: Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it. This word relates to الْكِتٰبُ (the Book) which was sent down.
This is a preposition that shows something being on top of or over something else. The root letters combine to form the basic preposition.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun. This is a common change in Arabic when adding pronouns to words ending in ى.

steps.m: upon us (object of تَقُولُوا - you say)
steps.n: The نا at the end means 'us', showing who is being acted upon. In this ayah, it's connected to the verb تَقُولُوا (you say), showing what you're saying about.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed the vowels and added alif to make it into a noun meaning the result of writing
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Changed the 'a' sound to be longer (ٰ) and added 'u' sound at the end to show this word is doing the action
Basic meaning showing existence or state of being
Changes root 'to be' into a command by adding damma (u) and sukun (stop mark)
Adds shadda and fatha to change from 'you be!' to 'we would be' - like changing from giving an order to talking about what could happen
Adds لَ to start of word to add emphasis, like saying 'indeed' or 'truly'
Adds ا at the end to emphasize 'we' even more

steps.m: to guide or show the way
steps.n: Basic meaning of guidance
Basic verb 'guide' turned into past tense 'guided' by adding vowels. The ي at the end becomes ى when writing the past tense.

steps.m: he caused to be guided/he made guide
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes meaning from 'guide' to 'cause to guide' or 'make guide'
Adding ى at end shows this is past tense and ends in a special 'aa' sound
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'
Added pronoun هُمْ (them) to show who is being referred from
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
The alif is lengthened to show the action happened with more emphasis
Added كُم at the end to show the action was done to multiple people being spoken to
Changed from basic root 'to make clear' into an adjective meaning 'very clear' by doubling the middle letter
Added feminine ending to make it a noun meaning 'clear evidence'
Added tanween (double damma) to make it indefinite - meaning 'a' clear evidence
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its source. In this ayah, it connects to رَّبِّكُمْ (your Lord) to show something coming from your Lord.
The doubled م (shown by ّ ) makes the word stronger, like saying 'directly from' instead of just 'from'

steps.m: to care for, nurture, help grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show this describes someone who does this action a lot
Added a shadda on the first letter to show extra emphasis, like saying 'the one and only Lord'
Added kasra to show this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Added كُمْ to show this is 'your Lord' when talking to many people

steps.m: to guide or show the way
steps.n: These are the three basic letters that give us the core meaning of guidance
The damma (ُ) on first letter and the ى at the end change this from an action (to guide) into a thing (guidance)
The tanween (ً) shows this is talking about guidance in general, not a specific instance of guidance
The وَ connects this word to the previous word بَيِّنَةٌ (clear proof), showing that both came to them
These letters together represent the basic concept of showing mercy
The root letters get vowels (َ and ْ) to form the noun 'mercy'
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific instance of mercy
Adding tanween makes it 'a mercy' instead of 'the mercy'
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it in the sentence

steps.m: who/whoever (asking about the subject of أَظْلَمُ - more wrong)
steps.n: This is a question word asking about people. In this ayah, it's asking 'who is more wrong?' connecting to the word أَظْلَمُ that follows it.

steps.m: so who/then whoever (asking about the subject of أَظْلَمُ - more wrong)
steps.n: The فَ shows this is a conclusion or result following from the previous statement about evidence (بَيِّنَةٌ) coming to them. It's saying: 'Since clear proof has come, then who could be more wrong than...'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: more unjust (than those who مِمَّن كَذَّبَ - who denied)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end to make it comparative, showing something is 'more' than something else. In this ayah, it's asking who is more unjust than those who denied Allah's signs.
Starting with the two separate words that will combine. In this ayah, مِن means 'than' because it's used for comparison, while مَن means 'who'.

steps.m: than who (comparing with the people mentioned before)
steps.n: When مِن and مَن come together, the ن from the first word combines with the م from the second word. This puts a shadda (ّ) on the م to show it's doubled.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he strongly denied/rejected (بِـَٔايٰتِ ٱللَّهِ - the signs of Allah)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter ذ makes the denial stronger and more emphatic. This word connects to 'signs of Allah' (بِـَٔايٰتِ ٱللَّهِ) in the ayah, showing what was being denied.
Forms the singular noun for 'sign' from the root meaning
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs
Adds بِ at the beginning to show these signs are being used or dealt with in some way
Adds ِ (kasra) at the end because it connects to 'Allah' right after it - showing these are Allah's signs
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters
Added ِ to show possession, shadda for emphasis, and changed initial alif to wasl form. This word shows who the signs belong to in the phrase 'signs of Allah'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This word is related to يَصْدِفُونَ (they turn away) later in the ayah, showing the same action.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it
This is a preposition that shows moving away from something or turning away from it. In this ayah, it's used with صَدَفَ (turned away) to show turning away from Allah's signs.
Added ها which refers back to Allah's signs (آيَات) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows what people are turning away from.

steps.m: to give what is deserved (good or bad)

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: we will give what is deserved (to الَّذِينَ يَصْدِفُونَ)
steps.n: Added سَ at beginning to show future tense. This word refers to how Allah will recompense those who turn away from His signs.
Added ن to make it plural. This changes it from referring to one person/thing to referring to many people/things.
Added ٱلَّ at the beginning to make it specific. The shadda (ّ) appears because ل is followed by ذ.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding ي at the start shows this is happening now

steps.m: they turn away (referring to those who reject Allah's signs)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding ون at the end, showing multiple people turning away instead of just one person

steps.m: away from (connected to يَصْدِفُونَ - they turn away)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something moving away from something else. In this ayah, it's used with the verb يَصْدِفُونَ (they turn away) to show what they're turning away from.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Changes from a verb root into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Makes the word plural by adding ات at the end and changing the middle vowel
Adds نَا at the end to show these are Allah's signs being mentioned in the verse. This word is being denied/rejected by the disbelievers as shown by the verb كَذَّبَ (denied/rejected).
This root gives us words about badness, harm, and evil
Changed the root into a noun that means the quality or state of being bad
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of سَنَجْزِى (We will recompense). The long alif (ٓ) helps with pronunciation.
Changes from verb 'to punish' into noun meaning 'punishment'
Added definite article ٱلْ and changed the ending to show this word is possessed by سُوٓءَ (worst) in the phrase 'worst punishment'
The word 'punishment' is owned by something else (shown by the kasra mark at the end)
This is the basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'

steps.m: because of what (they were turning away from)
steps.n: Adding بِ at the beginning changes 'what' to 'because of what'. In this ayah, it refers to the reason for their punishment, which was their turning away from Allah's signs.
This root represents the basic concept of existing or being in a state
The root letters ك-و-ن become كَانَ (past tense 'he was') by changing و to ا - this is common for words with و in the middle
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is the subject of يَصْدِفُونَ (they turn away) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they turn away (from آيَاتِنَا - Our signs)
steps.n: The ون shows multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, they are turning away specifically from Allah's signs (آيَاتِنَا)
This is a question word that changes a statement into a yes/no question. In this ayah, it's asking if they are waiting/looking for something.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows this is happening now (present tense) and 'he' is doing it

steps.m: they look/watch (referring to the disbelievers waiting for signs)
steps.n: Changed from 'he looks' to 'they look' by adding ون at the end - shows multiple people are doing the action
Basic exceptive particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before
Added elongated alif (آ) at the end to add emphasis, like saying 'except indeed' or 'only truly'
This is a connecting word that introduces what is being waited for or expected. In this ayah, it connects to the next word تَأْتِيَهُمُ (them coming), showing what the people are waiting for.
Connected the root letters together
Added ت at beginning to show present tense feminine/second person. This verb describes what those who are 'waiting' (يَنظُرُونَ) are waiting for.
Added هُمُ at the end to show who is being come to (them)

steps.m: to have power or authority over something

steps.n: Changed root 'to rule/have power' into a noun meaning 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a
Added letters to make the singular 'angel' into multiple 'angels' (broken plural form)
Added feminine ending to complete the plural form
Added ال to make it definite (specific angels)
Added final damma to show this is the subject doing the action in تَأْتِيَهُمُ (comes to them)

steps.m: or (connecting يَأْتِىَ رَبُّكَ with what comes after)
steps.n: This is a basic conjunction particle that connects alternative possibilities. Here it connects 'your Lord comes' with other possibilities mentioned after. Not derived from a root.

steps.m: or (connecting يَأْتِىَ رَبُّكَ with what comes after)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و
Connected the root letters together
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and 'he' as subject. Added vowel marks to show pronunciation.
Added fatha at end to show subjunctive mood

steps.m: one who cares for and helps grow
steps.n: Added fatha to first letter and doubled the middle letter to show intensity of the caring
The ُ shows this is the doer of the action 'comes' in the ayah
The suffix ك indicates possession/ownership by a male addressee. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَأْتِىَ (comes/arrives).
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two possibilities: either the angels will come to them OR their Lord will come. The vowel marks help show how it connects these two options.
Added يَ at beginning to show 'he' in present/future tense. The أْ has sukun to show it's not pronounced with a vowel.
Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this is in active voice
Added fatha and sukun to connect the letters and make it into a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'
Added damma at the end to show this word owns or possesses what comes after it. In this ayah, it's part of the phrase 'some of the signs'.

steps.m: to show, to be clear, to be apparent

steps.n: Adding ة turns this into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Changes from single sign/verse to multiple signs/verses by adding ات at the end
Adds kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by 'your Lord' (رَبِّكَ) in the ayah
The doubled middle letter shows intensity, indicating complete mastery and lordship. Added fatha on first letter to make it a noun.
Added kasra under the second letter to show this word is owned by something
Added كَ (ka) at the end to mean 'your' - showing the Lord belongs to you (if you're male). Works with ءَايٰتِ to mean 'signs of your Lord'
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun

steps.m: on the day (when يَأْتِى some signs come)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَأْتِى (comes) in this ayah
Added يَ at beginning to show 'he' in present tense. Added sukun (ْ) to help pronunciation.
Added ى at end to show present tense form

steps.m: to divide or separate into parts

steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it into a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'
Added damma at the end to show this word is doing the action in يَأْتِى (comes)

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Adding ة changes this into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Changed to plural form by adding ات and replacing the ة. The alif is written as ٰ when followed by ت.

steps.m: signs or miracles (possessed by رَبِّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by 'your Lord' (رَبِّكَ) in the ayah
Connected the root letters and added shadda (double letter) to show intensity of nurturing/lordship
Added kasra to show this word is owned by or connected to something that follows it
Added كَ to show the lord belongs to the person being addressed. This word is possessed by ءَايٰتِ (signs) in the phrase 'signs of your lord'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَنفَعُ (benefit) into 'will not benefit'. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Basic meaning of bringing good or advantage to someone
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense
Added يَ at start to show present tense and ُ at end to show this is the doer. This verb is negated by لا before it in the ayah, so it means 'does not benefit'
Adding the vowels creates a noun meaning the essence or core of something that breathes or is precious

steps.m: a soul (being acted upon by يَنفَعُ - benefits)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of the verb يَنفَعُ (benefits). It's indefinite (any soul) rather than a specific one.
Connected the root letters together
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the act or state of believing
Changed the ending to show this word is doing the action (like adding 'the' in English), with a slight spelling change
Added feminine possessive pronoun هَا to show this faith belongs to a feminine noun (نَفْسًا - soul) mentioned in the ayah
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَنفَعُ) from 'benefits' to 'does not benefit'. It also changes the verb's time to the past.
Connected the root letters with damma on first letter to create basic present tense form
Added تَ at beginning to make it mean 'she/it is' (present tense verb)
The و is dropped and final vowel changed to sukun because it comes after لَمْ which makes verbs jussive (like a command or negative past)
This root carries the basic meaning of trust and security
Adding hamza and alif at start changes meaning to actively putting trust in something, which evolved to mean 'believe'
Adding ت with sukūn at the end shows a female subject did this action in the past
This is a basic preposition that connects with قَبْلُ (before) to show the time reference point. Together مِن قَبْلُ means 'from before' or 'previously'.
This forms a noun that works like an adverb to tell when something happened
The ُ at the end shows this word is being used as an adverb to tell when something happened

steps.m: before (referring to previous time in this ayah)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this word is being used as an adverb to tell when something happened - specifically referring to the time before signs come in this verse
This is a basic connecting word that shows there are different possibilities. In this ayah, it connects different things that could come: the angels, your Lord, or some signs from your Lord.
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: she earned (referring to نَفْسًا - a soul)
steps.n: Adding ت at the end shows a feminine subject did the action. Here it refers back to نَفْسًا (a soul) mentioned earlier in the verse.

This is a preposition that shows location or position. It connects with the word إِيمٰنِهَا (their faith) to show what someone has faith in. The long vowel ى with ٓ (called alif maqsurah) is just how this word is written.

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'faith' by adding إِي at start and ا after second root letter
The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) when followed by certain suffixes
Added ِهَا to show this faith belongs to her (the soul mentioned in the verse)
Added short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to the root letters to make the word mean 'good' as a noun/adjective
Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of يَنفَعُ (benefits/helps)
The root letters ق-و-ل become قُل when making a command - just drop the و and add ُ (damma) to tell someone 'say!'

steps.m: say! (as a command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to connect it smoothly with the next word in the ayah (انتَظِرُوٓا۟)
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Form VIII (adding اِنْ) changes 'watching' into 'waiting while watching for something to happen'
Adding damma (ُ) at the end makes it present tense
Added plural command ending to address multiple people
Word is already in its final form with plural command ending
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more emphasized, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English
Added ا at the end to show this is about 'we/us'. This connects to the word منتظرون (waiting) that comes after it in the ayah, emphasizing that 'we are definitely waiting'

steps.m: to watch or wait for something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding اِنْتَ at the beginning changes 'watching' into 'waiting with expectation or hope for something'
Changed to active participle form to describe someone who does this action
Added ون to show this refers to a group of people waiting
This is a basic particle that adds emphasis to what follows. Here it emphasizes 'those who' (ٱلَّذِينَ).
Added the kasra (ِ) under the first letter and shadda (ّ) on the second letter to show this is a strong emphasis.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to what's being talked about
Added ال with a shadda to make it more specific
Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word connects to فَرَّقُوا (they divided) in the ayah, describing who did the dividing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter (ّ) shows this was done with extra force or many times. This relates to how they divided their religion into many parts, as mentioned in دِينَهُمْ that follows in the ayah.
The suffix وا shows a group of people (they) did this action. This word describes what the people mentioned earlier (ٱلَّذِينَ) did to their religion.
The doubled ر shows this was done multiple times or with intensity
Added kasra (ِ) under first letter and long vowel ي to make it a noun meaning the system of beliefs itself rather than the action of following beliefs

steps.m: their religion (object of فَرَّقُوا - they divided)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ at the end to show it belongs to them. The fatha (َ) comes before هُمْ to connect it smoothly. This word is what they divided in the phrase 'they divided their religion'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form. The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to the previous verb فَرَّقُوا۟ (they divided)

steps.m: to follow or spread out in groups

steps.n: Root letters ش-ي-ع became a broken plural noun شِيَع meaning groups/sects by adding special vowel marks
Added tanween fatha to show this word is receiving the action of 'dividing' (فَرَّقُوا) in the verse
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
Adding vowel marks to show how to pronounce this negation word correctly
Added تَ to show 'you' and dropped the weak letter ي
Added shadda for emphasis
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'

steps.m: from them (referring to those who divided their religion)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to. This connects back to 'those who divided their religion' mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: in (connects شَىْءٍ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects the word شَىْءٍ (thing) to the previous part of the sentence.

steps.m: in (connects شَىْءٍ to what came before)
steps.n: The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowels to the root letters to make the common noun meaning 'thing'
The ي changes to ى and we add ٍ (kasratan) at the end because this word comes after the word في (in/concerning) in the ayah
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
Adding ما changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction, showing that what follows is the only possibility

steps.m: to command, direct, or deal with matters

steps.n: This pattern turns the verb into a noun meaning the action itself or result of the action
Added هُمْ to show this belongs to them (a group). This word is the subject of the verb يُنَبِّئُهُم (He will inform them) in this ayah.
This is a preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to show that something is going towards/to Allah.
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one and only God
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to 'their matter' in the ayah. Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.
Starting with the basic particle that connects ideas

steps.m: then/thereafter (connecting to يُنَبِّئُهُم - He will inform them)
steps.n: Dots and marks added to show this word means 'then' - it connects one action to the next action
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) between the root letters to make it mean 'he told news'
