Doubled the middle letter to show doing something with extra effort or repeatedly
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added هُم to show who receives the action. This connects to ما (what) later in the ayah which shows what they are being informed about.
This is a question word that asks about things or can refer to whatever/what

steps.m: with/by what (they used to do يَفْعَلُونَ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show how or by what means something happens. In this ayah, it connects to يَفْعَلُونَ (they used to do) to explain they will be informed about their actions.

steps.m: he was (part of فَرَّقُوا۟ - they divided)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to make the past tense. The و changes to ا because it's in the middle of the word. This word connects to فَرَّقُوا۟ (they divided) in this ayah.

steps.m: they were (part of فَرَّقُوا۟ - they divided)
steps.n: Adding وا at the end makes it talk about a group (they) instead of one person (he). This matches with فَرَّقُوا۟ (they divided) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense form
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on middle letter, and ُ at end

steps.m: they do/act (referring to the group who divided their religion)
steps.n: Adding ون shows that the action is done by a group of people. This refers back to those mentioned earlier in the ayah who divided their religion.

This is a special word that introduces a conditional statement. It refers to any person who might do the action (coming with good deeds in this case). It connects to the verb جَآءَ (came) that follows it.
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter
Extended the alif to show emphasis/intensity of coming
Adding vowel marks changes this into a descriptive word meaning 'good'
Added ة to make it refer to one specific good action
Added ال to make it specific
Added بِ at beginning to show 'with/by'. The اَلْ changes to ٱلْ after بِ.

steps.m: with the good deed (object of جَاءَ)
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show this is the object that someone 'came with' in the verse
This is the basic particle that shows something belongs to or is for someone
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of what came before

steps.m: then for him (connected to عَشْرُ أَمْثَالِهَا - ten times its like)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who receives the reward mentioned later in the ayah (ten times its like)
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic number word

steps.m: ten (subject of فَلَهُۥ - meaning 'then for him is')
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the phrase 'then for him is'
Added ِ (kasra) under first letter and ْ (sukun) on second letter to create the noun meaning 'similar' or 'like'
Changed from singular to plural by adding أ at start and ا after ث, like changing 'example' to 'examples'
Added ِ and هَا at the end. The ِ shows this word belongs to عَشْرُ (ten), and هَا means 'its' referring to الْحَسَنَةِ (the good deed)
This is a question word that became a conditional word meaning 'whoever' or 'whosoever' when used at the start of a condition
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous one about good deeds. This word is the subject (doer) of جَآءَ (came) in this ayah.
Added vowels to make it past tense 'he came'
Added alif to make it a proper past tense verb
Extended the alif to emphasize the action of coming
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The و changes to ي and gets doubled to show strong quality
Added feminine ة ending to create noun meaning 'one instance of badness/evil'
Definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: with the bad deed (object of جَاءَ/came)
steps.n: The word بِ (with) connects this to the verb جَاءَ (came), showing someone came with a bad deed
The word means 'with the bad deed' and is connected to the verb 'came' using بِ (with). The ending ِ (kasra) shows it's affected by the word 'with' before it.
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no'. It's used to negate what comes after it.

Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. In this ayah, it's saying that after mentioning the reward for good deeds, then for bad deeds there is not a similar multiplied punishment.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: he is given what is due
steps.n: The يُ prefix shows someone is receiving the action instead of doing it

steps.m: he is given what is due
steps.n: The ى at the end shows this is a verb ending in a weak letter
This is the basic form of the word that means 'except'. It's a particle that doesn't come from a root.
Adding shadda makes the 'l' sound stronger, which emphasizes the meaning of exception
The alif at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it in the sentence. Here it connects to مِثْلَهَا (its like/equal)
Forms a noun meaning 'equal amount' or 'equivalent'. In this ayah, it refers to the equal punishment for a bad deed.

steps.m: equal to it (referring to السَّيِّئَةِ - the bad deed)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to السَّيِّئَةِ (the bad deed) mentioned earlier. This shows that the punishment will be equal to the bad deed itself.
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation - ُ (damma) shows it's 'hum' and ْ (sukun) shows the م is silent
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is the subject (doer) of لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (they are not wronged) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُجْزَىٰٓ (is rewarded) that comes after it. In this ayah, it's saying someone will not be rewarded except with the equivalent of their bad deed.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The يُ prefix shows someone is receiving the wronging instead of doing it
Adding ون shows multiple people (3 or more men) are being wronged

steps.m: they are being wronged (referring to those who do good deeds)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this is happening to multiple people. This word relates to the earlier part of the verse about those who do good deeds - they won't be wronged in their reward.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is telling one person to do something.
This is a basic emphasis particle that makes statements stronger. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English.
Added نِى at the end to show this is about 'me'. This word connects to هَدَىٰنِى (guided me) in the ayah, showing who was guided.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Added fathas to show past tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end. This word is connected to قُلْ (say) in the ayah.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a long vowel sound
Added نِى at the end to show who was guided (me)

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares for others perfectly and completely
The kasra shows this word connects to something coming after it in the sentence

steps.m: my Lord (the one guiding me in هَدَىٰنِى)
steps.n: Adding ي at the end shows this is MY Lord. This word connects to هَدَىٰنِى (guided me) in the ayah, showing it's the Lord who provides guidance.
Adding the fatha mark at the end shows this word's grammatical role in the sentence
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to the word صِرٰطٍ (path) to show 'to a path'
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end, which is a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning
Adding the basic vowels to make it readable
Adding alif makes it the standard word pattern for paths/ways
Changed the alif to an alif with a small line above (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation that means the same thing

steps.m: a path (that leads to مُّسْتَقِيمٍ - straight)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is referring to any path, which is then described as straight (مُّسْتَقِيمٍ) in the next word
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُستَ at the beginning to show something is becoming or being made straight by itself or something else
The و changes to ي and the vowels adjust to show this is now a description of something that has this quality
Added shadda on م for pronunciation when connecting with previous word
Added tanwin kasra to match the case of the word it describes (صِرٰطٍ - path)

steps.m: to follow/practice a way of life

steps.n: Adding the kasra (ِ) and connecting the letters turns this into a noun meaning the system or way that people follow
The ًا ending shows this is what Allah guided the speaker to - it's the object being acted upon in the phrase 'guided me to a religion'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the first vowel to kasra to transform from verb to noun
Changed middle letter و to ي and added tanween to make it describe دِينًا (religion) as being straight/upright

steps.m: to follow a path or way

steps.n: Added vowels and ة to make it into a noun meaning one specific way or path
Added shadda on م to emphasize the meaning

steps.m: way of life or religion (object of إِلَىٰ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the preposition إِلَىٰ (to) in the phrase 'to a straight path, a right religion'
This is a proper name that doesn't come from an Arabic root. It comes from the Hebrew name meaning 'father of many'.
The fatha at the end shows this is the object of the word مِلَّةَ (way/religion). It means this is Abraham's way or religion being talked about.
Added ي between second and third root letters to make it describe someone who does this action

steps.m: one who turns away (from falsehood) [describing دِينًا]
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the religion (دِينًا) is
This is a particle that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's negating كَانَ (was) in كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (was from the polytheists).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state/condition)

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter in the past tense
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or separation from something
Added َ (fatha) at the end because it connects to the next word ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (the polytheists). This helps make the words flow together smoothly when reading.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
The مُ prefix and vowel change turns the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action

steps.m: the one who associates partners (with God)
steps.n: Makes the noun definite

steps.m: the polytheists (those who associate partners with God)
steps.n: Changes from singular (one person) to plural (many people) by adding ين at the end
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma (ُ) on first letter, and 3) adding sukun (ْ) at the end. This is telling one person to do something.
This is a basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added kasra and shadda to show this is a strong emphasis particle. This particle introduces what follows (صَلَاتِى - my prayer) with certainty.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: prayer (act of directing oneself to Allah)
steps.n: Changes verb 'to direct/bend' into the noun 'prayer' by adding alif and ta marbuta

steps.m: my prayer (belonging to me, dedicated to Allah)
steps.n: The ة changes to ت before adding the possessive ى. This word appears in a list of things the speaker is declaring belong to Allah (لله), showing complete dedication.
Changes from verb 'to perform rituals' to plural noun meaning 'multiple rituals'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added و with fatha to connect this word to صَلَاتِى (my prayer) mentioned before it

steps.m: and my ritual acts of worship (connected to صَلَاتِى)
steps.n: Added ى to show these are my ritual acts, matching with 'my prayer' (صَلَاتِى) mentioned before
Connected the root letters with vowels and added shadda because two identical letters (ي) come together
Changes from 'lived' (verb) to 'my life' (noun) by adding مَ at start and ا at end
Added ى at the end to make it possessive. This word is connected to الله showing that my life belongs to Allah.

steps.m: and my life (connected to لِلَّهِ - belongs to Allah)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this word to the previous words in the ayah, showing that both my life and what came before belong to Allah
Root 'to die' becomes 'death' by adding مَ at start and ا in middle - makes it into a noun meaning 'the state of being dead'
Added و to connect this word with محياى (my life) mentioned before it in the ayah
Added ى at the end to show this is 'my death', connecting it with the speaker
This is Allah's proper name. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.
Added لِ at the beginning to show this belongs to or is for Allah. The first ا is removed when لِ is added.

steps.m: for/to Allah (belongs what was mentioned before)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this connects to what was mentioned before in the ayah (my prayer, my worship, my living, and my dying).

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop

steps.n: Doubling the ب shows intensity of caring/nurturing
The ِ at the end shows this word connects to the next word (العٰلَمِينَ), like saying 'Lord of' instead of just 'Lord'
Creates a noun meaning 'world/realm' - something that can be known or understood
Makes the word plural and shows it's owned by رَبِّ (Lord)
Makes it specific by adding 'the'
Changes alif to long alif (ٰ) for proper writing style
This is one of the main words used to say 'no' or 'not' in Arabic. Here it's saying there is no partner.
Added the fatha mark (َ) to show how to pronounce the first letter correctly
Connected the root letters with basic vowels
Added ي between second and third root letters to show who does the action
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by لا (no) and connects to لَهُ (to Him)
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added هُ at the end to show who/what is being referred to. This connects to the word شَرِيكَ (partner) in the phrase لَا شَرِيكَ (there is no partner), saying there is no partner for Him.
Added a small و mark (ۥ) after هُ which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Adds لِ and كَ to change 'this' to 'that' (pointing to something farther away)
Adds بِ to show how something is done or the means by which it's done

steps.m: and with that (what I was commanded to do)
steps.n: Adds وَ to connect this phrase with what came before. Here it connects to the previous statement about having no partners.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that someone received the action instead of doing it
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' received the action
This is a pronoun that means 'I'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added the small alif (۠) above the final alif. This is just a pronunciation mark that doesn't change the meaning.
Root letters connected with fatha marks to make the word meaning 'first'
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on و makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: the foremost one (of الْمُسْلِمِينَ - the Muslims)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this is the subject. In this ayah, it's describing who is the foremost among the Muslims.

steps.m: to submit, surrender, or make peace

steps.n: The مُ at the start and ِ in the middle work together to show who does the action of submitting
The ين ending shows this refers to three or more men who submit
The ٱلْ makes it specific
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) Dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) Adding damma on first letter, 3) Adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb أَبْغِى (I seek)

steps.m: is it other than? (object of أَبْغِى/I seek)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning makes it a question
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added ال and merged it with the word, doubling the ل with a shadda. This special word refers only to Allah, the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by أَغَيْرَ (other than). The أ at the start changed to ٱ for easier pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it present tense and first person (I)

steps.m: should I seek? (object of قُلْ - say)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question. This word is being asked as part of what to 'say' (قُلْ)
The doubled letter shows this is someone who nurtures and takes care of others completely

steps.m: a lord/master (object of أَبْغِى - I seek)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon by the verb أَبْغِى (I seek) in the question 'Should I seek other than Allah as a lord?'
Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This pronoun is the subject of رَبُّ (Lord) in this ayah.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
The doubled letter shows intensity and completeness of the nurturing quality, making it mean the ultimate caretaker or Lord. This word is connected to كُلِّ شَىْءٍ (of all things) in this ayah.
The damma at the end shows this is the subject (doer) in the sentence
Connected the root letters and added damma to show it's a noun meaning 'all'. The doubled ل shows emphasis.

steps.m: of all/of every/of each (describing شَىْءٍ - thing)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it, like saying 'of all' instead of just 'all'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun form. The ي becomes ى when written but keeps the same sound.
Added the genitive case ending ٍ because this word is owned by كُلِّ (everything) in the phrase كُلِّ شَىْءٍ (every thing)
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This word negates the verb تَكْسِبُ (earns) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he earned) to present tense (you earn)
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a word meaning 'all' or 'every'. This word describes شَىْءٍ (thing) in this ayah, showing that Allah is Lord of every single thing.
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of the phrase
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added the tanwin kasra ending to show this soul belongs to or is connected to something else in the sentence
Basic form of the word showing direction or movement towards something
This is not accurate - 'إِل' by itself is not a real Arabic word. 'إِلَّا' (with alif at end) is the complete word meaning 'except'

steps.m: except (negates what came before it in وَلَا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end completes the exception particle. In this ayah, it creates an exception to the statement that came before it about every soul's deeds.
This is a preposition that shows something is physically or metaphorically above something else. We start with this basic form since it's a particle without a root.
The word changes shape to عَلَيْ when we want to add a pronoun to it, just like how we prepare a word before adding 'ing' in English

Added ها which means 'it' when referring to feminine things. Here it refers back to نَفْسٍ (soul/person) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This word connects to تَكْسِبُ (earns), showing what the earning affects.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation. This word negates تَكْسِبُ (earns) in this ayah.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects the negation to the previous statement about Allah being Lord of all things.
This is the basic meaning of carrying or bearing something heavy, especially referring to burdens or responsibilities
Command form - drop the و and add a kasra (ِ) under ز to say 'you carry!'
Adding ت at start changes it from command to 'she does' something
This is the basic root meaning showing the concept of carrying or bearing a burden
Changes from verb (to bear) to noun (bearer) by adding ا after first letter
Makes the word refer to a female by adding ة at the end

steps.m: a female who bears/carries a burden (subject of تَزِرُ)
steps.n: Added tanween (ٌ) to show this is indefinite and is the subject doing the action in the verse
The noun form shows what is being carried as a burden
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This pattern makes abstract ideas into concrete things.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being carried in the phrase 'no bearer bears the burden of another'

steps.m: to delay or come after something
steps.n: This root gives us words about things that come after or are delayed
The vowel marks change to make the word mean 'others' or 'different ones'
Added ى at the end to make it mean 'another' for feminine things (like saying 'another girl')
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a special type of ى. This word describes وِزْرَ (burden) in the ayah, showing that no soul will bear another's burden.

steps.m: then/thereafter (connects this statement to what came before)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. The shadda (double letter mark) makes it stronger, like saying 'and then' instead of just 'then'. Here it connects what Allah will do (tell you about your differences) with what came before.
Basic preposition showing direction or destination
Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a complete thought that connects to the next word. In this ayah, it's showing direction towards 'your Lord' (رَبِّكُم)
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The doubled 'ب' (ba) is a common way to intensify the meaning of the root.  It makes the noun stronger and more emphatic.

steps.m: of the one who nurtures completely
steps.n: The suffix 'ِ' (kasra) shows possession, like saying 'of' or 'belonging to'. It changes رَبّ (lord) to رَبِّ (of the lord)

steps.m: your Lord (referring to all of you being addressed)
steps.n: Adding the possessive pronoun suffix كُم to indicate 'your' (masculine plural)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to show where/when the action happens
Added كُمْ at the end to show it belongs to multiple people. This word connects to رَبِّكُم (your Lord) in the ayah.
The shadda (ّ) doubles the middle letter to show the action is done with extra emphasis or intensity
Adding يُ at the start shows someone (he) is doing this action now or will do it soon

steps.m: and then he tells important news
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this happens after something else, like saying 'and then' in English

steps.m: and then he tells you all important news (about what you disagreed about)
steps.n: Adding كُم shows who receives the news - it's being told to multiple people ('you all'). This connects to بِمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ (about what you used to differ in) in the next part of the verse.

steps.m: with what/that which (relates to تَخْتَلِفُونَ - you differ)
steps.n: This is a combination of two parts: بِ (with) and مَا (what/that which). In this ayah, it connects to the verb تَخْتَلِفُونَ (you differ), showing what people differ about.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he was' to command form 'be!' by shortening the word and changing vowels
Added تُمْ at the end to show this is about 'you all' in the past. This word is the subject of تَخْتَلِفُونَ (you differ) in this ayah.
This is one of the basic prepositions in Arabic that shows location, being inside something, or involvement in something

steps.m: in it (referring to what is being differed about)
steps.n: Added هِ to show what we're talking about. This connects back to the differences mentioned in تَخْتَلِفُونَ (you differ) that comes right after this word in the ayah.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The added letters show that people are disagreeing with each other - it's a back-and-forth action
The ت at the start changes it from 'he' in the past to 'you/she' in the present/future
The ون suffix makes this plural masculine 'you all'
This is a basic pronoun used to refer to a male person or thing (in this case, referring to Allah)
The و at the beginning means 'and', connecting this to what came before. This pronoun refers to Allah who is the subject of the verb جَعَلَ (made/placed) in this ayah.
This is the basic demonstrative form that points to something
Adding ال changes this from pointing at something to connecting parts of a sentence
Final spelling adjustments: The alif becomes ٱ, the lam gets a shadda (ّ), and the ي becomes ى. This word connects 'He' (وَهُوَ) to what He did (جَعَلَكُمْ meaning 'made you')

steps.m: to make something into something else
steps.n: This root means changing one thing into another, like making someone a leader or putting something in a place
Added vowels to show this happened in the past and was done by 'he'

steps.m: he made you all (into خَلٰٓئِفَ successors)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this was done to 'you all'. This connects to the next word خَلٰٓئِفَ which tells what you were made into - successors/representatives on earth

steps.m: to come after or take the place of something
steps.n: This root is about something coming after or replacing something else
Added long ee sound (ي) and ending (ة) to make it mean 'someone who takes another's place'
Changed the word to show many representatives. This is the object of the verb جَعَلَكُمْ (made you) in this ayah.
This root directly refers to the earth or ground we walk on
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the basic noun pattern
Added 'al' to make it specific and kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word continues the actions that Allah did, after جَعَلَ (made/placed) in the ayah.
Added و (and) at the beginning to connect this action with the previous one (جَعَلَ)

steps.m: to separate or divide into parts

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'. This word is being acted upon by the word فَوْقَ (above) in this ayah.

steps.m: some of you (object of فَوْقَ - above)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'you all'. The َ (fatha) before كُمْ is needed to connect it smoothly.
Added vowels to the root letters to make a word meaning 'above' or 'over'

steps.m: above/over (describing the position of بَعْضَكُمْ relative to بَعْضٍ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is describing how Allah raised some people above others in ranks

steps.m: to separate or divide something into parts

steps.n: Changes from root to a noun meaning 'a part'
Added the -in sound at the end to show it belongs to or connects with another word (فَوْقَ)

steps.m: to move up step by step

steps.n: Changed from a verb root meaning 'to climb' into a noun meaning 'steps' by adding vowel marks
Added ة to show one single rank or level
Changed to plural form to show multiple ranks
Added the tanween kasra to show this word is the object being raised in the phrase 'raised some of you above others in ranks'
Added vowels (a) to make it 'he tested' in the past tense
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to silent (sukun) and last vowel to u sound
Added كُمْ to show who is being tested (you all)

steps.m: so that he tests you all
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose (to/so that)

steps.m: so that he tests you all
steps.n: The ل got stronger (doubled) because it came after another ل that merged into it

steps.m: in/within (shows location in relation to ما/what)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects with ما (what) to show 'in what' Allah has given you.
This is a basic particle used to refer to things or actions. Added fatha to show how it's pronounced.

steps.m: what/that which (referring to what Allah gave you)
steps.n: The alif is written with a special elongation mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This word connects to ءَاتَىٰكُمْ (gave you) in the next part of the ayah.
Basic meaning of root letters
Root letters joined together and the ي changed to ى to show past tense 'he came'
Changed initial hamza to make the action affect others (causative)
Added vowel marks and alif khanjariya (small alif) for pronunciation
Added كُمْ to show the action was done to multiple people being spoken to
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that 'your Lord is swift'. The shadda (ّ) on the ن makes the emphasis even stronger.
Added basic vowel pattern to connect the root letters

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares completely and perfectly
Adds كَ to show this is your Lord. The word is in accusative case because it comes after إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
Forms adjective meaning 'quick/swift' to describe the punishment mentioned next in the ayah
The ُ shows this is the first part of a compound description (quick in punishment)

steps.m: to punish, to follow with consequences

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun showing the result of punishment
Added ال meaning 'the' at the start, and ِ at the end to show this word belongs to or describes سَرِيعُ (swift), forming 'swift in punishment'
This is the basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added هُ with a small و (ۥ) after it to show we're talking about 'he/it'
Changed to فَعُول pattern which shows someone who forgives extensively or repeatedly
Adds emphatic لَ prefix to strengthen the meaning
Adding tanween dammah makes the noun indefinite

steps.m: to show mercy, to be kind and caring

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy
Changed from a past-tense verb (he showed mercy) to a strong quality (very merciful) by adding ي in the middle
Added tanween dammah to show this is indefinite - meaning 'a merciful one' rather than 'the merciful one'
The shadda (double letter) was added to make the word stronger, showing Allah's perfect mercy. The tanween was already there.
